Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 Now the earth was without shape and empty
Genesis1:3 God said
Genesis1:4 God saw that the light was good
Genesis1:5 God called the light “day” and the darkness “night.” There was evening
Genesis1:6 God said
Genesis1:7 So God made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above it. It was so.
Genesis1:8 God called the expanse “sky.” There was evening
Genesis1:9 God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry ground “land” and the gathered waters he called “seas.” God saw that it was good.
Genesis1:11 God said
Genesis1:12 The land produced vegetation — plants yielding seeds according to their kinds
Genesis1:13 There was evening
Genesis1:14 God said
Genesis1:15 and let them serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to give light on the earth.” It was so.
Genesis1:16 God made two great lights — the greater light to rule over the day and the lesser light to rule over the night. He made the stars also.
Genesis1:17 God placed the lights in the expanse of the sky to shine on the earth
Genesis1:18 to preside over the day and the night
Genesis1:19 There was evening
Genesis1:20 God said
Genesis1:21 God created the great sea creatures and every living and moving thing with which the water swarmed
Genesis1:22 God blessed them and said
Genesis1:23 There was evening
Genesis1:24 God said
Genesis1:25 God made the wild animals according to their kinds
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 God created humankind in his own image
Genesis1:28 God blessed them and said to them
Genesis1:29 Then God said
Genesis1:30 And to all the animals of the earth
Genesis1:31 God saw all that he had made — and it was very good! There was evening
Genesis2:1 The heavens and the earth were completed with everything that was in them.
Genesis2:2 By the seventh day God finished the work that he had been doing
Genesis2:3 God blessed the seventh day and made it holy because on it he ceased all the work that he had been doing in creation.
Genesis2:4 This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created — when the LORD God made the earth and heavens.
Genesis2:5 Now no shrub of the field had yet grown on the earth
Genesis2:6 Springs would well up from the earth and water the whole surface of the ground.
Genesis2:7 The LORD God formed the man from the soil of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life
Genesis2:8 The LORD God planted an orchard in the east
Genesis2:9 The LORD God made all kinds of trees grow from the soil
Genesis2:10 Now a river flows from Eden to water the orchard
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it runs through the entire land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 (The gold of that land is pure; pearls and lapis lazuli are also there).
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it runs through the entire land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is Tigris; it runs along the east side of Assyria. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 The LORD God took the man and placed him in the orchard in Eden to care for it and to maintain it.
Genesis2:16 Then the LORD God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil
Genesis2:18 The LORD God said
Genesis2:19 The LORD God formed out of the ground every living animal of the field and every bird of the air. He brought them to the man to see what he would name them
Genesis2:20 So the man named all the animals
Genesis2:21 So the LORD God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep
Genesis2:22 Then the LORD God made a woman from the part he had taken out of the man
Genesis2:23 Then the man said
Genesis2:24 That is why a man leaves his father and mother and unites with his wife
Genesis2:25 The man and his wife were both naked
Genesis3:1 Now the serpent was more shrewd than any of the wild animals that the LORD God had made. He said to the woman
Genesis3:2 The woman said to the serpent
Genesis3:3 but concerning the fruit of the tree that is in the middle of the orchard God said
Genesis3:4 The serpent said to the woman
Genesis3:5 for God knows that when you eat from it your eyes will open and you will be like divine beings who know good and evil.”
Genesis3:6 When the woman saw that the tree produced fruit that was good for food
Genesis3:7 Then the eyes of both of them opened
Genesis3:8 Then the man and his wife heard the sound of the LORD God moving about in the orchard at the breezy time of the day
Genesis3:9 But the LORD God called to the man and said to him
Genesis3:10 The man replied
Genesis3:11 And the LORD God said
Genesis3:12 The man said
Genesis3:13 So the LORD God said to the woman
Genesis3:14 The LORD God said to the serpent
Genesis3:15 And I will put hostility between you and the woman and between your offspring and her offspring; her offspring will attack your head
Genesis3:16 To the woman he said
Genesis3:17 But to Adam he said
Genesis3:18 It will produce thorns and thistles for you
Genesis3:19 By the sweat of your brow you will eat food until you return to the ground
Genesis3:20 The man named his wife Eve
Genesis3:21 The LORD God made garments from skin for Adam and his wife
Genesis3:22 And the LORD God said
Genesis3:23 So the LORD God expelled him from the orchard in Eden to cultivate the ground from which he had been taken.
Genesis3:24 When he drove the man out
Genesis4:1 Now the man had marital relations with his wife Eve
Genesis4:2 Then she gave birth to his brother Abel. Abel took care of the flocks
Genesis4:3 At the designated time Cain brought some of the fruit of the ground for an offering to the LORD.
Genesis4:4 But Abel brought some of the firstborn of his flock — even the fattest of them. And the LORD was pleased with Abel and his offering
Genesis4:5 but with Cain and his offering he was not pleased. So Cain became very angry
Genesis4:6 Then the LORD said to Cain
Genesis4:7 Is it not true that if you do what is right
Genesis4:8 Cain said to his brother Abel
Genesis4:9 Then the LORD said to Cain
Genesis4:10 But the LORD said
Genesis4:11 So now
Genesis4:12 When you try to cultivate the ground it will no longer yield its best for you. You will be a homeless wanderer on the earth.”
Genesis4:13 Then Cain said to the LORD
Genesis4:14 Look! You are driving me off the land today
Genesis4:15 But the LORD said to him
Genesis4:16 So Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and lived in the land of Nod
Genesis4:17 Cain had marital relations with his wife
Genesis4:18 To Enoch was born Irad
Genesis4:19 Lamech took two wives for himself; the name of the first was Adah
Genesis4:20 Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the first of those who live in tents and keep livestock.
Genesis4:21 The name of his brother was Jubal; he was the first of all who play the harp and the flute.
Genesis4:22 Now Zillah also gave birth to Tubal-Cain
Genesis4:23 Lamech said to his wives
Genesis4:24 If Cain is to be avenged seven times as much
Genesis4:25 And Adam had marital relations with his wife again
Genesis4:26 And a son was also born to Seth
Genesis5:1 This is the record of the family line of Adam. When God created humankind
Genesis5:2 He created them male and female; when they were created
Genesis5:3 When Adam had lived 130 years he fathered a son in his own likeness
Genesis5:4 The length of time Adam lived after he became the father of Seth was 800 years; during this time he had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:5 The entire lifetime of Adam was 930 years
Genesis5:6 When Seth had lived 105 years
Genesis5:7 Seth lived 807 years after he became the father of Enosh
Genesis5:8 The entire lifetime of Seth was 912 years
Genesis5:9 When Enosh had lived 90 years
Genesis5:10 Enosh lived 815 years after he became the father of Kenan
Genesis5:11 The entire lifetime of Enosh was 905 years
Genesis5:12 When Kenan had lived 70 years
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived 840 years after he became the father of Mahalalel
Genesis5:14 The entire lifetime of Kenan was 910 years
Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel had lived 65 years
Genesis5:16 Mahalalel lived 830 years after he became the father of Jared
Genesis5:17 The entire lifetime of Mahalalel was 895 years
Genesis5:18 When Jared had lived 162 years
Genesis5:19 Jared lived 800 years after he became the father of Enoch
Genesis5:20 The entire lifetime of Jared was 962 years
Genesis5:21 When Enoch had lived 65 years
Genesis5:22 After he became the father of Methuselah
Genesis5:23 The entire lifetime of Enoch was 365 years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch walked with God
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah had lived 187 years
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived 782 years after he became the father of Lamech
Genesis5:27 The entire lifetime of Methuselah was 969 years
Genesis5:28 When Lamech had lived 182 years
Genesis5:29 He named him Noah
Genesis5:30 Lamech lived 595 years after he became the father of Noah
Genesis5:31 The entire lifetime of Lamech was 777 years
Genesis5:32 After Noah was 500 years old
Genesis6:1 When humankind began to multiply on the face of the earth
Genesis6:2 the sons of God saw that the daughters of humankind were beautiful. Thus they took wives for themselves from any they chose.
Genesis6:3 So the LORD said
Genesis6:4 The Nephilim were on the earth in those days (and also after this) when the sons of God were having sexual relations with the daughters of humankind
Genesis6:5 But the LORD saw that the wickedness of humankind had become great on the earth. Every inclination of the thoughts of their minds was only evil all the time.
Genesis6:6 The LORD regretted that he had made humankind on the earth
Genesis6:7 So the LORD said
Genesis6:8 But Noah found favor in the sight of the LORD.
Genesis6:9 This is the account of Noah. Noah was a godly man; he was blameless among his contemporaries. He walked with God.
Genesis6:10 Noah had three sons: Shem
Genesis6:11 The earth was ruined in the sight of God; the earth was filled with violence.
Genesis6:12 God saw the earth
Genesis6:13 So God said to Noah
Genesis6:14 Make for yourself an ark of cypress wood. Make rooms in the ark
Genesis6:15 This is how you should make it: The ark is to be 450 feet long
Genesis6:16 Make a roof for the ark and finish it
Genesis6:17 I am about to bring floodwaters on the earth to destroy from under the sky all the living creatures that have the breath of life in them. Everything that is on the earth will die
Genesis6:18 but I will confirm my covenant with you. You will enter the ark — you
Genesis6:19 You must bring into the ark two of every kind of living creature from all flesh
Genesis6:20 Of the birds after their kinds
Genesis6:21 And you must take for yourself every kind of food that is eaten
Genesis6:22 And Noah did all that God commanded him — he did indeed.
Genesis7:1 The LORD said to Noah
Genesis7:2 You must take with you seven of every kind of clean animal
Genesis7:3 and also seven of every kind of bird in the sky
Genesis7:4 For in seven days I will cause it to rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights
Genesis7:5 And Noah did all that the LORD commanded him.
Genesis7:6 Noah was 600 years old when the floodwaters engulfed the earth.
Genesis7:7 Noah entered the ark along with his sons
Genesis7:8 Pairs of clean animals
Genesis7:9 male and female
Genesis7:10 And after seven days the floodwaters engulfed the earth.
Genesis7:11 In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life
Genesis7:12 And the rain fell on the earth forty days and forty nights.
Genesis7:13 On that very day Noah entered the ark
Genesis7:14 They entered
Genesis7:15 Pairs of all creatures that have the breath of life came into the ark to Noah.
Genesis7:16 Those that entered were male and female
Genesis7:17 The flood engulfed the earth for forty days. As the waters increased
Genesis7:18 The waters completely overwhelmed the earth
Genesis7:19 The waters completely inundated the earth so that even all the high mountains under the entire sky were covered.
Genesis7:20 The waters rose more than twenty feet above the mountains.
Genesis7:21 And all living things that moved on the earth died
Genesis7:22 Everything on dry land that had the breath of life in its nostrils died.
Genesis7:23 So the LORD destroyed every living thing that was on the surface of the ground
Genesis7:24 The waters prevailed over the earth for 150 days.
Genesis8:1 But God remembered Noah and all the wild animals and domestic animals that were with him in the ark. God caused a wind to blow over the earth and the waters receded.
Genesis8:2 The fountains of the deep and the floodgates of heaven were closed
Genesis8:3 The waters kept receding steadily from the earth
Genesis8:4 On the seventeenth day of the seventh month
Genesis8:5 The waters kept on receding until the tenth month. On the first day of the tenth month
Genesis8:6 At the end of forty days
Genesis8:7 and sent out a raven; it kept flying back and forth until the waters had dried up on the earth.
Genesis8:8 Then Noah sent out a dove to see if the waters had receded from the surface of the ground.
Genesis8:9 The dove could not find a resting place for its feet because water still covered the surface of the entire earth
Genesis8:10 He waited seven more days and then sent out the dove again from the ark.
Genesis8:11 When the dove returned to him in the evening
Genesis8:12 He waited another seven days and sent the dove out again
Genesis8:13 In Noah’s six hundred and first year
Genesis8:14 And by the twenty-seventh day of the second month the earth was dry.
Genesis8:15 Then God spoke to Noah and said
Genesis8:16 “Come out of the ark
Genesis8:17 Bring out with you all the living creatures that are with you. Bring out every living thing
Genesis8:18 Noah went out along with his sons
Genesis8:19 Every living creature
Genesis8:20 Noah built an altar to the LORD. He then took some of every kind of clean animal and clean bird and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
Genesis8:21 And the LORD smelled the soothing aroma and said to himself
Genesis8:22 “While the earth continues to exist

Genesis9:4 But you must not eat meat with its life (that is

Genesis17:11 You must circumcise the flesh of your foreskins. This will be a reminder of the covenant between me and you.

Genesis17:13 They must indeed be circumcised
Genesis17:14 Any uncircumcised male who has not been circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin will be cut off from his people — he has failed to carry out my requirement.”

Genesis41:27 The seven lean

Genesis41:29 Seven years of great abundance are coming throughout the whole land of Egypt.
Genesis41:30 But seven years of famine will occur after them
Genesis41:31 The previous abundance of the land will not be remembered because of the famine that follows

Genesis41:33 “So now Pharaoh should look for a wise and discerning man and give him authority over all the land of Egypt.
Genesis41:34 Pharaoh should do this — he should appoint officials throughout the land to collect one-fifth of the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.
Genesis41:35 They should gather all the excess food during these good years that are coming. By Pharaoh’s authority they should store up grain so the cities will have food
Genesis41:36 This food should be held in storage for the land in preparation for the seven years of famine that will occur throughout the land of Egypt. In this way the land will survive the famine.”
Genesis41:37 This advice made sense to Pharaoh and all his officials.

Genesis41:47 During the seven years of abundance the land produced large
Genesis41:48 Joseph collected all the excess food in the land of Egypt during the seven years and stored it in the cities. In every city he put the food gathered from the fields around it.
Genesis41:49 Joseph stored up a vast amount of grain
Genesis41:50 Two sons were born to Joseph before the famine came. Asenath daughter of Potiphera

Genesis41:52 He named the second child Ephraim

Exodus9:2 For if you refuse to release them and continue holding them

Exodus12:8 They will eat the meat the same night; they will eat it roasted over the fire with bread made without yeast and with bitter herbs.
Exodus12:9 Do not eat it raw or boiled in water
Exodus12:10 You must leave nothing until morning
Exodus12:11 This is how you are to eat it — dressed to travel
Exodus12:12 I will pass through the land of Egypt in the same night
Exodus12:13 The blood will be a sign for you on the houses where you are
Exodus12:14 This day will become a memorial for you
Exodus12:15 For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast. Surely on the first day you must put away yeast from your houses because anyone who eats bread made with yeast from the first day to the seventh day will be cut off from Israel.
Exodus12:16 On the first day there will be a holy convocation
Exodus12:17 So you will keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread
Exodus12:18 In the first month
Exodus12:19 For seven days yeast must not be found in your houses
Exodus12:20 You will not eat anything made with yeast; in all the places where you live you must eat bread made without yeast.’”

Exodus21:6 then his master must bring him to the judges

Exodus21:8 If she does not please her master
Exodus21:9 If he designated her for his son
Exodus21:10 If he takes another wife
Exodus21:11 If he does not provide her with these three things
Exodus21:12 “Whoever strikes someone so that he dies must surely be put to death.
Exodus21:13 But if he does not do it with premeditation
Exodus21:14 But if a man willfully attacks his neighbor to kill him cunningly
Exodus21:15 “Whoever strikes his father or his mother must surely be put to death.
Exodus21:16 “Whoever kidnaps someone and sells him
Exodus21:17 “Whoever treats his father or his mother disgracefully must surely be put to death.
Exodus21:18 “If men fight
Exodus21:19 and then if he gets up and walks about outside on his staff
Exodus21:20 “If a man strikes his male servant or his female servant with a staff so that he or she dies as a result of the blow
Exodus21:21 However
Exodus21:22 “If men fight and hit a pregnant woman and her child is born prematurely
Exodus21:23 But if there is serious injury
Exodus21:24 eye for eye
Exodus21:25 burn for burn
Exodus21:26 “If a man strikes the eye of his male servant or his female servant so that he destroys it
Exodus21:27 If he knocks out the tooth of his male servant or his female servant
Exodus21:28 “If an ox gores a man or a woman so that either dies
Exodus21:29 But if the ox had the habit of goring
Exodus21:30 If a ransom is set for him
Exodus21:31 If the ox gores a son or a daughter
Exodus21:32 If the ox gores a male servant or a female servant
Exodus21:33 “If a man opens a pit or if a man digs a pit and does not cover it
Exodus21:34 the owner of the pit must repay the loss. He must give money to its owner
Exodus21:35 If the ox of one man injures the ox of his neighbor so that it dies
Exodus21:36 Or if it is known that the ox had the habit of goring
Exodus22:1 (21:37) “If a man steals an ox or a sheep and kills it or sells it
Exodus22:2 “If a thief is caught breaking in and is struck so that he dies
Exodus22:3 If the sun has risen on him
Exodus22:4 If the stolen item should in fact be found alive in his possession
Exodus22:5 “If a man grazes his livestock in a field or a vineyard
Exodus22:6 “If a fire breaks out and spreads to thorn bushes
Exodus22:7 “If a man gives his neighbor money or articles for safekeeping
Exodus22:8 If the thief is not caught
Exodus22:9 In all cases of illegal possessions
Exodus22:10 If a man gives his neighbor a donkey or an ox or a sheep or any beast to keep
Exodus22:11 then there will be an oath to the LORD between the two of them
Exodus22:12 But if it was stolen from him
Exodus22:13 If it is torn in pieces
Exodus22:14 “If a man borrows an animal from his neighbor
Exodus22:15 If its owner was with it
Exodus22:16 “If a man seduces a virgin who is not engaged and has sexual relations with her
Exodus22:17 If her father refuses to give her to him
Exodus22:18 “You must not allow a sorceress to live.
Exodus22:19 “Whoever has sexual relations with a beast must surely be put to death.
Exodus22:20 “Whoever sacrifices to a god other than the LORD alone must be utterly destroyed.
Exodus22:21 “You must not wrong a foreigner nor oppress him
Exodus22:22 “You must not afflict any widow or orphan.
Exodus22:23 If you afflict them in any way and they cry to me
Exodus22:24 and my anger will burn and I will kill you with the sword
Exodus22:25 “If you lend money to any of my people who are needy among you
Exodus22:26 If you do take the garment of your neighbor in pledge
Exodus22:27 for it is his only covering — it is his garment for his body. What else can he sleep in? And when he cries out to me
Exodus22:28 “You must not blaspheme God or curse the ruler of your people.
Exodus22:29 “Do not hold back offerings from your granaries or your vats. You must give me the firstborn of your sons.
Exodus22:30 You must also do this for your oxen and for your sheep; seven days they may remain with their mothers
Exodus22:31 “You will be holy people to me; you must not eat any meat torn by animals in the field. You must throw it to the dogs.
Exodus23:1 “You must not give a false report. Do not make common cause with the wicked to be a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 “You must not follow a crowd in doing evil things; in a lawsuit you must not offer testimony that agrees with a crowd so as to pervert justice
Exodus23:3 and you must not show partiality to a poor man in his lawsuit.
Exodus23:4 “If you encounter your enemy’s ox or donkey wandering off
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of someone who hates you fallen under its load
Exodus23:6 “You must not turn away justice for your poor people in their lawsuits.
Exodus23:7 Keep your distance from a false charge — do not kill the innocent and the righteous
Exodus23:8 “You must not accept a bribe
Exodus23:9 “You must not oppress a foreigner
Exodus23:10 “For six years you are to sow your land and gather in its produce.
Exodus23:11 But in the seventh year you must let it lie fallow and leave it alone so that the poor of your people may eat
Exodus23:12 For six days you are to do your work
Exodus23:13 “Pay attention to do everything I have told you
Exodus23:14 “Three times in the year you must make a pilgrim feast to me.
Exodus23:15 You are to observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread; seven days you must eat bread made without yeast
Exodus23:16 “You are also to observe the Feast of Harvest
Exodus23:17 At three times in the year all your males will appear before the Lord GOD.
Exodus23:18 “You must not offer the blood of my sacrifice with bread containing yeast; the fat of my festal sacrifice must not remain until morning.
Exodus23:19 The first of the firstfruits of your soil you must bring to the house of the LORD your God. “You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.
Exodus23:20 “I am going to send an angel before you to protect you as you journey and to bring you into the place that I have prepared.
Exodus23:21 Take heed because of him
Exodus23:22 But if you diligently obey him and do all that I command
Exodus23:23 For my angel will go before you and bring you to the Amorites
Exodus23:24 “You must not bow down to their gods; you must not serve them or do according to their practices. Instead you must completely overthrow them and smash their standing stones to pieces.
Exodus23:25 You must serve the LORD your God
Exodus23:26 No woman will miscarry her young or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 “I will send my terror before you
Exodus23:28 I will send hornets before you that will drive out the Hivite
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out before you in one year
Exodus23:30 Little by little I will drive them out before you
Exodus23:31 I will set your boundaries from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 “You must make no covenant with them or with their gods.
Exodus23:33 They must not live in your land
Exodus24:1 But to Moses the LORD said
Exodus24:2 Moses alone may come near the LORD
Exodus24:3 Moses came and told the people all the LORD’s words and all the decisions. All the people answered together
Exodus24:4 and Moses wrote down all the words of the LORD. Early in the morning he built an altar at the foot of the mountain and arranged twelve standing stones — according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
Exodus24:5 He sent young Israelite men
Exodus24:6 Moses took half of the blood and put it in bowls
Exodus24:7 He took the Book of the Covenant and read it aloud to the people
Exodus24:8 So Moses took the blood and splashed it on the people and said
Exodus24:9 Moses and Aaron
Exodus24:10 and they saw the God of Israel. Under his feet there was something like a pavement made of sapphire
Exodus24:11 But he did not lay a hand on the leaders of the Israelites
Exodus24:12 The LORD said to Moses
Exodus24:13 So Moses set out with Joshua his attendant
Exodus24:14 He told the elders
Exodus24:15 Moses went up the mountain
Exodus24:16 The glory of the LORD resided on Mount Sinai
Exodus24:17 Now the appearance of the glory of the LORD was like a devouring fire on the top of the mountain in plain view of the people.
Exodus24:18 Moses went into the cloud when he went up the mountain
Exodus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus25:2 “Tell the Israelites to take an offering for me; from every person motivated by a willing heart you are to receive my offering.
Exodus25:3 This is the offering you are to accept from them: gold
Exodus25:4 blue
Exodus25:5 ram skins dyed red
Exodus25:6 oil for the light
Exodus25:7 onyx stones
Exodus25:8 Let them make for me a sanctuary
Exodus25:9 According to all that I am showing you — the pattern of the tabernacle and the pattern of all its furnishings — you must make it exactly so.
Exodus25:10 “They are to make an ark of acacia wood — its length is to be three feet nine inches
Exodus25:11 You are to overlay it with pure gold — both inside and outside you must overlay it
Exodus25:12 You are to cast four gold rings for it and put them on its four feet
Exodus25:13 You are to make poles of acacia wood
Exodus25:14 and put the poles into the rings at the sides of the ark in order to carry the ark with them.
Exodus25:15 The poles must remain in the rings of the ark; they must not be removed from it.
Exodus25:16 You are to put into the ark the testimony that I will give to you.
Exodus25:17 “You are to make an atonement lid of pure gold; its length is to be three feet nine inches
Exodus25:18 You are to make two cherubim of gold; you are to make them of hammered metal on the two ends of the atonement lid.
Exodus25:19 Make one cherub on one end and one cherub on the other end; from the atonement lid you are to make the cherubim on the two ends.
Exodus25:20 The cherubim are to be spreading their wings upward
Exodus25:21 You are to put the atonement lid on top of the ark
Exodus25:22 I will meet with you there
Exodus25:23 “You are to make a table of acacia wood; its length is to be three feet
Exodus25:24 You are to overlay it with pure gold
Exodus25:25 You are to make a surrounding frame for it about three inches broad
Exodus25:26 You are to make four rings of gold for it and attach the rings at the four corners where its four legs are.
Exodus25:27 The rings are to be close to the frame to provide places for the poles to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 You are to make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold
Exodus25:29 You are to make its plates
Exodus25:30 You are to set the Bread of the Presence on the table before me continually.
Exodus25:31 “You are to make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand is to be made of hammered metal; its base and its shaft
Exodus25:32 Six branches are to extend from the sides of the lampstand
Exodus25:33 Three cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms are to be on one branch
Exodus25:34 On the lampstand there are to be four cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms
Exodus25:35 with a bud under the first two branches from it
Exodus25:36 Their buds and their branches will be one piece
Exodus25:37 “You are to make its seven lamps
Exodus25:38 Its trimmers and its trays are to be of pure gold.
Exodus25:39 About seventy-five pounds of pure gold is to be used for it and for all these utensils.
Exodus25:40 Now be sure to make them according to the pattern you were shown on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 “The tabernacle itself you are to make with ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet; you are to make them with cherubim that are the work of an artistic designer.
Exodus26:2 The length of each curtain is to be forty-two feet
Exodus26:3 Five curtains are to be joined
Exodus26:4 You are to make loops of blue material along the edge of the end curtain in one set
Exodus26:5 You are to make fifty loops on the one curtain
Exodus26:6 You are to make fifty gold clasps and join the curtains together with the clasps
Exodus26:7 “You are to make curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; you are to make eleven curtains.
Exodus26:8 The length of each curtain is to be forty-five feet
Exodus26:9 You are to join five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves. You are to double over the sixth curtain at the front of the tent.
Exodus26:10 You are to make fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in one set and fifty loops along the edge of the curtain that joins the second set.
Exodus26:11 You are to make fifty bronze clasps and put the clasps into the loops and join the tent together so that it is a unit.
Exodus26:12 Now the part that remains of the curtains of the tent — the half curtain that remains will hang over at the back of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:13 The foot and a half on the one side and the foot and a half on the other side of what remains in the length of the curtains of the tent will hang over the sides of the tabernacle
Exodus26:14 “You are to make a covering for the tent out of ram skins dyed red and over that a covering of fine leather.
Exodus26:15 “You are to make the frames for the tabernacle out of acacia wood as uprights.
Exodus26:16 Each frame is to be fifteen feet long
Exodus26:17 with two projections per frame parallel one to another. You are to make all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.
Exodus26:18 So you are to make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side
Exodus26:19 and you are to make forty silver bases to go under the twenty frames — two bases under the first frame for its two projections
Exodus26:20 and for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:21 and their forty silver bases
Exodus26:22 And for the back of the tabernacle on the west you will make six frames.
Exodus26:23 You are to make two frames for the corners of the tabernacle on the back.
Exodus26:24 At the two corners they must be doubled at the lower end and finished together at the top in one ring. So it will be for both.
Exodus26:25 So there are to be eight frames and their silver bases
Exodus26:26 “You are to make bars of acacia wood
Exodus26:27 and five bars for the frames on the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:28 The middle bar in the center of the frames will reach from end to end.
Exodus26:29 You are to overlay the frames with gold and make their rings of gold to provide places for the bars
Exodus26:30 You are to set up the tabernacle according to the plan that you were shown on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 “You are to make a special curtain of blue
Exodus26:32 You are to hang it with gold hooks on four posts of acacia wood overlaid with gold
Exodus26:33 You are to hang this curtain under the clasps and bring the ark of the testimony in there behind the curtain. The curtain will make a division for you between the Holy Place and the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:34 You are to put the atonement lid on the ark of the testimony in the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:35 You are to put the table outside the curtain and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:36 “You are to make a hanging for the entrance of the tent of blue
Exodus26:37 You are to make for the hanging five posts of acacia wood and overlay them with gold

Exodus28:20 and the fourth row

Exodus28:26 You are to make two rings of gold and put them on the other two ends of the breastpiece
Exodus28:27 You are to make two more gold rings and attach them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces on the front of the ephod

Exodus28:30 “You are to put the Urim and the Thummim into the breastpiece of decision; and they are to be over Aaron’s heart when he goes in before the LORD. Aaron is to bear the decisions of the Israelites over his heart before the LORD continually.

Exodus28:32 There is to be an opening in its top in the center of it
Exodus28:33 You are to make pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 The pattern is to be a gold bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 The robe is to be on Aaron as he ministers
Exodus28:36 “You are to make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it the way a seal is engraved: “Holiness to the LORD.”
Exodus28:37 You are to attach to it a blue cord so that it will be on the turban; it is to be on the front of the turban
Exodus28:38 It will be on Aaron’s forehead
Exodus28:39 You are to weave the tunic of fine linen and make the turban of fine linen
Exodus28:40 “For Aaron’s sons you are to make tunics
Exodus28:41 “You are to clothe them — your brother Aaron and his sons with him — and anoint them and ordain them and set them apart as holy
Exodus28:42 Make for them linen undergarments to cover their naked bodies; they must cover from the waist to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 These must be on Aaron and his sons when they enter to the tent of meeting
Exodus29:1 “Now this is what you are to do for them to consecrate them so that they may minister as my priests. Take a young bull and two rams without blemish;
Exodus29:2 and bread made without yeast
Exodus29:3 You are to put them in one basket and present them in the basket
Exodus29:4 “You are to present Aaron and his sons at the entrance of the tent of meeting. You are to wash them with water
Exodus29:5 and take the garments and clothe Aaron with the tunic
Exodus29:6 You are to put the turban on his head and put the holy diadem on the turban.
Exodus29:7 You are to take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 You are to present his sons and clothe them with tunics
Exodus29:9 and wrap the sashes around Aaron and his sons and put headbands on them
Exodus29:10 “You are to present the bull at the front of the tent of meeting
Exodus29:11 You are to kill the bull before the LORD at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Exodus29:12 and take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; all the rest of the blood you are to pour out at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 You are to take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the meat of the bull
Exodus29:15 “You are to take one ram
Exodus29:16 and you are to kill the ram and take its blood and splash it all around on the altar.
Exodus29:17 Then you are to cut the ram into pieces and wash the entrails and its legs and put them on its pieces and on its head
Exodus29:18 and burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the LORD
Exodus29:19 “You are to take the second ram
Exodus29:20 and you are to kill the ram and take some of its blood and put it on the tip of the right ear of Aaron
Exodus29:21 You are to take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron
Exodus29:22 “You are to take from the ram the fat
Exodus29:23 and one round flat cake of bread
Exodus29:24 You are to put all these in Aaron’s hands and in his sons’ hands
Exodus29:25 Then you are to take them from their hands and burn them on the altar for a burnt offering
Exodus29:26 You are to take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s consecration; you are to wave it as a wave offering before the LORD
Exodus29:27 You are to sanctify the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution
Exodus29:28 It is to belong to Aaron and to his sons from the Israelites
Exodus29:29 “The holy garments that belong to Aaron are to belong to his sons after him
Exodus29:30 The priest who succeeds him from his sons
Exodus29:31 “You are to take the ram of the consecration and cook its meat in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons are to eat the meat of the ram and the bread that was in the basket at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:33 They are to eat those things by which atonement was made to consecrate and to set them apart
Exodus29:34 If any of the meat from the consecration offerings or any of the bread is left over until morning
Exodus29:35 “Thus you are to do for Aaron and for his sons
Exodus29:36 Every day you are to prepare a bull for a purification offering for atonement. You are to purge the altar by making atonement for it
Exodus29:37 For seven days you are to make atonement for the altar and set it apart as holy. Then the altar will be most holy. Anything that touches the altar will be holy.
Exodus29:38 “Now this is what you are to prepare on the altar every day continually: two lambs a year old.
Exodus29:39 The first lamb you are to prepare in the morning
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil from pressed olives
Exodus29:41 The second lamb you are to offer around sundown; you are to prepare for it the same meal offering as for the morning and the same drink offering
Exodus29:42 “This will be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD
Exodus29:43 There I will meet with the Israelites
Exodus29:44 “So I will set apart as holy the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus29:45 I will reside among the Israelites
Exodus29:46 and they will know that I am the LORD their God

Exodus30:3 You are to overlay it with pure gold — its top
Exodus30:4 You are to make two gold rings for it under its border
Exodus30:5 You are to make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus30:6 “You are to put it in front of the curtain that is before the ark of the testimony (before the atonement lid that is over the testimony)
Exodus30:7 Aaron is to burn sweet incense on it morning by morning; when he attends to the lamps he is to burn incense.
Exodus30:8 When Aaron sets up the lamps around sundown he is to burn incense on it; it is to be a regular incense offering before the LORD throughout your generations.
Exodus30:9 You must not offer strange incense on it
Exodus30:10 Aaron is to make atonement on its horns once in the year with some of the blood of the sin offering for atonement; once in the year he is to make atonement on it throughout your generations. It is most holy to the LORD.”

Exodus30:13 Everyone who crosses over to those who are numbered is to pay this: a half shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (a shekel weighs twenty gerahs). The half shekel is to be an offering to the LORD.
Exodus30:14 Everyone who crosses over to those numbered
Exodus30:15 The rich are not to increase it
Exodus30:16 You are to receive the atonement money from the Israelites and give it for the service of the tent of meeting. It will be a memorial for the Israelites before the LORD

Exodus30:18 “You are also to make a large bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. You are to put it between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it

Exodus30:20 When they enter the tent of meeting
Exodus30:21 they must wash their hands and their feet so that they do not die. And this will be a perpetual ordinance for them and for their descendants throughout their generations.”

Exodus30:32 It must not be applied to people’s bodies
Exodus30:33 Whoever makes perfume like it and whoever puts any of it on someone not a priest will be cut off from his people.’”

Exodus30:35 and make it into an incense
Exodus30:36 You are to beat some of it very fine and put some of it before the ark of the testimony in the tent of meeting where I will meet with you; it is to be most holy to you.

Exodus34:9 and said
Exodus34:10 He said
Exodus34:11 “Obey what I am commanding you this day. I am going to drive out before you the Amorite
Exodus34:12 Be careful not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land where you are going
Exodus34:13 Rather you must destroy their altars
Exodus34:14 For you must not worship any other god
Exodus34:15 Be careful not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land
Exodus34:16 and you then take his daughters for your sons
Exodus34:17 You must not make yourselves molten gods.
Exodus34:18 “You must keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast
Exodus34:19 “Every firstborn of the womb belongs to me
Exodus34:20 Now the firstling of a donkey you may redeem with a lamb
Exodus34:21 “On six days you may labor
Exodus34:22 “You must observe the Feast of Weeks — the firstfruits of the harvest of wheat — and the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year.
Exodus34:23 At three times in the year all your men must appear before the Lord GOD
Exodus34:24 For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders; no one will covet your land when you go up to appear before the LORD your God three times in the year.
Exodus34:25 “You must not offer the blood of my sacrifice with yeast; the sacrifice from the feast of Passover must not remain until the following morning.
Exodus34:26 “The first of the firstfruits of your soil you must bring to the house of the LORD your God. You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
Exodus34:27 The LORD said to Moses
Exodus34:28 So he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread
Exodus34:29 Now when Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand — when he came down from the mountain
Exodus34:30 When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses
Exodus34:31 But Moses called to them
Exodus34:32 After this all the Israelites approached
Exodus34:33 When Moses finished speaking with them
Exodus34:34 But when Moses went in before the LORD to speak with him
Exodus34:35 When the Israelites would see the face of Moses
Exodus35:1 Moses assembled the whole community of the Israelites and said to them
Exodus35:2 In six days work may be done
Exodus35:3 You must not kindle a fire in any of your homes on the Sabbath day.”
Exodus35:4 Moses spoke to the whole community of the Israelites
Exodus35:5 ‘Take an offering for the LORD. Let everyone who has a willing heart bring an offering to the LORD: gold
Exodus35:6 blue
Exodus35:7 ram skins dyed red
Exodus35:8 olive oil for the light
Exodus35:9 onyx stones
Exodus35:10 Every skilled person among you is to come and make all that the LORD has commanded:
Exodus35:11 the tabernacle with its tent
Exodus35:12 the ark
Exodus35:13 the table with its poles and all its vessels
Exodus35:14 the lampstand for the light and its accessories
Exodus35:15 and the altar of incense with its poles
Exodus35:16 the altar for the burnt offering with its bronze grating that is on it
Exodus35:17 the hangings of the courtyard
Exodus35:18 tent pegs for the tabernacle and tent pegs for the courtyard and their ropes;
Exodus35:19 the woven garments for serving in the holy place
Exodus35:20 So the whole community of the Israelites went out from the presence of Moses.
Exodus35:21 Everyone whose heart stirred him to action and everyone whose spirit was willing came and brought the offering for the LORD for the work of the tent of meeting
Exodus35:22 They came
Exodus35:23 Everyone who had blue
Exodus35:24 Everyone making an offering of silver or bronze brought it as an offering to the LORD
Exodus35:25 Every woman who was skilled spun with her hands and brought what she had spun
Exodus35:26 and all the women whose heart stirred them to action and who were skilled spun goats’ hair.
Exodus35:27 The leaders brought onyx stones and other gems to be mounted for the ephod and the breastpiece
Exodus35:28 and spices and olive oil for the light
Exodus35:29 The Israelites brought a freewill offering to the LORD
Exodus35:30 Moses said to the Israelites
Exodus35:31 He has filled him with the Spirit of God — with skill
Exodus35:32 to design artistic designs
Exodus35:33 and in cutting stones for their setting
Exodus35:34 And he has put it in his heart to teach
Exodus35:35 He has filled them with skill to do all kinds of work as craftsmen
Exodus36:1 So Bezalel and Oholiab and every skilled person in whom the LORD has put skill and ability to know how to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary are to do the work according to all that the LORD has commanded.”
Exodus36:2 Moses summoned Bezalel and Oholiab and every skilled person in whom the LORD had put skill — everyone whose heart stirred him to volunteer to do the work
Exodus36:3 and they received from Moses all the offerings the Israelites had brought to do the work for the service of the sanctuary
Exodus36:4 So all the skilled people who were doing all the work on the sanctuary came from the work they were doing
Exodus36:5 and told Moses
Exodus36:6 Moses instructed them to take his message throughout the camp
Exodus36:7 Now the materials were more than enough for them to do all the work.
Exodus36:8 All the skilled among those who were doing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet; they were made with cherubim that were the work of an artistic designer.
Exodus36:9 The length of one curtain was forty-two feet
Exodus36:10 He joined five of the curtains to one another
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue material along the edge of the end curtain in the first set; he did the same along the edge of the end curtain in the second set.
Exodus36:12 He made fifty loops on the first curtain
Exodus36:13 He made fifty gold clasps and joined the curtains together to one another with the clasps
Exodus36:14 He made curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains.
Exodus36:15 The length of one curtain was forty-five feet
Exodus36:16 He joined five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves.
Exodus36:17 He made fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set and fifty loops along the edge of the curtain that joined the second set.
Exodus36:18 He made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together so that it might be a unit.
Exodus36:19 He made a covering for the tent out of ram skins dyed red and over that a covering of fine leather.
Exodus36:20 He made the frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood as uprights.
Exodus36:21 The length of each frame was fifteen feet
Exodus36:22 with two projections per frame parallel one to another. He made all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.
Exodus36:23 So he made frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side.
Exodus36:24 He made forty silver bases under the twenty frames — two bases under the first frame for its two projections
Exodus36:25 and for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 and their forty silver bases
Exodus36:27 And for the back of the tabernacle on the west he made six frames.
Exodus36:28 He made two frames for the corners of the tabernacle on the back.
Exodus36:29 At the two corners they were doubled at the lower end and finished together at the top in one ring. So he did for both.
Exodus36:30 So there were eight frames and their silver bases
Exodus36:31 He made bars of acacia wood
Exodus36:32 and five bars for the frames on the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:33 He made the middle bar to reach from end to end in the center of the frames.
Exodus36:34 He overlaid the frames with gold and made their rings of gold to provide places for the bars
Exodus36:35 He made the special curtain of blue
Exodus36:36 He made for it four posts of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold
Exodus36:37 He made a hanging for the entrance of the tent of blue
Exodus36:38 and its five posts and their hooks. He overlaid their tops and their bands with gold
Exodus37:1 Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood; its length was three feet nine inches
Exodus37:2 He overlaid it with pure gold
Exodus37:3 He cast four gold rings for it that he put on its four feet
Exodus37:4 He made poles of acacia wood
Exodus37:5 and put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark in order to carry the ark.
Exodus37:6 He made an atonement lid of pure gold; its length was three feet nine inches
Exodus37:7 He made two cherubim of gold; he made them of hammered metal on the two ends of the atonement lid
Exodus37:8 one cherub on one end and one cherub on the other end. He made the cherubim from the atonement lid on its two ends.
Exodus37:9 The cherubim were spreading their wings upward
Exodus37:10 He made the table of acacia wood; its length was three feet
Exodus37:11 He overlaid it with pure gold
Exodus37:12 He made a surrounding frame for it about three inches wide
Exodus37:13 He cast four gold rings for it and attached the rings at the four corners where its four legs were.
Exodus37:14 The rings were close to the frame to provide places for the poles to carry the table.
Exodus37:15 He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold
Exodus37:16 He made the vessels which were on the table out of pure gold
Exodus37:17 He made the lampstand of pure gold. He made the lampstand of hammered metal; its base and its shaft
Exodus37:18 Six branches were extending from its sides
Exodus37:19 Three cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms were on the first branch
Exodus37:20 On the lampstand there were four cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms
Exodus37:21 with a bud under the first two branches from it
Exodus37:22 Their buds and their branches were of one piece; all of it was one hammered piece of pure gold.
Exodus37:23 He made its seven lamps
Exodus37:24 He made the lampstand and all its accessories with seventy-five pounds of pure gold.
Exodus37:25 He made the incense altar of acacia wood. Its length was a foot and a half and its width a foot and a half — a square — and its height was three feet. Its horns were of one piece with it.
Exodus37:26 He overlaid it with pure gold — its top
Exodus37:27 He also made two gold rings for it under its border
Exodus37:28 He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:29 He made the sacred anointing oil and the pure fragrant incense
Exodus38:1 He made the altar for the burnt offering of acacia wood seven feet six inches long and seven feet six inches wide — it was square — and its height was four feet six inches.
Exodus38:2 He made its horns on its four corners; its horns were part of it
Exodus38:3 He made all the utensils of the altar — the pots
Exodus38:4 He made a grating for the altar
Exodus38:5 He cast four rings for the four corners of the bronze grating
Exodus38:6 He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
Exodus38:7 He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar
Exodus38:8 He made the large basin of bronze and its pedestal of bronze from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Exodus38:9 He made the courtyard. For the south side the hangings of the courtyard were of fine twisted linen
Exodus38:10 with their twenty posts and their twenty bronze bases
Exodus38:11 For the north side the hangings were one hundred fifty feet
Exodus38:12 For the west side there were hangings seventy-five feet long
Exodus38:13 For the east side
Exodus38:14 with hangings on one side of the gate that were twenty-two and a half feet long
Exodus38:15 and for the second side of the gate of the courtyard
Exodus38:16 All the hangings around the courtyard were of fine twisted linen.
Exodus38:17 The bases for the posts were bronze. The hooks of the posts and their bands were silver
Exodus38:18 The curtain for the gate of the courtyard was of blue
Exodus38:19 with four posts and their four bronze bases. Their hooks and their bands were silver
Exodus38:20 All the tent pegs of the tabernacle and of the courtyard all around were bronze.
Exodus38:21 This is the inventory of the tabernacle
Exodus38:22 Now Bezalel son of Uri
Exodus38:23 and with him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus38:24 All the gold that was used for the work
Exodus38:25 The silver of those who were numbered of the community was one hundred talents and 1
Exodus38:26 one beka per person
Exodus38:27 The one hundred talents of silver were used for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the special curtain — one hundred bases for one hundred talents
Exodus38:28 From the remaining 1
Exodus38:29 The bronze of the wave offering was seventy talents and 2
Exodus38:30 With it he made the bases for the door of the tent of meeting
Exodus38:31 the bases for the courtyard all around
Exodus39:1 From the blue
Exodus39:2 He made the ephod of gold
Exodus39:3 They hammered the gold into thin sheets and cut it into narrow strips to weave them into the blue

Exodus39:7 He put them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as stones of memorial for the Israelites

Exodus39:9 It was square — they made the breastpiece doubled
Exodus39:10 They set on it four rows of stones: a row with a ruby
Exodus39:11 and the second row
Exodus39:12 and the third row
Exodus39:13 and the fourth row
Exodus39:14 The stones were for the names of the sons of Israel

Exodus39:16 and they made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings
Exodus39:17 They attached the two gold chains to the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece;
Exodus39:18 the other two ends of the two chains they attached to the two settings
Exodus39:19 They made two rings of gold and put them on the other two ends of the breastpiece on its edge
Exodus39:20 They made two more gold rings and attached them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces on the front of the ephod
Exodus39:21 They tied the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod by blue cord
Exodus39:22 He made the robe of the ephod completely blue
Exodus39:23 There was an opening in the center of the robe
Exodus39:24 They made pomegranates of blue
Exodus39:25 They made bells of pure gold and attached the bells between the pomegranates around the hem of the robe between the pomegranates.
Exodus39:26 There was a bell and a pomegranate
Exodus39:27 They made tunics of fine linen — the work of a weaver
Exodus39:28 and the turban of fine linen
Exodus39:29 The sash was of fine twisted linen and blue
Exodus39:30 They made a plate
Exodus39:31 They attached to it a blue cord
Exodus39:32 So all the work of the tabernacle

Exodus39:38 and the gold altar
