Exodus39:40 the hangings of the courtyard
Exodus39:41 the woven garments for serving in the sanctuary
Exodus39:42 The Israelites did all the work according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 Moses inspected all the work — and they had done it just as the LORD had commanded — they had done it exactly — and Moses blessed them.
Exodus40:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus40:2 “On the first day of the first month you are to set up the tabernacle
Exodus40:3 You are to place the ark of the testimony in it and shield the ark with the special curtain.
Exodus40:4 You are to bring in the table and set out the things that belong on it; then you are to bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.
Exodus40:5 You are to put the gold altar for incense in front of the ark of the testimony and put the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:6 You are to put the altar for the burnt offering in front of the entrance to the tabernacle
Exodus40:7 You are to put the large basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.
Exodus40:8 You are to set up the courtyard around it and put the curtain at the gate of the courtyard.
Exodus40:9 And take the anointing oil
Exodus40:10 Then you are to anoint the altar for the burnt offering with all its utensils; you are to sanctify the altar
Exodus40:11 You must also anoint the large basin and its pedestal
Exodus40:12 “You are to bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 Then you are to clothe Aaron with the holy garments and anoint him and sanctify him so that he may minister as my priest.
Exodus40:14 You are to bring his sons and clothe them with tunics
Exodus40:15 and anoint them just as you anointed their father
Exodus40:16 This is what Moses did
Exodus40:17 So the tabernacle was set up on the first day of the first month
Exodus40:18 When Moses set up the tabernacle and put its bases in place
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent over it
Exodus40:20 He took the testimony and put it in the ark
Exodus40:21 And he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 And he put the table in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:23 And he set the bread in order on it before the LORD
Exodus40:24 And he put the lampstand in the tent of meeting opposite the table
Exodus40:25 Then he set up the lamps before the LORD
Exodus40:26 And he put the gold altar in the tent of meeting in front of the curtain
Exodus40:27 and he burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 Then he put the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 He also put the altar for the burnt offering by the entrance to the tabernacle
Exodus40:30 Then he put the large basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 Moses and Aaron and his sons would wash their hands and their feet from it.
Exodus40:32 Whenever they entered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 And he set up the courtyard around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled on it and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
Exodus40:36 But when the cloud was lifted up from the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 but if the cloud was not lifted up
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 Then the LORD called to Moses and spoke to him from the Meeting Tent:
Leviticus1:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus1:3 “‘If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd he must present it as a flawless male; he must present it at the entrance of the Meeting Tent for its acceptance before the LORD.
Leviticus1:4 He must lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 Then the one presenting the offering must slaughter the bull before the LORD
Leviticus1:6 Next
Leviticus1:7 and the sons of Aaron
Leviticus1:8 Then the sons of Aaron
Leviticus1:9 Finally
Leviticus1:10 “‘If his offering is from the flock for a burnt offering — from the sheep or the goats — he must present a flawless male
Leviticus1:11 and must slaughter it on the north side of the altar before the LORD
Leviticus1:12 Next
Leviticus1:13 Then the one presenting the offering must wash the entrails and the legs in water
Leviticus1:14 “‘If his offering to the LORD is a burnt offering from the birds
Leviticus1:15 The priest must present it at the altar
Leviticus1:16 Then the priest must remove its entrails by cutting off its tail feathers
Leviticus1:17 and tear it open by its wings without dividing it into two parts. Finally
Leviticus2:1 “‘When a person presents a grain offering to the LORD
Leviticus2:2 Then he must bring it to the sons of Aaron
Leviticus2:3 The remainder of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and to his sons — it is most holy from the gifts of the LORD.
Leviticus2:4 “‘When you present an offering of grain baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 If your offering is a grain offering made on the griddle
Leviticus2:6 Crumble it in pieces and pour olive oil on it — it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is a grain offering made in a pan
Leviticus2:8 “‘You must bring the grain offering that must be made from these to the LORD. Present it to the priest
Leviticus2:9 Then the priest must take up from the grain offering its memorial portion and offer it up in smoke on the altar — it is a gift of a soothing aroma to the LORD.
Leviticus2:10 The remainder of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and to his sons — it is most holy from the gifts of the LORD.
Leviticus2:11 “‘No grain offering which you present to the LORD can be made with yeast
Leviticus2:12 You can present them to the LORD as an offering of first fruit
Leviticus2:13 Moreover
Leviticus2:14 “‘If you present a grain offering of first ripe grain to the LORD
Leviticus2:15 And you must put olive oil on it and set frankincense on it — it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 Then the priest must offer its memorial portion up in smoke — some of its crushed bits
Leviticus3:1 “‘Now if his offering is a peace offering sacrifice
Leviticus3:2 He must lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the Meeting Tent
Leviticus3:3 Then the one presenting the offering must present a gift to the LORD from the peace offering sacrifice: He must remove the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that surrounds the entrails
Leviticus3:4 the two kidneys with the fat on their sinews
Leviticus3:5 Then the sons of Aaron must offer it up in smoke on the altar atop the burnt offering that is on the wood in the fire as a gift of a soothing aroma to the LORD.
Leviticus3:6 “‘If his offering for a peace offering sacrifice to the LORD is from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he presents a sheep as his offering
Leviticus3:8 He must lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it before the Meeting Tent
Leviticus3:9 Then he must present a gift to the LORD from the peace offering sacrifice: He must remove all the fatty tail up to the end of the spine
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys with the fat on their sinews
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest must offer it up in smoke on the altar as a food gift to the LORD.
Leviticus3:12 “‘If his offering is a goat he must present it before the LORD
Leviticus3:13 lay his hand on its head
Leviticus3:14 Then he must present from it his offering as a gift to the LORD: the fat which covers the entrails and all the fat on the entrails
Leviticus3:15 the two kidneys with the fat on their sinews
Leviticus3:16 Then the priest must offer them up in smoke on the altar as a food gift for a soothing aroma — all the fat belongs to the LORD.
Leviticus3:17 This is a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all the places where you live: You must never eat any fat or any blood.’”
Leviticus4:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus4:2 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus4:3 “‘If the high priest sins so that the people are guilty
Leviticus4:4 He must bring the bull to the entrance of the Meeting Tent before the LORD
Leviticus4:5 Then that high priest must take some of the blood of the bull and bring it to the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus4:6 The priest must dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of it seven times before the LORD toward the front of the veil-canopy of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 The priest must put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense that is before the LORD in the Meeting Tent
Leviticus4:8 “‘Then he must take up all the fat from the sin offering bull: the fat covering the entrails and all the fat surrounding the entrails
Leviticus4:9 the two kidneys with the fat on their sinews
Leviticus4:10 — just as it is taken from the ox of the peace offering sacrifice — and the priest must offer them up in smoke on the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the hide of the bull
Leviticus4:12 all the rest of the bull — he must bring outside the camp to a ceremonially clean place
Leviticus4:13 “‘If the whole congregation of Israel strays unintentionally and the matter is not noticed by the assembly
Leviticus4:14 the assembly must present a young bull for a sin offering when the sin they have committed becomes known. They must bring it before the Meeting Tent
Leviticus4:15 the elders of the congregation must lay their hands on the head of the bull before the LORD
Leviticus4:16 Then the high priest must bring some of the blood of the bull to the Meeting Tent
Leviticus4:17 and that priest must dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the LORD toward the front of the veil-canopy.
Leviticus4:18 He must put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is before the LORD in the Meeting Tent
Leviticus4:19 “‘Then the priest must take all its fat and offer the fat up in smoke on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He must do with the rest of the bull just as he did with the bull of the sin offering; this is what he must do with it. So the priest will make atonement on their behalf and they will be forgiven.
Leviticus4:21 He must bring the rest of the bull outside the camp and burn it just as he burned the first bull — it is the sin offering of the assembly.
Leviticus4:22 “‘Whenever a leader
Leviticus4:23 or his sin that he committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:24 He must lay his hand on the head of the male goat and slaughter it in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered before the LORD — it is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest must take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:26 Then the priest must offer all of its fat up in smoke on the altar like the fat of the peace offering sacrifice. So the priest will make atonement on his behalf for his sin and he will be forgiven.
Leviticus4:27 “‘If an ordinary individual sins by straying unintentionally when he violates one of the Lord’s commandments which must not be violated
Leviticus4:28 or his sin that he committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:29 He must lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter the sin offering in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
Leviticus4:30 Then the priest must take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:31 Then he must remove all of its fat (just as fat was removed from the peace offering sacrifice) and the priest must offer it up in smoke on the altar for a soothing aroma to the LORD. So the priest will make atonement on his behalf and he will be forgiven.
Leviticus4:32 “‘But if he brings a sheep as his offering
Leviticus4:33 He must lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it for a sin offering in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
Leviticus4:34 Then the priest must take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:35 Then the one who brought the offering must remove all its fat (just as the fat of the sheep is removed from the peace offering sacrifice) and the priest must offer them up in smoke on the altar on top of the other gifts of the LORD. So the priest will make atonement on his behalf for his sin which he has committed and he will be forgiven.
Leviticus5:1 “‘When a person sins in that he hears a public curse against one who fails to testify and he is a witness (he either saw or knew what had happened ) and he does not make it known
Leviticus5:2 Or when there is a person who touches anything ceremonially unclean
Leviticus5:3 or when he touches human uncleanness with regard to anything by which he can become unclean
Leviticus5:4 or when a person swears an oath
Leviticus5:5 when an individual becomes guilty with regard to one of these things he must confess how he has sinned
Leviticus5:6 and he must bring his penalty for guilt to the LORD for his sin that he has committed
Leviticus5:7 “‘If he cannot afford an animal from the flock
Leviticus5:8 He must bring them to the priest and present first the one that is for a sin offering. The priest must pinch its head at the nape of its neck
Leviticus5:9 Then he must sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the wall of the altar
Leviticus5:10 The second bird he must make a burnt offering according to the standard regulation. So the priest will make atonement on behalf of this person for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:11 “‘If he cannot afford two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 He must bring it to the priest and the priest must scoop out from it a handful as its memorial portion and offer it up in smoke on the altar on top of the other gifts of the LORD — it is a sin offering.
Leviticus5:13 So the priest will make atonement on his behalf for his sin which he has committed by doing one of these things
Leviticus5:14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus5:15 “When a person commits a trespass and sins by straying unintentionally from the regulations about the Lord’s holy things
Leviticus5:16 And whatever holy thing he violated he must restore and must add one fifth to it and give it to the priest. So the priest will make atonement on his behalf with the guilt offering ram and he will be forgiven.”
Leviticus5:17 “If a person sins and violates any of the Lord’s commandments which must not be violated (although he did not know it at the time
Leviticus5:18 and must bring a flawless ram from the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; he was surely guilty before the LORD.”
Leviticus6:1 (5:20) Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:2 “When a person sins and commits a trespass against the LORD by deceiving his fellow citizen in regard to something held in trust
Leviticus6:3 or has found something lost and denies it and swears falsely concerning any one of the things that someone might do to sin —
Leviticus6:4 when it happens that he sins and he is found guilty
Leviticus6:5 or anything about which he swears falsely. He must restore it in full and add one fifth to it; he must give it to its owner when he is found guilty.
Leviticus6:6 Then he must bring his guilt offering to the LORD
Leviticus6:7 So the priest will make atonement on his behalf before the LORD and he will be forgiven for whatever he has done to become guilty.”
Leviticus6:8 (6:1) Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:9 “Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 Then the priest must put on his linen robe and must put linen leggings over his bare flesh
Leviticus6:11 Then he must take off his clothes and put on other clothes
Leviticus6:12 but the fire which is on the altar must be kept burning on it. It must not be extinguished. So the priest must kindle wood on it morning by morning
Leviticus6:13 A continual fire must be kept burning on the altar. It must not be extinguished.
Leviticus6:14 “‘This is the law of the grain offering. The sons of Aaron are to present it before the LORD in front of the altar
Leviticus6:15 and the priest must take up with his hand some of the choice wheat flour of the grain offering and some of its olive oil
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons are to eat what is left over from it. It must be eaten unleavened in a holy place; they are to eat it in the courtyard of the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus6:17 It must not be baked with yeast. I have given it as their portion from my gifts. It is most holy
Leviticus6:18 Every male among the sons of Aaron may eat it. It is a perpetual allotted portion throughout your generations from the gifts of the LORD. Anyone who touches these gifts must be holy.’”
Leviticus6:19 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:20 “This is the offering of Aaron and his sons which they must present to the LORD on the day when he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of choice wheat flour as a continual grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It must be made with olive oil on a griddle and you must bring it well soaked
Leviticus6:22 The high priest who succeeds him from among his sons must do it. It is a perpetual statute; it must be offered up in smoke as a whole offering to the LORD.
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering of a priest must be a whole offering; it must not be eaten.”
Leviticus6:24 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:25 “Tell Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it for sin is to eat it. It must be eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Anyone who touches its meat must be holy
Leviticus6:28 Any clay vessel it is boiled in must be broken
Leviticus6:29 Any male among the priests may eat it. It is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But any sin offering from which some of its blood is brought into the Meeting Tent to make atonement in the sanctuary must not be eaten. It must be burned up in the fire.
Leviticus7:1 “‘This is the law of the guilt offering. It is most holy.
Leviticus7:2 In the place where they slaughter the burnt offering they must slaughter the guilt offering
Leviticus7:3 Then the one making the offering must present all its fat: the fatty tail
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys and the fat on their sinews
Leviticus7:5 Then the priest must offer them up in smoke on the altar as a gift to the LORD. It is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Any male among the priests may eat it. It must be eaten in a holy place. It is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 The law is the same for the sin offering and the guilt offering; it belongs to the priest who makes atonement with it.
Leviticus7:8 “‘As for the priest who presents someone’s burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 Every grain offering which is baked in the oven or made in the pan or on the griddle belongs to the priest who presented it.
Leviticus7:10 Every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 “‘This is the law of the peace offering sacrifice which he is to present to the LORD.
Leviticus7:12 If he presents it on account of thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 He must present this grain offering in addition to ring-shaped loaves of leavened bread which regularly accompany the sacrifice of his thanksgiving peace offering.
Leviticus7:14 He must present one of each kind of grain offering as a contribution offering to the LORD; it belongs to the priest who splashes the blood of the peace offering.
Leviticus7:15 The meat of his thanksgiving peace offering must be eaten on the day of his offering; he must not set any of it aside until morning.
Leviticus7:16 “‘If his offering is a votive or freewill sacrifice
Leviticus7:17 but the leftovers from the meat of the sacrifice must be burned up in the fire on the third day.
Leviticus7:18 If some of the meat of his peace offering sacrifice is ever eaten on the third day it will not be accepted; it will not be accounted to the one who presented it
Leviticus7:19 The meat which touches anything ceremonially unclean must not be eaten; it must be burned up in the fire. As for ceremonially clean meat
Leviticus7:20 The person who eats meat from the peace offering sacrifice which belongs to the LORD while his uncleanness persists will be cut off from his people.
Leviticus7:21 When a person touches anything unclean (whether human uncleanness
Leviticus7:22 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus7:23 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus7:24 Moreover
Leviticus7:25 If anyone eats fat from the animal from which he presents a gift to the LORD
Leviticus7:26 And you must not eat any blood of the birds or the domesticated land animals in any of the places where you live.
Leviticus7:27 Any person who eats any blood — that person will be cut off from his people.’”
Leviticus7:28 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus7:29 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus7:30 With his own hands he must bring the LORD’s gifts. He must bring the fat with the breast to wave the breast as a wave offering before the LORD
Leviticus7:31 and the priest must offer the fat up in smoke on the altar
Leviticus7:32 The right thigh you must give as a contribution offering to the priest from your peace offering sacrifices.
Leviticus7:33 The one from Aaron’s sons who presents the blood of the peace offering and fat will have the right thigh as his share
Leviticus7:34 for the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution offering I have taken from the Israelites out of their peace offering sacrifices and have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons from the people of Israel as a perpetual allotted portion.’”
Leviticus7:35 This is the allotment of Aaron and the allotment of his sons from the LORD’s gifts on the day Moses presented them to serve as priests to the LORD.
Leviticus7:36 This is what the LORD commanded to give to them from the Israelites on the day Moses anointed them — a perpetual allotted portion throughout their generations.
Leviticus7:37 This is the law for the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai on the day he commanded the Israelites to present their offerings to the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai.
Leviticus8:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus8:2 “Take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 and assemble the whole congregation at the entrance of the Meeting Tent.”
Leviticus8:4 So Moses did just as the LORD commanded him
Leviticus8:5 Then Moses said to the congregation: “This is what the LORD has commanded to be done.”
Leviticus8:6 So Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 Then he put the tunic on Aaron
Leviticus8:8 He then set the breastpiece on him and put the Urim and Thummim into the breastpiece.
Leviticus8:9 Finally
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and everything in it
Leviticus8:11 Next he sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times and so anointed the altar
Leviticus8:12 He then poured some of the anointing oil on the head of Aaron and anointed him to consecrate him.
Leviticus8:13 Moses also brought forward Aaron’s sons
Leviticus8:14 Then he brought near the sin offering bull and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the sin offering bull
Leviticus8:15 and he slaughtered it. Moses then took the blood and put it all around on the horns of the altar with his finger and decontaminated the altar
Leviticus8:16 Then he took all the fat on the entrails
Leviticus8:17 but the rest of the bull — its hide
Leviticus8:18 Then he presented the burnt offering ram and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram
Leviticus8:19 and he slaughtered it. Moses then splashed the blood against the altar’s sides.
Leviticus8:20 Then he cut the ram into parts
Leviticus8:21 but the entrails and the legs he washed with water
Leviticus8:22 Then he presented the second ram
Leviticus8:23 and he slaughtered it. Moses then took some of its blood and put it on Aaron’s right earlobe
Leviticus8:24 Next he brought Aaron’s sons forward
Leviticus8:25 Then he took the fat (the fatty tail
Leviticus8:26 and from the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD he took one unleavened loaf
Leviticus8:27 He then put all of them on the palms of Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:28 Moses then took them from their palms and offered them up in smoke on the altar on top of the burnt offering — they were an ordination offering for a soothing aroma; it was a gift to the LORD.
Leviticus8:29 Finally
Leviticus8:30 Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood which was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aaron and his garments
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 but the remainder of the meat and the bread you must burn with fire.
Leviticus8:33 And you must not go out from the entrance of the Meeting Tent for seven days
Leviticus8:34 What has been done on this day the LORD has commanded to be done to make atonement for you.
Leviticus8:35 You must reside at the entrance of the Meeting Tent day and night for seven days and keep the charge of the LORD so that you will not die
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did all the things the LORD had commanded through Moses.
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel
Leviticus9:2 and said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 Then tell the Israelites: ‘Take a male goat for a sin offering and a calf and lamb
Leviticus9:4 and an ox and a ram for peace offerings to sacrifice before the LORD
Leviticus9:5 So they took what Moses had commanded to the front of the Meeting Tent and the whole congregation presented them and stood before the LORD.
Leviticus9:6 Then Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Moses then said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron approached the altar and slaughtered the sin offering calf which was for himself.
Leviticus9:9 Then Aaron’s sons presented the blood to him and he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar
Leviticus9:10 The fat and the kidneys and the protruding lobe of the liver from the sin offering he offered up in smoke on the altar just as the LORD had commanded Moses
Leviticus9:11 but the flesh and the hide he completely burned up outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 He then slaughtered the burnt offering
Leviticus9:13 The burnt offering itself they handed to him by its parts
Leviticus9:14 and he washed the entrails and the legs and offered them up in smoke on top of the burnt offering on the altar.
Leviticus9:15 Then he presented the people’s offering. He took the sin offering male goat which was for the people
Leviticus9:16 He then presented the burnt offering
Leviticus9:17 Next he presented the grain offering
Leviticus9:18 Then he slaughtered the ox and the ram — the peace offering sacrifices which were for the people — and Aaron’s sons handed the blood to him and he splashed it against the altar’s sides.
Leviticus9:19 As for the fat parts from the ox and from the ram (the fatty tail
Leviticus9:20 they set those on the breasts and he offered the fat parts up in smoke on the altar.
Leviticus9:21 Finally Aaron waved the breasts and the right thigh as a wave offering before the LORD just as Moses had commanded.
Leviticus9:22 Then Aaron lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them and descended from making the sin offering
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron then entered into the Meeting Tent. When they came out
Leviticus9:24 Then fire went out from the presence of the LORD and consumed the burnt offering and the fat parts on the altar

Leviticus10:2 So fire went out from the presence of the LORD and consumed them so that they died before the LORD.
Leviticus10:3 Moses then said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Moses then called to Mishael and Elzaphan
Leviticus10:5 So they came near and carried them away in their tunics to a place outside the camp just as Moses had spoken.
Leviticus10:6 Then Moses said to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar his other two sons
Leviticus10:7 but you must not go out from the entrance of the Meeting Tent lest you die
Leviticus10:8 Then the LORD spoke to Aaron
Leviticus10:9 “Do not drink wine or strong drink
Leviticus10:10 as well as to distinguish between the holy and the common
Leviticus10:11 and to teach the Israelites all the statutes that the LORD has spoken to them through Moses.”
Leviticus10:12 Then Moses spoke to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:13 You must eat it in a holy place because it is your allotted portion and the allotted portion of your sons from the gifts of the LORD
Leviticus10:14 Also
Leviticus10:15 The thigh of the contribution offering and the breast of the wave offering they must bring in addition to the gifts of the fat parts to wave them as a wave offering before the LORD
Leviticus10:16 Later Moses sought diligently for the sin offering male goat
Leviticus10:17 “Why did you not eat the sin offering in the sanctuary? For it is most holy and he gave it to you to bear the iniquity of the congregation
Leviticus10:18 See here! Its blood was not brought into the holy place within! You should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary just as I commanded!”
Leviticus10:19 But Aaron spoke to Moses
Leviticus10:20 When Moses heard this explanation
Leviticus11:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 “Tell the Israelites: ‘This is the kind of creature you may eat from among all the animals that are on the land.
Leviticus11:3 You may eat any among the animals that has a divided hoof (the hooves are completely split in two ) and that also chews the cud.
Leviticus11:4 However
Leviticus11:5 The rock badger is unclean to you because it chews the cud even though its hoof is not divided.
Leviticus11:6 The hare is unclean to you because it chews the cud even though its hoof is not divided.
Leviticus11:7 The pig is unclean to you because its hoof is divided (the hoof is completely split in two )
Leviticus11:8 You must not eat from their meat and you must not touch their carcasses; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:9 “‘These you can eat from all creatures that are in the water: Any creatures in the water that have both fins and scales
Leviticus11:10 But any creatures that do not have both fins and scales
Leviticus11:11 Since they are detestable to you
Leviticus11:12 Any creature in the water that does not have both fins and scales is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 “‘These you are to detest from among the birds — they must not be eaten
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 every kind of crow
Leviticus11:16 the eagle owl
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the white owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 “‘Every winged swarming thing that walks on all fours is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 However
Leviticus11:22 These you may eat from them: the locust of any kind
Leviticus11:23 But any other winged swarming thing that has four legs is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 “‘By these you defile yourselves; anyone who touches their carcass will be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:25 and anyone who carries their carcass must wash his clothes and will be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:26 “‘All animals that divide the hoof but it is not completely split in two and do not chew the cud are unclean to you; anyone who touches them becomes unclean.
Leviticus11:27 All that walk on their paws among all the creatures that walk on all fours are unclean to you. Anyone who touches their carcass will be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:28 and the one who carries their carcass must wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 “‘Now this is what is unclean to you among the swarming things that swarm on the land: the rat
Leviticus11:30 the Mediterranean gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are the ones that are unclean to you among all the swarming things. Anyone who touches them when they die will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 Also
Leviticus11:33 As for any clay vessel they fall into
Leviticus11:34 Any food that may be eaten which becomes soaked with water will become unclean. Anything drinkable in any such vessel will become unclean.
Leviticus11:35 Anything their carcass may fall on will become unclean. An oven or small stove must be smashed to pieces; they are unclean
Leviticus11:36 However
Leviticus11:37 Now
Leviticus11:38 but if water is put on the seed and such a carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 “‘Now if an animal that you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 One who eats from its carcass must wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:41 Every swarming thing that swarms on the land is detestable; it must not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 You must not eat anything that crawls on its belly or anything that walks on all fours or on any number of legs of all the swarming things that swarm on the land
Leviticus11:43 Do not make yourselves detestable by any of the swarming things. You must not defile yourselves by them and become unclean by them
Leviticus11:44 for I am the LORD your God and you are to sanctify yourselves and be holy because I am holy. You must not defile yourselves by any of the swarming things that creep on the ground
Leviticus11:45 for I am the LORD who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God
Leviticus11:46 This is the law of the land animals
Leviticus11:47 to distinguish between the unclean and the clean
Leviticus12:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus12:2 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus12:3 On the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin must be circumcised.
Leviticus12:4 Then she will remain thirty-three days in blood purity. She must not touch anything holy and she must not enter the sanctuary until the days of her purification are fulfilled.
Leviticus12:5 If she bears a female child
Leviticus12:6 “‘When the days of her purification are completed for a son or for a daughter
Leviticus12:7 The priest is to present it before the LORD and make atonement on her behalf
Leviticus12:8 If she cannot afford a sheep
Leviticus13:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus13:2 “When someone has a swelling or a scab or a bright spot on the skin of his body that may become a diseased infection
Leviticus13:3 The priest must then examine the infection on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:4 “If it is a white bright spot on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:5 The priest must then examine it on the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 The priest must then examine it again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 If
Leviticus13:8 The priest must then examine it
Leviticus13:9 “When someone has a diseased infection
Leviticus13:10 The priest will then examine it
Leviticus13:11 it is a chronic disease on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:12 If
Leviticus13:13 the priest must then examine it
Leviticus13:14 But whenever raw flesh appears in it he will be unclean
Leviticus13:15 so the priest is to examine the raw flesh and pronounce him unclean — it is diseased.
Leviticus13:16 If
Leviticus13:17 The priest will then examine it
Leviticus13:18 “When someone’s body has a boil on its skin and it heals
Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there is a white swelling or a reddish white bright spot
Leviticus13:20 The priest will then examine it
Leviticus13:21 If
Leviticus13:22 If it is spreading further on the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the bright spot stays in its place and has not spread
Leviticus13:24 “When a body has a burn on its skin and the raw area of the burn becomes a reddish white or white bright spot
Leviticus13:25 the priest must examine it
Leviticus13:26 If
Leviticus13:27 The priest must then examine it on the seventh day
Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot stays in its place
Leviticus13:29 “When a man or a woman has an infection on the head or in the beard
Leviticus13:30 the priest is to examine the infection
Leviticus13:31 But if the priest examines the scall infection and it does not appear to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:32 The priest must then examine the infection on the seventh day
Leviticus13:33 then the individual is to shave himself
Leviticus13:34 The priest must then examine the scall on the seventh day
Leviticus13:35 If
Leviticus13:36 then the priest is to examine it
Leviticus13:37 If
Leviticus13:38 “When a man or a woman has bright spots — white bright spots — on the skin of their body
Leviticus13:39 the priest is to examine them
Leviticus13:40 “When a man’s head is bare so that he is balding in back
Leviticus13:41 If his head is bare on the forehead so that he is balding in front
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a reddish white infection in the back or front bald area
Leviticus13:43 The priest is to examine it
Leviticus13:44 he is a diseased man. He is unclean. The priest must surely pronounce him unclean because of his infection on his head.
Leviticus13:45 “As for the diseased person who has the infection
Leviticus13:46 The whole time he has the infection he will be continually unclean. He must live in isolation
Leviticus13:47 “When a garment has a diseased infection in it
Leviticus13:48 or in the warp or woof of the linen or the wool
Leviticus13:49 if the infection in the garment or leather or warp or woof or any article of leather is yellowish green or reddish
Leviticus13:50 The priest is to examine and then quarantine the article with the infection for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 He must then examine the infection on the seventh day. If the infection has spread in the garment
Leviticus13:52 He must burn the garment or the warp or the woof
Leviticus13:53 But if the priest examines it and the infection has not spread in the garment or in the warp or in the woof or in any article of leather
Leviticus13:54 the priest is to command that they wash whatever has the infection and quarantine it for another seven days.
Leviticus13:55 The priest must then examine it after the infection has been washed out
Leviticus13:56 But if the priest has examined it and the infection has faded after it has been washed
Leviticus13:57 Then if it still appears again in the garment or the warp or the woof
Leviticus13:58 But the garment or the warp or the woof or any article of leather which you wash and infection disappears from it is to be washed a second time and it will be clean.”
Leviticus13:59 This is the law of the diseased infection in the garment of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus14:2 “This is the law of the diseased person on the day of his purification
Leviticus14:3 The priest is to go outside the camp and examine the infection. If the infection of the diseased person has been healed
Leviticus14:4 then the priest will command that two live clean birds
Leviticus14:5 The priest will then command that one bird be slaughtered into a clay vessel over fresh water.
Leviticus14:6 Then he is to take the live bird along with the piece of cedar wood
Leviticus14:7 and sprinkle it seven times on the one being cleansed from the disease
Leviticus14:8 “The one being cleansed must then wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 When the seventh day comes he must shave all his hair — his head
Leviticus14:10 “On the eighth day he must take two flawless male lambs
Leviticus14:11 and the priest who pronounces him clean will have the man who is being cleansed stand along with these offerings before the LORD at the entrance of the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus14:12 “The priest is to take one male lamb and present it for a guilt offering along with the log of olive oil and present them as a wave offering before the LORD.
Leviticus14:13 He must then slaughter the male lamb in the place where the sin offering and the burnt offering are slaughtered
Leviticus14:14 Then the priest is to take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed
Leviticus14:15 The priest will then take some of the log of olive oil and pour it into his own left hand.
Leviticus14:16 Then the priest is to dip his right forefinger into the olive oil that is in his left hand
Leviticus14:17 The priest will then put some of the rest of the olive oil that is in his hand on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed
Leviticus14:18 and the remainder of the olive oil that is in his hand the priest is to put on the head of the one being cleansed. So the priest is to make atonement for him before the LORD.
Leviticus14:19 “The priest must then perform the sin offering and make atonement for the one being cleansed from his impurity. After that he is to slaughter the burnt offering
Leviticus14:20 and the priest is to offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. So the priest is to make atonement for him and he will be clean.
Leviticus14:21 “If the person is poor and does not have sufficient means
Leviticus14:22 and two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus14:23 “On the eighth day he must bring them for his purification to the priest at the entrance of the Meeting Tent before the LORD
Leviticus14:24 and the priest is to take the male lamb of the guilt offering and the log of olive oil and wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.
Leviticus14:25 Then he is to slaughter the male lamb of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:26 The priest will then pour some of the olive oil into his own left hand
Leviticus14:27 and sprinkle some of the olive oil that is in his left hand with his right forefinger seven times before the LORD.
Leviticus14:28 Then the priest is to put some of the olive oil that is in his hand on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed
Leviticus14:29 and the remainder of the olive oil that is in the hand of the priest he is to put on the head of the one being cleansed to make atonement for him before the LORD.
Leviticus14:30 “He will then make one of the turtledoves or young pigeons
Leviticus14:31 a sin offering and the other a burnt offering along with the grain offering. So the priest is to make atonement for the one being cleansed before the LORD.
Leviticus14:32 This is the law of the one in whom there is a diseased infection
Leviticus14:33 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus14:34 “When you enter the land of Canaan which I am about to give to you for a possession
Leviticus14:35 then whoever owns the house must come and declare to the priest
Leviticus14:36 Then the priest will command that the house be cleared before the priest enters to examine the infection so that everything in the house does not become unclean
Leviticus14:37 He is to examine the infection
Leviticus14:38 then the priest is to go out of the house to the doorway of the house and quarantine the house for seven days.
Leviticus14:39 The priest must return on the seventh day and examine it
Leviticus14:40 then the priest is to command that the stones that had the infection in them be pulled and thrown outside the city into an unclean place.
Leviticus14:41 Then he is to have the house scraped all around on the inside
Leviticus14:42 They are then to take other stones and replace those stones
Leviticus14:43 “If the infection returns and breaks out in the house after he has pulled out the stones
Leviticus14:44 the priest is to come and examine it
Leviticus14:45 He must tear down the house
Leviticus14:46 Anyone who enters the house all the days the priest has quarantined it will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 Anyone who lies down in the house must wash his clothes. Anyone who eats in the house must wash his clothes.
Leviticus14:48 “If
Leviticus14:49 Then he is to take two birds
Leviticus14:50 and he is to slaughter one bird into a clay vessel over fresh water.
Leviticus14:51 He must then take the piece of cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 So he is to decontaminate the house with the blood of the bird
Leviticus14:53 and he is to send the live bird away outside the city into the open countryside. So he is to make atonement for the house and it will be clean.
Leviticus14:54 “This is the law for all diseased infections
Leviticus14:55 for the diseased garment
Leviticus14:56 for the swelling
Leviticus14:57 to teach when something is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law for dealing with infectious disease.”
Leviticus15:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus15:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus15:3 Now this is his uncleanness in regard to his discharge — whether his body secretes his discharge or blocks his discharge
Leviticus15:4 “‘Any bed the man with a discharge lies on will be unclean
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches his bed must wash his clothes
Leviticus15:6 The one who sits on the furniture the man with a discharge sits on must wash his clothes
Leviticus15:7 The one who touches the body of the man with a discharge must wash his clothes
Leviticus15:8 If the man with a discharge spits on a person who is ceremonially clean
Leviticus15:9 Any means of riding the man with a discharge rides on will be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Anyone who touches anything that was under him will be unclean until evening
Leviticus15:11 Anyone whom the man with the discharge touches without having rinsed his hands in water must wash his clothes
Leviticus15:12 A clay vessel which the man with the discharge touches must be broken
Leviticus15:13 “‘When the man with the discharge becomes clean from his discharge he is to count off for himself seven days for his purification
Leviticus15:14 Then on the eighth day he is to take for himself two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus15:15 and the priest is to make one of them a sin offering and the other a burnt offering. So the priest is to make atonement for him before the LORD for his discharge.
Leviticus15:16 “‘When a man has a seminal emission
Leviticus15:17 and he must wash in water any clothing or leather that has semen on it
Leviticus15:18 When a man has sexual intercourse with a woman and there is a seminal emission
Leviticus15:19 “‘When a woman has a discharge and her discharge is blood from her body
Leviticus15:20 Anything she lies on during her menstruation will be unclean
Leviticus15:21 Anyone who touches her bed must wash his clothes
Leviticus15:22 Anyone who touches any furniture she sits on must wash his clothes
Leviticus15:23 If there is something on the bed or on the furniture she sits on
Leviticus15:24 and if a man actually has sexual intercourse with her so that her menstrual impurity touches him
Leviticus15:25 “‘When a woman’s discharge of blood flows many days not at the time of her menstruation
Leviticus15:26 Any bed she lies on all the days of her discharge will be to her like the bed of her menstruation
Leviticus15:27 and anyone who touches them will be unclean
Leviticus15:28 “‘If she becomes clean from her discharge
Leviticus15:29 Then on the eighth day she must take for herself two turtledoves or two young pigeons and she must bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Meeting Tent
Leviticus15:30 and the priest is to make one a sin offering and the other a burnt offering. So the priest is to make atonement for her before the LORD from her discharge of impurity.
Leviticus15:31 “‘Thus you are to set the Israelites apart from their impurity so that they do not die in their impurity by defiling my tabernacle which is in their midst.
Leviticus15:32 This is the law of the one with a discharge: the one who has a seminal emission and becomes unclean by it
Leviticus15:33 the one who is sick in her menstruation
Leviticus16:1 The LORD spoke to Moses after the death of Aaron’s two sons when they approached the presence of the LORD and died
Leviticus16:2 and the LORD said to Moses: “Tell Aaron your brother that he must not enter at any time into the holy place inside the veil-canopy in front of the atonement plate that is on the ark so that he may not die
Leviticus16:3 “In this way Aaron is to enter into the sanctuary — with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He must put on a holy linen tunic
Leviticus16:5 He must also take two male goats from the congregation of the Israelites for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Then Aaron is to present the sin offering bull which is for himself and is to make atonement on behalf of himself and his household.
Leviticus16:7 He must then take the two goats and stand them before the LORD at the entrance of the Meeting Tent
Leviticus16:8 and Aaron is to cast lots over the two goats
Leviticus16:9 Aaron must then present the goat which has been designated by lot for the LORD
Leviticus16:10 but the goat which has been designated by lot for Azazel is to be stood alive before the LORD to make atonement on it by sending it away to Azazel into the wilderness.
Leviticus16:11 “Aaron is to present the sin offering bull which is for himself
Leviticus16:12 and take a censer full of coals of fire from the altar before the LORD and a full double handful of finely ground fragrant incense
Leviticus16:13 He must then put the incense on the fire before the LORD
Leviticus16:14 Then he is to take some of the blood of the bull and sprinkle it with his finger on the eastern face of the atonement plate
Leviticus16:15 “He must then slaughter the sin offering goat which is for the people. He is to bring its blood inside the veil-canopy
Leviticus16:16 So he is to make atonement for the holy place from the impurities of the Israelites and from their transgressions with regard to all their sins
Leviticus16:17 Nobody is to be in the Meeting Tent when he enters to make atonement in the holy place until he goes out
Leviticus16:18 “Then he is to go out to the altar which is before the LORD and make atonement for it. He is to take some of the blood of the bull and some of the blood of the goat
Leviticus16:19 Then he is to sprinkle on it some of the blood with his finger seven times
Leviticus16:20 “When he has finished purifying the holy place
Leviticus16:21 Aaron is to lay his two hands on the head of the live goat and confess over it all the iniquities of the Israelites and all their transgressions in regard to all their sins
Leviticus16:22 The goat is to bear on itself all their iniquities into an inaccessible land
Leviticus16:23 “Aaron must then enter the Meeting Tent and take off the linen garments which he had put on when he entered the sanctuary
Leviticus16:24 Then he must bathe his body in water in a holy place
Leviticus16:25 “Then he is to offer up the fat of the sin offering in smoke on the altar
Leviticus16:26 and the one who sent the goat away to Azazel must wash his clothes
Leviticus16:27 The bull of the sin offering and the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 and the one who burns them must wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Leviticus16:29 “This is to be a perpetual statute for you. In the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 for on this day atonement is to be made for you to cleanse you from all your sins; you must be clean before the LORD.
Leviticus16:31 It is to be a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus16:32 “The priest who is anointed and ordained to act as high priest in place of his father is to make atonement. He is to put on the linen garments
Leviticus16:33 and he is to purify the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:34 This is to be a perpetual statute for you to make atonement for the Israelites for all their sins once a year.” So he did just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Leviticus17:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus17:2 “Speak to Aaron
Leviticus17:3 “Blood guilt will be accounted to any man from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox or a lamb or a goat inside the camp or outside the camp
Leviticus17:4 but has not brought it to the entrance of the Meeting Tent to present it as an offering to the LORD before the tabernacle of the LORD. He has shed blood
Leviticus17:5 This is so that the Israelites will bring their sacrifices that they are sacrificing in the open field to the LORD at the entrance of the Meeting Tent to the priest and sacrifice them there as peace offering sacrifices to the LORD.
Leviticus17:6 The priest is to splash the blood on the altar of the LORD at the entrance of the Meeting Tent
Leviticus17:7 So they must no longer offer their sacrifices to the goat demons
Leviticus17:8 “You are to say to them: ‘Any man from the house of Israel or from the foreigners who reside in their midst
Leviticus17:9 but does not bring it to the entrance of the Meeting Tent to offer it to the LORD — that person will be cut off from his people.
Leviticus17:10 “‘Any man from the house of Israel or from the foreigners who reside in their midst who eats any blood
Leviticus17:11 for the life of every living thing is in the blood. So I myself have assigned it to you on the altar to make atonement for your lives
Leviticus17:12 Therefore
Leviticus17:13 “‘Any man from the Israelites or from the foreigners who reside in their midst who hunts a wild animal or a bird that may be eaten must pour out its blood and cover it with soil
Leviticus17:14 for the life of all flesh is its blood. So I have said to the Israelites: You must not eat the blood of any living thing because the life of every living thing is its blood — all who eat it will be cut off.
Leviticus17:15 “‘Any person who eats an animal that has died of natural causes or an animal torn by beasts
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash his clothes and does not bathe his body
Leviticus18:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus18:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus18:3 You must not do as they do in the land of Egypt where you have been living
Leviticus18:4 You must observe my regulations and you must be sure to walk in my statutes. I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus18:5 So you must keep my statutes and my regulations; anyone who does so will live by keeping them. I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:6 “‘No man is to approach any close relative to have sexual intercourse with her. I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:7 You must not expose your father’s nakedness by having sexual intercourse with your mother. She is your mother; you must not have intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:8 You must not have sexual intercourse with your father’s wife; she is your father’s nakedness.
Leviticus18:9 You must not have sexual intercourse with your sister
Leviticus18:10 You must not expose the nakedness of your son’s daughter or your daughter’s daughter by having sexual intercourse with them
Leviticus18:11 You must not have sexual intercourse with the daughter of your father’s wife born of your father; she is your sister. You must not have intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:12 You must not have sexual intercourse with your father’s sister; she is your father’s flesh.
Leviticus18:13 You must not have sexual intercourse with your mother’s sister
Leviticus18:14 You must not expose the nakedness of your father’s brother; you must not approach his wife to have sexual intercourse with her. She is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You must not have sexual intercourse with your daughter-in-law; she is your son’s wife. You must not have intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:16 You must not have sexual intercourse with your brother’s wife; she is your brother’s nakedness.
Leviticus18:17 You must not have sexual intercourse with both a woman and her daughter; you must not take as wife either her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter to have intercourse with them. They are closely related to her — it is lewdness.
Leviticus18:18 You must not take a woman in marriage and then marry her sister as a rival wife while she is still alive
Leviticus18:19 “‘You must not approach a woman in her menstrual impurity to have sexual intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:20 You must not have sexual intercourse with the wife of your fellow citizen to become unclean with her.
Leviticus18:21 You must not give any of your children as an offering to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You must not have sexual intercourse with a male as one has sexual intercourse with a woman; it is a detestable act.
Leviticus18:23 You must not have sexual intercourse with any animal to become defiled with it
Leviticus18:24 “‘Do not defile yourselves with any of these things
Leviticus18:25 Therefore the land has become unclean and I have brought the punishment for its iniquity upon it
Leviticus18:26 You yourselves must obey my statutes and my regulations and must not do any of these abominations
Leviticus18:27 for the people who were in the land before you have done all these abominations
Leviticus18:28 So do not make the land vomit you out because you defile it just as it has vomited out the nations that were before you.
Leviticus18:29 For if anyone does any of these abominations
Leviticus18:30 You must obey my charge to not practice any of the abominable statutes that have been done before you
Leviticus19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus19:2 “Speak to the whole congregation of the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus19:3 Each of you must respect his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols
Leviticus19:5 “‘When you sacrifice a peace offering sacrifice to the LORD
Leviticus19:6 It must be eaten on the day of your sacrifice and on the following day
Leviticus19:7 If
Leviticus19:8 and the one who eats it will bear his punishment for iniquity because he has profaned what is holy to the LORD. That person will be cut off from his people.
Leviticus19:9 “‘When you gather in the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 You must not pick your vineyard bare
Leviticus19:11 “‘You must not steal
Leviticus19:12 You must not swear falsely in my name
Leviticus19:13 You must not oppress your neighbor or commit robbery against him. You must not withhold the wages of the hired laborer overnight until morning.
Leviticus19:14 You must not curse a deaf person or put a stumbling block in front of a blind person. You must fear your God; I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:15 “‘You must not deal unjustly in judgment: you must neither show partiality to the poor nor honor the rich. You must judge your fellow citizen fairly.
Leviticus19:16 You must not go about as a slanderer among your people. You must not stand idly by when your neighbor’s life is at stake. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:17 You must not hate your brother in your heart. You must surely reprove your fellow citizen so that you do not incur sin on account of him.
Leviticus19:18 You must not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the children of your people
Leviticus19:19 You must keep my statutes. You must not allow two different kinds of your animals to breed
Leviticus19:20 “‘When a man has sexual intercourse with a woman
Leviticus19:21 He must bring his guilt offering to the LORD at the entrance of the Meeting Tent
Leviticus19:22 and the priest is to make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the LORD for his sin that he has committed
Leviticus19:23 “‘When you enter the land and plant any fruit tree
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all its fruit will be holy
Leviticus19:25 Then in the fifth year you may eat its fruit to add its produce to your harvest. I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:26 “‘You must not eat anything with the blood still in it. You must not practice either divination or soothsaying.
Leviticus19:27 You must not round off the corners of the hair on your head or ruin the corners of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 You must not slash your body for a dead person or incise a tattoo on yourself. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:29 Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 “‘You must keep my Sabbaths and fear my sanctuary. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:31 Do not turn to the spirits of the dead and do not seek familiar spirits to become unclean by them. I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:32 You must stand up in the presence of the aged
Leviticus19:33 When a foreigner resides with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 The foreigner who resides with you must be to you like a native citizen among you; so you must love him as yourself
Leviticus19:35 You must not do injustice in the regulation of measures
Leviticus19:36 You must have honest balances
Leviticus19:37 You must be sure to obey all my statutes and regulations. I am the LORD.’”

Leviticus20:8 You must be sure to obey my statutes. I am the LORD who sanctifies you.

Leviticus20:27 “‘A man or woman who has in them a spirit of the dead or a familiar spirit must be put to death. They must pelt them with stones; their blood guilt is on themselves.’”

Leviticus21:3 and his virgin sister who is near to him

Leviticus21:8 You must sanctify him because he presents the food of your God. He must be holy to you because I

Leviticus21:10 “‘The high priest — who is greater than his brothers
Leviticus21:11 He must not go where there is any dead person; he must not defile himself even for his father and his mother.
Leviticus21:12 He must not go out from the sanctuary and must not profane the sanctuary of his God
Leviticus21:13 He must take a wife who is a virgin.

Leviticus21:20 or a hunchback

Leviticus22:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus22:2 “Tell Aaron and his sons that they must deal respectfully with the holy offerings of the Israelites
Leviticus22:3 Say to them
Leviticus22:4 No man from the descendants of Aaron who is diseased or has a discharge may eat the holy offerings until he becomes clean. The one who touches anything made unclean by contact with a dead person
Leviticus22:5 or a man who touches a swarming thing by which he becomes unclean
Leviticus22:6 the person who touches any of these will be unclean until evening and must not eat from the holy offerings unless he has bathed his body in water.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun goes down he will be clean
Leviticus22:8 He must not eat an animal that has died of natural causes or an animal torn by beasts and thus become unclean by it. I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:9 They must keep my charge so that they do not incur sin on account of it and therefore die because they profane it. I am the LORD who sanctifies them.
Leviticus22:10 “‘No lay person may eat anything holy. Neither a priest’s lodger nor a hired laborer may eat anything holy
Leviticus22:11 but if a priest buys a person with his own money
Leviticus22:12 If a priest’s daughter marries a lay person
Leviticus22:13 but if a priest’s daughter is a widow or divorced
Leviticus22:14 “‘If a man eats a holy offering by mistake
Leviticus22:15 They must not profane the holy offerings which the Israelites contribute to the LORD
Leviticus22:16 and so cause them to incur a penalty for guilt when they eat their holy offerings
Leviticus22:17 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus22:18 “Speak to Aaron
Leviticus22:19 if it is to be acceptable for your benefit it must be a flawless male from the cattle
Leviticus22:20 You must not present anything that has a flaw
Leviticus22:21 If a man presents a peace offering sacrifice to the LORD for a special votive offering or for a freewill offering from the herd or the flock
Leviticus22:22 “‘You must not present to the LORD something blind
Leviticus22:23 As for an ox or a sheep with a limb too long or stunted
Leviticus22:24 You must not present to the LORD something with testicles that are bruised
Leviticus22:25 Even from a foreigner you must not present the food of your God from such animals as these

Leviticus22:27 “When an ox
Leviticus22:28 You must not slaughter an ox or a sheep and its young on the same day.

Leviticus22:30 On that very day it must be eaten; you must not leave any part of it over until morning. I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:31 “You must be sure to do my commandments. I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:32 You must not profane my holy name
Leviticus22:33 the one who brought you out from the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD.”

Leviticus23:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them

Leviticus23:4 “‘These are the LORD’s appointed times
Leviticus23:5 In the first month

Leviticus23:7 On the first day there will be a holy assembly for you; you must not do any regular work.
Leviticus23:8 You must present a gift to the LORD for seven days
Leviticus23:9 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:10 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus23:11 and he must wave the sheaf before the LORD to be accepted for your benefit — on the day after the Sabbath the priest is to wave it.
Leviticus23:12 On the day you wave the sheaf you must also offer a flawless yearling lamb for a burnt offering to the LORD
Leviticus23:13 along with its grain offering
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat bread
Leviticus23:15 “‘You must count for yourselves seven weeks from the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You must count fifty days — until the day after the seventh Sabbath — and then you must present a new grain offering to the LORD.
Leviticus23:17 From the places where you live you must bring two loaves of bread for a wave offering; they must be made from two tenths of an ephah of fine wheat flour
Leviticus23:18 Along with the loaves of bread
Leviticus23:19 You must also offer one male goat for a sin offering and two yearling lambs for a peace offering sacrifice
Leviticus23:20 and the priest is to wave them — the two lambs — along with the bread of the first fruits
Leviticus23:21 “‘On this very day you must proclaim an assembly; it is to be a holy assembly for you. You must not do any regular work. This is a perpetual statute in all the places where you live throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 When you gather in the harvest of your land

Leviticus23:24 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any regular work

Leviticus23:28 You must not do any work on this particular day
Leviticus23:29 Indeed
Leviticus23:30 As for any person who does any work on this particular day
Leviticus23:31 You must not do any work. This is a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all the places where you live.
Leviticus23:32 It is a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus23:33 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:34 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus23:35 On the first day is a holy assembly; you must do no regular work.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you must present a gift to the LORD. On the eighth day there is to be a holy assembly for you
Leviticus23:37 “‘These are the appointed times of the LORD that you must proclaim as holy assemblies to present a gift to the LORD — burnt offering
Leviticus23:38 besides the Sabbaths of the LORD and all your gifts
Leviticus23:39 “‘On the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you must take for yourselves branches from majestic trees — palm branches
Leviticus23:41 You must celebrate it as a pilgrim festival to the LORD for seven days in the year. This is a perpetual statute throughout your generations; you must celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You must live in temporary shelters for seven days; every native citizen in Israel must live in temporary shelters
Leviticus23:43 so that your future generations may know that I made the Israelites live in temporary shelters when I brought them out from the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.’”
Leviticus23:44 So Moses spoke to the Israelites about the appointed times of the LORD.
Leviticus24:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus24:2 “Command the Israelites to bring to you pure oil of beaten olives for the light
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil-canopy of the congregation in the Meeting Tent Aaron must arrange it from evening until morning before the LORD continually. This is a perpetual statute throughout your generations.
Leviticus24:4 On the ceremonially pure lampstand he must arrange the lamps before the LORD continually.
Leviticus24:5 “You must take choice wheat flour and bake twelve loaves; there must be two tenths of an ephah of flour in each loaf
Leviticus24:6 and you must set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 You must put pure frankincense on each row
Leviticus24:8 Each Sabbath day Aaron must arrange it before the LORD continually; this portion is from the Israelites as a perpetual covenant.
Leviticus24:9 It will belong to Aaron and his sons

Leviticus24:11 The Israelite woman’s son misused the Name and cursed
Leviticus24:12 So they placed him in custody until they were able to make a clear legal decision for themselves based on words from the mouth of the LORD.

Leviticus24:14 “Bring the one who cursed outside the camp
Leviticus24:15 Moreover
