Leviticus24:21 One who beats an animal to death must make restitution for it
Leviticus24:22 There will be one regulation for you
Leviticus24:23 Then Moses spoke to the Israelites and they brought the one who cursed outside the camp and stoned him with stones. So the Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Leviticus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai:
Leviticus25:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus25:3 Six years you may sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year the land must have a Sabbath of complete rest — a Sabbath to the LORD. You must not sow your field or prune your vineyard.
Leviticus25:5 You must not gather in the aftergrowth of your harvest and you must not pick the grapes of your unpruned vines; the land must have a year of complete rest.
Leviticus25:6 You may have the Sabbath produce of the land to eat — you
Leviticus25:7 your cattle
Leviticus25:8 “‘You must count off seven weeks of years
Leviticus25:9 You must sound loud horn blasts — in the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 So you must consecrate the fiftieth year
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year will be your jubilee; you must not sow the land
Leviticus25:12 Because that year is a jubilee
Leviticus25:13 “‘In this year of jubilee you must each return to your property.
Leviticus25:14 If you make a sale to your fellow citizen or buy from your fellow citizen
Leviticus25:15 You may buy it from your fellow citizen according to the number of years since the last jubilee; he may sell it to you according to the years of produce that are left.
Leviticus25:16 The more years there are
Leviticus25:17 No one is to oppress his fellow citizen
Leviticus25:18 You must obey my statutes and my regulations; you must be sure to keep them so that you may live securely in the land.
Leviticus25:19 “‘The land will give its fruit and you may eat until you are satisfied
Leviticus25:20 If you say
Leviticus25:21 I will command my blessing for you in the sixth year so that it may yield the produce for three years
Leviticus25:22 and you may sow the eighth year and eat from that sixth year’s produce — old produce. Until you bring in the ninth year’s produce
Leviticus25:23 The land must not be sold without reclaim because the land belongs to me
Leviticus25:24 In all your landed property you must provide for the right of redemption of the land.
Leviticus25:25 “‘If your brother becomes impoverished and sells some of his property
Leviticus25:26 If a man has no redeemer
Leviticus25:27 he is to calculate the value of the years it was sold
Leviticus25:28 If he has not prospered enough to refund a balance to him
Leviticus25:29 “‘If a man sells a residential house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed before the full calendar year is ended
Leviticus25:31 The houses of villages
Leviticus25:32 As for the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 Whatever someone among the Levites might redeem — the sale of a house which is his property in a city — must revert in the jubilee
Leviticus25:34 Moreover
Leviticus25:35 “‘If your brother becomes impoverished and is indebted to you
Leviticus25:36 Do not take interest or profit from him
Leviticus25:37 You must not lend him your money at interest and you must not sell him food for profit.
Leviticus25:38 I am the LORD your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan — to be your God.
Leviticus25:39 “‘If your brother becomes impoverished with regard to you so that he sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 He must be with you as a hired worker
Leviticus25:41 but then he may go free
Leviticus25:42 Since they are my servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt
Leviticus25:43 You must not rule over him harshly
Leviticus25:44 “‘As for your male and female slaves who may belong to you — you may buy male and female slaves from the nations all around you.
Leviticus25:45 Also you may buy slaves from the children of the foreigners who reside with you
Leviticus25:46 You may give them as inheritance to your children after you to possess as property. You may enslave them perpetually. However
Leviticus25:47 “‘If a resident foreigner who is with you prospers and your brother becomes impoverished with regard to him so that he sells himself to a resident foreigner who is with you or to a member of a foreigner’s family
Leviticus25:48 after he has sold himself he retains a right of redemption. One of his brothers may redeem him
Leviticus25:49 or his uncle or his cousin may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 He must calculate with the one who bought him the number of years from the year he sold himself to him until the jubilee year
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years
Leviticus25:52 but if only a few years remain until the jubilee
Leviticus25:53 He must be with the one who bought him like a yearly hired worker. The one who bought him must not rule over him harshly in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 If
Leviticus25:55 because the Israelites are my own servants; they are my servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus26:1 “‘You must not make for yourselves idols
Leviticus26:2 You must keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary. I am the LORD.
Leviticus26:3 “‘If you walk in my statutes and are sure to obey my commandments
Leviticus26:4 I will give you your rains in their time so that the land will give its yield and the trees of the field will produce their fruit.
Leviticus26:5 Threshing season will extend for you until the season for harvesting grapes
Leviticus26:6 I will grant peace in the land so that you will lie down to sleep without anyone terrifying you. I will remove harmful animals from the land
Leviticus26:7 You will pursue your enemies and they will fall before you by the sword.
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will pursue a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will turn to you
Leviticus26:10 You will still be eating stored produce from the previous year and will have to clean out what is stored from the previous year to make room for new.
Leviticus26:11 “‘I will put my tabernacle in your midst and I will not abhor you.
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you
Leviticus26:13 I am the LORD your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt
Leviticus26:14 “‘If
Leviticus26:15 if you reject my statutes and abhor my regulations so that you do not keep all my commandments and you break my covenant —
Leviticus26:16 I for my part will do this to you: I will inflict horror on you
Leviticus26:17 I will set my face against you. You will be struck down before your enemies
Leviticus26:18 “‘If
Leviticus26:19 I will break your strong pride and make your sky like iron and your land like bronze.
Leviticus26:20 Your strength will be used up in vain
Leviticus26:21 “‘If you walk in hostility against me and are not willing to obey me
Leviticus26:22 I will send the wild animals against you and they will bereave you of your children
Leviticus26:23 “‘If in spite of these things you do not allow yourselves to be disciplined and you walk in hostility against me
Leviticus26:24 I myself will also walk in hostility against you and strike you seven times on account of your sins.
Leviticus26:25 I will bring on you an avenging sword
Leviticus26:26 When I break off your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 “‘If in spite of this you do not obey me but walk in hostility against me
Leviticus26:28 I will walk in hostile rage against you and I myself will also discipline you seven times on account of your sins.
Leviticus26:29 You will eat the flesh of your sons and the flesh of your daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars
Leviticus26:31 I will lay your cities waste and make your sanctuaries desolate
Leviticus26:32 I myself will make the land desolate and your enemies who live in it will be appalled.
Leviticus26:33 I will scatter you among the nations and unsheathe the sword after you
Leviticus26:34 “‘Then the land will make up for its Sabbaths all the days it lies desolate while you are in the land of your enemies; then the land will rest and make up its Sabbaths.
Leviticus26:35 All the days of the desolation it will have the rest it did not have on your Sabbaths when you lived on it.
Leviticus26:36 “‘As for the ones who remain among you
Leviticus26:37 They will stumble over each other as those who flee before a sword
Leviticus26:38 You will perish among the nations; the land of your enemies will consume you.
Leviticus26:39 “‘As for the ones who remain among you
Leviticus26:40 However
Leviticus26:41 (and I myself will walk in hostility against them and bring them into the land of their enemies)
Leviticus26:42 I will remember my covenant with Jacob and also my covenant with Isaac and also my covenant with Abraham
Leviticus26:43 The land will be abandoned by them in order that it may make up for its Sabbaths while it is made desolate without them
Leviticus26:44 In spite of this
Leviticus26:45 I will remember for them the covenant with their ancestors whom I brought out from the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations to be their God. I am the LORD.’”
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes
Leviticus27:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus27:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus27:3 the conversion value of the male from twenty years old up to sixty years old is fifty shekels by the standard of the sanctuary shekel.
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the person is from five years old up to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 If the person is one month old up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 If the person is from sixty years old and older
Leviticus27:8 If he is too poor to pay the conversion value
Leviticus27:9 “‘If what is vowed is a kind of animal from which an offering may be presented to the LORD
Leviticus27:10 He must not replace or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 If what is vowed is an unclean animal from which an offering must not be presented to the LORD
Leviticus27:12 and the priest will establish its conversion value
Leviticus27:13 If
Leviticus27:14 “‘If a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 If the one who consecrates it redeems his house
Leviticus27:16 “‘If a man consecrates to the LORD some of his own landed property
Leviticus27:17 If he consecrates his field in the jubilee year
Leviticus27:18 but if he consecrates his field after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If
Leviticus27:20 If he does not redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 When it reverts in the jubilee
Leviticus27:22 “‘If he consecrates to the LORD a field he has purchased
Leviticus27:23 the priest will calculate for him the amount of its conversion value until the jubilee year
Leviticus27:24 In the jubilee year the field will return to the one from whom he bought it
Leviticus27:25 Every conversion value must be calculated by the standard of the sanctuary shekel; twenty gerahs to the shekel.
Leviticus27:26 “‘Surely no man may consecrate a firstborn that already belongs to the LORD as a firstborn among the animals; whether it is an ox or a sheep
Leviticus27:27 If
Leviticus27:28 “‘Surely anything which a man permanently dedicates to the LORD from all that belongs to him
Leviticus27:29 Any human being who is permanently dedicated must not be ransomed; such a person must be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 “‘Any tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 If a man redeems part of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 All the tithe of herd or flock
Leviticus27:33 The owner must not examine the animals to distinguish between good and bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which the LORD commanded Moses to tell the Israelites at Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 Now the LORD spoke to Moses in the tent of meeting in the wilderness of Sinai on the first day of the second month of the second year after the Israelites departed from the land of Egypt. He said:
Numbers1:2 “Take a census of the entire Israelite community by their clans and families
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron are to number all in Israel who can serve in the army
Numbers1:4 And to help you there is to be a man from each tribe
Numbers1:5 Now these are the names of the men who are to help you: from Reuben
Numbers1:6 from Simeon
Numbers1:7 from Judah
Numbers1:8 from Issachar
Numbers1:9 from Zebulun
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim
Numbers1:11 from Benjamin
Numbers1:12 from Dan
Numbers1:13 from Asher
Numbers1:14 from Gad
Numbers1:15 from Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were the ones chosen from the community
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been mentioned specifically by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled the entire community together on the first day of the second month. Then the people recorded their ancestry by their clans and families
Numbers1:19 just as the LORD had commanded Moses. And so he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers1:20 And they were as follows: The descendants of Reuben
Numbers1:21 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Reuben were 46
Numbers1:22 From the descendants of Simeon: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:23 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Simeon were 59
Numbers1:24 From the descendants of Gad: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:25 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Gad were 45
Numbers1:26 From the descendants of Judah: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:27 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Judah were 74
Numbers1:28 From the descendants of Issachar: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:29 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Issachar were 54
Numbers1:30 From the descendants of Zebulun: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:31 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Zebulun were 57
Numbers1:32 From the sons of Joseph: From the descendants of Ephraim: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:33 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Ephraim were 40
Numbers1:34 From the descendants of Manasseh: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:35 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Manasseh were 32
Numbers1:36 From the descendants of Benjamin: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:37 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Benjamin were 35
Numbers1:38 From the descendants of Dan: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:39 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Dan were 62
Numbers1:40 From the descendants of Asher: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:41 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Asher were 41
Numbers1:42 From the descendants of Naphtali: According to the records of their clans and families
Numbers1:43 Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Naphtali were 53
Numbers1:44 These were the men whom Moses and Aaron numbered along with the twelve leaders of Israel
Numbers1:45 All the Israelites who were twenty years old or older
Numbers1:46 And all those numbered totaled 603
Numbers1:47 But the Levites
Numbers1:48 The LORD had said to Moses
Numbers1:49 “Only the tribe of Levi you must not number or count with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:50 But appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:51 Whenever the tabernacle is to move
Numbers1:52 “The Israelites will camp according to their divisions
Numbers1:53 But the Levites must camp around the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:54 The Israelites did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses — that is what they did.
Numbers2:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron:
Numbers2:2 “Every one of the Israelites must camp under his standard with the emblems of his family; they must camp at some distance around the tent of meeting.
Numbers2:3 “Now those who will be camping on the east
Numbers2:4 Those numbered in his division are 74
Numbers2:5 Those who will be camping next to them are the tribe of Issachar. The leader of the people of Issachar is Nethanel son of Zuar.
Numbers2:6 Those numbered in his division are 54
Numbers2:7 Next will be the tribe of Zebulun. The leader of the people of Zebulun is Eliab son of Helon.
Numbers2:8 Those numbered in his division are 57
Numbers2:9 All those numbered of the camp of Judah
Numbers2:10 “On the south will be the divisions of the camp of Reuben under their standard. The leader of the people of Reuben is Elizur son of Shedeur.
Numbers2:11 Those numbered in his division are 46
Numbers2:12 Those who will be camping next to them are the tribe of Simeon. The leader of the people of Simeon is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
Numbers2:13 Those numbered in his division are 59
Numbers2:14 Next will be the tribe of Gad. The leader of the people of Gad is Eliasaph son of Deuel.
Numbers2:15 Those numbered in his division are 45
Numbers2:16 All those numbered of the camp of Reuben
Numbers2:17 “Then the tent of meeting with the camp of the Levites will travel in the middle of the camps. They will travel in the same order as they camped
Numbers2:18 “On the west will be the divisions of the camp of Ephraim under their standard. The leader of the people of Ephraim is Elishama son of Amihud.
Numbers2:19 Those numbered in his division are 40
Numbers2:20 Next to them will be the tribe of Manasseh. The leader of the people of Manasseh is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
Numbers2:21 Those numbered in his division are 32
Numbers2:22 Next will be the tribe of Benjamin. The leader of the people of Benjamin is Abidan son of Gideoni.
Numbers2:23 Those numbered in his division are 35
Numbers2:24 All those numbered of the camp of Ephraim
Numbers2:25 “On the north will be the divisions of the camp of Dan
Numbers2:26 Those numbered in his division are 62
Numbers2:27 Those who will be camping next to them are the tribe of Asher. The leader of the people of Asher is Pagiel son of Ocran.
Numbers2:28 Those numbered in his division are 41
Numbers2:29 Next will be the tribe of Naphtali. The leader of the people of Naphtali is Ahira son of Enan.
Numbers2:30 Those numbered in his division are 53
Numbers2:31 All those numbered of the camp of Dan are 157
Numbers2:32 These are the Israelites
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not numbered among the other Israelites
Numbers2:34 So the Israelites did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses; that is the way they camped under their standards
Numbers3:1 Now these are the records of Aaron and Moses when the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered strange fire before the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:6 “Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 They are responsible for his needs and the needs of the whole community before the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 And they are responsible for all the furnishings of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 You are to assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they will be assigned exclusively to him out of all the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 So you are to appoint Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:11 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:12 “Look
Numbers3:13 because all the firstborn are mine. When I destroyed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai:
Numbers3:15 “Number the Levites by their clans and their families; every male from a month old and upward you are to number.”
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them according to the word of the LORD
Numbers3:17 These were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon
Numbers3:18 These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 The sons of Kohath by their families were: Amram
Numbers3:20 The sons of Merari by their families were Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their clans.
Numbers3:21 From Gershon came the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimeites; these were the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Those of them who were numbered
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle toward the west.
Numbers3:24 Now the leader of the clan of the Gershonites was Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 And the responsibilities of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting included the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the courtyard
Numbers3:27 From Kohath came the family of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 Counting every male from a month old and upward
Numbers3:29 The families of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:30 Now the leader of the clan of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their responsibilities included the ark
Numbers3:32 Now the head of all the Levitical leaders was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest. He was appointed over those who were responsible for the sanctuary.
Numbers3:33 From Merari came the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites; these were the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Those of them who were numbered
Numbers3:35 Now the leader of the clan of the families of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail. These were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 The appointed responsibilities of the Merarites included the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 and the pillars of the courtyard all around
Numbers3:38 But those who were to camp in front of the tabernacle on the east
Numbers3:39 All who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers3:40 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:41 And take the Levites for me — I am the LORD — instead of all the firstborn males among the Israelites
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered all the firstborn males among the Israelites
Numbers3:43 And all the firstborn males
Numbers3:44 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:45 “Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn males among the Israelites
Numbers3:46 And for the redemption of the 273 firstborn males of the Israelites who exceed the number of the Levites
Numbers3:47 collect five shekels for each one individually; you are to collect this amount in the currency of the sanctuary shekel (this shekel is twenty gerahs).
Numbers3:48 And give the money for the redemption of the excess number of them to Aaron and his sons.”
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the redemption money from those who were in excess of those redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 From the firstborn males of the Israelites he collected the money
Numbers3:51 Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:2 “Take a census of the Kohathites from among the Levites
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old
Numbers4:4 This is the service of the Kohathites in the tent of meeting
Numbers4:5 When it is time for the camp to journey
Numbers4:6 Then they must put over it a covering of fine leather and spread over that a cloth entirely of blue
Numbers4:7 “On the table of the presence they must spread a blue cloth
Numbers4:8 They must spread over them a scarlet cloth
Numbers4:9 “They must take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand of the light
Numbers4:10 Then they must put it with all its utensils in a covering of fine leather
Numbers4:11 “They must spread a blue cloth on the gold altar
Numbers4:12 Then they must take all the utensils of the service
Numbers4:13 Also
Numbers4:14 Then they must place on it all its implements with which they serve there — the trays
Numbers4:15 “When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 “The appointed responsibility of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest is for the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:18 “Do not allow the tribe of the families of the Kohathites to be cut off from among the Levites;
Numbers4:19 but in order that they will live and not die when they approach the most holy things
Numbers4:20 But the Kohathites are not to go in to watch while the holy things are being covered
Numbers4:21 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers4:22 “Also take a census of the Gershonites
Numbers4:23 You must number them from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the families of Gershonites
Numbers4:25 They must carry the curtains for the tabernacle and the tent of meeting with its covering
Numbers4:26 the hangings for the courtyard
Numbers4:27 “All the service of the Gershonites
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites concerning the tent of meeting. Their responsibilities will be under the authority of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Numbers4:29 “As for the sons of Merari
Numbers4:30 You must number them from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old
Numbers4:31 This is what they are responsible to carry as their entire service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers4:32 and the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their sockets
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the families of the Merarites

Numbers4:38 Those numbered from the Gershonites
Numbers4:39 from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old
Numbers4:40 those of them numbered by their families
Numbers4:41 These were those numbered from the families of the Gershonites
Numbers4:42 Those numbered from the families of the Merarites
Numbers4:43 from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 those of them numbered by their families were 3
Numbers4:45 These are those numbered from the families of the Merarites
Numbers4:46 All who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 those of them numbered were 8
Numbers4:49 According to the word of the LORD they were numbered
Numbers5:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:2 “Command the Israelites to expel from the camp every leper
Numbers5:3 You must expel both men and women; you must put them outside the camp
Numbers5:4 So the Israelites did so
Numbers5:5 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:6 “Tell the Israelites
Numbers5:7 then he must confess his sin that he has committed and must make full reparation
Numbers5:8 But if the individual has no close relative to whom reparation can be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 Every offering of all the Israelites’ holy things that they bring to the priest will be his.
Numbers5:10 Every man’s holy things will be his; whatever any man gives the priest will be his.’”
Numbers5:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:12 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Numbers5:13 and a man has sexual relations with her without her husband knowing it
Numbers5:14 and if jealous feelings come over him and he becomes suspicious of his wife
Numbers5:15 then the man must bring his wife to the priest
Numbers5:16 “‘Then the priest will bring her near and have her stand before the LORD.
Numbers5:17 The priest will then take holy water in a pottery jar
Numbers5:18 Then the priest will have the woman stand before the LORD
Numbers5:19 Then the priest will put the woman under oath and say to the her
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority
Numbers5:21 Then the priest will put the woman under the oath of the curse and will say to the her
Numbers5:22 and this water that causes the curse will go into your stomach
Numbers5:23 “‘Then the priest will write these curses on a scroll and then scrape them off into the bitter water.
Numbers5:24 He will make the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse
Numbers5:25 The priest will take the grain offering of suspicion from the woman’s hand
Numbers5:26 Then the priest will take a handful of the grain offering as its memorial portion
Numbers5:27 When he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself
Numbers5:29 “‘This is the law for cases of jealousy
Numbers5:30 or when jealous feelings come over a man and he becomes suspicious of his wife; then he must have the woman stand before the LORD
Numbers5:31 Then the man will be free from iniquity
Numbers6:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers6:2 “Speak to the Israelites
Numbers6:3 he must separate himself from wine and strong drink
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation he must not eat anything that is produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 “‘All the days of the vow of his separation no razor may be used on his head until the time is fulfilled for which he separated himself to the LORD. He will be holy
Numbers6:6 “‘All the days that he separates himself to the LORD he must not contact a dead body.
Numbers6:7 He must not defile himself even for his father or his mother or his brother or his sister if they die
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation he must be holy to the LORD.
Numbers6:9 “‘If anyone dies very suddenly beside him and he defiles his consecrated head
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he is to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest
Numbers6:11 Then the priest will offer one for a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 He must rededicate to the LORD the days of his separation and bring a male lamb in its first year as a reparation offering
Numbers6:13 “‘Now this is the law of the Nazirite: When the days of his separation are fulfilled
Numbers6:14 and he must present his offering to the LORD: one male lamb in its first year without blemish for a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 and a basket of bread made without yeast
Numbers6:16 “‘Then the priest must present all these before the LORD and offer his purification offering and his burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 Then he must offer the ram as a peace offering to the LORD
Numbers6:18 “‘Then the Nazirite must shave his consecrated head at the entrance to the tent of meeting and must take the hair from his consecrated head and put it on the fire where the peace offering is burning.
Numbers6:19 And the priest must take the boiled shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 then the priest must wave them as a wave offering before the LORD; it is a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 “This is the law of the Nazirite who vows to the LORD his offering according to his separation
Numbers6:22 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers6:23 “Tell Aaron and his sons
Numbers6:24 “The LORD bless you and protect you;
Numbers6:25 The LORD make his face to shine upon you
Numbers6:26 The LORD lift up his countenance upon you and give you peace.”’
Numbers6:27 So they will put my name on the Israelites

Numbers7:2 Then the leaders of Israel
Numbers7:3 They brought their offering before the LORD
Numbers7:4 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers7:5 “Receive these gifts from them
Numbers7:6 So Moses accepted the carts and the oxen and gave them to the Levites.
Numbers7:7 He gave two carts and four oxen to the Gershonites
Numbers7:8 and he gave four carts and eight oxen to the Merarites
Numbers7:9 But to the Kohathites he gave none
Numbers7:10 The leaders offered gifts for the dedication of the altar when it was anointed. And the leaders presented their offering before the altar.
Numbers7:11 For the LORD said to Moses
Numbers7:12 The one who presented his offering on the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab
Numbers7:13 His offering was one silver platter weighing 130 shekels
Numbers7:14 one gold pan weighing 10 shekels
Numbers7:15 one young bull
Numbers7:16 one male goat for a purification offering;
Numbers7:17 and for the sacrifice of peace offerings: two bulls
Numbers7:18 On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar
Numbers7:19 He offered for his offering one silver platter weighing 130 shekels and one silver sprinkling bowl weighing 70
Numbers7:20 one gold pan weighing 10 shekels
Numbers7:21 one young bull
Numbers7:22 one male goat for a purification offering;
Numbers7:23 and for the sacrifice of peace offerings: two bulls
Numbers7:24 On the third day Eliab son of Helon
Numbers7:25 His offering was one silver platter weighing 130 shekels and one silver sprinkling bowl weighing 70 shekels
Numbers7:26 one gold pan weighing 10 shekels
Numbers7:27 one young bull
Numbers7:28 one male goat for a purification offering;
Numbers7:29 and for the sacrifice of peace offerings: two bulls

Numbers7:89 Now when Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with the LORD
Numbers8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:2 “Speak to Aaron and tell him
Numbers8:3 And Aaron did so; he set up the lamps to face toward the front of the lampstand
Numbers8:4 This is how the lampstand was made: It was beaten work in gold; from its shaft to its flowers it was beaten work. According to the pattern which the LORD had shown Moses
Numbers8:5 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:6 “Take the Levites from among the Israelites and purify them.
Numbers8:7 And do this to them to purify them: Sprinkle water of purification on them; then have them shave all their body and wash their clothes
Numbers8:8 Then they are to take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with olive oil; and you are to take a second young bull for a purification offering.
Numbers8:9 You are to bring the Levites before the tent of meeting and assemble the entire community of the Israelites.
Numbers8:10 Then you are to bring the Levites before the LORD
Numbers8:11 and Aaron is to offer the Levites before the LORD as a wave offering from the Israelites
Numbers8:12 When the Levites lay their hands on the heads of the bulls
Numbers8:13 You are to have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons
Numbers8:14 And so you are to separate the Levites from among the Israelites
Numbers8:15 “After this
Numbers8:16 For they are entirely given to me from among the Israelites. I have taken them for myself instead of all who open the womb
Numbers8:17 For all the firstborn males among the Israelites are mine
Numbers8:18 So I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn sons among the Israelites.
Numbers8:19 I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites
Numbers8:20 So Moses and Aaron and the entire community of the Israelites did this with the Levites. According to all that the LORD commanded Moses concerning the Levites
Numbers8:21 The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothing; then Aaron presented them like a wave offering before the LORD
Numbers8:22 After this
Numbers8:23 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:24 “This is what pertains to the Levites: At the age of twenty-five years and upward one may begin to join the company in the work of the tent of meeting
Numbers8:25 and at the age of fifty years they must retire from performing the work and may no longer work.
Numbers8:26 They may assist their colleagues in the tent of meeting

Numbers15:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers15:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Numbers15:3 and you make an offering by fire to the LORD from the herd or from the flock (whether a burnt offering or a sacrifice for discharging a vow or as a freewill offering or in your solemn feasts) to create a pleasing aroma to the LORD
Numbers15:4 then the one who presents his offering to the LORD must bring a grain offering of one-tenth of an ephah of finely ground flour mixed with one fourth of a hin of olive oil.
Numbers15:5 You must also prepare one-fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering with the burnt offering or the sacrifice for each lamb.
Numbers15:6 Or for a ram
Numbers15:7 and for a drink offering you must offer one-third of a hin of wine as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:8 And when you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice for discharging a vow or as a peace offering to the LORD
Numbers15:9 then a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of finely ground flour mixed with half a hin of olive oil must be presented with the young bull
Numbers15:10 and you must present as the drink offering half a hin of wine with the fire offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:11 This is what is to be done for each ox
Numbers15:12 You must do so for each one according to the number that you prepare.
Numbers15:13 “‘Every native-born person must do these things in this way to present an offering made by fire as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:14 If a resident foreigner is living with you — or whoever is among you in future generations — and prepares an offering made by fire as a pleasing aroma to the LORD
Numbers15:15 One statute must apply to you who belong to the congregation and to the resident foreigner who is living among you
Numbers15:16 One law and one custom must apply to you and to the resident foreigner who lives alongside you.’”
Numbers15:17 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers15:18 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them
Numbers15:19 and you eat some of the food of the land
Numbers15:20 You must offer up a cake of the first of your finely ground flour as a raised offering; as you offer the raised offering of the threshing floor
Numbers15:21 You must give to the LORD some of the first of your finely ground flour as a raised offering in your future generations.
Numbers15:22 “‘If you sin unintentionally and do not observe all these commandments that the LORD has spoken to Moses —
Numbers15:23 all that the LORD has commanded you by the authority of Moses
Numbers15:24 then if anything is done unintentionally without the knowledge of the community
Numbers15:25 And the priest is to make atonement for the whole community of the Israelites
Numbers15:26 And the whole community of the Israelites and the resident foreigner who lives among them will be forgiven
Numbers15:27 “‘If any person sins unintentionally
Numbers15:28 And the priest must make atonement for the person who sins unintentionally — when he sins unintentionally before the LORD — to make atonement for him
Numbers15:29 You must have one law for the person who sins unintentionally
Numbers15:30 “‘But the person who acts defiantly
Numbers15:31 Because he has despised the word of the LORD and has broken his commandment

Numbers15:35 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers15:36 So the whole community took him outside the camp and stoned him to death

Numbers15:38 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them to make tassels for themselves on the corners of their garments throughout their generations
Numbers15:39 You must have this tassel so that you may look at it and remember all the commandments of the LORD and obey them and so that you do not follow after your own heart and your own eyes that lead you to unfaithfulness.
Numbers15:40 Thus you will remember and obey all my commandments and be holy to your God.
Numbers15:41 I am the LORD your God

Numbers18:16 And those that must be redeemed you are to redeem when they are a month old
Numbers18:17 But you must not redeem the firstborn of a cow or a sheep or a goat; they are holy. You must splash their blood on the altar and burn their fat for an offering made by fire for a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers18:18 And their meat will be yours

Numbers18:26 “You are to speak to the Levites

Numbers19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers19:2 “This is the ordinance of the law which the LORD has commanded: ‘Instruct the Israelites to bring you a red heifer without blemish
Numbers19:3 You must give it to Eleazar the priest so that he can take it outside the camp
Numbers19:4 Eleazar the priest is to take some of its blood with his finger
Numbers19:5 Then the heifer must be burned in his sight — its skin
Numbers19:6 And the priest must take cedar wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Numbers19:8 The one who burns it must wash his clothes in water and bathe himself in water. He will be ceremonially unclean until evening.
Numbers19:9 “‘Then a man who is ceremonially clean must gather up the ashes of the red heifer and put them in a ceremonially clean place outside the camp. They must be kept for the community of the Israelites for use in the water of purification — it is a purification for sin.
Numbers19:10 The one who gathers the ashes of the heifer must wash his clothes and be ceremonially unclean until evening. This will be a permanent ordinance both for the Israelites and the resident foreigner who lives among them.
Numbers19:11 “‘Whoever touches the corpse of any person will be ceremonially unclean seven days.
Numbers19:12 He must purify himself with water on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Anyone who touches the corpse of any dead person and does not purify himself defiles the tabernacle of the LORD. And that person must be cut off from Israel
Numbers19:14 “‘This is the law: When a man dies in a tent
Numbers19:15 And every open container that has no covering fastened on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 And whoever touches the body of someone killed with a sword in the open fields
Numbers19:17 “‘For a ceremonially unclean person you must take some of the ashes of the heifer burnt for purification from sin and pour fresh running water over them in a vessel.
Numbers19:18 Then a ceremonially clean person must take hyssop
Numbers19:19 And the clean person must sprinkle the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day; and on the seventh day he must purify him
Numbers19:20 But the man who is unclean and does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 “‘So this will be a perpetual ordinance for them: The one who sprinkles the water of purification must wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 And whatever the unclean person touches will be unclean

Numbers35:6 Now from these towns that you will give to the Levites you must select six towns of refuge to which a person who has killed someone may flee. And you must give them forty-two other towns.
Numbers35:7 “So the total of the towns you will give the Levites is forty-eight. You must give these together with their grazing lands.
Numbers35:8 The towns you will give must be from the possession of the Israelites. From the larger tribes you must give more; and from the smaller tribes fewer. Each must contribute some of its own towns to the Levites in proportion to the inheritance allocated to each.

Numbers35:11 you must then designate some towns as towns of refuge for you

Numbers35:14 “You must give three towns on this side of the Jordan
Numbers35:15 These six towns will be places of refuge for the Israelites
Numbers35:16 “But if he hits someone with an iron tool so that he dies
Numbers35:17 If he strikes him by throwing a stone large enough that he could die
Numbers35:18 Or if he strikes him with a wooden hand weapon so that he could die
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood himself must kill the murderer; when he meets him
Numbers35:20 “But if he strikes him out of hatred or throws something at him intentionally so that he dies
Numbers35:21 or with enmity he strikes him with his hand and he dies
Numbers35:22 “But if he strikes him suddenly
Numbers35:23 or with any stone large enough that a man could die
Numbers35:24 then the community must judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood according to these decisions.
Numbers35:25 The community must deliver the slayer out of the hand of the avenger of blood
Numbers35:26 But if the slayer at any time goes outside the boundary of the town to which he had fled
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the borders of the town of refuge
Numbers35:28 because the slayer should have stayed in his town of refuge until the death of the high priest. But after the death of the high priest
Numbers35:29 So these things must be a statutory ordinance for you throughout your generations
Numbers35:30 “Whoever kills any person
Numbers35:31 Moreover
Numbers35:32 And you must not accept a ransom for anyone who has fled to a town of refuge
Numbers35:33 “You must not pollute the land where you live
Numbers35:34 Therefore do not defile the land that you will inhabit

Deuteronomy5:12 Be careful to observe the Sabbath day just as the LORD your God has commanded you.
Deuteronomy5:13 You are to work and do all your tasks in six days
Deuteronomy5:14 but the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD your God. On that day you must not do any work
Deuteronomy5:15 Recall that you were slaves in the land of Egypt and that the LORD your God brought you out of there by strength and power. That is why the LORD your God has commanded you to observe the Sabbath day.
Deuteronomy5:16 Honor your father and your mother just as the LORD your God has commanded you to do
Deuteronomy5:17 You must not murder.
Deuteronomy5:18 You must not commit adultery.
Deuteronomy5:19 You must not steal.
Deuteronomy5:20 You must not offer false testimony against another.

Deuteronomy21:2 your elders and judges must go out and measure how far it is to the cities in the vicinity of the corpse.
Deuteronomy21:3 Then the elders of the city nearest to the corpse must take from the herd a heifer that has not been worked — that has never pulled with the yoke —
Deuteronomy21:4 and bring the heifer down to a wadi with flowing water
Deuteronomy21:5 Then the Levitical priests will approach (for the LORD your God has chosen them to serve him and to pronounce blessings in his name
Deuteronomy21:6 and all the elders of that city nearest the corpse must wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken in the valley.
Deuteronomy21:7 Then they must proclaim
Deuteronomy21:8 Do not blame your people Israel whom you redeemed
Deuteronomy21:9 In this manner you will purge out the guilt of innocent blood from among you
Deuteronomy21:10 When you go out to do battle with your enemies and the LORD your God allows you to prevail and you take prisoners
Deuteronomy21:11 if you should see among them an attractive woman whom you wish to take as a wife
Deuteronomy21:12 you may bring her back to your house. She must shave her head
Deuteronomy21:13 discard the clothing she was wearing when captured
Deuteronomy21:14 If you are not pleased with her
Deuteronomy21:15 Suppose a man has two wives
Deuteronomy21:16 In the day he divides his inheritance he must not appoint as firstborn the son of the favorite wife in place of the other wife’s son who is actually the firstborn.
Deuteronomy21:17 Rather
Deuteronomy21:18 If a person has a stubborn
Deuteronomy21:19 his father and mother must seize him and bring him to the elders at the gate of his city.
Deuteronomy21:20 They must declare to the elders of his city
Deuteronomy21:21 Then all the men of his city must stone him to death. In this way you will purge out wickedness from among you
Deuteronomy21:22 If a person commits a sin punishable by death and is executed
Deuteronomy21:23 his body must not remain all night on the tree; instead you must make certain you bury him that same day
Deuteronomy22:1 When you see your neighbor’s ox or sheep going astray
Deuteronomy22:2 If the owner does not live near you or you do not know who the owner is
Deuteronomy22:3 You shall do the same to his donkey
Deuteronomy22:4 When you see your neighbor’s donkey or ox fallen along the road
Deuteronomy22:5 A woman must not wear men’s clothing
Deuteronomy22:6 If you happen to notice a bird’s nest along the road
Deuteronomy22:7 You must be sure to let the mother go
Deuteronomy22:8 If you build a new house
Deuteronomy22:9 You must not plant your vineyard with two kinds of seed; otherwise the entire yield
Deuteronomy22:10 You must not plow with an ox and a donkey harnessed together.
Deuteronomy22:11 You must not wear clothing made with wool and linen meshed together.
Deuteronomy22:12 You shall make yourselves tassels for the four corners of the clothing you wear.
Deuteronomy22:13 Suppose a man marries a woman
Deuteronomy22:14 accusing her of impropriety and defaming her reputation by saying
Deuteronomy22:15 Then the father and mother of the young woman must produce the evidence of virginity for the elders of the city at the gate.
Deuteronomy22:16 The young woman’s father must say to the elders
Deuteronomy22:17 Moreover
Deuteronomy22:18 The elders of that city must then seize the man and punish him.
Deuteronomy22:19 They will fine him one hundred shekels of silver and give them to the young woman’s father
Deuteronomy22:20 But if the accusation is true and the young woman was not a virgin
Deuteronomy22:21 the men of her city must bring the young woman to the door of her father’s house and stone her to death
Deuteronomy22:22 If a man is caught having sexual relations with a married woman both the man who had relations with the woman and the woman herself must die; in this way you will purge evil from Israel.
Deuteronomy22:23 If a virgin is engaged to a man and another man meets her in the city and has sexual relations with her
Deuteronomy22:24 you must bring the two of them to the gate of that city and stone them to death
Deuteronomy22:25 But if the man came across the engaged woman in the field and overpowered her and raped her
Deuteronomy22:26 You must not do anything to the young woman — she has done nothing deserving of death. This case is the same as when someone attacks another person and murders him
Deuteronomy22:27 for the man met her in the field and the engaged woman cried out
Deuteronomy22:28 Suppose a man comes across a virgin who is not engaged and overpowers and rapes her and they are discovered.
Deuteronomy22:29 The man who has raped her must pay her father fifty shekels of silver and she must become his wife because he has violated her; he may never divorce her as long as he lives.
Deuteronomy22:30 (23:1) A man may not marry his father’s former wife and in this way dishonor his father.
Deuteronomy23:1 A man with crushed or severed genitals may not enter the assembly of the LORD.
Deuteronomy23:2 A person of illegitimate birth may not enter the assembly of the LORD; to the tenth generation no one related to him may do so.
Deuteronomy23:3 An Ammonite or Moabite may not enter the assembly of the LORD; to the tenth generation none of their descendants shall ever do so
Deuteronomy23:4 for they did not meet you with food and water on the way as you came from Egypt
Deuteronomy23:5 But the LORD your God refused to listen to Balaam and changed the curse to a blessing
Deuteronomy23:6 You must not seek peace and prosperity for them through all the ages to come.
Deuteronomy23:7 You must not hate an Edomite
Deuteronomy23:8 Children of the third generation born to them may enter the assembly of the LORD.
Deuteronomy23:9 When you go out as an army against your enemies
Deuteronomy23:10 If there is someone among you who is impure because of some nocturnal emission
Deuteronomy23:11 When evening arrives he must wash himself with water and then at sunset he may reenter the camp.
Deuteronomy23:12 You are to have a place outside the camp to serve as a latrine.
Deuteronomy23:13 You must have a spade among your other equipment and when you relieve yourself outside you must dig a hole with the spade and then turn and cover your excrement.
Deuteronomy23:14 For the LORD your God walks about in the middle of your camp to deliver you and defeat your enemies for you. Therefore your camp should be holy
Deuteronomy23:15 You must not return an escaped slave to his master when he has run away to you.
Deuteronomy23:16 Indeed
Deuteronomy23:17 There must never be a sacred prostitute among the young women of Israel nor a sacred male prostitute among the young men of Israel.
Deuteronomy23:18 You must never bring the pay of a female prostitute or the wage of a male prostitute into the temple of the LORD your God in fulfillment of any vow
Deuteronomy23:19 You must not charge interest on a loan to your fellow Israelite
Deuteronomy23:20 You may lend with interest to a foreigner
Deuteronomy23:21 When you make a vow to the LORD your God you must not delay in fulfilling it
Deuteronomy23:22 If you refrain from making a vow
Deuteronomy23:23 Whatever you vow
Deuteronomy23:24 When you enter the vineyard of your neighbor you may eat as many grapes as you please
Deuteronomy23:25 When you go into the ripe grain fields of your neighbor you may pluck off the kernels with your hand
Deuteronomy24:1 If a man marries a woman and she does not please him because he has found something offensive in her
Deuteronomy24:2 When she has left him she may go and become someone else’s wife.
Deuteronomy24:3 If the second husband rejects her and then divorces her
Deuteronomy24:4 her first husband who divorced her is not permitted to remarry her after she has become ritually impure
Deuteronomy24:5 When a man is newly married
Deuteronomy24:6 One must not take either lower or upper millstones as security on a loan
Deuteronomy24:7 If a man is found kidnapping a person from among his fellow Israelites
Deuteronomy24:8 Be careful during an outbreak of leprosy to follow precisely all that the Levitical priests instruct you; as I have commanded them
Deuteronomy24:9 Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam along the way after you left Egypt.
Deuteronomy24:10 When you make any kind of loan to your neighbor
Deuteronomy24:11 You must stand outside and the person to whom you are making the loan will bring out to you what he is offering as security.
Deuteronomy24:12 If the person is poor you may not use what he gives you as security for a covering.
Deuteronomy24:13 You must by all means return to him at sunset the item he gave you as security so that he may sleep in his outer garment and bless you for it; it will be considered a just deed by the LORD your God.
Deuteronomy24:14 You must not oppress a lowly and poor servant
Deuteronomy24:15 You must pay his wage that very day before the sun sets
Deuteronomy24:16 Fathers must not be put to death for what their children do
Deuteronomy24:17 You must not pervert justice due a resident foreigner or an orphan
Deuteronomy24:18 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt and that the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I am commanding you to do all this.
Deuteronomy24:19 Whenever you reap your harvest in your field and leave some unraked grain there
Deuteronomy24:20 When you beat your olive tree you must not repeat the procedure; the remaining olives belong to the resident foreigner
Deuteronomy24:21 When you gather the grapes of your vineyard you must not do so a second time; they should go to the resident foreigner
Deuteronomy24:22 Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt; therefore
Deuteronomy25:1 If controversy arises between people
Deuteronomy25:2 Then
Deuteronomy25:3 The judge may sentence him to forty blows
Deuteronomy25:4 You must not muzzle your ox when it is treading grain.
Deuteronomy25:5 If brothers live together and one of them dies without having a son
Deuteronomy25:6 Then the first son she bears will continue the name of the dead brother
Deuteronomy25:7 But if the man does not want to marry his brother’s widow
Deuteronomy25:8 Then the elders of his city must summon him and speak to him. If he persists
Deuteronomy25:9 then his sister-in-law must approach him in view of the elders
Deuteronomy25:10 His family name will be referred to in Israel as “the family of the one whose sandal was removed.”
Deuteronomy25:11 If two men get into a hand-to-hand fight
Deuteronomy25:12 then you must cut off her hand — do not pity her.
Deuteronomy25:13 You must not have in your bag different stone weights
Deuteronomy25:14 You must not have in your house different measuring containers
Deuteronomy25:15 You must have an accurate and correct stone weight and an accurate and correct measuring container
Deuteronomy25:16 For anyone who acts dishonestly in these ways is abhorrent to the LORD your God.
Deuteronomy25:17 Remember what the Amalekites did to you on your way from Egypt
Deuteronomy25:18 how they met you along the way and cut off all your stragglers in the rear of the march when you were exhausted and tired; they were unafraid of God.
Deuteronomy25:19 So when the LORD your God gives you relief from all the enemies who surround you in the land he is giving you as an inheritance
Deuteronomy26:1 When you enter the land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance
Deuteronomy26:2 you must take the first of all the ground’s produce you harvest from the land the LORD your God is giving you
Deuteronomy26:3 You must go to the priest in office at that time and say to him
Deuteronomy26:4 The priest will then take the basket from you and set it before the altar of the LORD your God.
Deuteronomy26:5 Then you must affirm before the LORD YOUR GOD
Deuteronomy26:6 But the Egyptians mistreated and oppressed us
Deuteronomy26:7 So we cried out to the LORD
Deuteronomy26:8 Therefore the LORD brought us out of Egypt with tremendous strength and power
Deuteronomy26:9 Then he brought us to this place and gave us this land
Deuteronomy26:10 So now
Deuteronomy26:11 You will celebrate all the good things that the LORD your God has given you and your family
Deuteronomy26:12 When you finish tithing all your income in the third year (the year of tithing)
Deuteronomy26:13 Then you shall say before the LORD your God
Deuteronomy26:14 I have not eaten anything when I was in mourning

Deuteronomy26:16 Today the LORD your God is commanding you to keep these statutes and ordinances
Deuteronomy26:17 Today you have declared the LORD to be your God
Deuteronomy26:18 And today the LORD has declared you to be his special people (as he already promised you) so you may keep all his commandments.
Deuteronomy26:19 Then he will elevate you above all the nations he has made and you will receive praise
Deuteronomy27:1 Then Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people: “Pay attention to all the commandments I am giving you today.

Deuteronomy27:4 So when you cross the Jordan you must erect on Mount Ebal these stones about which I am commanding you today
Deuteronomy27:5 Then you must build an altar there to the LORD your God
Deuteronomy27:6 You must build the altar of the LORD your God with whole stones and offer burnt offerings on it to the LORD your God.
Deuteronomy27:7 Also you must offer fellowship offerings and eat them there
Deuteronomy27:8 You must inscribe on the stones all the words of this law
Deuteronomy27:9 Then Moses and the Levitical priests spoke to all Israel: “Be quiet and pay attention
Deuteronomy27:10 You must obey him and keep his commandments and statutes that I am giving you today.”
Deuteronomy27:11 Moreover
Deuteronomy27:12 “The following tribes must stand to bless the people on Mount Gerizim when you cross the Jordan: Simeon
Deuteronomy27:13 And these other tribes must stand for the curse on Mount Ebal: Reuben
Deuteronomy27:14 “The Levites will call out to every Israelite with a loud voice:
Deuteronomy27:15 ‘Cursed is the one who makes a carved or metal image — something abhorrent to the LORD
Deuteronomy27:16 ‘Cursed is the one who disrespects his father and mother.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:17 ‘Cursed is the one who moves his neighbor’s boundary marker.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:18 ‘Cursed is the one who misleads a blind person on the road.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:19 ‘Cursed is the one who perverts justice for the resident foreigner
Deuteronomy27:20 ‘Cursed is the one who has sexual relations with his father’s former wife
Deuteronomy27:21 ‘Cursed is the one who commits bestiality.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:22 ‘Cursed is the one who has sexual relations with his sister
Deuteronomy27:23 ‘Cursed is the one who has sexual relations with his mother-in-law.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:24 ‘Cursed is the one who kills his neighbor in private.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:25 ‘Cursed is the one who takes a bribe to kill an innocent person.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy27:26 ‘Cursed is the one who refuses to keep the words of this law.’ Then all the people will say
Deuteronomy28:1 “If you indeed obey the LORD your God and are careful to observe all his commandments I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:2 All these blessings will come to you in abundance if you obey the LORD your God:
Deuteronomy28:3 You will be blessed in the city and blessed in the field.
Deuteronomy28:4 Your children will be blessed
Deuteronomy28:5 Your basket and your mixing bowl will be blessed.
Deuteronomy28:6 You will be blessed when you come in and blessed when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:7 The LORD will cause your enemies who attack you to be struck down before you; they will attack you from one direction but flee from you in seven different directions.
Deuteronomy28:8 The LORD will decree blessing for you with respect to your barns and in everything you do — yes
Deuteronomy28:9 The LORD will designate you as his holy people just as he promised you
Deuteronomy28:10 Then all the peoples of the earth will see that you belong to the LORD
Deuteronomy28:11 The LORD will greatly multiply your children
Deuteronomy28:12 The LORD will open for you his good treasure house
Deuteronomy28:13 The LORD will make you the head and not the tail
Deuteronomy28:14 But you must not turn away from all the commandments I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:15 “But if you ignore the LORD your God and are not careful to keep all his commandments and statutes I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:16 You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the field.
Deuteronomy28:17 Your basket and your mixing bowl will be cursed.
Deuteronomy28:18 Your children will be cursed
Deuteronomy28:19 You will be cursed when you come in and cursed when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:20 “The LORD will send on you a curse
Deuteronomy28:21 The LORD will plague you with deadly diseases until he has completely removed you from the land you are about to possess.
Deuteronomy28:22 He will afflict you with weakness
Deuteronomy28:23 The sky above your heads will be bronze and the earth beneath you iron.
Deuteronomy28:24 The LORD will make the rain of your land powder and dust; it will come down on you from the sky until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:25 “The LORD will allow you to be struck down before your enemies; you will attack them from one direction but flee from them in seven directions and will become an object of terror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
Deuteronomy28:26 Your carcasses will be food for every bird of the sky and wild animal of the earth
Deuteronomy28:27 The LORD will afflict you with the boils of Egypt and with tumors
Deuteronomy28:28 The LORD will also subject you to madness
Deuteronomy28:29 You will feel your way along at noon like the blind person does in darkness and you will not succeed in anything you do; you will be constantly oppressed and continually robbed
Deuteronomy28:30 You will be engaged to a woman and another man will rape her. You will build a house but not live in it. You will plant a vineyard but not even begin to use it.
Deuteronomy28:31 Your ox will be slaughtered before your very eyes but you will not eat of it. Your donkey will be stolen from you as you watch and will not be returned to you. Your flock of sheep will be given to your enemies and there will be no one to save you.
Deuteronomy28:32 Your sons and daughters will be given to another people while you look on in vain all day
Deuteronomy28:33 As for the produce of your land and all your labor
Deuteronomy28:34 You will go insane from seeing all this.
Deuteronomy28:35 The LORD will afflict you in your knees and on your legs with painful
Deuteronomy28:36 The LORD will force you and your king whom you will appoint over you to go away to a people whom you and your ancestors have not known
Deuteronomy28:37 You will become an occasion of horror
Deuteronomy28:38 “You will take much seed to the field but gather little harvest
Deuteronomy28:39 You will plant vineyards and cultivate them
Deuteronomy28:40 You will have olive trees throughout your territory but you will not anoint yourself with olive oil
Deuteronomy28:41 You will bear sons and daughters but not keep them
Deuteronomy28:42 Whirring locusts will take over every tree and all the produce of your soil.
Deuteronomy28:43 The foreigners who reside among you will become higher and higher over you and you will become lower and lower.
Deuteronomy28:44 They will lend to you but you will not lend to them; they will become the head and you will become the tail!
Deuteronomy28:45 All these curses will fall on you
Deuteronomy28:46 These curses will be a perpetual sign and wonder with reference to you and your descendants.
Deuteronomy28:47 “Because you have not served the LORD your God joyfully and wholeheartedly with the abundance of everything you have
Deuteronomy28:48 instead in hunger
Deuteronomy28:49 The LORD will raise up a distant nation against you
Deuteronomy28:50 a nation of stern appearance that will have no regard for the elderly or pity for the young.
Deuteronomy28:51 They will devour the offspring of your livestock and the produce of your soil until you are destroyed. They will not leave you with any grain
Deuteronomy28:52 They will besiege all of your villages until all of your high and fortified walls collapse — those in which you put your confidence throughout the land. They will besiege all your villages throughout the land the LORD your God has given you.
Deuteronomy28:53 You will then eat your own offspring
Deuteronomy28:54 The man among you who is by nature tender and sensitive will turn against his brother
Deuteronomy28:55 He will withhold from all of them his children’s flesh that he is eating (since there is nothing else left)
Deuteronomy28:56 Likewise
Deuteronomy28:57 and will secretly eat her afterbirth and her newborn children (since she has nothing else)
Deuteronomy28:58 “If you refuse to obey all the words of this law
Deuteronomy28:59 then the LORD will increase your punishments and those of your descendants — great and long-lasting afflictions and severe
Deuteronomy28:60 He will infect you with all the diseases of Egypt that you dreaded
Deuteronomy28:61 Moreover
Deuteronomy28:62 There will be very few of you left
Deuteronomy28:63 This is what will happen: Just as the LORD delighted to do good for you and make you numerous
Deuteronomy28:64 The LORD will scatter you among all nations
Deuteronomy28:65 Among those nations you will have no rest nor will there be a place of peaceful rest for the soles of your feet
Deuteronomy28:66 Your life will hang in doubt before you; you will be terrified by night and day and will have no certainty of surviving from one day to the next.
Deuteronomy28:67 In the morning you will say
Deuteronomy28:68 Then the LORD will make you return to Egypt by ship
Deuteronomy29:1 (28:69) These are the words of the covenant that the LORD commanded Moses to make with the people of Israel in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy29:2 Moses proclaimed to all Israel as follows: “You have seen all that the LORD did in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh
Deuteronomy29:3 Your eyes have seen the great judgments
Deuteronomy29:4 But to this very day the LORD has not given you an understanding mind
Deuteronomy29:5 I have led you through the desert for forty years. Your clothing has not worn out nor have your sandals deteriorated.
Deuteronomy29:6 You have eaten no bread and drunk no wine or beer — all so that you might know that I am the LORD your God!
Deuteronomy29:7 When you came to this place King Sihon of Heshbon and King Og of Bashan came out to make war and we defeated them.
Deuteronomy29:8 Then we took their land and gave it as an inheritance to Reuben
Deuteronomy29:9 “Therefore
Deuteronomy29:10 You are standing today
Deuteronomy29:11 your infants
Deuteronomy29:12 so that you may enter by oath into the covenant the LORD your God is making with you today.
Deuteronomy29:13 Today he will affirm that you are his people and that he is your God
Deuteronomy29:14 It is not with you alone that I am making this covenant by oath
Deuteronomy29:15 but with whoever stands with us here today before the LORD our God as well as those not with us here today.
Deuteronomy29:16 “(For you know how we lived in the land of Egypt and how we crossed through the nations as we traveled.
Deuteronomy29:17 You have seen their detestable things and idols of wood
Deuteronomy29:18 Beware that the heart of no man
Deuteronomy29:19 When such a person hears the words of this oath he secretly blesses himself and says
Deuteronomy29:20 The LORD will be unwilling to forgive him
Deuteronomy29:21 The LORD will single him out for judgment from all the tribes of Israel according to all the curses of the covenant written in this scroll of the law.
Deuteronomy29:22 The generation to come — your descendants who will rise up after you

Deuteronomy29:29 Secret things belong to the LORD our God
Deuteronomy30:1 “When you have experienced all these things
Deuteronomy30:2 Then if you and your descendants turn to the LORD your God and obey him with your whole mind and being just as I am commanding you today
Deuteronomy30:3 the LORD your God will reverse your captivity and have pity on you. He will turn and gather you from all the peoples among whom he has scattered you.
Deuteronomy30:4 Even if your exiles are in the most distant land
Deuteronomy30:5 Then he will bring you to the land your ancestors possessed and you also will possess it; he will do better for you and multiply you more than he did your ancestors.
Deuteronomy30:6 The LORD your God will also cleanse your heart and the hearts of your descendants so that you may love him with all your mind and being and so that you may live.
Deuteronomy30:7 Then the LORD your God will put all these curses on your enemies
Deuteronomy30:8 You will return and obey the LORD
Deuteronomy30:9 The LORD your God will make the labor of your hands abundantly successful and multiply your children
Deuteronomy30:10 if you obey the LORD your God and keep his commandments and statutes that are written in this scroll of the law. But you must turn to him with your whole mind and being.
Deuteronomy30:11 “This commandment I am giving you today is not too difficult for you
Deuteronomy30:12 It is not in heaven
Deuteronomy30:13 And it is not across the sea
Deuteronomy30:14 For the thing is very near you — it is in your mouth and in your mind so that you can do it.
Deuteronomy30:15 “Look! I have set before you today life and prosperity on the one hand
Deuteronomy30:16 What I am commanding you today is to love the LORD your God
Deuteronomy30:17 However
Deuteronomy30:18 I declare to you this very day that you will certainly perish! You will not extend your time in the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess.
Deuteronomy30:19 Today I invoke heaven and earth as a witness against you that I have set life and death
Deuteronomy30:20 I also call on you to love the LORD your God

Deuteronomy31:8 The LORD is indeed going before you — he will be with you; he will not fail you or abandon you. Do not be afraid or discouraged!”

Deuteronomy31:11 when all Israel comes to appear before the LORD your God in the place he chooses
Deuteronomy31:12 Gather the people — men
Deuteronomy31:13 Then their children
Deuteronomy31:14 Then the LORD said to Moses
Deuteronomy31:15 The LORD appeared in the tent in a pillar of cloud that stood above the door of the tent.

Deuteronomy31:18 But I will certainly hide myself at that time because of all the wickedness they will have done by turning to other gods.

Deuteronomy31:20 For after I have brought them to the land I promised to their ancestors — one flowing with milk and honey — and they eat their fill and become fat
Deuteronomy31:21 Then when many disasters and distresses overcome them this song will testify against them
Deuteronomy31:22 So on that day Moses wrote down this song and taught it to the Israelites
