Deuteronomy31:26 “Take this scroll of the law and place it beside the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God. It will remain there as a witness against you
Deuteronomy31:27 for I know about your rebellion and stubbornness. Indeed
Deuteronomy31:28 Gather to me all your tribal elders and officials so I can speak to them directly about these things and call the heavens and the earth to witness against them.
Deuteronomy31:29 For I know that after I die you will totally corrupt yourselves and turn away from the path I have commanded you to walk. Disaster will confront you in the days to come because you will act wickedly before the LORD
Deuteronomy31:30 Then Moses recited the words of this song from start to finish in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel.
Deuteronomy32:1 Listen
Deuteronomy32:2 My teaching will drop like the rain
Deuteronomy32:3 For I will proclaim the name of the LORD; you must acknowledge the greatness of our God.
Deuteronomy32:4 As for the Rock
Deuteronomy32:5 His people have been unfaithful to him; they have not acted like his children — this is their sin. They are a perverse and deceitful generation.
Deuteronomy32:6 Is this how you repay the LORD
Deuteronomy32:7 Remember the ancient days; bear in mind the years of past generations. Ask your father and he will inform you
Deuteronomy32:8 When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance
Deuteronomy32:9 For the LORD’s allotment is his people
Deuteronomy32:10 The LORD found him in a desolate land
Deuteronomy32:11 Like an eagle that stirs up its nest
Deuteronomy32:12 The LORD alone was guiding him
Deuteronomy32:13 He enabled him to travel over the high terrain of the land
Deuteronomy32:14 butter from the herd and milk from the flock
Deuteronomy32:15 But Jeshurun became fat and kicked
Deuteronomy32:16 They made him jealous with other gods
Deuteronomy32:17 They sacrificed to demons
Deuteronomy32:18 You have forgotten the Rock who fathered you
Deuteronomy32:19 But the LORD took note and despised them because his sons and daughters enraged him.
Deuteronomy32:20 He said
Deuteronomy32:21 They have made me jealous with false gods
Deuteronomy32:22 For a fire has been kindled by my anger
Deuteronomy32:23 I will increase their disasters
Deuteronomy32:24 They will be starved by famine
Deuteronomy32:25 The sword will make people childless outside
Deuteronomy32:26 “I said
Deuteronomy32:27 But I fear the reaction of their enemies
Deuteronomy32:28 They are a nation devoid of wisdom
Deuteronomy32:29 I wish that they were wise and could understand this
Deuteronomy32:30 How can one man chase a thousand of them
Deuteronomy32:31 For our enemies’ rock is not like our Rock
Deuteronomy32:32 For their vine is from the stock of Sodom
Deuteronomy32:33 Their wine is snakes’ poison
Deuteronomy32:34 “Is this not stored up with me?” says the LORD
Deuteronomy32:35 I will get revenge and pay them back at the time their foot slips; for the day of their disaster is near
Deuteronomy32:36 The LORD will judge his people
Deuteronomy32:37 He will say
Deuteronomy32:38 who ate the best of their sacrifices
Deuteronomy32:39 “See now that I
Deuteronomy32:40 For I raise up my hand to heaven
Deuteronomy32:41 I will sharpen my lightning-like sword
Deuteronomy32:42 I will make my arrows drunk with blood
Deuteronomy32:43 Cry out
Deuteronomy32:44 Then Moses went with Joshua son of Nun and recited all the words of this song to the people.
Deuteronomy32:45 When Moses finished reciting all these words to all Israel
Deuteronomy32:46 he said to them
Deuteronomy32:47 For this is no idle word for you — it is your life! By this word you will live a long time in the land you are about to cross the Jordan to possess.”
Deuteronomy32:48 Then the LORD said to Moses that same day
Deuteronomy32:49 “Go up to this Abarim hill country
Deuteronomy32:50 You will die on the mountain that you ascend and join your deceased ancestors
Deuteronomy32:51 for both of you rebelled against me among the Israelites at the waters of Meribah Kadesh in the desert of Zin when you did not show me proper respect among the Israelites.
Deuteronomy32:52 You will see the land before you
Deuteronomy33:1 This is the blessing Moses the man of God pronounced upon the Israelites before his death.
Deuteronomy33:2 He said: The LORD came from Sinai and revealed himself to Israel from Seir. He appeared in splendor from Mount Paran
Deuteronomy33:3 Surely he loves the people; all your holy ones are in your power. And they sit at your feet
Deuteronomy33:4 Moses delivered to us a law
Deuteronomy33:5 The LORD was king over Jeshurun
Deuteronomy33:6 May Reuben live and not die
Deuteronomy33:7 And this is the blessing to Judah. He said
Deuteronomy33:8 Of Levi he said: Your Thummim and Urim belong to your godly one
Deuteronomy33:9 He said to his father and mother
Deuteronomy33:10 They will teach Jacob your ordinances and Israel your law; they will offer incense as a pleasant odor
Deuteronomy33:11 Bless
Deuteronomy33:12 Of Benjamin he said: The beloved of the LORD will live safely by him; he protects him all the time
Deuteronomy33:13 Of Joseph he said: May the LORD bless his land with the harvest produced by the sky
Deuteronomy33:14 with the harvest produced by the daylight and by the moonlight;
Deuteronomy33:15 with the best of the ancient mountains and the harvest produced by the age-old hills;
Deuteronomy33:16 with the harvest of the earth and its fullness and the pleasure of him who resided in the burning bush. May blessing rest on Joseph’s head
Deuteronomy33:17 May the firstborn of his bull bring him honor
Deuteronomy33:18 Of Zebulun he said: Rejoice
Deuteronomy33:19 They will summon peoples to the mountain
Deuteronomy33:20 Of Gad he said: Blessed be the one who enlarges Gad. Like a lioness he will dwell; he will tear at an arm — indeed
Deuteronomy33:21 He has selected the best part for himself
Deuteronomy33:22 Of Dan he said: Dan is a lion’s cub; he will leap forth from Bashan.
Deuteronomy33:23 Of Naphtali he said: O Naphtali
Deuteronomy33:24 Of Asher he said: Asher is blessed with children
Deuteronomy33:25 The bars of your gates will be made of iron and bronze
Deuteronomy33:26 There is no one like God
Deuteronomy33:27 The everlasting God is a refuge
Deuteronomy33:28 Israel lives in safety
Deuteronomy33:29 You have joy
Deuteronomy34:1 Then Moses ascended from the deserts of Moab to Mount Nebo
Deuteronomy34:2 and all of Naphtali
Deuteronomy34:3 the Negev
Deuteronomy34:4 Then the LORD said to him
Deuteronomy34:5 So Moses
Deuteronomy34:6 He buried him in the land of Moab near Beth Peor
Deuteronomy34:7 Moses was 120 years old when he died
Deuteronomy34:8 The Israelites mourned for Moses in the deserts of Moab for thirty days; then the days of mourning for Moses ended.
Deuteronomy34:9 Now Joshua son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom
Deuteronomy34:10 No prophet ever again arose in Israel like Moses
Deuteronomy34:11 He did all the signs and wonders the LORD had sent him to do in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy34:12 and he displayed great power and awesome might in view of all Israel.
Joshua1:1 After Moses the LORD’s servant died
Joshua1:2 “Moses my servant is dead. Get ready! Cross the Jordan River! Lead these people into the land which I am ready to hand over to them.
Joshua1:3 I am handing over to you every place you set foot
Joshua1:4 Your territory will extend from the wilderness in the south to Lebanon in the north. It will extend all the way to the great River Euphrates in the east (including all of Syria) and all the way to the Mediterranean Sea in the west.
Joshua1:5 No one will be able to resist you all the days of your life. As I was with Moses
Joshua1:6 Be strong and brave! You must lead these people in the conquest of this land that I solemnly promised their ancestors I would hand over to them.
Joshua1:7 Make sure you are very strong and brave! Carefully obey all the law my servant Moses charged you to keep! Do not swerve from it to the right or to the left
Joshua1:8 This law scroll must not leave your lips! You must memorize it day and night so you can carefully obey all that is written in it. Then you will prosper and be successful.
Joshua1:9 I repeat
Joshua1:10 Joshua instructed the leaders of the people:
Joshua1:11 “Go through the camp and command the people
Joshua1:12 Joshua told the Reubenites
Joshua1:13 “Remember what Moses the LORD’s servant commanded you. The LORD your God is giving you a place to settle and is handing this land over to you.
Joshua1:14 Your wives
Joshua1:15 until the LORD gives your brothers a place like yours to settle and they conquer the land the LORD your God is ready to hand over to them. Then you may go back to your allotted land and occupy the land Moses the LORD’s servant assigned you east of the Jordan.”
Joshua1:16 They told Joshua
Joshua1:17 Just as we obeyed Moses
Joshua1:18 Any man who rebels against what you say and does not obey all your commands will be executed. But be strong and brave!”
Joshua2:1 Joshua son of Nun sent two spies out from Shittim secretly and instructed them: “Find out what you can about the land
Joshua2:2 The king of Jericho received this report: “Note well! Israelite men have come here tonight to spy on the land.”
Joshua2:3 So the king of Jericho sent this order to Rahab: “Turn over the men who came to you — the ones who came to your house — for they have come to spy on the whole land!”
Joshua2:4 But the woman hid the two men and replied
Joshua2:5 When it was time to shut the city gate for the night
Joshua2:6 (Now she had taken them up to the roof and had hidden them in the stalks of flax she had spread out on the roof.)
Joshua2:7 Meanwhile the king’s men tried to find them on the road to the Jordan River near the fords. The city gate was shut as soon as they set out in pursuit of them.
Joshua2:8 Now before the spies went to sleep
Joshua2:9 She said to the men
Joshua2:10 For we heard how the LORD dried up the water of the Red Sea before you when you left Egypt and how you annihilated the two Amorite kings
Joshua2:11 When we heard the news we lost our courage and no one could even breathe for fear of you. For the LORD your God is God in heaven above and on earth below!
Joshua2:12 So now
Joshua2:13 that you will spare the lives of my father
Joshua2:14 The men said to her
Joshua2:15 Then Rahab let them down by a rope through the window. (Her house was built as part of the city wall; she lived in the wall.)
Joshua2:16 She told them
Joshua2:17 The men said to her
Joshua2:18 When we invade the land
Joshua2:19 Anyone who leaves your house will be responsible for his own death — we are innocent in that case! But if anyone with you in the house is harmed
Joshua2:20 If you should report what we’ve been up to
Joshua2:21 She said
Joshua2:22 They went to the hill country and stayed there for three days
Joshua2:23 Then the two men returned — they came down from the hills
Joshua2:24 They told Joshua
Joshua3:1 Bright and early the next morning Joshua and the Israelites left Shittim and came to the Jordan. They camped there before crossing the river.
Joshua3:2 After three days the leaders went through the camp
Joshua3:3 and commanded the people: “When you see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God being carried by the Levitical priests
Joshua3:4 But stay about three thousand feet behind it. Keep your distance so you can see which way you should go
Joshua3:5 Joshua told the people
Joshua3:6 Joshua told the priests
Joshua3:7 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua3:8 Instruct the priests carrying the ark of the covenant
Joshua3:9 Joshua told the Israelites
Joshua3:10 Joshua continued
Joshua3:11 Look! The ark of the covenant of the Ruler of the whole earth is ready to enter the Jordan ahead of you.
Joshua3:12 Now select for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel
Joshua3:13 When the feet of the priests carrying the ark of the LORD
Joshua3:14 So when the people left their tents to cross the Jordan
Joshua3:15 When the ones carrying the ark reached the Jordan and the feet of the priests carrying the ark touched the surface of the water — (the Jordan is at flood stage all during harvest time) —
Joshua3:16 the water coming downstream toward them stopped flowing. It piled up far upstream at Adam (the city near Zarethan); there was no water at all flowing to the sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea). The people crossed the river opposite Jericho.
Joshua3:17 The priests carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD stood firmly on dry ground in the middle of the Jordan. All Israel crossed over on dry ground until the entire nation was on the other side.
Joshua4:1 When the entire nation was on the other side
Joshua4:2 “Select for yourselves twelve men from the people
Joshua4:3 Instruct them
Joshua4:4 Joshua summoned the twelve men he had appointed from the Israelites
Joshua4:5 Joshua told them
Joshua4:6 The stones will be a reminder to you. When your children ask someday
Joshua4:7 tell them how the water of the Jordan stopped flowing before the ark of the covenant of the LORD. When it crossed the Jordan
Joshua4:8 The Israelites did just as Joshua commanded. They picked up twelve stones
Joshua4:9 Joshua also set up twelve stones in the middle of the Jordan in the very place where the priests carrying the ark of the covenant stood. They remain there to this very day.
Joshua4:10 Now the priests carrying the ark of the covenant were standing in the middle of the Jordan until everything the Lord had commanded Joshua to tell the people was accomplished
Joshua4:11 and when all the people had finished crossing
Joshua4:12 The Reubenites
Joshua4:13 About forty thousand battle-ready troops marched past the LORD to fight on the plains of Jericho.
Joshua4:14 That day the LORD brought honor to Joshua before all Israel. They respected him all his life
Joshua4:15 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua4:16 “Instruct the priests carrying the ark of the covenantal laws to come up from the Jordan.”
Joshua4:17 So Joshua instructed the priests
Joshua4:18 The priests carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD came up from the middle of the Jordan
Joshua4:19 The people went up from the Jordan on the tenth day of the first month and camped in Gilgal on the eastern border of Jericho.
Joshua4:20 Now Joshua set up in Gilgal the twelve stones they had taken from the Jordan.
Joshua4:21 He told the Israelites
Joshua4:22 explain to your children
Joshua4:23 For the LORD your God dried up the water of the Jordan before you while you crossed over. It was just like when the LORD your God dried up the Red Sea before us while we crossed it.
Joshua4:24 He has done this so all the nations of the earth might recognize the LORD’s power and so you might always obey the LORD your God.”
Joshua5:1 When all the Amorite kings on the west side of the Jordan and all the Canaanite kings along the seacoast heard how the LORD had dried up the water of the Jordan before the Israelites while they crossed
Joshua5:2 At that time the LORD told Joshua
Joshua5:3 So Joshua made flint knives and circumcised the Israelites on the Hill of the Foreskins.
Joshua5:4 This is why Joshua had to circumcise them: All the men old enough to fight when they left Egypt died on the journey through the desert after they left Egypt.
Joshua5:5 Now all the men who left were circumcised
Joshua5:6 Indeed
Joshua5:7 He replaced them with their sons
Joshua5:8 When all the men had been circumcised
Joshua5:9 The LORD said to Joshua
Joshua5:10 So the Israelites camped in Gilgal and celebrated the Passover in the evening of the fourteenth day of the month on the plains of Jericho.
Joshua5:11 They ate some of the produce of the land the day after the Passover
Joshua5:12 The manna stopped appearing the day they ate some of the produce of the land; the Israelites never ate manna again.
Joshua5:13 When Joshua was near Jericho
Joshua5:14 He answered
Joshua5:15 The commander of the LORD’s army answered Joshua
Joshua6:1 Now Jericho was shut tightly because of the Israelites. No one was allowed to leave or enter.
Joshua6:2 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua6:3 Have all the warriors march around the city one time; do this for six days.
Joshua6:4 Have seven priests carry seven rams’ horns in front of the ark. On the seventh day march around the city seven times
Joshua6:5 When you hear the signal from the ram’s horn
Joshua6:6 So Joshua son of Nun summoned the priests and instructed them
Joshua6:7 And he told the army
Joshua6:8 When Joshua gave the army its orders
Joshua6:9 Armed troops marched ahead of the priests blowing the horns
Joshua6:10 Now Joshua had instructed the army
Joshua6:11 So Joshua made sure they marched the ark of the LORD around the city one time. Then they went back to the camp and spent the night there.
Joshua6:12 Bright and early the next morning Joshua had the priests pick up the ark of the LORD.
Joshua6:13 The seven priests carrying the seven rams’ horns before the ark of the LORD marched along blowing their horns. Armed troops marched ahead of them
Joshua6:14 They marched around the city one time on the second day
Joshua6:15 On the seventh day they were up at the crack of dawn and marched around the city as before — only this time they marched around it seven times.
Joshua6:16 The seventh time around
Joshua6:17 The city and all that is in it must be set apart for the LORD
Joshua6:18 But be careful when you are setting apart the riches for the LORD. If you take any of it
Joshua6:19 All the silver and gold
Joshua6:20 The rams’ horns sounded and when the army heard the signal
Joshua6:21 They annihilated with the sword everything that breathed in the city
Joshua6:22 Joshua told the two men who had spied on the land
Joshua6:23 So the young spies went and brought out Rahab
Joshua6:24 But they burned the city and all that was in it
Joshua6:25 Yet Joshua spared Rahab the prostitute
Joshua6:26 At that time Joshua made this solemn declaration: “The man who attempts to rebuild this city of Jericho will stand condemned before the LORD. He will lose his firstborn son when he lays its foundations and his youngest son when he erects its gates!”
Joshua6:27 The LORD was with Joshua and he became famous throughout the land.
Joshua7:1 But the Israelites disobeyed the command about the city’s riches. Achan son of Carmi
Joshua7:2 Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai (which is located near Beth Aven
Joshua7:3 They returned and reported to Joshua
Joshua7:4 So about three thousand men went up
Joshua7:5 The men of Ai killed about thirty-six of them and chased them from in front of the city gate all the way to the fissures and defeated them on the steep slope. The people’s courage melted away like water.
Joshua7:6 Joshua tore his clothes; he and the leaders of Israel lay face down on the ground before the ark of the LORD until evening and threw dirt on their heads.
Joshua7:7 Joshua prayed
Joshua7:8 If only we had been satisfied to live on the other side of the Jordan! O Lord
Joshua7:9 When the Canaanites and all who live in the land hear about this
Joshua7:10 The LORD responded to Joshua
Joshua7:11 Israel has sinned; they have violated my covenantal commandment! They have taken some of the riches; they have stolen them and deceitfully put them among their own possessions.
Joshua7:12 The Israelites are unable to stand before their enemies; they retreat because they have become subject to annihilation. I will no longer be with you
Joshua7:13 Get up! Ritually consecrate the people and tell them this: ‘Ritually consecrate yourselves for tomorrow
Joshua7:14 In the morning you must approach in tribal order. The tribe the LORD selects must approach by clans. The clan the LORD selects must approach by families. The family the LORD selects must approach man by man.
Joshua7:15 The one caught with the riches must be burned up along with all who belong to him
Joshua7:16 Bright and early the next morning Joshua made Israel approach in tribal order and the tribe of Judah was selected.
Joshua7:17 He then made the clans of Judah approach and the clan of the Zerahites was selected. He made the clan of the Zerahites approach and Zabdi was selected.
Joshua7:18 He then made Zabdi’s family approach man by man and Achan son of Carmi
Joshua7:19 So Joshua said to Achan
Joshua7:20 Achan told Joshua
Joshua7:21 I saw among the goods we seized a nice robe from Babylon
Joshua7:22 Joshua sent messengers who ran to the tent. The things were hidden right in his tent
Joshua7:23 They took it all from the middle of the tent
Joshua7:24 Then Joshua and all Israel took Achan
Joshua7:25 Joshua said
Joshua7:26 Then they erected over him a large pile of stones (it remains to this very day ) and the LORD’s anger subsided. So that place is called the Valley of Disaster to this very day.
Joshua8:1 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua8:2 Do to Ai and its king what you did to Jericho and its king
Joshua8:3 Joshua and the whole army marched against Ai. Joshua selected thirty thousand brave warriors and sent them out at night.
Joshua8:4 He told them
Joshua8:5 I and all the troops who are with me will approach the city. When they come out to fight us like before
Joshua8:6 They will attack us until we have lured them from the city
Joshua8:7 Then you rise up from your hiding place and seize the city. The LORD your God will hand it over to you.
Joshua8:8 When you capture the city
Joshua8:9 Joshua sent them away and they went to their hiding place west of Ai
Joshua8:10 Bright and early the next morning Joshua gathered the army
Joshua8:11 All the troops that were with him marched up and drew near the city. They camped north of Ai on the other side of the valley.
Joshua8:12 He took five thousand men and set an ambush west of the city between Bethel and Ai.
Joshua8:13 The army was in position — the main army north of the city and the rear guard west of the city. That night Joshua went into the middle of the valley.
Joshua8:14 When the king of Ai saw Israel
Joshua8:15 Joshua and all Israel pretended to be defeated by them and they retreated along the way to the desert.
Joshua8:16 All the reinforcements in Ai were ordered to chase them; they chased Joshua and were lured away from the city.
Joshua8:17 No men were left in Ai or Bethel; they all went out after Israel. They left the city wide open and chased Israel.
Joshua8:18 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua8:19 When he held out his hand
Joshua8:20 When the men of Ai turned around
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the men in ambush had captured the city and that the city was going up in smoke
Joshua8:22 At the same time the men who had taken the city came out to fight
Joshua8:23 But they captured the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 When Israel had finished killing all the men of Ai who had chased them toward the desert (they all fell by the sword)
Joshua8:25 Twelve thousand men and women died that day
Joshua8:26 Joshua kept holding out his curved sword until Israel had annihilated all who lived in Ai.
Joshua8:27 But Israel did plunder the cattle and the goods of the city
Joshua8:28 Joshua burned Ai and made it a permanently uninhabited mound (it remains that way to this very day).
Joshua8:29 He hung the king of Ai on a tree
Joshua8:30 Then Joshua built an altar for the LORD God of Israel on Mount Ebal
Joshua8:31 just as Moses the LORD’s servant had commanded the Israelites. As described in the law scroll of Moses
Joshua8:32 There
Joshua8:33 All the people
Joshua8:34 Then Joshua read aloud all the words of the law
Joshua8:35 Joshua read aloud every commandment Moses had given before the whole assembly of Israel
Joshua9:1 When the news reached all the kings on the west side of the Jordan — in the hill country
Joshua9:2 they formed an alliance to fight against Joshua and Israel.
Joshua9:3 When the residents of Gibeon heard what Joshua did to Jericho and Ai
Joshua9:4 they did something clever. They collected some provisions and put worn-out sacks on their donkeys
Joshua9:5 They had worn-out
Joshua9:6 They came to Joshua at the camp in Gilgal and said to him and the men of Israel
Joshua9:7 The men of Israel said to the Hivites
Joshua9:8 But they said to Joshua
Joshua9:9 They told him
Joshua9:10 and all he did to the two Amorite kings on the other side of the Jordan — King Sihon of Heshbon and King Og of Bashan in Ashtaroth.
Joshua9:11 Our leaders and all who live in our land told us
Joshua9:12 This bread of ours was warm when we packed it in our homes the day we started out to meet you
Joshua9:13 These wineskins we filled were brand new
Joshua9:14 The men examined some of their provisions
Joshua9:15 Joshua made a peace treaty with them and agreed to let them live. The leaders of the community sealed it with an oath.
Joshua9:16 Three days after they made the treaty with them
Joshua9:17 So the Israelites set out and on the third day arrived at their cities — Gibeon
Joshua9:18 The Israelites did not attack them because the leaders of the community had sworn an oath to them in the name of the LORD God of Israel. The whole community criticized the leaders
Joshua9:19 but all the leaders told the whole community
Joshua9:20 We must let them live so we can escape the curse attached to the oath we swore to them.”
Joshua9:21 The leaders then added
Joshua9:22 Joshua summoned the Gibeonites and said to them
Joshua9:23 Now you are condemned to perpetual servitude as woodcutters and water carriers for the house of my God.”
Joshua9:24 They said to Joshua
Joshua9:25 So now we are in your power. Do to us what you think is good and appropriate.
Joshua9:26 Joshua did as they said; he kept the Israelites from killing them
Joshua9:27 and that day made them woodcutters and water carriers for the community and for the altar of the LORD at the divinely chosen site. (They continue in that capacity to this very day.)
Joshua10:1 Adoni-Zedek
Joshua10:2 All Jerusalem was terrified because Gibeon was a large city
Joshua10:3 So King Adoni-Zedek of Jerusalem sent this message to King Hoham of Hebron
Joshua10:4 “Come to my aid so we can attack Gibeon
Joshua10:5 So the five Amorite kings (the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:6 The men of Gibeon sent this message to Joshua at the camp in Gilgal
Joshua10:7 So Joshua and his whole army
Joshua10:8 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua10:9 Joshua attacked them by surprise after marching all night from Gilgal.
Joshua10:10 The LORD routed them before Israel. Israel thoroughly defeated them at Gibeon. They chased them up the road to the pass of Beth Horon and struck them down all the way to Azekah and Makkedah.
Joshua10:11 As they fled from Israel on the slope leading down from Beth Horon
Joshua10:12 The day the LORD delivered the Amorites over to the Israelites
Joshua10:13 The sun stood still and the moon stood motionless while the nation took vengeance on its enemies. The event is recorded in the Scroll of the Upright One. The sun stood motionless in the middle of the sky and did not set for about a full day.
Joshua10:14 There has not been a day like it before or since. The LORD obeyed a man
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua and all Israel returned to the camp at Gilgal.
Joshua10:16 The five Amorite kings ran away and hid in the cave at Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 Joshua was told
Joshua10:18 Joshua said
Joshua10:19 But don’t you delay! Chase your enemies and catch them! Don’t allow them to retreat to their cities
Joshua10:20 Joshua and the Israelites almost totally wiped them out
Joshua10:21 Then the whole army safely returned to Joshua at the camp in Makkedah. No one dared threaten the Israelites.
Joshua10:22 Joshua said
Joshua10:23 They did as ordered; they brought the five kings out of the cave to him — the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they brought the kings out to Joshua
Joshua10:25 Then Joshua said to them
Joshua10:26 Then Joshua executed them and hung them on five trees. They were left hanging on the trees until evening.
Joshua10:27 At sunset Joshua ordered his men to take them down from the trees. They threw them into the cave where they had hidden and piled large stones over the mouth of the cave. (They remain to this very day.)
Joshua10:28 That day Joshua captured Makkedah and put the sword to it and its king. He annihilated everyone who lived in it; he left no survivors. He did to its king what he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:29 Joshua and all Israel marched from Makkedah to Libnah and fought against it.
Joshua10:30 The LORD handed it and its king over to Israel
Joshua10:31 Joshua and all Israel marched from Libnah to Lachish. He deployed his troops and fought against it.
Joshua10:32 The LORD handed Lachish over to Israel and they captured it on the second day. They put the sword to all who lived there
Joshua10:33 Then King Horam of Gezer came up to help Lachish
Joshua10:34 Joshua and all Israel marched from Lachish to Eglon. They deployed troops and fought against it.
Joshua10:35 That day they captured it and put the sword to all who lived there. That day they annihilated it just as they had done to Lachish.
Joshua10:36 Joshua and all Israel marched up from Eglon to Hebron and fought against it.
Joshua10:37 They captured it and put the sword to its king
Joshua10:38 Joshua and all Israel turned to Debir and fought against it.
Joshua10:39 They captured it
Joshua10:40 Joshua defeated the whole land
Joshua10:41 Joshua conquered the area between Kadesh Barnea and Gaza and the whole region of Goshen
Joshua10:42 Joshua captured in one campaign all these kings and their lands
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua and all Israel returned to the camp at Gilgal.
Joshua11:1 When King Jabin of Hazor heard the news
Joshua11:2 and the northern kings who ruled in the hill country
Joshua11:3 Canaanites came from the east and west; Amorites
Joshua11:4 These kings came out with their armies; they were as numerous as the sand on the seashore and had a large number of horses and chariots.
Joshua11:5 All these kings gathered and joined forces at the Waters of Merom to fight Israel.
Joshua11:6 The LORD told Joshua
Joshua11:7 Joshua and his whole army caught them by surprise at the Waters of Merom and attacked them.
Joshua11:8 The LORD handed them over to Israel and they struck them down and chased them all the way to Greater Sidon
Joshua11:9 Joshua did to them as the LORD had commanded him; he hamstrung their horses and burned their chariots.
Joshua11:10 At that time Joshua turned
Joshua11:11 They annihilated everyone who lived there with the sword — no one who breathed remained — and burned Hazor.
Joshua11:12 Joshua captured all these royal cities and all their kings and annihilated them with the sword
Joshua11:13 But Israel did not burn any of the cities located on mounds
Joshua11:14 The Israelites plundered all the goods of these cities and the cattle
Joshua11:15 Moses the LORD’s servant passed on the LORD’s commands to Joshua
Joshua11:16 Joshua conquered the whole land
Joshua11:17 from Mount Halak on up to Seir
Joshua11:18 Joshua campaigned against these kings for quite some time.
Joshua11:19 No city made peace with the Israelites (except the Hivites living in Gibeon); they had to conquer all of them
Joshua11:20 for the LORD determined to make them obstinate so they would attack Israel. He wanted Israel to annihilate them without mercy
Joshua11:21 At that time Joshua attacked and eliminated the Anakites from the hill country — from Hebron
Joshua11:22 No Anakites were left in Israelite territory
Joshua11:23 Joshua conquered the whole land
Joshua12:1 Now these are the kings of the land whom the Israelites defeated and drove from their land on the east side of the Jordan
Joshua12:2 King Sihon of the Amorites who lived in Heshbon and ruled from Aroer (on the edge of the Arnon Valley) — including the city in the middle of the valley and half of Gilead — all the way to the Jabbok Valley bordering Ammonite territory.
Joshua12:3 His kingdom included the eastern Arabah from the Sea of Kinnereth to the Sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea)
Joshua12:4 The territory of King Og of Bashan

Judges1:24 the spies spotted a man leaving the city. They said to him
Judges1:25 He showed them a secret entrance into the city
Judges1:26 He moved to Hittite country and built a city. He named it Luz
Judges1:27 The men of Manasseh did not conquer Beth Shan
Judges1:28 Whenever Israel was strong militarily
Judges1:29 The men of Ephraim did not conquer the Canaanites living in Gezer. The Canaanites lived among them in Gezer.
Judges1:30 The men of Zebulun did not conquer the people living in Kitron and Nahalol. The Canaanites lived among them and were forced to do hard labor.
Judges1:31 The men of Asher did not conquer the people living in Acco or Sidon
Judges1:32 The people of Asher live among the Canaanites residing in the land because they did not conquer them.
Judges1:33 The men of Naphtali did not conquer the people living in Beth Shemesh or Beth Anath. They live among the Canaanites residing in the land. The Canaanites living in Beth Shemesh and Beth Anath were forced to do hard labor for them.
Judges1:34 The Amorites forced the people of Dan to live in the hill country. They did not allow them to live in the coastal plain.
Judges1:35 The Amorites managed to remain in Har Heres
Judges1:36 The border of Amorite territory ran from the Scorpion Ascent to Sela and on up.
Judges2:1 The LORD’s angelic messenger went up from Gilgal to Bokim. He said
Judges2:2 but you must not make an agreement with the people who live in this land. You should tear down the altars where they worship.’ But you have disobeyed me. Why would you do such a thing?
Judges2:3 At that time I also warned you
Judges2:4 When the LORD’s messenger finished speaking these words to all the Israelites
Judges2:5 They named that place Bokim and offered sacrifices to the LORD there.
Judges2:6 When Joshua dismissed the people
Judges2:7 The people worshiped the LORD throughout Joshua’s lifetime and as long as the elderly men who outlived him remained alive. These men had witnessed all the great things the LORD had done for Israel.
Judges2:8 Joshua son of Nun
Judges2:9 The people buried him in his allotted land in Timnath Heres in the hill country of Ephraim
Judges2:10 That entire generation passed away; a new generation grew up that had not personally experienced the LORD’s presence or seen what he had done for Israel.
Judges2:11 The Israelites did evil before the LORD by worshiping the Baals.
Judges2:12 They abandoned the LORD God of their ancestors who brought them out of the land of Egypt. They followed other gods — the gods of the nations who lived around them. They worshiped them and made the LORD angry.
Judges2:13 They abandoned the LORD and worshiped Baal and the Ashtars.
Judges2:14 The LORD was furious with Israel and handed them over to robbers who plundered them. He turned them over to their enemies who lived around them. They could not withstand their enemies’ attacks.
Judges2:15 Whenever they went out to fight
Judges2:16 The LORD raised up leaders who delivered them from these robbers.
Judges2:17 But they did not obey their leaders. Instead they prostituted themselves to other gods and worshiped them. They quickly turned aside from the path their ancestors had walked. Their ancestors had obeyed the LORD’s commands
Judges2:18 When the LORD raised up leaders for them
Judges2:19 When a leader died
Judges2:20 The LORD was furious with Israel. He said
Judges2:21 So I will no longer remove before them any of the nations that Joshua left unconquered when he died.
Judges2:22 Joshua left those nations to test Israel. I wanted to see whether or not the people would carefully walk in the path marked out by the LORD
Judges2:23 This is why the LORD permitted these nations to remain and did not conquer them immediately; he did not hand them over to Joshua.
Judges3:1 These were the nations the LORD permitted to remain so he could use them to test Israel — he wanted to test all those who had not experienced battle against the Canaanites.
Judges3:2 He left those nations simply because he wanted to teach the subsequent generations of Israelites
Judges3:3 These were the nations: the five lords of the Philistines
Judges3:4 They were left to test Israel
Judges3:5 The Israelites lived among the Canaanites
Judges3:6 They took the Canaanites’ daughters as wives and gave their daughters to the Canaanites; they worshiped their gods as well.
Judges3:7 The Israelites did evil in the LORD’s sight. They forgot the LORD their God and worshiped the Baals and the Asherahs.
Judges3:8 The LORD was furious with Israel and turned them over to King Cushan-Rishathaim of Aram-Naharaim. They were Cushan-Rishathaim’s subjects for eight years.
Judges3:9 When the Israelites cried out for help to the LORD
Judges3:10 The LORD’s spirit empowered him and he led Israel. When he went to do battle
Judges3:11 The land had rest for forty years; then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
Judges3:12 The Israelites again did evil in the LORD’s sight. The LORD gave King Eglon of Moab control over Israel because they had done evil in the LORD’s sight.
Judges3:13 Eglon formed alliances with the Ammonites and Amalekites. He came and defeated Israel
Judges3:14 The Israelites were subject to King Eglon of Moab for eighteen years.
Judges3:15 When the Israelites cried out for help to the LORD
Judges3:16 Ehud made himself a sword — it had two edges and was eighteen inches long. He strapped it under his coat on his right thigh.

Judges4:13 he ordered all his chariotry — nine hundred chariots with iron-rimmed wheels — and all the troops he had with him to go from Harosheth-Haggoyim to the River Kishon.

Judges4:15 The LORD routed Sisera

Judges6:2 The Midianites overwhelmed Israel. Because of Midian the Israelites made shelters for themselves in the hills

Judges6:5 When they invaded with their cattle and tents

Judges9:4 They paid him seventy silver shekels out of the temple of Baal-Berith. Abimelech then used the silver to hire some lawless
Judges9:5 He went to his father’s home in Ophrah and murdered his half-brothers
Judges9:6 All the leaders of Shechem and Beth Millo assembled and then went and made Abimelech king by the oak near the pillar in Shechem.

Judges9:45 Abimelech fought against the city all that day. He captured the city and killed all the people in it. Then he leveled the city and spread salt over it.
Judges9:46 When all the leaders of the Tower of Shechem heard the news
Judges9:47 Abimelech heard that all the leaders of the Tower of Shechem were in one place.

Judges9:49 So each of his men also cut off a branch and followed Abimelech. They put the branches against the stronghold and set fire to it. All the people of the Tower of Shechem died — about a thousand men and women.
Judges9:50 Abimelech moved on to Thebez; he besieged and captured it.
Judges9:51 There was a fortified tower in the center of the city
Judges9:52 Abimelech came and attacked the tower. When he approached the entrance of the tower to set it on fire

Judges9:54 He quickly called to the young man who carried his weapons

Judges9:57 God also repaid the men of Shechem for their evil deeds. The curse spoken by Jotham son of Jerub-Baal fell on them.

1Samuel3:1 Now the boy Samuel continued serving the LORD under Eli’s supervision. Word from the LORD was rare in those days; revelatory visions were infrequent.

2Samuel22:1 David sang to the LORD the words of this song when the LORD rescued him from the power of all his enemies
2Samuel22:2 He said: “The LORD is my high ridge
2Samuel22:3 My God is my rocky summit where I take shelter
2Samuel22:4 I called to the LORD
2Samuel22:5 The waves of death engulfed me; the currents of chaos overwhelmed me.
2Samuel22:6 The ropes of Sheol tightened around me; the snares of death trapped me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called to the LORD; I called to my God. From his heavenly temple he heard my voice; he listened to my cry for help.
2Samuel22:8 The earth heaved and shook; the foundations of the sky trembled. They heaved because he was angry.
2Samuel22:9 Smoke ascended from his nose; fire devoured as it came from his mouth; he hurled down fiery coals.
2Samuel22:10 He made the sky sink as he descended; a thick cloud was under his feet.
2Samuel22:11 He mounted a winged angel and flew; he glided on the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 He shrouded himself in darkness
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness in front of him came coals of fire.
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from the sky; the sovereign One shouted loudly.
2Samuel22:15 He shot arrows and scattered them
2Samuel22:16 The depths of the sea were exposed; the inner regions of the world were uncovered by the LORD’s battle cry
2Samuel22:17 He reached down from above and grabbed me; he pulled me from the surging water.
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in my day of calamity
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out into a wide open place; he delivered me because he was pleased with me.
2Samuel22:21 The LORD repaid me for my godly deeds; he rewarded my blameless behavior.
2Samuel22:22 For I have obeyed the LORD’s commands; I have not rebelled against my God.
2Samuel22:23 For I am aware of all his regulations
2Samuel22:24 I was blameless before him; I kept myself from sinning.
2Samuel22:25 The LORD rewarded me for my godly deeds; he took notice of my blameless behavior.
2Samuel22:26 You prove to be loyal to one who is faithful; you prove to be trustworthy to one who is innocent.
2Samuel22:27 You prove to be reliable to one who is blameless
2Samuel22:28 You deliver oppressed people
2Samuel22:29 Indeed
2Samuel22:30 Indeed
2Samuel22:31 The one true God acts in a faithful manner; the LORD’s promise is reliable; he is a shield to all who take shelter in him.
2Samuel22:32 Indeed
2Samuel22:33 The one true God is my mighty refuge; he removes the obstacles in my way.
2Samuel22:34 He gives me the agility of a deer; he enables me to negotiate the rugged terrain.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend even the strongest bow.
2Samuel22:36 You give me your protective shield; your willingness to help enables me to prevail.
2Samuel22:37 You widen my path; my feet do not slip.
2Samuel22:38 I chase my enemies and destroy them; I do not turn back until I wipe them out.
2Samuel22:39 I wipe them out and beat them to death; they cannot get up; they fall at my feet.
2Samuel22:40 You give me strength for battle; you make my foes kneel before me.
2Samuel22:41 You make my enemies retreat; I destroy those who hate me.
2Samuel22:42 They cry out
2Samuel22:43 I grind them as fine as the dust of the ground; I crush them and stomp on them like clay in the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You rescue me from a hostile army; you preserve me as a leader of nations; people over whom I had no authority are now my subjects.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners are powerless before me; when they hear of my exploits
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lose their courage; they shake with fear as they leave their strongholds.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD is alive! My protector is praiseworthy! The God who delivers me is exalted as king!
2Samuel22:48 The one true God completely vindicates me; he makes nations submit to me.
2Samuel22:49 He delivers me from my enemies; you snatch me away from those who attack me; you rescue me from violent men.
2Samuel22:50 So I will give you thanks
2Samuel22:51 He gives his chosen king magnificent victories; he is faithful to his chosen ruler

1Kings1:1 King David was very old; even when they covered him with blankets
1Kings1:2 His servants advised him
1Kings1:3 So they looked through all Israel for a beautiful young woman and found Abishag
1Kings1:4 The young woman was very beautiful; she became the king’s nurse and served him
1Kings1:5 Now Adonijah
1Kings1:6 (Now his father had never corrected him by saying
1Kings1:7 He collaborated with Joab son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest
1Kings1:8 But Zadok the priest
1Kings1:9 Adonijah sacrificed sheep
1Kings1:10 But he did not invite Nathan the prophet
1Kings1:11 Nathan said to Bathsheba
1Kings1:12 Now let me give you some advice as to how you can save your life and your son Solomon’s life.
1Kings1:13 Visit King David and say to him
1Kings1:14 While you are still there speaking to the king
1Kings1:15 So Bathsheba visited the king in his private quarters. (The king was very old
1Kings1:16 Bathsheba bowed down on the floor before the king. The king said
1Kings1:17 She replied to him
1Kings1:18 But now
1Kings1:19 He has sacrificed many cattle
1Kings1:20 Now
1Kings1:21 If a decision is not made
1Kings1:22 Just then
1Kings1:23 The king was told
1Kings1:24 Nathan said
1Kings1:25 For today he has gone down and sacrificed many cattle
1Kings1:26 But he did not invite me — your servant — or Zadok the priest
1Kings1:27 Has my master the king authorized this without informing your servants who should succeed my master the king on his throne?”
1Kings1:28 King David responded
1Kings1:29 The king swore an oath: “As certainly as the LORD lives (he who has rescued me from every danger)
1Kings1:30 I will keep today the oath I swore to you by the LORD God of Israel: ‘Surely Solomon your son will be king after me; he will sit in my place on my throne.’”
1Kings1:31 Bathsheba bowed down to the king with her face to the floor and said
1Kings1:32 King David said
1Kings1:33 and he told them
1Kings1:34 There Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet will anoint him king over Israel; then blow the trumpet and declare
1Kings1:35 Then follow him up as he comes and sits on my throne. He will be king in my place; I have decreed that he will be ruler over Israel and Judah.”
1Kings1:36 Benaiah son of Jehoiada responded to the king: “So be it! May the LORD God of my master the king confirm it!
1Kings1:37 As the LORD is with my master the king
1Kings1:38 So Zadok the priest
1Kings1:39 Zadok the priest took a horn filled with olive oil from the tent and poured it on Solomon; the trumpet was blown and all the people declared
1Kings1:40 All the people followed him up
1Kings1:41 Now Adonijah and all his guests heard the commotion just as they had finished eating. When Joab heard the sound of the trumpet
1Kings1:42 As he was still speaking
1Kings1:43 Jonathan replied to Adonijah: “No! Our master King David has made Solomon king.
1Kings1:44 The king sent with him Zadok the priest
1Kings1:45 Then Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anointed him king in Gihon. They went up from there rejoicing
1Kings1:46 Furthermore
1Kings1:47 The king’s servants have even come to congratulate our master King David
1Kings1:48 and said this: ‘The LORD God of Israel is worthy of praise because today he has placed a successor on my throne and allowed me to see it.’”
1Kings1:49 All of Adonijah’s guests panicked; they jumped up and rushed off their separate ways.
1Kings1:50 Adonijah feared Solomon
1Kings1:51 Solomon was told
1Kings1:52 Solomon said
1Kings1:53 King Solomon sent men to bring him down from the altar. He came and bowed down to King Solomon
1Kings2:1 When David was close to death
1Kings2:2 “I am about to die. Be strong and become a man!
1Kings2:3 Do the job the LORD your God has assigned you by following his instructions and obeying his rules
1Kings2:4 and the LORD will fulfill his promise to me
1Kings2:5 “You know what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me — how he murdered two commanders of the Israelite armies
1Kings2:6 Do to him what you think is appropriate
1Kings2:7 “Treat fairly the sons of Barzillai of Gilead and provide for their needs
1Kings2:8 “Note well
1Kings2:9 But now don’t treat him as if he were innocent. You are a wise man and you know how to handle him; make sure he has a bloody death.”
1Kings2:10 Then David passed away and was buried in the city of David.
1Kings2:11 David reigned over Israel forty years; he reigned in Hebron seven years
1Kings2:12 Solomon sat on his father David’s throne
1Kings2:13 Haggith’s son Adonijah visited Bathsheba
1Kings2:14 He added
1Kings2:15 He said
1Kings2:16 Now I’d like to ask you for just one thing. Please don’t refuse me.” She said
1Kings2:17 He said
1Kings2:18 Bathsheba replied
1Kings2:19 So Bathsheba visited King Solomon to speak to him on Adonijah’s behalf. The king got up to greet her
1Kings2:20 She said
1Kings2:21 She said
1Kings2:22 King Solomon answered his mother
1Kings2:23 King Solomon then swore an oath by the LORD
1Kings2:24 Now
1Kings2:25 King Solomon then sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada
1Kings2:26 The king then told Abiathar the priest
1Kings2:27 Solomon dismissed Abiathar from his position as priest of the LORD
1Kings2:28 When the news reached Joab (for Joab had supported Adonijah
1Kings2:29 When King Solomon heard that Joab had run to the tent of the LORD and was right there beside the altar
1Kings2:30 When Benaiah arrived at the tent of the LORD
1Kings2:31 The king told him
1Kings2:32 May the LORD punish him for the blood he shed; behind my father David’s back he struck down and murdered with the sword two men who were more innocent and morally upright than he — Abner son of Ner
1Kings2:33 May Joab and his descendants be perpetually guilty of their shed blood
1Kings2:34 So Benaiah son of Jehoiada went up and executed Joab; he was buried at his home in the wilderness.
1Kings2:35 The king appointed Benaiah son of Jehoiada to take his place at the head of the army
1Kings2:36 Next the king summoned Shimei and told him
1Kings2:37 If you ever do leave and cross the Kidron Valley
1Kings2:38 Shimei said to the king
1Kings2:39 Three years later two of Shimei’s servants ran away to King Achish son of Maacah of Gath. Shimei was told
1Kings2:40 So Shimei got up
1Kings2:41 When Solomon was told that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and had then returned
1Kings2:42 the king summoned Shimei and said to him
1Kings2:43 Why then have you broken the oath you made before the LORD and disobeyed the order I gave you?”
1Kings2:44 Then the king said to Shimei
1Kings2:45 But King Solomon will be empowered and David’s dynasty will endure permanently before the LORD.”
1Kings2:46 The king then gave the order to Benaiah son of Jehoiada who went and executed Shimei. So Solomon took firm control of the kingdom.
1Kings3:1 Solomon made an alliance by marriage with Pharaoh
1Kings3:2 Now the people were offering sacrifices at the high places
1Kings3:3 Solomon demonstrated his loyalty to the LORD by following the practices of his father David
1Kings3:4 The king went to Gibeon to offer sacrifices
1Kings3:5 One night in Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon in a dream. God said
1Kings3:6 Solomon replied
1Kings3:7 Now
1Kings3:8 Your servant stands among your chosen people; they are a great nation that is too numerous to count or number.
1Kings3:9 So give your servant a discerning mind so he can make judicial decisions for your people and distinguish right from wrong. Otherwise no one is able to make judicial decisions for this great nation of yours.”
1Kings3:10 The Lord was pleased that Solomon made this request.
1Kings3:11 God said to him
1Kings3:12 I grant your request
1Kings3:13 Furthermore
1Kings3:14 If you follow my instructions by obeying my rules and regulations
1Kings3:15 Solomon then woke up and realized it was a dream. He went to Jerusalem
1Kings3:16 Then two prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.
1Kings3:17 One of the women said
1Kings3:18 Then three days after I had my baby
1Kings3:19 This woman’s child suffocated during the night when she rolled on top of him.
1Kings3:20 She got up in the middle of the night and took my son from my side
1Kings3:21 I got up in the morning to nurse my son
1Kings3:22 The other woman said
1Kings3:23 The king said
1Kings3:24 The king ordered
1Kings3:25 The king then said
1Kings3:26 The real mother spoke up to the king
1Kings3:27 The king responded
1Kings3:28 When all Israel heard about the judicial decision which the king had rendered
1Kings4:1 King Solomon ruled over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 These were his officials: Azariah son of Zadok was the priest.
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was commander of the army. Zadok and Abiathar were priests.
1Kings4:5 Azariah son of Nathan was supervisor of the district governors. Zabud son of Nathan was a priest and adviser to the king.
1Kings4:6 Ahishar was supervisor of the palace. Adoniram son of Abda was supervisor of the work crews.
1Kings4:7 Solomon had twelve district governors appointed throughout Israel who acquired supplies for the king and his palace. Each was responsible for one month in the year.
1Kings4:8 These were their names: Ben-Hur was in charge of the hill country of Ephraim.
1Kings4:9 Ben-Deker was in charge of Makaz
1Kings4:10 Ben-Hesed was in charge of Arubboth; he controlled Socoh and all the territory of Hepher.
1Kings4:11 Ben-Abinadab was in charge of Naphath Dor. (He was married to Solomon’s daughter Taphath.)
1Kings4:12 Baana son of Ahilud was in charge of Taanach and Megiddo
1Kings4:13 Ben-Geber was in charge of Ramoth Gilead; he controlled the tent villages of Jair son of Manasseh in Gilead
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab son of Iddo was in charge of Mahanaim.
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz was in charge of Naphtali. (He married Solomon’s daughter Basemath.)
1Kings4:16 Baana son of Hushai was in charge of Asher and Aloth.
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat son of Paruah was in charge of Issachar.
1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela was in charge of Benjamin.
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri was in charge of the land of Gilead (the territory which had once belonged to King Sihon of the Amorites and to King Og of Bashan). He was sole governor of the area.
1Kings4:20 The people of Judah and Israel were as innumerable as the sand on the seashore; they had plenty to eat and drink and were happy.
1Kings4:21 (5:1) Solomon ruled all the kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 Each day Solomon’s royal court consumed thirty cors of finely milled flour
1Kings4:23 ten calves fattened in the stall
1Kings4:24 His royal court was so large because he ruled over all the kingdoms west of the Euphrates River from Tiphsah to Gaza; he was at peace with all his neighbors.
1Kings4:25 All the people of Judah and Israel had security; everyone from Dan to Beer Sheba enjoyed the produce of their vines and fig trees throughout Solomon’s lifetime.
1Kings4:26 Solomon had 4
1Kings4:27 The district governors acquired supplies for King Solomon and all who ate in his royal palace. Each was responsible for one month in the year; they made sure nothing was lacking.
1Kings4:28 Each one also brought to the assigned location his quota of barley and straw for the various horses.
1Kings4:29 God gave Solomon wisdom and very great discernment; the breadth of his understanding was as infinite as the sand on the seashore.
1Kings4:30 Solomon was wiser than all the men of the east and all the sages of Egypt.
1Kings4:31 He was wiser than any man
1Kings4:32 He composed 3
1Kings4:33 He produced manuals on botany
1Kings4:34 People from all nations came to hear Solomon’s display of wisdom; they came from all the kings of the earth who heard about his wisdom.
1Kings5:1 (5:15) King Hiram of Tyre sent messengers to Solomon when he heard that he had been anointed king in his father’s place. (Hiram had always been an ally of David.)
1Kings5:2 Solomon then sent this message to Hiram:
1Kings5:3 “You know that my father David was unable to build a temple to honor the LORD his God
1Kings5:4 But now the LORD my God has made me secure on all fronts; there is no adversary or dangerous threat.
1Kings5:5 So I have decided to build a temple to honor the LORD my God
1Kings5:6 So now order some cedars of Lebanon to be cut for me. My servants will work with your servants. I will pay your servants whatever you say is appropriate
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard Solomon’s message
1Kings5:8 Hiram then sent this message to Solomon: “I received the message you sent to me. I will give you all the cedars and evergreens you need.
1Kings5:9 My servants will bring the timber down from Lebanon to the sea. I will send it by sea in raft-like bundles to the place you designate. There I will separate the logs and you can carry them away. In exchange you will supply the food I need for my royal court.”
1Kings5:10 So Hiram supplied the cedars and evergreens Solomon needed
1Kings5:11 and Solomon supplied Hiram annually with 20
1Kings5:12 So the LORD gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 King Solomon conscripted work crews from throughout Israel
1Kings5:14 He sent them to Lebanon in shifts of 10
1Kings5:15 Solomon also had 70
1Kings5:16 besides 3
1Kings5:17 By royal order they supplied large valuable stones in order to build the temple’s foundation with chiseled stone.
1Kings5:18 Solomon’s and Hiram’s construction workers
1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites left Egypt
1Kings6:2 The temple King Solomon built for the LORD was 90 feet long
1Kings6:3 The porch in front of the main hall of the temple was 30 feet long
1Kings6:4 He made framed windows for the temple.
1Kings6:5 He built an extension all around the walls of the temple’s main hall and holy place and constructed side rooms in it.
1Kings6:6 The bottom floor of the extension was seven and a half feet wide
1Kings6:7 As the temple was being built
1Kings6:8 The entrance to the bottom level of side rooms was on the south side of the temple; stairs went up to the middle floor and then on up to the third floor.
1Kings6:9 He finished building the temple and covered it with rafters and boards made of cedar.
1Kings6:10 He built an extension all around the temple; it was seven and a half feet high and it was attached to the temple by cedar beams.
1Kings6:11 The LORD said to Solomon:
1Kings6:12 “As for this temple you are building
1Kings6:13 I will live among the Israelites and will not abandon my people Israel.”
1Kings6:14 So Solomon finished building the temple.
1Kings6:15 He constructed the walls inside the temple with cedar planks; he paneled the inside with wood from the floor of the temple to the rafters of the ceiling. He covered the temple floor with boards made from the wood of evergreens.
1Kings6:16 He built a wall 30 feet in from the rear of the temple as a partition for an inner sanctuary that would be the most holy place. He paneled the wall with cedar planks from the floor to the rafters.
1Kings6:17 The main hall in front of the inner sanctuary was 60 feet long.
1Kings6:18 The inside of the temple was all cedar and was adorned with carvings of round ornaments and of flowers in bloom. Everything was cedar; no stones were visible.
1Kings6:19 He prepared the inner sanctuary inside the temple so that the ark of the covenant of the LORD could be placed there.
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was 30 feet long
1Kings6:21 Solomon plated the inside of the temple with gold. He hung golden chains in front of the inner sanctuary and plated the inner sanctuary with gold.
1Kings6:22 He plated the entire inside of the temple with gold
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubs of olive wood; each stood 15 feet high.
1Kings6:24 Each of the first cherub’s wings was seven and a half feet long; its entire wingspan was 15 feet.
1Kings6:25 The second cherub also had a wingspan of 15 feet; it was identical to the first in measurements and shape.
1Kings6:26 Each cherub stood 15 feet high.
1Kings6:27 He put the cherubs in the inner sanctuary of the temple. Their wings were spread out. One of the first cherub’s wings touched one wall and one of the other cherub’s wings touched the opposite wall. The first cherub’s other wing touched the second cherub’s other wing in the middle of the room.
1Kings6:28 He plated the cherubs with gold.
1Kings6:29 On all the walls around the temple
1Kings6:30 He plated the floor of the temple with gold
1Kings6:31 He made doors of olive wood at the entrance to the inner sanctuary; the pillar on each doorpost was five-sided.
1Kings6:32 On the two doors made of olive wood he carved cherubs
1Kings6:33 In the same way he made doorposts of olive wood for the entrance to the main hall
1Kings6:34 He also made two doors out of wood from evergreens; each door had two folding leaves.
1Kings6:35 He carved cherubs
1Kings6:36 He built the inner courtyard with three rows of chiseled stones and a row of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the month Ziv of the fourth year of Solomon’s reign the foundation was laid for the LORD’s temple.
1Kings6:38 In the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 Solomon took thirteen years to build his palace.
1Kings7:2 He named it “The Palace of the Lebanon Forest”; it was 150 feet long
1Kings7:3 The roof above the beams supported by the pillars was also made of cedar; there were forty-five beams
1Kings7:4 There were three rows of windows arranged in sets of three.
1Kings7:5 All of the entrances were rectangular in shape and they were arranged in sets of three.
1Kings7:6 He made a colonnade 75 feet long and 45 feet wide. There was a porch in front of this and pillars and a roof in front of the porch.
1Kings7:7 He also made a throne room
1Kings7:8 The palace where he lived was constructed in a similar way. He also constructed a palace like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter
1Kings7:9 All of these were built with the best stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was made of large valuable stones
1Kings7:11 Above the foundation the best stones
1Kings7:12 Around the great courtyard were three rows of chiseled stones and one row of cedar beams
1Kings7:13 King Solomon sent for Hiram of Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He fashioned two bronze pillars; each pillar was 27 feet high and 18 feet in circumference.
1Kings7:16 He made two bronze tops for the pillars; each was seven-and-a-half feet high.
1Kings7:17 The latticework on the tops of the pillars was adorned with ornamental wreaths and chains; the top of each pillar had seven groupings of ornaments.
1Kings7:18 When he made the pillars
1Kings7:19 The tops of the two pillars in the porch were shaped like lilies and were six feet high.
1Kings7:20 On the top of each pillar
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars on the porch in front of the main hall. He erected one pillar on the right side and called it Jakin; he erected the other pillar on the left side and called it Boaz.
1Kings7:22 The tops of the pillars were shaped like lilies. So the construction of the pillars was completed.
1Kings7:23 He also made the large bronze basin called “The Sea.” It measured 15 feet from rim to rim
1Kings7:24 Under the rim all the way around it were round ornaments arranged in settings 15 feet long. The ornaments were in two rows and had been cast with “The Sea.”
1Kings7:25 “The Sea” stood on top of twelve bulls. Three faced northward
1Kings7:26 It was four fingers thick and its rim was like that of a cup shaped like a lily blossom. It could hold about 12
1Kings7:27 He also made ten bronze movable stands. Each stand was six feet long
1Kings7:28 The stands were constructed with frames between the joints.
1Kings7:29 On these frames and joints were ornamental lions
1Kings7:30 Each stand had four bronze wheels with bronze axles and four supports. Under the basin the supports were fashioned on each side with wreaths.
1Kings7:31 Inside the stand was a round opening that was a foot-and-a-half deep; it had a support that was two and one-quarter feet long. On the edge of the opening were carvings in square frames.
1Kings7:32 The four wheels were under the frames and the crossbars of the axles were connected to the stand. Each wheel was two and one-quarter feet high.
1Kings7:33 The wheels were constructed like chariot wheels; their crossbars
1Kings7:34 Each stand had four supports
1Kings7:35 On top of each stand was a round opening three-quarters of a foot deep; there were also supports and frames on top of the stands.
1Kings7:36 He engraved ornamental cherubs
1Kings7:37 He made the ten stands in this way. All of them were cast in one mold and were identical in measurements and shape.
1Kings7:38 He also made ten bronze basins
1Kings7:39 He put five basins on the south side of the temple and five on the north side. He put “The Sea” on the south side
1Kings7:40 Hiram also made basins
1Kings7:41 He made the two pillars
1Kings7:42 the four hundred pomegranate-shaped ornaments for the latticework of the two pillars (each latticework had two rows of these ornaments at the bowl-shaped top of the pillar)
1Kings7:43 the ten movable stands with their ten basins
1Kings7:44 the big bronze basin called “The Sea” with its twelve bulls underneath
1Kings7:45 and the pots
1Kings7:46 The king had them cast in earth foundries in the region of the Jordan between Succoth and Zarethan.
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all these items unweighed; there were so many of them they did not weigh the bronze.
1Kings7:48 Solomon also made all these items for the LORD’s temple: the gold altar
1Kings7:49 the pure gold lampstands at the entrance to the inner sanctuary (five on the right and five on the left)
1Kings7:50 the pure gold bowls
1Kings7:51 When King Solomon finished constructing the LORD’s temple
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon convened in Jerusalem Israel’s elders
1Kings8:2 All the men of Israel assembled before King Solomon during the festival in the month Ethanim (the seventh month).
1Kings8:3 When all Israel’s elders had arrived
1Kings8:4 The priests and Levites carried the ark of the LORD
1Kings8:5 Now King Solomon and all the Israelites who had assembled with him went on ahead of the ark and sacrificed more sheep and cattle than could be counted or numbered.
1Kings8:6 The priests brought the ark of the LORD’s covenant to its assigned place in the inner sanctuary of the temple
1Kings8:7 The cherubs’ wings extended over the place where the ark sat; the cherubs overshadowed the ark and its poles.
1Kings8:8 The poles were so long their ends were visible from the holy place in front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two stone tablets Moses had placed there in Horeb. It was there that the LORD made an agreement with the Israelites after he brought them out of the land of Egypt.
1Kings8:10 Once the priests left the holy place
1Kings8:11 The priests could not carry out their duties because of the cloud; the LORD’s glory filled his temple.
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said
1Kings8:13 O LORD
1Kings8:14 Then the king turned around and pronounced a blessing over the whole Israelite assembly as they stood there.
1Kings8:15 He said
1Kings8:16 He told David
1Kings8:17 Now my father David had a strong desire to build a temple to honor the LORD God of Israel.
1Kings8:18 The LORD told my father David
1Kings8:19 But you will not build the temple; your very own son will build the temple for my honor.’
1Kings8:20 The LORD has kept the promise he made. I have taken my father David’s place and have occupied the throne of Israel
1Kings8:21 and set up in it a place for the ark containing the covenant the LORD made with our ancestors when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.”
1Kings8:22 Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the entire assembly of Israel and spread out his hands toward the sky.
1Kings8:23 He prayed: “O LORD
1Kings8:24 You have kept your word to your servant
1Kings8:25 Now
1Kings8:26 Now
1Kings8:27 “God does not really live on the earth! Look
1Kings8:28 But respond favorably to your servant’s prayer and his request for help
1Kings8:29 Night and day may you watch over this temple
1Kings8:30 Respond to the request of your servant and your people Israel for this place. Hear from inside your heavenly dwelling place and respond favorably.
1Kings8:31 “When someone is accused of sinning against his neighbor and the latter pronounces a curse on the alleged offender before your altar in this temple
1Kings8:32 Listen from heaven and make a just decision about your servants’ claims. Condemn the guilty party
1Kings8:33 “The time will come when your people Israel are defeated by an enemy because they sinned against you. If they come back to you
1Kings8:34 then listen from heaven
1Kings8:35 “The time will come when the skies are shut up tightly and no rain falls because your people sinned against you. When they direct their prayers toward this place
1Kings8:36 then listen from heaven and forgive the sin of your servants
1Kings8:37 “The time will come when the land suffers from a famine
1Kings8:38 When all your people Israel pray and ask for help
1Kings8:39 then listen from your heavenly dwelling place
1Kings8:40 Then they will obey you throughout their lifetimes as they live on the land you gave to our ancestors.
1Kings8:41 “Foreigners
1Kings8:42 When they hear about your great reputation and your ability to accomplish mighty deeds
1Kings8:43 Then listen from your heavenly dwelling place and answer all the prayers of the foreigners. Then all the nations of the earth will acknowledge your reputation
1Kings8:44 “When you direct your people to march out and fight their enemies
1Kings8:45 then listen from heaven to their prayers for help and vindicate them.
1Kings8:46 “The time will come when your people will sin against you (for there is no one who is sinless!) and you will be angry with them and deliver them over to their enemies
1Kings8:47 When your people come to their senses in the land where they are held prisoner
1Kings8:48 When they return to you with all their heart and being in the land where they are held prisoner
1Kings8:49 then listen from your heavenly dwelling place to their prayers for help and vindicate them.
1Kings8:50 Forgive all the rebellious acts of your sinful people and cause their captors to have mercy on them.
1Kings8:51 After all
1Kings8:52 “May you be attentive to your servant’s and your people Israel’s requests for help and may you respond to all their prayers to you.
1Kings8:53 After all
1Kings8:54 When Solomon finished presenting all these prayers and requests to the LORD
1Kings8:55 When he stood up
1Kings8:56 “The LORD is worthy of praise because he has made Israel his people secure just as he promised! Not one of all the faithful promises he made through his servant Moses is left unfulfilled!
1Kings8:57 May the LORD our God be with us
1Kings8:58 May he make us submissive
1Kings8:59 May the LORD our God be constantly aware of these requests of mine I have presented to him
1Kings8:60 Then all the nations of the earth will recognize that the LORD is the only genuine God.
1Kings8:61 May you demonstrate wholehearted devotion to the LORD our God by following his rules and obeying his commandments
1Kings8:62 The king and all Israel with him were presenting sacrifices to the LORD.
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered as peace offerings to the LORD 22
1Kings8:64 That day the king consecrated the middle of the courtyard that is in front of the LORD’s temple. He offered there burnt sacrifices
1Kings8:65 At that time Solomon and all Israel with him celebrated a festival before the LORD our God for two entire weeks. This great assembly included people from all over the land
1Kings8:66 On the fifteenth day after the festival started
1Kings9:1 After Solomon finished building the LORD’s temple
1Kings9:2 the LORD appeared to Solomon a second time
1Kings9:3 The LORD said to him
1Kings9:4 You must serve me with integrity and sincerity
1Kings9:5 Then I will allow your dynasty to rule over Israel permanently
1Kings9:6 “But if you or your sons ever turn away from me
1Kings9:7 then I will remove Israel from the land I have given them
1Kings9:8 This temple will become a heap of ruins; everyone who passes by it will be shocked and will hiss out their scorn
1Kings9:9 Others will then answer
1Kings9:10 After twenty years

1Kings10:1 When the queen of Sheba heard about Solomon
1Kings10:2 She arrived in Jerusalem with a great display of pomp
1Kings10:3 Solomon answered all her questions; there was no question too complex for the king.
1Kings10:4 When the queen of Sheba saw for herself Solomon’s extensive wisdom
1Kings10:5 the food in his banquet hall
1Kings10:6 She said to the king
1Kings10:7 I did not believe these things until I came and saw them with my own eyes. Indeed
1Kings10:8 Your attendants

1Kings10:11 (Hiram’s fleet
1Kings10:12 With the timber the king made supports for the LORD’s temple and for the royal palace and stringed instruments for the musicians. No one has seen so much of this fine timber to this very day. )
1Kings10:13 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba everything she requested
1Kings10:14 Solomon received 666 talents of gold per year
1Kings10:15 besides what he collected from the merchants
1Kings10:16 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold; 600 measures of gold were used for each shield.
1Kings10:17 He also made three hundred small shields of hammered gold; three minas of gold were used for each of these shields. The king placed them in the Palace of the Lebanon Forest.
1Kings10:18 The king made a large throne decorated with ivory and overlaid it with pure gold.
1Kings10:19 There were six steps leading up to the throne
1Kings10:20 There were twelve statues of lions on the six steps
1Kings10:21 All of King Solomon’s cups were made of gold
1Kings10:22 Along with Hiram’s fleet
1Kings10:23 King Solomon was wealthier and wiser than any of the kings of the earth.
1Kings10:24 Everyone in the world wanted to visit Solomon to see him display his God-given wisdom.
1Kings10:25 Year after year visitors brought their gifts
1Kings10:26 Solomon accumulated chariots and horses. He had 1
1Kings10:27 The king made silver as plentiful in Jerusalem as stones; cedar was as plentiful as sycamore fig trees are in the lowlands.
1Kings10:28 Solomon acquired his horses from Egypt and from Que; the king’s traders purchased them from Que.
1Kings10:29 They paid 600 silver pieces for each chariot from Egypt and 150 silver pieces for each horse. They also sold chariots and horses to all the kings of the Hittites and to the kings of Syria.
1Kings11:1 King Solomon fell in love with many foreign women (besides Pharaoh’s daughter)
1Kings11:2 They came from nations about which the LORD had warned the Israelites
1Kings11:3 He had 700 royal wives and 300 concubines; his wives had a powerful influence over him.
1Kings11:4 When Solomon became old
1Kings11:5 Solomon worshiped the Sidonian goddess Astarte and the detestable Ammonite god Milcom.
1Kings11:6 Solomon did evil in the LORD’s sight; he did not remain loyal to the LORD
1Kings11:7 Furthermore
1Kings11:8 He built high places for all his foreign wives so they could burn incense and make sacrifices to their gods.
1Kings11:9 The LORD was angry with Solomon because he had shifted his allegiance away from the LORD

1Kings11:11 So the LORD said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 However

1Kings14:19 The rest of the events of Jeroboam’s reign
1Kings14:20 Jeroboam ruled for twenty-two years; then he passed away. His son Nadab replaced him as king.
1Kings14:21 Now Rehoboam son of Solomon ruled in Judah. He was forty-one years old when he became king and he ruled for seventeen years in Jerusalem
1Kings14:22 Judah did evil in the sight of the LORD. They made him more jealous by their sins than their ancestors had done.
1Kings14:23 They even built for themselves high places
1Kings14:24 There were also male cultic prostitutes in the land. They committed the same horrible sins as the nations that the LORD had driven out from before the Israelites.

1Kings14:26 He took away the treasures of the LORD’s temple and of the royal palace; he took everything

1Kings14:28 Whenever the king visited the LORD’s temple

1Kings14:31 Rehoboam passed away and was buried with his ancestors in the city of David. His mother was an Ammonite named Naamah. His son Abijah replaced him as king.
1Kings15:1 In the eighteenth year of the reign of Jeroboam son of Nebat
1Kings15:2 He ruled for three years in Jerusalem. His mother was Maacah
1Kings15:3 He followed all the sinful practices of his father before him. He was not wholeheartedly devoted to the LORD his God
1Kings15:4 Nevertheless for David’s sake the LORD his God maintained his dynasty in Jerusalem by giving him a son to succeed him and by protecting Jerusalem.
1Kings15:5 He did this because David had done what he approved and had not disregarded any of his commandments his entire lifetime
1Kings15:6 Rehoboam and Jeroboam were continually at war with each other throughout Abijah’s lifetime.
1Kings15:7 The rest of the events of Abijah’s reign
1Kings15:8 Abijah passed away and was buried in the city of David. His son Asa replaced him as king.
1Kings15:9 In the twentieth year of Jeroboam’s reign over Israel
1Kings15:10 He ruled for forty-one years in Jerusalem. His grandmother was Maacah daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:11 Asa did what the LORD approved like his ancestor David had done.
1Kings15:12 He removed the male cultic prostitutes from the land and got rid of all the disgusting idols his ancestors had made.
1Kings15:13 He also removed Maacah his grandmother from her position as queen because she had made a loathsome Asherah pole. Asa cut down her Asherah pole and burned it in the Kidron Valley.
1Kings15:14 The high places were not eliminated
1Kings15:15 He brought the holy items that he and his father had made into the LORD’s temple
1Kings15:16 Now Asa and King Baasha of Israel were continually at war with each other.
1Kings15:17 King Baasha of Israel attacked Judah and established Ramah as a military outpost to prevent anyone from leaving or entering the land of King Asa of Judah.
1Kings15:18 Asa took all the silver and gold that was left in the treasuries of the LORD’s temple and of the royal palace and handed it to his servants. He then told them to deliver it to Ben Hadad son of Tabrimmon
1Kings15:19 “I want to make a treaty with you
1Kings15:20 Ben Hadad accepted King Asa’s offer and ordered his army commanders to attack the cities of Israel. They conquered Ijon
1Kings15:21 When Baasha heard the news
1Kings15:22 King Asa ordered all the men of Judah (no exemptions were granted) to carry away the stones and wood that Baasha had used to build Ramah. King Asa used the materials to build up Geba (in Benjamin) and Mizpah.
1Kings15:23 The rest of the events of Asa’s reign
1Kings15:24 Asa passed away and was buried with his ancestors in the city of his ancestor David. His son Jehoshaphat replaced him as king.
1Kings15:25 In the second year of Asa’s reign over Judah
1Kings15:26 He did evil in the sight of the LORD. He followed in his father’s footsteps and encouraged Israel to sin.
1Kings15:27 Baasha son of Ahijah
1Kings15:28 Baasha killed him in the third year of Asa’s reign over Judah and replaced him as king.
1Kings15:29 When he became king
1Kings15:30 This happened because of the sins which Jeroboam committed and which he made Israel commit. These sins angered the LORD God of Israel.

1Kings15:32 Asa and King Nadab of Israel were continually at war with each other.
1Kings15:33 In the third year of Asa’s reign over Judah
1Kings15:34 He did evil in the sight of the LORD; he followed in Jeroboam’s footsteps and encouraged Israel to sin.

1Kings19:21 Elisha went back and took his pair of oxen and slaughtered them. He cooked the meat over a fire that he made by burning the harness and yoke. He gave the people meat and they ate. Then he got up and followed Elijah and became his assistant.

2Kings17:10 They set up sacred pillars and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 They burned incense on all the high places just like the nations whom the LORD had driven away from before them. Their evil practices made the LORD angry.
2Kings17:12 They worshiped the disgusting idols in blatant disregard of the LORD’s command.
2Kings17:13 The LORD solemnly warned Israel and Judah through all his prophets and all the seers
