2Kings17:17 They passed their sons and daughters through the fire
2Kings17:18 So the LORD was furious with Israel and rejected them; only the tribe of Judah was left.
2Kings17:19 Judah also failed to keep the commandments of the LORD their God; they followed Israel’s example.
2Kings17:20 So the LORD rejected all of Israel’s descendants; he humiliated them and handed them over to robbers
2Kings17:21 He tore Israel away from David’s dynasty
2Kings17:22 The Israelites followed in the sinful ways of Jeroboam son of Nebat and did not repudiate them.
2Kings17:23 Finally the LORD rejected Israel just as he had warned he would do through all his servants the prophets. Israel was deported from its land to Assyria and remains there to this very day.

2Kings20:10 Hezekiah answered
2Kings20:11 Isaiah the prophet called out to the LORD
2Kings20:12 At that time Merodach-Baladan son of Baladan
2Kings20:13 Hezekiah welcomed them and showed them his whole storehouse
2Kings20:14 Isaiah the prophet visited King Hezekiah and asked him
2Kings20:15 Isaiah asked
2Kings20:16 Isaiah said to Hezekiah
2Kings20:17 ‘Look
2Kings20:18 ‘Some of your very own descendants whom you father will be taken away and will be made eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.’”
2Kings20:19 Hezekiah said to Isaiah
2Kings20:20 The rest of the events of Hezekiah’s reign and all his accomplishments
2Kings20:21 Hezekiah passed away and his son Manasseh replaced him as king.
2Kings21:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king
2Kings21:2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and committed the same horrible sins practiced by the nations whom the LORD drove out from before the Israelites.
2Kings21:3 He rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had destroyed; he set up altars for Baal and made an Asherah pole just like King Ahab of Israel had done. He bowed down to all the stars in the sky and worshiped them.
2Kings21:4 He built altars in the LORD’s temple
2Kings21:5 In the two courtyards of the LORD’s temple he built altars for all the stars in the sky.
2Kings21:6 He passed his son through the fire and practiced divination and omen reading. He set up a ritual pit to conjure up underworld spirits
2Kings21:7 He put an idol of Asherah he had made in the temple
2Kings21:8 I will not make Israel again leave the land I gave to their ancestors
2Kings21:9 But they did not obey
2Kings21:10 So the LORD announced through his servants the prophets:
2Kings21:11 “King Manasseh of Judah has committed horrible sins. He has sinned more than the Amorites before him and has encouraged Judah to sin by worshiping his disgusting idols.
2Kings21:12 So this is what the LORD God of Israel says
2Kings21:13 I will destroy Jerusalem the same way I did Samaria and the dynasty of Ahab. I will wipe Jerusalem clean
2Kings21:14 I will abandon this last remaining tribe among my people and hand them over to their enemies; they will be plundered and robbed by all their enemies
2Kings21:15 because they have done evil in my sight and have angered me from the time their ancestors left Egypt right up to this very day!’”
2Kings21:16 Furthermore Manasseh killed so many innocent people
2Kings21:17 The rest of the events of Manasseh’s reign and all his accomplishments
2Kings21:18 Manasseh passed away and was buried in his palace garden
2Kings21:19 Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king
2Kings21:20 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings21:21 He followed in the footsteps of his father and worshiped and bowed down to the disgusting idols which his father had worshiped.
2Kings21:22 He abandoned the LORD God of his ancestors and did not follow the LORD’s instructions.
2Kings21:23 Amon’s servants conspired against him and killed the king in his palace.
2Kings21:24 The people of the land executed all those who had conspired against King Amon
2Kings21:25 The rest of Amon’s accomplishments are recorded in the scroll called the Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings21:26 He was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzzah
2Kings22:1 Josiah was eight years old when he became king
2Kings22:2 He did what the LORD approved and followed in his ancestor David’s footsteps; he did not deviate to the right or the left.
2Kings22:3 In the eighteenth year of King Josiah’s reign
2Kings22:4 “Go up to Hilkiah the high priest and have him melt down the silver that has been brought by the people to the LORD’s temple and has been collected by the guards at the door.
2Kings22:5 Have them hand it over to the construction foremen assigned to the LORD’s temple. They in turn should pay the temple workers to repair it
2Kings22:6 including craftsmen
2Kings22:7 Do not audit the foremen who disburse the silver
2Kings22:8 Hilkiah the high priest informed Shaphan the scribe
2Kings22:9 Shaphan the scribe went to the king and reported
2Kings22:10 Then Shaphan the scribe told the king
2Kings22:11 When the king heard the words of the law scroll
2Kings22:12 The king ordered Hilkiah the priest
2Kings22:13 “Go
2Kings22:14 So Hilkiah the priest
2Kings22:15 and she said to them: “This is what the LORD God of Israel says: ‘Say this to the man who sent you to me:
2Kings22:16 “This is what the LORD says: ‘I am about to bring disaster on this place and its residents
2Kings22:17 This will happen because they have abandoned me and offered sacrifices to other gods
2Kings22:18 Say this to the king of Judah
2Kings22:19 ‘You displayed a sensitive spirit and humbled yourself before the LORD when you heard how I intended to make this place and its residents into an appalling example of an accursed people. You tore your clothes and wept before me
2Kings22:20 ‘Therefore I will allow you to die and be buried in peace. You will not have to witness all the disaster I will bring on this place.’”’” Then they reported back to the king.
2Kings23:1 The king summoned all the leaders of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Kings23:2 The king went up to the LORD’s temple
2Kings23:3 The king stood by the pillar and renewed the covenant before the LORD
2Kings23:4 The king ordered Hilkiah the high priest
2Kings23:5 He eliminated the pagan priests whom the kings of Judah had appointed to offer sacrifices on the high places in the cities of Judah and in the area right around Jerusalem. (They offered sacrifices to Baal
2Kings23:6 He removed the Asherah pole from the LORD’s temple and took it outside Jerusalem to the Kidron Valley
2Kings23:7 He tore down the quarters of the male cultic prostitutes in the LORD’s temple
2Kings23:8 He brought all the priests from the cities of Judah and ruined the high places where the priests had offered sacrifices
2Kings23:9 (Now the priests of the high places did not go up to the altar of the LORD in Jerusalem
2Kings23:10 The king ruined Topheth in the Valley of Ben Hinnom so that no one could pass his son or his daughter through the fire to Molech.
2Kings23:11 He removed from the entrance to the LORD’s temple the statues of horses that the kings of Judah had placed there in honor of the sun god. (They were kept near the room of Nathan Melech the eunuch
2Kings23:12 The king tore down the altars the kings of Judah had set up on the roof of Ahaz’s upper room
2Kings23:13 The king ruined the high places east of Jerusalem
2Kings23:14 He smashed the sacred pillars to bits
2Kings23:15 He also tore down the altar in Bethel at the high place made by Jeroboam son of Nebat
2Kings23:16 When Josiah turned around
2Kings23:17 He asked
2Kings23:18 The king said
2Kings23:19 Josiah also removed all the shrines on the high places in the cities of Samaria. The kings of Israel had made them and angered the LORD. He did to them what he had done to the high place in Bethel.
2Kings23:20 He sacrificed all the priests of the high places on the altars located there
2Kings23:21 The king ordered all the people
2Kings23:22 He issued this edict because a Passover like this had not been observed since the days of the judges; it was neglected for the entire period of the kings of Israel and Judah.
2Kings23:23 But in the eighteenth year of King Josiah’s reign
2Kings23:24 Josiah also got rid of the ritual pits used to conjure up spirits
2Kings23:25 No king before or after repented before the LORD as he did
2Kings23:26 Yet the LORD’s great anger against Judah did not subside; he was still infuriated by all the things Manasseh had done.
2Kings23:27 The LORD announced
2Kings23:28 The rest of the events of Josiah’s reign and all his accomplishments are recorded in the scroll called the Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings23:29 During Josiah’s reign Pharaoh Necho king of Egypt marched toward the Euphrates River to help the king of Assyria. King Josiah marched out to fight him
2Kings23:30 His servants transported his dead body from Megiddo in a chariot and brought it to Jerusalem
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king
2Kings23:32 He did evil in the sight of the LORD as his ancestors had done.
2Kings23:33 Pharaoh Necho imprisoned him in Riblah in the land of Hamath and prevented him from ruling in Jerusalem. He imposed on the land a special tax of one hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
2Kings23:34 Pharaoh Necho made Josiah’s son Eliakim king in Josiah’s place
2Kings23:35 Jehoiakim paid Pharaoh the required amount of silver and gold
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king
2Kings23:37 He did evil in the sight of the LORD as his ancestors had done.
2Kings24:1 During Jehoiakim’s reign
2Kings24:2 The LORD sent against him Babylonian
2Kings24:3 Just as the LORD had announced
2Kings24:4 Because he killed innocent people and stained Jerusalem with their blood
2Kings24:5 The rest of the events of Jehoiakim’s reign and all his accomplishments
2Kings24:6 He passed away and his son Jehoiachin replaced him as king.
2Kings24:7 The king of Egypt did not march out from his land again
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king
2Kings24:9 He did evil in the sight of the LORD as his ancestors had done.
2Kings24:10 At that time the generals of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon marched to Jerusalem and besieged the city.
2Kings24:11 King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to the city while his generals were besieging it.
2Kings24:12 King Jehoiachin of Judah
2Kings24:13 Nebuchadnezzar took from there all the riches in the treasuries of the LORD’s temple and of the royal palace. He removed all the gold items which King Solomon of Israel had made for the LORD’s temple
2Kings24:14 He deported all the residents of Jerusalem
2Kings24:15 He deported Jehoiachin from Jerusalem to Babylon
2Kings24:16 The king of Babylon deported to Babylon all the soldiers (there were 7
2Kings24:17 The king of Babylon made Mattaniah
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
2Kings24:19 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:20 What follows is a record of what happened to Jerusalem and Judah because of the LORD’s anger; he finally threw them out of his presence. Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
2Kings25:1 So King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came against Jerusalem with his whole army and set up camp outside it. They built siege ramps all around it. He arrived on the tenth day of the tenth month in the ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign.
2Kings25:2 The city remained under siege until King Zedekiah’s eleventh year.
2Kings25:3 By the ninth day of the fourth month the famine in the city was so severe the residents had no food.
2Kings25:4 The enemy broke through the city walls
2Kings25:5 But the Babylonian army chased after the king. They caught up with him in the plains of Jericho
2Kings25:6 They captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah
2Kings25:7 Zedekiah’s sons were executed while Zedekiah was forced to watch. The king of Babylon then had Zedekiah’s eyes put out
2Kings25:8 On the seventh day of the fifth month
2Kings25:9 He burned down the LORD’s temple
2Kings25:10 The whole Babylonian army that came with the captain of the royal guard tore down the walls that surrounded Jerusalem.
2Kings25:11 Nebuzaradan
2Kings25:12 But he left behind some of the poor of the land and gave them fields and vineyards.
2Kings25:13 The Babylonians broke the two bronze pillars in the LORD’s temple
2Kings25:14 They also took the pots
2Kings25:15 The captain of the royal guard took the golden and silver censers and basins.
2Kings25:16 The bronze of the items that King Solomon made for the LORD’s temple — including the two pillars
2Kings25:17 Each of the pillars was about twenty-seven feet high. The bronze top of one pillar was about four and a half feet high and had bronze latticework and pomegranate shaped ornaments all around it. The second pillar with its latticework was like it.
2Kings25:18 The captain of the royal guard took Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah
2Kings25:19 From the city he took a eunuch who was in charge of the soldiers
2Kings25:20 Nebuzaradan
2Kings25:21 The king of Babylon ordered them to be executed at Riblah in the territory of Hamath. So Judah was deported from its land.
2Kings25:22 Now King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon appointed Gedaliah son of Ahikam
2Kings25:23 All of the officers of the Judahite army and their troops heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah to govern. So they came to Gedaliah at Mizpah. The officers who came were Ishmael son of Nethaniah
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah took an oath so as to give them and their troops some assurance of safety. He said
2Kings25:25 But in the seventh month Ishmael son of Nethaniah
2Kings25:26 Then all the people
2Kings25:27 In the thirty-seventh year of the exile of King Jehoiachin of Judah
2Kings25:28 He spoke kindly to him and gave him a more prestigious position than the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
2Kings25:29 Jehoiachin took off his prison clothes and ate daily in the king’s presence for the rest of his life.
2Kings25:30 He was given daily provisions by the king for the rest of his life until the day he died.

1Chronicles10:1 Now the Philistines fought against Israel. The Israelites fled before the Philistines and many of them fell dead on Mount Gilboa.
1Chronicles10:2 The Philistines stayed right on the heels of Saul and his sons. They struck down Saul’s sons Jonathan
1Chronicles10:3 The battle was thick around Saul; the archers spotted him and wounded him.
1Chronicles10:4 Saul told his armor bearer
1Chronicles10:5 When his armor bearer saw that Saul was dead
1Chronicles10:6 So Saul and his three sons died; his whole household died together.
1Chronicles10:7 When all the Israelites who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead
1Chronicles10:8 The next day
1Chronicles10:9 They stripped his corpse
1Chronicles10:10 They placed his armor in the temple of their gods and hung his head in the temple of Dagon.
1Chronicles10:11 When all the residents of Jabesh Gilead heard about everything the Philistines had done to Saul
1Chronicles10:12 all the warriors went and recovered the bodies of Saul and his sons and brought them to Jabesh. They buried their remains under the oak tree in Jabesh and fasted for seven days.
1Chronicles10:13 So Saul died because he was unfaithful to the LORD and did not obey the LORD’s instructions; he even tried to conjure up underworld spirits.
1Chronicles10:14 He did not seek the LORD’s guidance

1Chronicles16:10 Boast about his holy name! Let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice!

1Chronicles16:13 O children of Israel
1Chronicles16:14 He is the LORD our God; he carries out judgment throughout the earth.

2Chronicles2:1 (1:18) Solomon ordered a temple to be built to honor the LORD
2Chronicles2:2 (2:1) Solomon had 70
2Chronicles2:3 Solomon sent a message to King Huram of Tyre: “Help me as you did my father David
2Chronicles2:4 Look
2Chronicles2:5 I will build a great temple
2Chronicles2:6 Of course
2Chronicles2:7 “Now send me a man who is skilled in working with gold
2Chronicles2:8 Send me cedars
2Chronicles2:9 to supply me with large quantities of timber
2Chronicles2:10 Look
2Chronicles2:11 King Huram of Tyre sent this letter to Solomon: “Because the LORD loves his people
2Chronicles2:12 Huram also said
2Chronicles2:13 Now I am sending you Huram Abi
2Chronicles2:14 whose mother is a Danite and whose father is a Tyrian. He knows how to work with gold
2Chronicles2:15 Now let my lord send to his servants the wheat
2Chronicles2:16 we will get all the timber you need from Lebanon and bring it in raft-like bundles by sea to Joppa. You can then haul it on up to Jerusalem.”
2Chronicles2:17 Solomon took a census of all the male resident foreigners in the land of Israel
2Chronicles2:18 He designated 70
2Chronicles3:1 Solomon began building the LORD’s temple in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 He began building on the second day of the second month of the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 Solomon laid the foundation for God’s temple; its length (determined according to the old standard of measure) was 90 feet
2Chronicles3:4 The porch in front of the main hall was 30 feet long
2Chronicles3:5 He paneled the main hall with boards made from evergreen trees and plated it with fine gold
2Chronicles3:6 He decorated the temple with precious stones; the gold he used came from Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 He overlaid the temple’s rafters
2Chronicles3:8 He made the most holy place; its length was 30 feet
2Chronicles3:9 The gold nails weighed 50 shekels; he also plated the upper areas with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 In the most holy place he made two images of cherubim and plated them with gold.
2Chronicles3:11 The combined wing span of the cherubs was 30 feet. One of the first cherub’s wings was seven and one-half feet long and touched one wall of the temple; its other wing was also seven and one-half feet long and touched one of the second cherub’s wings.
2Chronicles3:12 Likewise one of the second cherub’s wings was seven and one-half feet long and touched the other wall of the temple; its other wing was also seven and one-half feet long and touched one of the first cherub’s wings.
2Chronicles3:13 The combined wingspan of these cherubim was 30 feet. They stood upright
2Chronicles3:14 He made the curtain out of violet
2Chronicles3:15 In front of the temple he made two pillars which had a combined length of 52Â½ feet
2Chronicles3:16 He made ornamental chains and put them on top of the pillars. He also made one hundred pomegranate-shaped ornaments and arranged them within the chains.
2Chronicles3:17 He set up the pillars in front of the temple
2Chronicles4:1 He made a bronze altar
2Chronicles4:2 He also made the big bronze basin called “The Sea.” It measured 15 feet from rim to rim
2Chronicles4:3 Images of bulls were under it all the way around
2Chronicles4:4 “The Sea” stood on top of twelve bulls. Three faced northward
2Chronicles4:5 It was four fingers thick and its rim was like that of a cup shaped like a lily blossom. It could hold 18
2Chronicles4:6 He made ten washing basins; he put five on the south side and five on the north side. In them they rinsed the items used for burnt sacrifices; the priests washed in “The Sea.”
2Chronicles4:7 He made ten gold lampstands according to specifications and put them in the temple
2Chronicles4:8 He made ten tables and set them in the temple
2Chronicles4:9 He made the courtyard of the priests and the large enclosure and its doors; he plated their doors with bronze.
2Chronicles4:10 He put “The Sea” on the south side
2Chronicles4:11 Huram Abi made the pots
2Chronicles4:12 He made the two pillars
2Chronicles4:13 the four hundred pomegranate-shaped ornaments for the latticework of the two pillars (each latticework had two rows of these ornaments at the bowl-shaped top of the pillar)
2Chronicles4:14 the ten movable stands with their ten basins
2Chronicles4:15 the big bronze basin called “The Sea” with its twelve bulls underneath
2Chronicles4:16 and the pots
2Chronicles4:17 The king had them cast in earthen foundries in the region of the Jordan between Succoth and Zarethan.
2Chronicles4:18 Solomon made so many of these items they did not weigh the bronze.
2Chronicles4:19 Solomon also made these items for God’s temple: the gold altar
2Chronicles4:20 the pure gold lampstands and their lamps which burned as specified at the entrance to the inner sanctuary
2Chronicles4:21 the pure gold flower-shaped ornaments
2Chronicles4:22 the pure gold trimming shears
2Chronicles5:1 When Solomon had finished constructing the LORD’s temple
2Chronicles5:2 Then Solomon convened Israel’s elders — all the leaders of the Israelite tribes and families — in Jerusalem
2Chronicles5:3 All the men of Israel assembled before the king during the festival in the seventh month.
2Chronicles5:4 When all Israel’s elders had arrived
2Chronicles5:5 The priests and Levites carried the ark
2Chronicles5:6 Now King Solomon and all the Israelites who had assembled with him went on ahead of the ark and sacrificed more sheep and cattle than could be counted or numbered.
2Chronicles5:7 The priests brought the ark of the covenant of the LORD to its assigned place in the inner sanctuary of the temple
2Chronicles5:8 The cherubs’ wings extended over the place where the ark sat; the cherubs overshadowed the ark and its poles.
2Chronicles5:9 The poles were so long their ends extending out from the ark were visible from in front of the inner sanctuary
2Chronicles5:10 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets Moses had placed there in Horeb. (It was there that the LORD made an agreement with the Israelites after he brought them out of the land of Egypt.)
2Chronicles5:11 The priests left the holy place. All the priests who participated had consecrated themselves
2Chronicles5:12 All the Levites who were musicians
2Chronicles5:13 The trumpeters and musicians played together
2Chronicles5:14 The priests could not carry out their duties because of the cloud; the LORD’s splendor filled God’s temple.
2Chronicles6:1 Then Solomon said
2Chronicles6:2 O LORD
2Chronicles6:3 Then the king turned around and pronounced a blessing over the whole Israelite assembly as they stood there.
2Chronicles6:4 He said
2Chronicles6:5 He told David
2Chronicles6:6 But now I have chosen Jerusalem as a place to live
2Chronicles6:7 Now my father David had a strong desire to build a temple to honor the LORD God of Israel.
2Chronicles6:8 The LORD told my father David
2Chronicles6:9 But you will not build the temple; your very own son will build the temple for my honor.’
2Chronicles6:10 The LORD has kept the promise he made. I have taken my father David’s place and have occupied the throne of Israel
2Chronicles6:11 and set up in it a place for the ark containing the covenant the LORD made with the Israelites.”
2Chronicles6:12 He stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the entire assembly of Israel and spread out his hands.
2Chronicles6:13 Solomon had made a bronze platform and had placed it in the middle of the enclosure. It was seven and one-half feet long
2Chronicles6:14 and prayed: “O LORD God of Israel
2Chronicles6:15 You have kept your word to your servant
2Chronicles6:16 Now
2Chronicles6:17 Now
2Chronicles6:18 “God does not really live with humankind on the earth! Look
2Chronicles6:19 But respond favorably to your servant’s prayer and his request for help
2Chronicles6:20 Night and day may you watch over this temple
2Chronicles6:21 Respond to the requests of your servant and your people Israel for this place. Hear from your heavenly dwelling place and respond favorably and forgive.
2Chronicles6:22 “When someone is accused of sinning against his neighbor and the latter pronounces a curse on the alleged offender before your altar in this temple
2Chronicles6:23 listen from heaven and make a just decision about your servants’ claims. Condemn the guilty party
2Chronicles6:24 “If your people Israel are defeated by an enemy because they sinned against you
2Chronicles6:25 then listen from heaven
2Chronicles6:26 “The time will come when the skies are shut up tightly and no rain falls because your people sinned against you. When they direct their prayers toward this place
2Chronicles6:27 then listen from heaven and forgive the sin of your servants
2Chronicles6:28 “The time will come when the land suffers from a famine
2Chronicles6:29 When all your people Israel pray and ask for help
2Chronicles6:30 then listen from your heavenly dwelling place
2Chronicles6:31 Then they will honor you by obeying you throughout their lifetimes as they live on the land you gave to our ancestors.
2Chronicles6:32 “Foreigners
2Chronicles6:33 Then listen from your heavenly dwelling place and answer all the prayers of the foreigners. Then all the nations of the earth will acknowledge your reputation
2Chronicles6:34 “When you direct your people to march out and fight their enemies
2Chronicles6:35 then listen from heaven to their prayers for help and vindicate them.
2Chronicles6:36 “The time will come when your people will sin against you (for there is no one who is sinless!) and you will be angry at them and deliver them over to their enemies
2Chronicles6:37 When your people come to their senses in the land where they are held prisoner
2Chronicles6:38 When they return to you with all their heart and being in the land where they are held prisoner and direct their prayers toward the land you gave to their ancestors
2Chronicles6:39 then listen from your heavenly dwelling place to their prayers for help
2Chronicles6:40 “Now
2Chronicles6:41 Now ascend
2Chronicles6:42 O LORD God
2Chronicles7:1 When Solomon finished praying
2Chronicles7:2 The priests were unable to enter the LORD’s temple because the LORD’s splendor filled the LORD’s temple.
2Chronicles7:3 When all the Israelites saw the fire come down and the LORD’s splendor over the temple
2Chronicles7:4 The king and all the people were presenting sacrifices to the LORD.
2Chronicles7:5 King Solomon sacrificed 22
2Chronicles7:6 The priests stood in their assigned spots
2Chronicles7:7 Solomon consecrated the middle of the courtyard that is in front of the LORD’s temple. He offered burnt sacrifices
2Chronicles7:8 At that time Solomon and all Israel with him celebrated a festival for seven days. This great assembly included people from Lebo Hamath in the north to the Brook of Egypt in the south.
2Chronicles7:9 On the eighth day they held an assembly
2Chronicles7:10 On the twenty-third day of the seventh month
2Chronicles7:11 After Solomon finished building the LORD’s temple and the royal palace
2Chronicles7:12 the LORD appeared to Solomon at night and said to him: “I have answered your prayer and chosen this place to be my temple where sacrifices are to be made.
2Chronicles7:13 When I close up the sky so that it doesn’t rain
2Chronicles7:14 if my people
2Chronicles7:15 Now I will be attentive and responsive to the prayers offered in this place.
2Chronicles7:16 Now I have chosen and consecrated this temple by making it my permanent home; I will be constantly present there.
2Chronicles7:17 You must serve me as your father David did. Do everything I commanded and obey my rules and regulations.
2Chronicles7:18 Then I will establish your dynasty
2Chronicles7:19 “But if you people ever turn away from me
2Chronicles7:20 then I will remove you from my land I have given you
2Chronicles7:21 As for this temple
2Chronicles7:22 Others will then answer
2Chronicles8:1 After twenty years
2Chronicles8:2 Solomon rebuilt the cities that Huram had given him and settled Israelites there.
2Chronicles8:3 Solomon went to Hamath Zobah and seized it.
2Chronicles8:4 He built up Tadmor in the wilderness and all the storage cities he had built in Hamath.
2Chronicles8:5 He made upper Beth Horon and lower Beth Horon fortified cities with walls and barred gates
2Chronicles8:6 and built up Baalath
2Chronicles8:7 Now several non-Israelite peoples were left in the land after the conquest of Joshua
2Chronicles8:8 Their descendants remained in the land (the Israelites were unable to wipe them out). Solomon conscripted them for his work crews and they continue in that role to this very day.
2Chronicles8:9 Solomon did not assign Israelites to these work crews; the Israelites served as his soldiers
2Chronicles8:10 These men worked for Solomon as supervisors; there were a total of 250 of them who were in charge of the people.
2Chronicles8:11 Solomon moved Pharaoh’s daughter up from the City of David to the palace he had built for her
2Chronicles8:12 Then Solomon offered burnt sacrifices to the LORD on the altar of the LORD which he had built in front of the temple’s porch.
2Chronicles8:13 He observed the daily requirements for sacrifices that Moses had specified for Sabbaths
2Chronicles8:14 As his father David had decreed
2Chronicles8:15 They did not neglect any detail of the king’s orders pertaining to the priests
2Chronicles8:16 All the work ordered by Solomon was completed
2Chronicles8:17 Then Solomon went to Ezion Geber and to Elat on the coast in the land of Edom.
2Chronicles8:18 Huram sent him ships and some of his sailors
2Chronicles9:1 When the queen of Sheba heard about Solomon
2Chronicles9:2 Solomon answered all her questions; there was no question too complex for the king.
2Chronicles9:3 When the queen of Sheba saw for herself Solomon’s extensive wisdom
2Chronicles9:4 the food in his banquet hall
2Chronicles9:5 She said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 I did not believe these things until I came and saw them with my own eyes. Indeed
2Chronicles9:7 Your attendants
2Chronicles9:8 May the LORD your God be praised because he favored you by placing you on his throne as the one ruling on his behalf! Because of your God’s love for Israel and his lasting commitment to them
2Chronicles9:9 She gave the king 120 talents of gold and a very large quantity of spices and precious gems. The quantity of spices the queen of Sheba gave King Solomon has never been matched.
2Chronicles9:10 (Huram’s servants
2Chronicles9:11 With the timber the king made steps for the LORD’s temple and royal palace as well as stringed instruments for the musicians. No one had seen anything like them in the land of Judah prior to that. )
2Chronicles9:12 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba everything she requested
2Chronicles9:13 Solomon received 666 talents of gold per year
2Chronicles9:14 besides what he collected from the merchants and traders. All the Arabian kings and the governors of the land also brought gold and silver to Solomon.
2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold; 600 measures of hammered gold were used for each shield.
2Chronicles9:16 He also made three hundred small shields of hammered gold; 300 measures of gold were used for each of those shields. The king placed them in the Palace of the Lebanon Forest.
2Chronicles9:17 The king made a large throne decorated with ivory and overlaid it with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 There were six steps leading up to the throne
2Chronicles9:19 There were twelve statues of lions on the six steps
2Chronicles9:20 All of King Solomon’s cups were made of gold
2Chronicles9:21 The king had a fleet of large merchant ships manned by Huram’s men that sailed the sea. Once every three years the fleet came into port with cargoes of gold
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon was wealthier and wiser than any of the kings of the earth.
2Chronicles9:23 All the kings of the earth wanted to visit Solomon to see him display his God-given wisdom.
2Chronicles9:24 Year after year visitors brought their gifts
2Chronicles9:25 Solomon had 4
2Chronicles9:26 He ruled all the kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines as far as the border of Egypt.
2Chronicles9:27 The king made silver as plentiful in Jerusalem as stones; cedar was as plentiful as sycamore fig trees are in the lowlands.
2Chronicles9:28 Solomon acquired horses from Egypt and from all the lands.
2Chronicles9:29 The rest of the events of Solomon’s reign
2Chronicles9:30 Solomon ruled over all Israel from Jerusalem for forty years.
2Chronicles9:31 Then Solomon passed away and was buried in the city of his father David. His son Rehoboam replaced him as king.
2Chronicles10:1 Rehoboam traveled to Shechem
2Chronicles10:2 When Jeroboam son of Nebat heard the news
2Chronicles10:3 They sent for him and Jeroboam and all Israel came and spoke to Rehoboam
2Chronicles10:4 “Your father made us work too hard! Now if you lighten the demands he made and don’t make us work as hard
2Chronicles10:5 He said to them
2Chronicles10:6 King Rehoboam consulted with the older advisers who had served his father Solomon when he had been alive. He asked them
2Chronicles10:7 They said to him
2Chronicles10:8 But Rehoboam rejected their advice and consulted the young advisers who served him
2Chronicles10:9 He asked them
2Chronicles10:10 The young advisers with whom Rehoboam had grown up said to him
2Chronicles10:11 My father imposed heavy demands on you; I will make them even heavier. My father punished you with ordinary whips; I will punish you with whips that really sting your flesh.’”
2Chronicles10:12 Jeroboam and all the people reported to Rehoboam on the third day
2Chronicles10:13 The king responded to the people harshly. He rejected the advice of the older men
2Chronicles10:14 and followed the advice of the younger ones. He said
2Chronicles10:15 The king refused to listen to the people
2Chronicles10:16 When all Israel saw that the king refused to listen to them
2Chronicles10:17 (Rehoboam continued to rule over the Israelites who lived in the cities of Judah.)
2Chronicles10:18 King Rehoboam sent Hadoram
2Chronicles10:19 So Israel has been in rebellion against the Davidic dynasty to this very day.
2Chronicles11:1 When Rehoboam arrived in Jerusalem
2Chronicles11:2 But the LORD told Shemaiah the prophet
2Chronicles11:3 “Say this to King Rehoboam son of Solomon of Judah and to all the Israelites in Judah and Benjamin
2Chronicles11:4 ‘The LORD says this: “Do not attack and make war with your brothers. Each of you go home
2Chronicles11:5 Rehoboam lived in Jerusalem; he built up these fortified cities throughout Judah:
2Chronicles11:6 Bethlehem
2Chronicles11:7 Beth Zur
2Chronicles11:8 Gath
2Chronicles11:9 Adoraim
2Chronicles11:10 Zorah
2Chronicles11:11 He fortified these cities and placed officers in them
2Chronicles11:12 In each city there were shields and spears; he strongly fortified them. Judah and Benjamin belonged to him.
2Chronicles11:13 The priests and Levites who lived throughout Israel supported him
2Chronicles11:14 The Levites even left their pasturelands and their property behind and came to Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles11:15 Jeroboam appointed his own priests to serve at the worship centers and to lead in the worship of the goat idols and calf idols he had made.
2Chronicles11:16 Those among all the Israelite tribes who were determined to worship the LORD God of Israel followed them to Jerusalem to sacrifice to the LORD God of their ancestors.
2Chronicles11:17 They supported the kingdom of Judah and were loyal to Rehoboam son of Solomon for three years; they followed the edicts of David and Solomon for three years.
2Chronicles11:18 Rehoboam married Mahalath the daughter of David’s son Jerimoth and of Abihail
2Chronicles11:19 She bore him sons named Jeush
2Chronicles11:20 He later married Maacah the daughter of Absalom. She bore to him Abijah
2Chronicles11:21 Rehoboam loved Maacah daughter of Absalom more than his other wives and concubines. He had eighteen wives and sixty concubines; he fathered twenty-eight sons and sixty daughters.
2Chronicles11:22 Rehoboam appointed Abijah son of Maacah as the leader over his brothers
2Chronicles11:23 He wisely placed some of his many sons throughout the regions of Judah and Benjamin in the various fortified cities. He supplied them with abundant provisions and acquired many wives for them.

2Chronicles12:2 Because they were unfaithful to the LORD
2Chronicles12:3 He had 1

2Chronicles12:10 King Rehoboam made bronze shields to replace them and assigned them to the officers of the royal guard who protected the entrance to the royal palace.
2Chronicles12:11 Whenever the king visited the LORD’s temple
2Chronicles12:12 So when Rehoboam humbled himself
2Chronicles12:13 King Rehoboam solidified his rule in Jerusalem; he was forty-one years old when he became king and he ruled for seventeen years in Jerusalem
2Chronicles12:14 He did evil because he was not determined to follow the LORD.
2Chronicles12:15 The events of Rehoboam’s reign

2Chronicles13:1 In the eighteenth year of the reign of King Jeroboam
2Chronicles13:2 He ruled for three years in Jerusalem. His mother was Michaiah
2Chronicles13:3 Abijah launched the attack with 400
2Chronicles13:4 Abijah ascended Mount Zemaraim
2Chronicles13:5 Don’t you realize that the LORD God of Israel has given David and his dynasty lasting dominion over Israel by a formal agreement?
2Chronicles13:6 Jeroboam son of Nebat
2Chronicles13:7 Lawless good-for-nothing men gathered around him and conspired against Rehoboam son of Solomon
2Chronicles13:8 Now you are declaring that you will resist the LORD’s rule through the Davidic dynasty. You have a huge army
2Chronicles13:9 But you banished the LORD’s priests
2Chronicles13:10 But as for us
2Chronicles13:11 They offer burnt sacrifices to the LORD every morning and every evening
2Chronicles13:12 Now look
2Chronicles13:13 Now Jeroboam had sent some men to ambush the Judahite army from behind. The main army was in front of the Judahite army; the ambushers were behind it.
2Chronicles13:14 The men of Judah turned around and realized they were being attacked from the front and the rear. So they cried out for help to the LORD. The priests blew their trumpets
2Chronicles13:15 and the men of Judah gave the battle cry. As the men of Judah gave the battle cry
2Chronicles13:16 The Israelites fled from before the Judahite army
2Chronicles13:17 Abijah and his army thoroughly defeated them; 500
2Chronicles13:18 That day the Israelites were defeated; the men of Judah prevailed because they relied on the LORD God of their ancestors.
2Chronicles13:19 Abijah chased Jeroboam; he seized from him these cities: Bethel and its surrounding towns
2Chronicles13:20 Jeroboam did not regain power during the reign of Abijah. The LORD struck him down and he died.
2Chronicles13:21 Abijah’s power grew; he had fourteen wives and fathered twenty-two sons and sixteen daughters.
2Chronicles13:22 The rest of the events of Abijah’s reign
2Chronicles14:1 (13:23) Abijah passed away and was buried in the City of David. His son Asa replaced him as king. During his reign the land had rest for ten years.
2Chronicles14:2 (14:1) Asa did what the LORD his God desired and approved.
2Chronicles14:3 He removed the pagan altars and the high places
2Chronicles14:4 He ordered Judah to seek the LORD God of their ancestors and to observe his law and commands.
2Chronicles14:5 He removed the high places and the incense altars from all the cities of Judah. The kingdom had rest under his rule.
2Chronicles14:6 He built fortified cities throughout Judah
2Chronicles14:7 He said to the people of Judah: “Let’s build these cities and fortify them with walls
2Chronicles14:8 Asa had an army of 300
2Chronicles14:9 Zerah the Cushite marched against them with an army of 1
2Chronicles14:10 and Asa went out to oppose him. They deployed for battle in the Valley of Zephathah near Mareshah.
2Chronicles14:11 Asa prayed to the LORD his God: “O LORD
2Chronicles14:12 The LORD struck down the Cushites before Asa and Judah. The Cushites fled
2Chronicles14:13 and Asa and his army chased them as far as Gerar. The Cushites were wiped out; they were shattered before the LORD and his army. The men of Judah carried off a huge amount of plunder.
2Chronicles14:14 They defeated all the cities surrounding Gerar
2Chronicles14:15 They also attacked the tents of the herdsmen in charge of the livestock. They carried off many sheep and camels and then returned to Jerusalem.
2Chronicles15:1 God’s Spirit came upon Azariah son of Oded.
2Chronicles15:2 He met Asa and told him
2Chronicles15:3 For a long time Israel had no true God
2Chronicles15:4 Because of their distress
2Chronicles15:5 In those days no one could travel safely
2Chronicles15:6 One nation was crushed by another
2Chronicles15:7 But as for you
2Chronicles15:8 When Asa heard these words and the prophecy of Oded the prophet
2Chronicles15:9 He assembled all Judah and Benjamin
2Chronicles15:10 They assembled in Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of Asa’s reign.
2Chronicles15:11 At that time they sacrificed to the LORD some of the plunder they had brought back
2Chronicles15:12 They solemnly agreed to seek the LORD God of their ancestors with their whole heart and being.
2Chronicles15:13 Anyone who would not seek the LORD God of Israel would be executed
2Chronicles15:14 They swore their allegiance to the LORD
2Chronicles15:15 All Judah was happy about the oath
2Chronicles15:16 King Asa also removed Maacah his grandmother from her position as queen mother because she had made a loathsome Asherah pole. Asa cut down her Asherah pole and crushed and burned it in the Kidron Valley.
2Chronicles15:17 The high places were not eliminated from Israel
2Chronicles15:18 He brought the holy items that his father and he had made into God’s temple
2Chronicles15:19 There was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of Asa’s reign.
2Chronicles16:1 In the thirty-sixth year of Asa’s reign
2Chronicles16:2 Asa took all the silver and gold that was left in the treasuries of the LORD’s temple and of the royal palace and sent it to King Ben Hadad of Syria
2Chronicles16:3 “I want to make a treaty with you
2Chronicles16:4 Ben Hadad accepted King Asa’s offer and ordered his army commanders to attack the cities of Israel. They conquered Ijon
2Chronicles16:5 When Baasha heard the news
2Chronicles16:6 King Asa ordered all the men of Judah to carry away the stones and wood that Baasha had used to build Ramah. He used the materials to build up Geba and Mizpah.
2Chronicles16:7 At that time Hanani the prophet visited King Asa of Judah and said to him: “Because you relied on the king of Syria and did not rely on the LORD your God
2Chronicles16:8 Did not the Cushites and Libyans have a huge army with chariots and a very large number of horsemen? But when you relied on the LORD
2Chronicles16:9 Certainly the LORD watches the whole earth carefully and is ready to strengthen those who are devoted to him. You have acted foolishly in this matter; from now on you will have war.
2Chronicles16:10 Asa was so angry at the prophet
2Chronicles16:11 The events of Asa’s reign
2Chronicles16:12 In the thirty-ninth year of his reign
2Chronicles16:13 Asa passed away in the forty-first year of his reign.
2Chronicles16:14 He was buried in the tomb he had carved out in the City of David. They laid him to rest on a bier covered with spices and assorted mixtures of ointments. They made a huge bonfire to honor him.
2Chronicles17:1 His son Jehoshaphat replaced him as king and solidified his rule over Israel.
2Chronicles17:2 He placed troops in all of Judah’s fortified cities and posted garrisons throughout the land of Judah and in the cities of Ephraim that his father Asa had seized.
2Chronicles17:3 The LORD was with Jehoshaphat because he followed in his ancestor David’s footsteps at the beginning of his reign. He did not seek the Baals
2Chronicles17:4 but instead sought the God of his ancestors and obeyed his commands
2Chronicles17:5 The LORD made his kingdom secure; all Judah brought tribute to Jehoshaphat

2Chronicles30:13 A huge crowd assembled in Jerusalem to observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread in the second month.
2Chronicles30:14 They removed the altars in Jerusalem; they also removed all the incense altars and threw them into the Kidron Valley.
2Chronicles30:15 They slaughtered the Passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the second month. The priests and Levites were ashamed
2Chronicles30:16 They stood at their posts according to the regulations outlined in the law of Moses
2Chronicles30:17 Because many in the assembly had not consecrated themselves
2Chronicles30:18 The majority of the many people from Ephraim
2Chronicles30:19 everyone who has determined to follow God
2Chronicles30:20 The LORD responded favorably to Hezekiah and forgave the people.
2Chronicles30:21 The Israelites who were in Jerusalem observed the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days with great joy. The Levites and priests were praising the LORD every day with all their might.
2Chronicles30:22 Hezekiah expressed his appreciation to all the Levites
2Chronicles30:23 The entire assembly then decided to celebrate for seven more days; so they joyfully celebrated for seven more days.
2Chronicles30:24 King Hezekiah of Judah supplied 1
2Chronicles30:25 The celebration included the entire assembly of Judah
2Chronicles30:26 There was a great celebration in Jerusalem
2Chronicles30:27 The priests and Levites got up and pronounced blessings on the people. The LORD responded favorably to them as their prayers reached his holy dwelling place in heaven.
2Chronicles31:1 When all this was over
2Chronicles31:2 Hezekiah appointed the divisions of the priests and Levites to do their assigned tasks — to offer burnt sacrifices and present offerings and to serve
2Chronicles31:3 The king contributed some of what he owned for burnt sacrifices
2Chronicles31:4 He ordered the people living in Jerusalem to contribute the portion prescribed for the priests and Levites so they might be obedient to the law of the LORD.
2Chronicles31:5 When the edict was issued
2Chronicles31:6 The Israelites and people of Judah who lived in the cities of Judah also contributed a tenth of their cattle and sheep
2Chronicles31:7 In the third month they began piling their contributions in heaps and finished in the seventh month.
2Chronicles31:8 When Hezekiah and the officials came and saw the heaps
2Chronicles31:9 When Hezekiah asked the priests and Levites about the heaps
2Chronicles31:10 Azariah
2Chronicles31:11 Hezekiah ordered that storerooms be prepared in the LORD’s temple. When this was done
2Chronicles31:12 they brought in the contributions
2Chronicles31:13 Jehiel
2Chronicles31:14 Kore son of Imnah
2Chronicles31:15 In the cities of the priests

2Chronicles31:18 and to all the infants
2Chronicles31:19 As for the descendants of Aaron

2Chronicles32:1 After these faithful deeds were accomplished
2Chronicles32:2 When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had invaded and intended to attack Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 he consulted with his advisers and military officers about stopping up the springs outside the city
2Chronicles32:4 A large number of people gathered together and stopped up all the springs and the stream that flowed through the district. They reasoned
2Chronicles32:5 Hezekiah energetically rebuilt every broken wall. He erected towers and an outer wall
2Chronicles32:6 He appointed military officers over the army and assembled them in the square at the city gate. He encouraged them
2Chronicles32:7 “Be strong and brave! Don’t be afraid and don’t panic because of the king of Assyria and this huge army that is with him! We have with us one who is stronger than those who are with him.
2Chronicles32:8 He has with him mere human strength
2Chronicles32:9 Afterward King Sennacherib of Assyria
2Chronicles32:10 “This is what King Sennacherib of Assyria says: ‘Why are you so confident that you remain in Jerusalem while it is under siege?
2Chronicles32:11 Hezekiah says
2Chronicles32:12 Hezekiah is the one who eliminated the LORD’s high places and altars and then told Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:13 Are you not aware of what I and my predecessors have done to all the nations of the surrounding lands? Have the gods of the surrounding lands actually been able to rescue their lands from my power?
2Chronicles32:14 Who among all the gods of these nations whom my predecessors annihilated was able to rescue his people from my power?
2Chronicles32:15 Now don’t let Hezekiah deceive you or mislead you like this. Don’t believe him
2Chronicles32:16 Sennacherib’s servants further insulted the LORD God and his servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 He wrote letters mocking the LORD God of Israel and insulting him with these words: “The gods of the surrounding nations could not rescue their people from my power. Neither can Hezekiah’s god rescue his people from my power.”
2Chronicles32:18 They called out loudly in the Judahite dialect to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall
2Chronicles32:19 They talked about the God of Jerusalem as if he were one of the man-made gods of the nations of the earth.
2Chronicles32:20 King Hezekiah and the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz prayed about this and cried out to heaven.
2Chronicles32:21 The LORD sent a messenger and he wiped out all the soldiers
2Chronicles32:22 The LORD delivered Hezekiah and the residents of Jerusalem from the power of King Sennacherib of Assyria and from all the other nations. He made them secure on every side.
2Chronicles32:23 Many were bringing presents to the LORD in Jerusalem and precious gifts to King Hezekiah of Judah. From that time on he was respected by all the nations.
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah was stricken with a terminal illness. He prayed to the LORD
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah was ungrateful; he had a proud attitude
2Chronicles32:26 But then Hezekiah and the residents of Jerusalem humbled themselves and abandoned their pride
2Chronicles32:27 Hezekiah was very wealthy and greatly respected. He made storehouses for his silver
2Chronicles32:28 He made storerooms for the harvest of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He built royal cities and owned a large number of sheep and cattle
2Chronicles32:30 Hezekiah dammed up the source of the waters of the Upper Gihon and directed them down to the west side of the City of David. Hezekiah succeeded in all that he did.
2Chronicles32:31 So when the envoys arrived from the Babylonian officials to visit him and inquire about the sign that occurred in the land
2Chronicles32:32 The rest of the events of Hezekiah’s reign
2Chronicles32:33 Hezekiah passed away and was buried on the ascent of the tombs of the descendants of David. All the people of Judah and the residents of Jerusalem buried him with great honor. His son Manasseh replaced him as king.
2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king
2Chronicles33:2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and committed the same horrible sins practiced by the nations whom the LORD drove out ahead of the Israelites.
2Chronicles33:3 He rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had destroyed; he set up altars for the Baals and made Asherah poles. He bowed down to all the stars in the sky and worshiped them.
2Chronicles33:4 He built altars in the LORD’s temple
2Chronicles33:5 In the two courtyards of the LORD’s temple he built altars for all the stars in the sky.
2Chronicles33:6 He passed his sons through the fire in the Valley of Ben-Hinnom and practiced divination
2Chronicles33:7 He put an idolatrous image he had made in God’s temple
2Chronicles33:8 I will not make Israel again leave the land I gave to their ancestors
2Chronicles33:9 But Manasseh misled the people of Judah and the residents of Jerusalem so that they sinned more than the nations whom the LORD had destroyed ahead of the Israelites.
2Chronicles33:10 The LORD confronted Manasseh and his people
2Chronicles33:11 So the LORD brought against them the commanders of the army of the king of Assyria. They seized Manasseh
2Chronicles33:12 In his pain Manasseh asked the LORD his God for mercy and truly humbled himself before the God of his ancestors.
2Chronicles33:13 When he prayed to the LORD
2Chronicles33:14 After this Manasseh built up the outer wall of the City of David on the west side of the Gihon in the valley to the entrance of the Fish Gate and all around the terrace; he made it much higher. He placed army officers in all the fortified cities in Judah.
2Chronicles33:15 He removed the foreign gods and images from the LORD’s temple and all the altars he had built on the hill of the LORD’s temple and in Jerusalem; he threw them outside the city.
2Chronicles33:16 He erected the altar of the LORD and offered on it peace offerings and thank offerings. He told the people of Judah to serve the LORD God of Israel.
2Chronicles33:17 The people continued to offer sacrifices at the high places
2Chronicles33:18 The rest of the events of Manasseh’s reign
2Chronicles33:19 The Annals of the Prophets include his prayer
2Chronicles33:20 Manasseh passed away and was buried in his palace. His son Amon replaced him as king.
2Chronicles33:21 Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king
2Chronicles33:22 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles33:23 He did not humble himself before the LORD as his father Manasseh had done. Amon was guilty of great sin.
2Chronicles33:24 His servants conspired against him and killed him in his palace.
2Chronicles33:25 The people of the land executed all who had conspired against King Amon
2Chronicles34:1 Josiah was eight years old when he became king
2Chronicles34:2 He did what the LORD approved and followed in his ancestor David’s footsteps; he did not deviate to the right or the left.
2Chronicles34:3 In the eighth year of his reign
2Chronicles34:4 He ordered the altars of the Baals to be torn down
2Chronicles34:5 He burned the bones of the pagan priests on their altars; he purified Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:6 In the cities of Manasseh
2Chronicles34:7 he tore down the altars and Asherah poles
2Chronicles34:8 In the eighteenth year of his reign
2Chronicles34:9 They went to Hilkiah the high priest and gave him the silver that had been brought to God’s temple. The Levites who guarded the door had collected it from the people of Manasseh and Ephraim and from all who were left in Israel
2Chronicles34:10 They handed it over to the construction foremen assigned to the LORD’s temple. They in turn paid the temple workers to restore and repair it.
2Chronicles34:11 They gave money to the craftsmen and builders to buy chiseled stone and wood for the braces and rafters of the buildings that the kings of Judah had allowed to fall into disrepair.
2Chronicles34:12 The men worked faithfully. Their supervisors were Jahath and Obadiah (Levites descended from Merari)
2Chronicles34:13 supervised the laborers and all the foremen on their various jobs. Some of the Levites were scribes
2Chronicles34:14 When they took out the silver that had been brought to the LORD’s temple

2Chronicles34:16 Shaphan brought the scroll to the king and reported

2Chronicles34:18 Then Shaphan the scribe told the king
2Chronicles34:19 When the king heard the words of the law scroll
2Chronicles34:20 The king ordered Hilkiah
2Chronicles34:21 “Go
2Chronicles34:22 So Hilkiah and the others sent by the king went to Huldah the prophetess
2Chronicles34:23 and she said to them: “This is what the LORD God of Israel says: ‘Say this to the man who sent you to me:
2Chronicles34:24 “This is what the LORD says: ‘I am about to bring disaster on this place and its residents
2Chronicles34:25 This will happen because they have abandoned me and offered sacrifices to other gods
2Chronicles34:26 Say this to the king of Judah
2Chronicles34:27 ‘You displayed a sensitive spirit and humbled yourself before God when you heard his words concerning this place and its residents. You humbled yourself before me
2Chronicles34:28 ‘Therefore I will allow you to die and be buried in peace. You will not have to witness all the disaster I will bring on this place and its residents.’”’” Then they reported back to the king.
2Chronicles34:29 The king summoned all the leaders of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:30 The king went up to the LORD’s temple
2Chronicles34:31 The king stood by his pillar and renewed the covenant before the LORD
2Chronicles34:32 He made all who were in Jerusalem and Benjamin agree to it. The residents of Jerusalem acted in accordance with the covenant of God
2Chronicles34:33 Josiah removed all the detestable idols from all the areas belonging to the Israelites and encouraged all who were in Israel to worship the LORD their God. Throughout the rest of his reign they did not turn aside from following the LORD God of their ancestors.

Ezra4:9 From Rehum the commander
Ezra4:10 and the rest of nations whom the great and noble Ashurbanipal deported and settled in the cities of Samaria and other places in Trans-Euphrates.
Ezra4:11 (This is a copy of the letter they sent to him:) “To King Artaxerxes
Ezra4:12 Now let the king be aware that the Jews who came up to us from you have gone to Jerusalem. They are rebuilding that rebellious and odious city. They are completing its walls and repairing its foundations.
Ezra4:13 Let the king also be aware that if this city is built and its walls are completed
Ezra4:14 In light of the fact that we are loyal to the king
Ezra4:15 so that he may initiate a search of the records of his predecessors and discover in those records that this city is rebellious and injurious to both kings and provinces
Ezra4:16 We therefore are informing the king that if this city is rebuilt and its walls are completed
Ezra4:17 The king sent the following response: “To Rehum the commander
Ezra4:18 The letter you sent to us has been translated and read in my presence.
Ezra4:19 So I gave orders
Ezra4:20 Powerful kings have been over Jerusalem who ruled throughout the entire Trans-Euphrates and who were the beneficiaries of tribute
Ezra4:21 Now give orders that these men cease their work and that this city not be rebuilt until such time as I so instruct.
Ezra4:22 Exercise appropriate caution so that there is no negligence in this matter. Why should danger increase to the point that kings sustain damage?”
Ezra4:23 Then
Ezra4:24 So the work on the temple of God in Jerusalem came to a halt. It remained halted until the second year of the reign of King Darius of Persia.

Ezra6:9 Whatever is needed — whether oxen or rams or lambs or burnt offerings for the God of heaven or wheat or salt or wine or oil
Ezra6:10 so that they may be offering incense to the God of heaven and may be praying for the good fortune of the king and his family.
Ezra6:11 “I hereby give orders that if anyone changes this directive a beam is to be pulled out from his house and he is to be raised up and impaled on it
Ezra6:12 May God who makes his name to reside there overthrow any king or nation who reaches out to cause such change so as to destroy this temple of God in Jerusalem. I
Ezra6:13 Then Tattenai governor of Trans-Euphrates
Ezra6:14 The elders of the Jews continued building and prospering
Ezra6:15 They finished this temple on the third day of the month Adar
Ezra6:16 The people of Israel — the priests
Ezra6:17 For the dedication of this temple of God they offered one hundred bulls
Ezra6:18 They appointed the priests by their divisions and the Levites by their divisions over the worship of God at Jerusalem
Ezra6:19 The exiles observed the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month.
Ezra6:20 The priests and the Levites had purified themselves
Ezra6:21 The Israelites who were returning from the exile ate it
Ezra6:22 They observed the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days with joy

Ezra7:6 This Ezra is the one who came up from Babylon. He was a scribe who was skilled in the law of Moses which the LORD God of Israel had given. The king supplied him with everything he requested
Ezra7:7 In the seventh year of King Artaxerxes
Ezra7:8 He entered Jerusalem in the fifth month of the seventh year of the king.
Ezra7:9 On the first day of the first month he had determined to make the ascent from Babylon
Ezra7:10 Now Ezra had dedicated himself to the study of the law of the LORD
Ezra7:11 What follows is a copy of the letter that King Artaxerxes gave to Ezra the priestly scribe. Ezra was a scribe in matters pertaining to the commandments of the LORD and his statutes over Israel:
Ezra7:12 “Artaxerxes
Ezra7:13 I have now issued a decree that anyone in my kingdom from the people of Israel — even the priests and Levites — who wishes to do so may go up with you to Jerusalem.
Ezra7:14 You are authorized by the king and his seven advisers to inquire concerning Judah and Jerusalem
Ezra7:15 and to bring silver and gold which the king and his advisers have freely contributed to the God of Israel
Ezra7:16 along with all the silver and gold that you may collect throughout all the province of Babylon and the contributions of the people and the priests for the temple of their God which is in Jerusalem.
Ezra7:17 With this money you should be sure to purchase bulls
Ezra7:18 You may do whatever seems appropriate to you and your colleagues with the rest of the silver and the gold
Ezra7:19 Deliver to the God of Jerusalem the vessels that are given to you for the service of the temple of your God.
Ezra7:20 The rest of the needs for the temple of your God that you may have to supply
Ezra7:21 “I
Ezra7:22 up to 100 talents of silver
Ezra7:23 Everything that the God of heaven has required should be precisely done for the temple of the God of heaven. Why should there be wrath against the empire of the king and his sons?
Ezra7:24 Furthermore
Ezra7:25 “Now you
Ezra7:26 Everyone who does not observe both the law of your God and the law of the king will be completely liable to the appropriate penalty
Ezra7:27 Blessed be the LORD God of our fathers
Ezra7:28 He has also conferred his favor on me before the king

Nehemiah9:1 On the twenty-fourth day of this same month the Israelites assembled; they were fasting and wearing sackcloth
Nehemiah9:2 Those truly of Israelite descent separated from all the foreigners
Nehemiah9:3 For one-fourth of the day they stood in their place and read from the book of the law of the LORD their God
Nehemiah9:4 Then the Levites — Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 The Levites — Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 You alone are the LORD. You made the heavens
Nehemiah9:7 “You are the LORD God who chose Abram and brought him forth from Ur of the Chaldeans. You changed his name to Abraham.
Nehemiah9:8 When you perceived that his heart was faithful toward you
Nehemiah9:9 “You saw the affliction of our ancestors in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You performed awesome signs against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 You split the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 You guided them with a pillar of cloud by day and with a pillar of fire by night to illumine for them the path they were to travel.
Nehemiah9:13 “You came down on Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven. You provided them with just judgments
Nehemiah9:14 You made known to them your holy Sabbath; you issued commandments
Nehemiah9:15 You provided bread from heaven for them in their time of hunger
Nehemiah9:16 “But they — our ancestors — behaved presumptuously; they rebelled and did not obey your commandments.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey and did not recall your miracles that you had performed among them. Instead
Nehemiah9:18 even when they made a cast image of a calf for themselves and said
Nehemiah9:19 “Due to your great compassion you did not abandon them in the desert. The pillar of cloud did not stop guiding them in the path by day
Nehemiah9:20 You imparted your good Spirit to instruct them. You did not withhold your manna from their mouths; you provided water for their thirst.
Nehemiah9:21 For forty years you sustained them. Even in the desert they never lacked anything. Their clothes did not wear out and their feet did not swell.
Nehemiah9:22 “You gave them kingdoms and peoples
Nehemiah9:23 You multiplied their descendants like the stars of the sky. You brought them to the land you had told their ancestors to enter in order to possess.
Nehemiah9:24 Their descendants entered and possessed the land. You subdued before them the Canaanites who were the inhabitants of the land. You delivered them into their hand
Nehemiah9:25 They captured fortified cities and fertile land. They took possession of houses full of all sorts of good things — wells previously dug
Nehemiah9:26 “Nonetheless they grew disobedient and rebelled against you; they disregarded your law. They killed your prophets who had solemnly admonished them in order to cause them to return to you. They committed atrocious blasphemies.
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore you delivered them into the hand of their adversaries
Nehemiah9:28 “Then
Nehemiah9:29 And you solemnly admonished them in order to return them to your law
Nehemiah9:30 You prolonged your kindness with them for many years
Nehemiah9:31 However
Nehemiah9:32 “So now
Nehemiah9:33 You are righteous with regard to all that has happened to us
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 Even when they were in their kingdom and benefiting from your incredible goodness that you had lavished on them in the spacious and fertile land you had set before them
Nehemiah9:36 “So today we are slaves! In the very land you gave to our ancestors to eat its fruit and to enjoy its good things — we are slaves!
Nehemiah9:37 Its abundant produce goes to the kings you have placed over us due to our sins. They rule over our bodies and our livestock as they see fit

Nehemiah13:1 On that day the book of Moses was read aloud in the hearing of the people. They found written in it that no Ammonite or Moabite may ever enter the assembly of God
Nehemiah13:2 for they had not met the Israelites with food and water
Nehemiah13:3 When they heard the law
Nehemiah13:4 But prior to this time
Nehemiah13:5 He made for himself a large storeroom where previously they had been keeping the grain offering
Nehemiah13:6 During all this time I was not in Jerusalem
Nehemiah13:7 and I returned to Jerusalem. Then I discovered the evil that Eliashib had done for Tobiah by supplying him with a storeroom in the courts of the temple of God.
Nehemiah13:8 I was very upset
Nehemiah13:9 Then I gave instructions that the storerooms should be purified
Nehemiah13:10 I also discovered that the grain offerings for the Levites had not been provided
Nehemiah13:11 So I registered a complaint with the leaders
Nehemiah13:12 Then all of Judah brought the tithe of the grain
Nehemiah13:13 I gave instructions that Shelemiah the priest
Nehemiah13:14 Please remember me for this
Nehemiah13:15 In those days I saw people in Judah treading winepresses on the Sabbath
Nehemiah13:16 The people from Tyre who lived there were bringing fish and all kinds of merchandise and were selling it on the Sabbath to the people of Judah — and in Jerusalem
Nehemiah13:17 So I registered a complaint with the nobles of Judah
Nehemiah13:18 Isn’t this the way your ancestors acted
Nehemiah13:19 When the evening shadows began to fall on the gates of Jerusalem before the Sabbath
Nehemiah13:20 The traders and sellers of all kinds of merchandise spent the night outside Jerusalem once or twice.
Nehemiah13:21 But I warned them and said
Nehemiah13:22 Then I directed the Levites to purify themselves and come and guard the gates in order to keep the Sabbath day holy. For this please remember me
Nehemiah13:23 Also in those days I saw the men of Judah who had married women from Ashdod
Nehemiah13:24 Half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod (or the language of one of the other peoples mentioned ) and were unable to speak the language of Judah.
Nehemiah13:25 So I entered a complaint with them. I called down a curse on them
Nehemiah13:26 Was it not because of things like these that King Solomon of Israel sinned? Among the many nations there was no king like him. He was loved by his God
Nehemiah13:27 Should we then in your case hear that you do all this great evil
Nehemiah13:28 Now one of the sons of Joiada son of Eliashib the high priest was a son-in-law of Sanballat the Horonite. So I banished him from my sight.
Nehemiah13:29 Please remember them
Nehemiah13:30 So I purified them of everything foreign
Nehemiah13:31 I also provided for the wood offering at the appointed times and also for the first fruits. Please remember me for good
Esther1:1 The following events happened in the days of Ahasuerus. (I am referring to that Ahasuerus who used to rule over a hundred and twenty-seven provinces extending all the way from India to Ethiopia. )
Esther1:2 In those days
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign he provided a banquet for all his officials and his servants. The army of Persia and Media was present
Esther1:4 He displayed the riches of his royal glory and the splendor of his majestic greatness for a lengthy period of time — a hundred and eighty days
Esther1:5 When those days were completed
Esther1:6 The furnishings included linen and purple curtains hung by cords of the finest linen and purple wool on silver rings
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in golden containers
Esther1:8 There were no restrictions on the drinking
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a banquet for the women in King Ahasuerus’ royal palace.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti into the king’s presence wearing her royal high turban. He wanted to show the people and the officials her beauty
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king’s bidding conveyed through the eunuchs. Then the king became extremely angry
Esther1:13 The king then inquired of the wise men who were discerners of the times — for it was the royal custom to confer with all those who were proficient in laws and legalities.
Esther1:14 Those who were closest to him were Carshena
Esther1:15 The king asked
Esther1:16 Memucan then replied to the king and the officials
Esther1:17 For the matter concerning the queen will spread to all the women
Esther1:18 And this very day the noble ladies of Persia and Media who have heard the matter concerning the queen will respond in the same way to all the royal officials
Esther1:19 If the king is so inclined
Esther1:20 And let the king’s decision which he will enact be disseminated throughout all his kingdom
Esther1:21 The matter seemed appropriate to the king and the officials. So the king acted on the advice of Memucan.
Esther1:22 He sent letters throughout all the royal provinces
Esther2:1 When these things had been accomplished and the rage of King Ahasuerus had diminished
Esther2:2 The king’s servants who attended him said
Esther2:3 And let the king appoint officers throughout all the provinces of his kingdom to gather all the attractive young women to Susa the citadel
Esther2:4 Let the young woman whom the king finds most attractive become queen in place of Vashti.” This seemed like a good idea to the king
Esther2:5 Now there happened to be a Jewish man in Susa the citadel whose name was Mordecai. He was the son of Jair
Esther2:6 who had been taken into exile from Jerusalem with the captives who had been carried into exile with Jeconiah king of Judah
Esther2:7 Now he was acting as the guardian of Hadassah (that is
Esther2:8 It so happened that when the king’s edict and his law became known many young women were taken to Susa the citadel to be placed under the authority of Hegai. Esther also was taken to the royal palace to be under the authority of Hegai
Esther2:9 This young woman pleased him
Esther2:10 Now Esther had not disclosed her people or her lineage
Esther2:11 And day after day Mordecai used to walk back and forth in front of the court of the harem in order to learn how Esther was doing and what might happen to her.
Esther2:12 At the end of the twelve months that were required for the women
Esther2:13 the woman would go to the king in the following way: Whatever she asked for would be provided for her to take with her from the harem to the royal palace.
Esther2:14 In the evening she went
Esther2:15 When it became the turn of Esther daughter of Abihail the uncle of Mordecai (who had raised her as if she were his own daughter ) to go to the king
Esther2:16 Then Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus at his royal residence in the tenth month (that is
Esther2:17 And the king loved Esther more than all the other women
Esther2:18 Then the king prepared a large banquet for all his officials and his servants — it was actually Esther’s banquet. He also set aside a holiday for the provinces
Esther2:19 Now when the young women were being gathered again
Esther2:20 Esther was still not divulging her lineage or her people
Esther2:21 In those days while Mordecai was sitting at the king’s gate
Esther2:22 When Mordecai learned of the conspiracy
Esther2:23 The king then had the matter investigated and

Esther3:2 As a result
Esther3:3 Then the servants of the king who were at the king’s gate asked Mordecai
Esther3:4 And after they had spoken to him day after day without his paying any attention to them
Esther3:5 When Haman saw that Mordecai was not bowing or paying homage to him
Esther3:6 But the thought of striking out against Mordecai alone was repugnant to him
Esther3:7 In the first month (that is
Esther3:8 Then Haman said to King Ahasuerus
Esther3:9 If the king is so inclined
Esther3:10 So the king removed his signet ring from his hand and gave it to Haman the son of Hammedatha
Esther3:11 The king replied to Haman
Esther3:12 So the royal scribes were summoned in the first month
Esther3:13 Letters were sent by the runners to all the king’s provinces stating that they should destroy
Esther3:14 A copy of this edict was to be presented as law throughout every province; it was to be made known to all the inhabitants
Esther3:15 The messengers scurried forth with the king’s order. The edict was issued in Susa the citadel. While the king and Haman sat down to drink
Esther4:1 Now when Mordecai became aware of all that had been done
Esther4:2 But he went no further than the king’s gate
Esther4:3 Throughout each and every province where the king’s edict and law were announced there was considerable mourning among the Jews
Esther4:4 When Esther’s female attendants and her eunuchs came and informed her about Mordecai’s behavior
Esther4:5 So Esther called for Hathach
Esther4:6 So Hathach went to Mordecai at the plaza of the city in front of the king’s gate.
Esther4:7 Then Mordecai related to him everything that had happened to him
Esther4:8 He also gave him a written copy of the law that had been disseminated in Susa for their destruction so that he could show it to Esther and talk to her about it. He also gave instructions that she should go to the king to implore him and petition him on behalf of her people.
Esther4:9 So Hathach returned and related Mordecai’s instructions to Esther.
Esther4:10 Then Esther replied to Hathach with instructions for Mordecai:
Esther4:11 “All the servants of the king and the people of the king’s provinces know that there is only one law applicable to any man or woman who comes uninvited to the king in the inner court — that person will be put to death
Esther4:12 When Esther’s reply was conveyed to Mordecai
Esther4:13 he said to take back this answer to Esther:
Esther4:14 “Don’t imagine that because you are part of the king’s household you will be the one Jew who will escape. If you keep quiet at this time
Esther4:15 Then Esther sent this reply to Mordecai:
Esther4:16 “Go
Esther4:17 So Mordecai set out to do everything that Esther had instructed him.

Esther8:1 On that same day King Ahasuerus gave the estate of Haman
Esther8:2 The king then removed his signet ring (the very one he had taken back from Haman) and gave it to Mordecai. And Esther designated Mordecai to be in charge of Haman’s estate.
Esther8:3 Then Esther again spoke with the king
Esther8:4 When the king extended to Esther the gold scepter
Esther8:5 She said
Esther8:6 For how can I watch the calamity that will befall my people

Esther8:8 Now you write in the king’s name whatever in your opinion is appropriate concerning the Jews and seal it with the king’s signet ring. Any decree that is written in the king’s name and sealed with the king’s signet ring cannot be rescinded.
Esther8:9 The king’s scribes were quickly summoned — in the third month (that is
Esther8:10 Mordecai wrote in the name of King Ahasuerus and sealed it with the king’s signet ring. He then sent letters by couriers on horses
Esther8:11 The king thereby allowed the Jews who were in every city to assemble and to stand up for themselves — to destroy
Esther8:12 This was to take place on a certain day throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus — namely
Esther8:13 A copy of the edict was to be presented as law throughout each and every province and made known to all peoples

Esther9:2 The Jews assembled themselves in their cities throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus to strike out against those who were seeking their harm. No one was able to stand before them

Esther9:15 The Jews who were in Susa then assembled on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar
Esther9:16 The rest of the Jews who were throughout the provinces of the king assembled in order to stand up for themselves and to have rest from their enemies. They killed seventy-five thousand of their adversaries
Esther9:17 All of this happened on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar. They then rested on the fourteenth day and made it a day for banqueting and happiness.
Esther9:18 But the Jews who were in Susa assembled on the thirteenth and fourteenth days
Esther9:19 This is why the Jews who are in the rural country — those who live in rural cities — set aside the fourteenth day of the month of Adar as a holiday for happiness
Esther9:20 Mordecai wrote these matters down and sent letters to all the Jews who were throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus
Esther9:21 to have them observe the fourteenth and the fifteenth day of the month of Adar each year
Esther9:22 as the time when the Jews gave themselves rest from their enemies — the month when their trouble was turned to happiness and their mourning to a holiday. These were to be days of banqueting
Esther9:23 So the Jews committed themselves to continue what they had begun to do and to what Mordecai had written to them.

Esther9:25 But when the matter came to the king’s attention

Esther9:27 Therefore
Esther9:28 These days were to be remembered and to be celebrated in every generation and in every family
Esther9:29 So Queen Esther

Esther9:31 to establish these days of Purim in their proper times
Esther9:32 Esther’s command established these matters of Purim
