Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job. And that man was pure and upright
Job1:2 Seven sons and three daughters were born to him.
Job1:3 His possessions included 7
Job1:4 Now his sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one in turn
Job1:5 When the days of their feasting were finished
Job1:6 Now the day came when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD — and Satan also arrived among them.
Job1:7 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:8 So the LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Then Satan answered the LORD
Job1:10 Have you not made a hedge around him and his household and all that he has on every side? You have blessed the work of his hands
Job1:11 But extend your hand and strike everything he has
Job1:12 So the LORD said to Satan
Job1:13 Now the day came when Job’s sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house
Job1:14 and a messenger came to Job
Job1:15 and the Sabeans swooped down and carried them all away
Job1:16 While this one was still speaking
Job1:17 While this one was still speaking another messenger arrived and said
Job1:18 While this one was still speaking another messenger arrived and said
Job1:19 and suddenly a great wind swept across the wilderness and struck the four corners of the house
Job1:20 Then Job got up and tore his robe. He shaved his head
Job1:21 He said
Job1:22 In all this Job did not sin
Job2:1 Again the day came when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job2:2 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:3 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job2:4 But Satan answered the LORD
Job2:5 But extend your hand and strike his bone and his flesh
Job2:6 So the LORD said to Satan
Job2:7 So Satan went out from the presence of the LORD
Job2:8 Job took a shard of broken pottery to scrape himself with while he was sitting among the ashes.
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he replied
Job2:11 When Job’s three friends heard about all this calamity that had happened to him
Job2:12 But when they gazed intently from a distance but did not recognize him
Job2:13 Then they sat down with him on the ground for seven days and seven nights
Job3:1 After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day he was born.
Job3:2 Job spoke up and said:
Job3:3 “Let the day on which I was born perish
Job3:4 That day — let it be darkness; let not God on high regard it
Job3:5 Let darkness and the deepest shadow claim it; let a cloud settle on it; let whatever blackens the day terrify it!
Job3:6 That night — let darkness seize it; let it not be included among the days of the year; let it not enter among the number of the months!
Job3:7 Indeed
Job3:8 Let those who curse the day curse it — those who are prepared to rouse Leviathan.
Job3:9 Let its morning stars be darkened; let it wait for daylight but find none
Job3:10 because it did not shut the doors of my mother’s womb on me
Job3:11 “Why did I not die at birth
Job3:12 Why did the knees welcome me
Job3:13 For now I would be lying down and would be quiet
Job3:14 with kings and counselors of the earth who built for themselves places now desolate
Job3:15 or with princes who possessed gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not buried like a stillborn infant
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from turmoil
Job3:18 There the prisoners relax together; they do not hear the voice of the oppressor.
Job3:19 Small and great are there
Job3:20 “Why does God give light to one who is in misery
Job3:21 to those who wait for death that does not come
Job3:22 who rejoice even to jubilation
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my sighing comes in place of my food
Job3:25 For the very thing I dreaded has happened to me
Job3:26 I have no ease
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job4:2 “If someone should attempt a word with you
Job4:3 Look
Job4:4 Your words have supported those who stumbled
Job4:5 But now the same thing comes to you
Job4:6 Is not your piety your confidence
Job4:7 Call to mind now: Who
Job4:8 Even as I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 There is the roaring of the lion and the growling of the young lion
Job4:11 The mighty lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 “Now a word was secretly brought to me
Job4:13 In the troubling thoughts of the dreams in the night when a deep sleep falls on men
Job4:14 a trembling gripped me — and a terror! — and made all my bones shake.
Job4:15 Then a breath of air passes by my face; it makes the hair of my flesh stand up.
Job4:16 It stands still
Job4:17 “Is a mortal man righteous before God? Or a man pure before his Creator?
Job4:18 If God puts no trust in his servants and attributes folly to his angels
Job4:19 how much more to those who live in houses of clay
Job4:20 They are destroyed between morning and evening; they perish forever without anyone regarding it.
Job4:21 Is not their excess wealth taken away from them? They die
Job5:1 “Call now! Is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
Job5:2 For wrath kills the foolish person
Job5:3 I myself have seen the fool taking root
Job5:4 His children are far from safety
Job5:5 The hungry eat up his harvest
Job5:6 For evil does not come up from the dust
Job5:7 but people are born to trouble
Job5:8 “But as for me
Job5:9 He does great and unsearchable things
Job5:10 he gives rain on the earth
Job5:11 he sets the lowly on high
Job5:12 He frustrates the plans of the crafty so that their hands cannot accomplish what they had planned!
Job5:13 He catches the wise in their own craftiness
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the daytime
Job5:15 So he saves from the sword that comes from their mouth
Job5:16 Thus the poor have hope
Job5:17 “Therefore
Job5:18 For he wounds
Job5:19 He will deliver you from six calamities; yes
Job5:20 In time of famine he will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You will be protected from malicious gossip
Job5:22 You will laugh at destruction and famine and need not be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
Job5:23 For you will have a pact with the stones of the field
Job5:24 And you will know that your home will be secure
Job5:25 You will also know that your children will be numerous
Job5:26 You will come to your grave in a full age
Job5:27 Look
Job6:1 Then Job responded:
Job6:2 “Oh
Job6:3 But because it is heavier than the sand of the sea
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me; my spirit drinks their poison; God’s sudden terrors are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 “Does the wild donkey bray when it is near grass? Or does the ox low near its fodder?
Job6:6 Can food that is tasteless be eaten without salt? Or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 I have refused to touch such things; they are like loathsome food to me.
Job6:8 “Oh that my request would be realized
Job6:9 And that God would be willing to crush me
Job6:10 Then I would yet have my comfort
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength like that of stones? or is my flesh made of bronze?
Job6:13 Is not my power to help myself nothing
Job6:14 “To the one in despair
Job6:15 My brothers have been as treacherous as a seasonal stream
Job6:16 They are dark because of ice; snow is piled up over them.
Job6:17 When they are scorched
Job6:18 Caravans turn aside from their routes; they go into the wasteland and perish.
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema looked intently for these streams; the traveling merchants of Sheba hoped for them.
Job6:20 They were distressed
Job6:21 For now you have become like these streams that are no help; you see a terror
Job6:22 “Have I ever said
Job6:23 Or ‘Deliver me from the enemy’s power
Job6:24 “Teach me and I
Job6:25 How painful are honest words! But what does your reproof prove?
Job6:26 Do you intend to criticize mere words
Job6:27 Yes
Job6:28 “Now then
Job6:29 Relent
Job6:30 Is there any falsehood on my lips? Can my mouth not discern evil things?
Job7:1 “Does not humanity have hard service on earth? Are not their days also like the days of a hired man?
Job7:2 Like a servant longing for the evening shadow
Job7:3 thus I have been made to inherit months of futility
Job7:4 If I lie down
Job7:5 My body is clothed with worms and dirty scabs; my skin is broken and festering.
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle and they come to an end without hope.
Job7:7 Remember that my life is but a breath
Job7:8 The eye of him who sees me now will see me no more; your eyes will look for me
Job7:9 As a cloud is dispersed and then disappears
Job7:10 He returns no more to his house
Job7:11 “Therefore
Job7:12 Am I the sea
Job7:13 If I say
Job7:14 then you scare me with dreams and terrify me with visions
Job7:15 so that I would prefer strangling
Job7:16 I loathe it; I do not want to live forever; leave me alone
Job7:17 “What is mankind that you make so much of them
Job7:18 And that you visit them every morning
Job7:19 Will you never look away from me
Job7:20 If I have sinned — what have I done to you
Job7:21 And why do you not pardon my transgression
Job8:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite spoke up and said:
Job8:2 “How long will you speak these things
Job8:3 Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert what is right?
Job8:4 If your children sinned against him
Job8:5 But if you will look to God
Job8:6 if you become pure and upright
Job8:7 Your beginning will seem so small
Job8:8 “For inquire now of the former generation
Job8:9 For we were born yesterday and do not have knowledge
Job8:10 Will they not instruct you and speak to you
Job8:11 Can the papyrus plant grow tall where there is no marsh? Can reeds flourish without water?
Job8:12 While they are still beginning to flower and not ripe for cutting
Job8:13 Such is the destiny of all who forget God; the hope of the godless perishes
Job8:14 whose trust is in something futile
Job8:15 He leans against his house but it does not hold up
Job8:16 He is a well-watered plant in the sun
Job8:17 It wraps its roots around a heap of stones and it looks for a place among stones.
Job8:18 If he is uprooted from his place
Job8:19 Indeed
Job8:20 “Surely
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter
Job8:22 Those who hate you will be clothed with shame
Job9:1 Then Job answered:
Job9:2 “Truly
Job9:3 If someone wishes to contend with him
Job9:4 He is wise in heart and mighty in strength — who has resisted him and remained safe?
Job9:5 He who removes mountains suddenly
Job9:6 he who shakes the earth out of its place so that its pillars tremble;
Job9:7 he who commands the sun and it does not shine and seals up the stars;
Job9:8 he alone spreads out the heavens
Job9:9 he makes the Bear
Job9:10 he does great and unsearchable things
Job9:11 If he passes by me
Job9:12 If he snatches away
Job9:13 God does not restrain his anger; under him the helpers of Rahab lie crushed.
Job9:14 “How much less
Job9:15 Although I am innocent
Job9:16 If I summoned him
Job9:17 he who crushes me with a tempest
Job9:18 He does not allow me to recover my breath
Job9:19 If it is a matter of strength
Job9:20 Although I am innocent
Job9:21 I am blameless. I do not know myself. I despise my life.
Job9:22 “It is all one! That is why I say
Job9:23 If a scourge brings sudden death
Job9:24 If a land has been given into the hand of a wicked man
Job9:25 “My days are swifter than a runner
Job9:26 They glide by like reed boats
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I dread all my sufferings
Job9:29 If I am guilty
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow water
Job9:31 then you plunge me into a slimy pit and my own clothes abhor me.
Job9:32 For he is not a human being like I am
Job9:33 Nor is there an arbiter between us
Job9:34 who would take his rod away from me so that his terror would not make me afraid.
Job9:35 Then would I speak and not fear him
Job10:1 “I am weary of my life; I will complain without restraint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God
Job10:3 Is it good for you to oppress
Job10:4 “Do you have eyes of flesh
Job10:5 Are your days like the days of a mortal
Job10:6 that you must search out my iniquity
Job10:7 although you know that I am not guilty
Job10:8 “Your hands have shaped me and made me
Job10:9 Remember that you have made me as with the clay; will you return me to dust?
Job10:10 Did you not pour me out like milk
Job10:11 You clothed me with skin and flesh and knit me together with bones and sinews.
Job10:12 You gave me life and favor
Job10:13 “But these things you have concealed in your heart; I know that this is with you:
Job10:14 If I sinned
Job10:15 If I am guilty
Job10:16 If I lift myself up
Job10:17 You bring new witnesses against me
Job10:18 “Why then did you bring me out from the womb? I should have died and no eye would have seen me!
Job10:19 I should have been as though I had never existed; I should have been carried right from the womb to the grave!
Job10:20 Are not my days few? Cease
Job10:21 before I depart
Job10:22 to the land of utter darkness
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite spoke up and said:
Job11:2 “Should not this abundance of words be answered
Job11:3 Will your idle talk reduce people to silence
Job11:4 For you have said
Job11:5 But if only God would speak
Job11:6 and reveal to you the secrets of wisdom — for true wisdom has two sides — so that you would know that God has forgiven some of your sins.
Job11:7 “Can you discover the essence of God? Can you find out the perfection of the Almighty?
Job11:8 It is higher than the heavens — what can you do? It is deeper than Sheol — what can you know?
Job11:9 Its measure is longer than the earth
Job11:10 If he comes by and confines you and convenes a court
Job11:11 For he knows deceitful men; when he sees evil
Job11:12 But an empty man will become wise
Job11:13 “As for you
Job11:14 if iniquity is in your hand — put it far away
Job11:15 For then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be securely established and will not fear.
Job11:16 For you will forget your trouble; you will remember it like water that has flowed away.
Job11:17 And life will be brighter than the noonday; though there be darkness
Job11:18 And you will be secure
Job11:19 You will lie down with no one to make you afraid
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked fail
Job12:1 Then Job answered:
Job12:2 “Without a doubt you are the people
Job12:3 I also have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
Job12:4 I am a laughingstock to my friends
Job12:5 For calamity
Job12:6 But the tents of robbers are peaceful
Job12:7 “But now
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth and it will teach you
Job12:9 Which of all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this
Job12:10 in whose hand is the life of every creature and the breath of all the human race.
Job12:11 Does not the ear test words
Job12:12 Is not wisdom found among the aged? Does not long life bring understanding?
Job12:13 “With God are wisdom and power; counsel and understanding are his.
Job12:14 If he tears down
Job12:15 If he holds back the waters
Job12:16 With him are strength and prudence; both the one who goes astray and the one who misleads are his.
Job12:17 He leads counselors away stripped and makes judges into fools.
Job12:18 He loosens the bonds of kings and binds a loincloth around their waist.
Job12:19 He leads priests away stripped and overthrows the potentates.
Job12:20 He deprives the trusted advisers of speech and takes away the discernment of elders.
Job12:21 He pours contempt on noblemen and disarms the powerful.
Job12:22 He reveals the deep things of darkness
Job12:23 He makes nations great
Job12:24 He deprives the leaders of the earth of their understanding; he makes them wander in a trackless desert waste.
Job12:25 They grope about in darkness without light; he makes them stagger like drunkards.
Job13:1 “Indeed
Job13:2 What you know
Job13:3 But I wish to speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But you
Job13:5 If only you would keep completely silent! For you
Job13:6 “Listen now to my argument
Job13:7 Will you speak wickedly on God’s behalf? Will you speak deceitfully for him?
Job13:8 Will you show him partiality? Will you argue the case for God?
Job13:9 Would it turn out well if he would examine you? Or as one deceives a man would you deceive him?
Job13:10 He would certainly rebuke you if you secretly showed partiality!
Job13:11 Would not his splendor terrify you and the fear he inspires fall on you?
Job13:12 Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
Job13:13 “Refrain from talking with me so that I may speak; then let come to me what may.
Job13:14 Why do I put myself in peril
Job13:15 Even if he slays me
Job13:16 Moreover
Job13:17 Listen carefully to my words; let your ears be attentive to my explanation.
Job13:18 See now
Job13:19 Who will contend with me? If anyone can
Job13:20 Only in two things spare me
Job13:21 Remove your hand far from me and stop making me afraid with your terror.
Job13:22 Then call
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and sins? Show me my transgression and my sin.
Job13:24 Why do you hide your face and regard me as your enemy?
Job13:25 Do you wish to torment a windblown leaf and chase after dry chaff?
Job13:26 For you write down bitter things against me and cause me to inherit the sins of my youth.
Job13:27 And you put my feet in the stocks and you watch all my movements; you put marks on the soles of my feet.
Job13:28 So I waste away like something rotten
Job14:1 “Man
Job14:2 He grows up like a flower and then withers away; he flees like a shadow
Job14:3 Do you fix your eye on such a one? And do you bring me before you for judgment?
Job14:4 Who can make a clean thing come from an unclean? No one!
Job14:5 Since man’s days are determined
Job14:6 Look away from him and let him desist
Job14:7 “But there is hope for a tree: If it is cut down
Job14:8 Although its roots may grow old in the ground and its stump begins to die in the soil
Job14:9 at the scent of water it will flourish and put forth shoots like a new plant.
Job14:10 But man dies and is powerless; he expires — and where is he?
Job14:11 As water disappears from the sea
Job14:12 so man lies down and does not rise; until the heavens are no more
Job14:13 “O that you would hide me in Sheol
Job14:14 If a man dies
Job14:15 You will call and I — I will answer you; you will long for the creature you have made.
Job14:16 “Surely now you count my steps; then you would not mark my sin.
Job14:17 My offenses would be sealed up in a bag; you would cover over my sin.
Job14:18 But as a mountain falls away and crumbles
Job14:19 as water wears away stones
Job14:20 You overpower him once for all
Job14:21 If his sons are honored
Job14:22 Only his flesh has pain for himself
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job15:2 “Does a wise man answer with blustery knowledge
Job15:3 Does he argue with useless talk
Job15:4 But you even break off piety
Job15:5 Your sin inspires your mouth; you choose the language of the crafty.
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 “Were you the first man ever born? Were you brought forth before the hills?
Job15:8 Do you listen in on God’s secret council? Do you limit wisdom to yourself?
Job15:9 What do you know that we don’t know? What do you understand that we don’t understand?
Job15:10 The gray-haired and the aged are on our side
Job15:11 Are God’s consolations too trivial for you; or a word spoken in gentleness to you?
Job15:12 Why has your heart carried you away
Job15:13 when you turn your rage against God and allow such words to escape from your mouth?
Job15:14 What is man that he should be pure
Job15:15 If God places no trust in his holy ones
Job15:16 how much less man
Job15:17 “I will explain to you; listen to me
Job15:18 what wise men declare
Job15:19 to whom alone the land was given when no foreigner passed among them.
Job15:20 All his days the wicked man suffers torment
Job15:21 Terrifying sounds fill his ears; in a time of peace marauders attack him.
Job15:22 He does not expect to escape from darkness; he is marked for the sword;
Job15:23 he wanders about — food for vultures; he knows that the day of darkness is at hand.
Job15:24 Distress and anguish terrify him; they prevail against him like a king ready to launch an attack
Job15:25 for he stretches out his hand against God
Job15:26 defiantly charging against him with a thick
Job15:27 Because he covered his face with fat
Job15:28 he lived in ruined towns and in houses where no one lives
Job15:29 He will not grow rich
Job15:30 He will not escape the darkness; a flame will wither his shoots and he will depart by the breath of God’s mouth.
Job15:31 Let him not trust in what is worthless
Job15:32 Before his time he will be paid in full
Job15:33 Like a vine he will let his sour grapes fall
Job15:34 For the company of the godless is barren
Job15:35 They conceive trouble and bring forth evil; their belly prepares deception.”
Job16:1 Then Job replied:
Job16:2 “I have heard many things like these before. What miserable comforters are you all!
Job16:3 Will there be an end to your windy words? Or what provokes you that you answer?
Job16:4 I also could speak like you
Job16:5 But I would strengthen you with my words; comfort from my lips would bring you relief.
Job16:6 “But if I speak
Job16:7 Surely now he has worn me out
Job16:8 You have seized me
Job16:9 His anger has torn me and persecuted me; he has gnashed at me with his teeth; my adversary locks his eyes on me.
Job16:10 People have opened their mouths against me
Job16:11 God abandons me to evil men
Job16:12 I was in peace
Job16:13 his archers surround me. Without pity he pierces my kidneys and pours out my gall on the ground.
Job16:14 He breaks through against me
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth on my skin
Job16:16 my face is reddened because of weeping
Job16:17 although there is no violence in my hands and my prayer is pure.
Job16:18 “O earth
Job16:19 Even now my witness is in heaven; my advocate is on high.
Job16:20 My intercessor is my friend as my eyes pour out tears to God;
Job16:21 and he contends with God on behalf of man as a man pleads for his friend.
Job16:22 For the years that lie ahead are few
Job17:1 My spirit is broken
Job17:2 Surely mockery is with me; my eyes must dwell on their hostility.
Job17:3 Make then my pledge with you. Who else will put up security for me?
Job17:4 Because you have closed their minds to understanding
Job17:5 If a man denounces his friends for personal gain
Job17:6 He has made me a byword to people
Job17:7 My eyes have grown dim with grief; my whole frame is but a shadow.
Job17:8 Upright men are appalled at this; the innocent man is troubled with the godless.
Job17:9 But the righteous man holds to his way
Job17:10 “But turn
Job17:11 My days have passed
Job17:12 These men change night into day; they say
Job17:13 If I hope for the grave to be my home
Job17:14 If I cry to corruption
Job17:15 where then is my hope? And my hope
Job17:16 Will it go down to the barred gates of death? Will we descend together into the dust?”
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
Job18:2 “How long until you make an end of words? You must consider
Job18:3 Why should we be regarded as beasts
Job18:4 You who tear yourself to pieces in your anger
Job18:5 “Yes
Job18:6 The light in his tent grows dark; his lamp above him is extinguished.
Job18:7 His vigorous steps are restricted
Job18:8 For he has been thrown into a net by his feet and he wanders into a mesh.
Job18:9 A trap seizes him by the heel; a snare grips him.
Job18:10 A rope is hidden for him on the ground and a trap for him lies on the path.
Job18:11 Terrors frighten him on all sides and dog his every step.
Job18:12 Calamity is hungry for him
Job18:13 It eats away parts of his skin; the most terrible death devours his limbs.
Job18:14 He is dragged from the security of his tent
Job18:15 Fire resides in his tent; over his residence burning sulfur is scattered.
Job18:16 Below his roots dry up
Job18:17 His memory perishes from the earth
Job18:18 He is driven from light into darkness and is banished from the world.
Job18:19 He has neither children nor descendants among his people
Job18:20 People of the west are appalled at his fate; people of the east are seized with horror
Job18:21 ‘Surely such is the residence of an evil man; and this is the place of one who has not known God.’”
Job19:1 Then Job answered:
Job19:2 “How long will you torment me and crush me with your words?
Job19:3 These ten times you have been reproaching me; you are not ashamed to attack me!
Job19:4 But even if it were true that I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed you would exalt yourselves above me and plead my disgrace against me
Job19:6 know then that God has wronged me and encircled me with his net.
Job19:7 “If I cry out
Job19:8 He has blocked my way so I cannot pass
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my honor and has taken the crown off my head.
Job19:10 He tears me down on every side until I perish; he uproots my hope like one uproots a tree.
Job19:11 Thus his anger burns against me
Job19:12 His troops advance together; they throw up a siege ramp against me
Job19:13 “He has put my relatives far from me; my acquaintances only turn away from me.
Job19:14 My kinsmen have failed me; my friends have forgotten me.
Job19:15 My guests and my servant girls consider me a stranger; I am a foreigner in their eyes.
Job19:16 I summon my servant
Job19:17 My breath is repulsive to my wife; I am loathsome to my brothers.
Job19:18 Even youngsters have scorned me; when I get up
Job19:19 All my closest friends detest me; and those whom I love have turned against me.
Job19:20 My bones stick to my skin and my flesh; I have escaped alive with only the skin of my teeth.
Job19:21 Have pity on me
Job19:22 Why do you pursue me like God does? Will you never be satiated with my flesh?

Job19:25 As for me
Job19:26 And after my skin has been destroyed
Job19:27 whom I will see for myself

Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
Job20:2 “This is why my troubled thoughts bring me back — because of my feelings within me.
Job20:3 When I hear a reproof that dishonors me
Job20:4 “Surely you know that it has been from old
Job20:5 that the elation of the wicked is brief
Job20:6 Even though his stature reaches to the heavens and his head touches the clouds
Job20:7 he will perish forever
Job20:8 Like a dream he flies away
Job20:9 People who had seen him will not see him again
Job20:10 His sons must recompense the poor; his own hands must return his wealth.
Job20:11 His bones were full of his youthful vigor
Job20:12 “If evil is sweet in his mouth and he hides it under his tongue
Job20:13 if he retains it for himself and does not let it go
Job20:14 his food is turned sour in his stomach; it becomes the venom of serpents within him.
Job20:15 The wealth that he consumed he vomits up
Job20:16 He sucks the poison of serpents; the fangs of a viper kill him.
Job20:17 He will not look on the streams
Job20:18 He gives back the ill-gotten gain without assimilating it; he will not enjoy the wealth from his commerce.
Job20:19 For he has oppressed the poor and abandoned them; he has seized a house which he did not build.
Job20:20 For he knows no satisfaction in his appetite; he does not let anything he desires escape.
Job20:21 “Nothing is left for him to devour; that is why his prosperity does not last.
Job20:22 In the fullness of his sufficiency
Job20:23 “While he is filling his belly
Job20:24 If he flees from an iron weapon
Job20:25 When he pulls it out and it comes out of his back
Job20:26 Total darkness waits to receive his treasures; a fire which has not been kindled will consume him and devour what is left in his tent.
Job20:27 The heavens reveal his iniquity; the earth rises up against him.
Job20:28 A flood will carry off his house
Job20:29 Such is the lot God allots the wicked
Job21:1 Then Job answered:
Job21:2 “Listen carefully to my words; let this be the consolation you offer me.
Job21:3 Bear with me and I will speak
Job21:4 Is my complaint against a man? If so
Job21:5 Look at me and be appalled; put your hands over your mouths.
Job21:6 For
Job21:7 “Why do the wicked go on living
Job21:8 Their children are firmly established in their presence
Job21:9 Their houses are safe and without fear; and no rod of punishment from God is upon them.
Job21:10 Their bulls breed without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
Job21:11 They allow their children to run like a flock; their little ones dance about.
Job21:12 They sing to the accompaniment of tambourine and harp
Job21:13 They live out their years in prosperity and go down to the grave in peace.
Job21:14 So they say to God
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 But their prosperity is not their own doing. The counsel of the wicked is far from me!
Job21:17 “How often is the lamp of the wicked extinguished? How often does their misfortune come upon them? How often does God apportion pain to them in his anger?
Job21:18 How often are they like straw before the wind
Job21:19 You may say
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see his destruction; let him drink of the anger of the Almighty.
Job21:21 For what is his interest in his home after his death
Job21:22 Can anyone teach God knowledge
Job21:23 “One man dies in his full vigor
Job21:24 his body well nourished
Job21:25 And another man dies in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 Together they lie down in the dust
Job21:27 “Yes
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you never questioned those who travel the roads? Do you not recognize their accounts —
Job21:30 that the evil man is spared from the day of his misfortune
Job21:31 No one denounces his conduct to his face; no one repays him for what he has done.
Job21:32 And when he is carried to the tombs
Job21:33 The clods of the torrent valley are sweet to him; behind him everybody follows in procession
Job21:34 So how can you console me with your futile words? Nothing is left of your answers but deception!”
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job22:2 “Is it to God that a strong man is of benefit? Is it to him that even a wise man is profitable?
Job22:3 Is it of any special benefit to the Almighty that you should be righteous
Job22:4 Is it because of your piety that he rebukes you and goes to judgment with you?
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great and is there no end to your iniquity?
Job22:6 “For you took pledges from your brothers for no reason
Job22:7 You gave the weary no water to drink and from the hungry you withheld food.
Job22:8 Although you were a powerful man
Job22:9 you sent widows away empty-handed
Job22:10 That is why snares surround you
Job22:11 why it is so dark you cannot see
Job22:12 “Is not God on high in heaven? And see the lofty stars
Job22:13 But you have said
Job22:14 Thick clouds are a veil for him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the old path that evil men have walked —
Job22:16 men who were carried off before their time
Job22:17 They were saying to God
Job22:18 But it was he who filled their houses with good things — yet the counsel of the wicked was far from me.
Job22:19 The righteous see their destruction and rejoice; the innocent mock them scornfully
Job22:20 ‘Surely our enemies are destroyed
Job22:21 “Reconcile yourself with God
Job22:22 Accept instruction from his mouth and store up his words in your heart.
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 and throw your gold in the dust — your gold of Ophir among the rocks in the ravines —
Job22:25 then the Almighty himself will be your gold
Job22:26 Surely then you will delight yourself in the Almighty
Job22:27 You will pray to him and he will hear you
Job22:28 Whatever you decide on a matter
Job22:29 When people are brought low and you say ‘Lift them up!’ then he will save the downcast;
Job22:30 he will deliver even someone who is not innocent
Job23:1 Then Job answered:
Job23:2 “Even today my complaint is still bitter; his hand is heavy despite my groaning.
Job23:3 O that I knew where I might find him
Job23:4 I would lay out my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would know with what words he would answer me
Job23:6 Would he contend with me with great power? No
Job23:7 There an upright person could present his case before him
Job23:8 “If I go to the east
Job23:9 In the north when he is at work
Job23:10 But he knows the pathway that I take; if he tested me
Job23:11 My feet have followed his steps closely; I have kept to his way and have not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commands of his lips; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my allotted portion.
Job23:13 But he is unchangeable
Job23:14 For he fulfills his decree against me
Job23:15 That is why I am terrified in his presence; when I consider
Job23:16 Indeed
Job23:17 Yet I have not been silent because of the darkness
Job24:1 “Why are times not appointed by the Almighty? Why do those who know him not see his days?
Job24:2 Men move boundary stones; they seize the flock and pasture them.
Job24:3 They drive away the orphan’s donkey; they take the widow’s ox as a pledge.
Job24:4 They turn the needy from the pathway
Job24:5 Like wild donkeys in the desert they go out to their labor
Job24:6 They reap fodder in the field
Job24:7 They spend the night naked because they lack clothing; they have no covering against the cold.
Job24:8 They are soaked by mountain rains and huddle in the rocks because they lack shelter.
Job24:9 The fatherless child is snatched from the breast
Job24:10 They go about naked
Job24:11 They press out the olive oil between the rows of olive trees; they tread the winepresses while they are thirsty.
Job24:12 From the city the dying groan
Job24:13 There are those who rebel against the light; they do not know its ways and they do not stay on its paths.
Job24:14 Before daybreak the murderer rises up; he kills the poor and the needy; in the night he is like a thief.
Job24:15 And the eye of the adulterer watches for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark the robber breaks into houses
Job24:17 For all of them
Job24:18 “You say
Job24:19 The drought as well as the heat carry away the melted snow; so the grave takes away those who have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb forgets him
Job24:21 He preys on the barren and childless woman
Job24:22 But God drags off the mighty by his power; when God rises up against him
Job24:23 God may let them rest in a feeling of security
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little while
Job24:25 “If this is not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
Job25:2 “Dominion and awesome might belong to God; he establishes peace in his heights.
Job25:3 Can his armies be numbered? On whom does his light not rise?
Job25:4 How then can a human being be righteous before God? How can one born of a woman be pure?
Job25:5 If even the moon is not bright
Job25:6 how much less a mortal man
Job26:1 Then Job replied:
Job26:2 “How you have helped the powerless! How you have saved the person who has no strength!
Job26:3 How you have advised the one without wisdom
Job26:4 To whom did you utter these words? And whose spirit has come forth from your mouth?
Job26:5 “The dead tremble — those beneath the waters and all that live in them.
Job26:6 The underworld is naked before God; the place of destruction lies uncovered.
Job26:7 He spreads out the northern skies over empty space; he suspends the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He locks the waters in his clouds
Job26:9 He conceals the face of the full moon
Job26:10 He marks out the horizon on the surface of the waters as a boundary between light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars of the heavens tremble and are amazed at his rebuke.
Job26:12 By his power he stills the sea; by his wisdom he cut Rahab the great sea monster to pieces.
Job26:13 By his breath the skies became fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Indeed
Job27:1 And Job took up his discourse again:
Job27:2 “As surely as God lives
Job27:3 for while my spirit is still in me
Job27:4 my lips will not speak wickedness
Job27:5 I will never declare that you three are in the right; until I die
Job27:6 I will maintain my righteousness and never let it go; my conscience will not reproach me for as long as I live.
Job27:7 “May my enemy be like the wicked
Job27:8 For what hope does the godless have when he is cut off
Job27:9 Does God listen to his cry when distress overtakes him?
Job27:10 Will he find delight in the Almighty? Will he call out to God at all times?
Job27:11 I will teach you about the power of God; What is on the Almighty’s mind I will not conceal.
Job27:12 If you yourselves have all seen this
Job27:13 This is the portion of the wicked man allotted by God
Job27:14 If his children increase — it is for the sword! His offspring never have enough to eat.
Job27:15 Those who survive him are buried by the plague
Job27:16 If he piles up silver like dust and stores up clothing like mounds of clay
Job27:17 what he stores up a righteous man will wear
Job27:18 The house he builds is as fragile as a moth’s cocoon
Job27:19 He goes to bed wealthy
Job27:20 Terrors overwhelm him like a flood; at night a whirlwind carries him off.
Job27:21 The east wind carries him away
Job27:22 It hurls itself against him without pity as he flees headlong from its power.
Job27:23 It claps its hands at him in derision and hisses him away from his place.
Job28:1 “Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the ground
Job28:3 Man puts an end to the darkness; he searches the farthest recesses for the ore in the deepest darkness.
Job28:4 Far from where people live he sinks a shaft
Job28:5 The earth
Job28:6 a place whose stones are sapphires and which contains dust of gold;
Job28:7 a hidden path no bird of prey knows — no falcon’s eye has spotted it.
Job28:8 Proud beasts have not set foot on it
Job28:9 On the flinty rock man has set to work with his hand; he has overturned mountains at their bases.
Job28:10 He has cut out channels through the rocks; his eyes have spotted every precious thing.
Job28:11 He has searched the sources of the rivers and what was hidden he has brought into the light.
Job28:12 “But wisdom — where can it be found? Where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Mankind does not know its place; it cannot be found in the land of the living.
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 Fine gold cannot be given in exchange for it
Job28:16 It cannot be measured out for purchase with the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Neither gold nor crystal can be compared with it
Job28:18 Of coral and jasper no mention will be made; the price of wisdom is more than pearls.
Job28:19 The topaz of Cush cannot be compared with it; it cannot be purchased with pure gold.
Job28:20 “But wisdom — where does it come from? Where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 For it has been hidden from the eyes of every living creature
Job28:22 Destruction and Death say
Job28:23 God understands the way to it
Job28:24 For he looks to the ends of the earth and observes everything under the heavens.
Job28:25 When he made the force of the wind and measured the waters with a gauge.
Job28:26 When he imposed a limit for the rain
Job28:27 then he looked at wisdom and assessed its value; he established it and examined it closely.
Job28:28 And he said to mankind
Job29:1 Then Job continued his speech:
Job29:2 “O that I could be as I was in the months now gone
Job29:3 when he caused his lamp to shine upon my head
Job29:4 just as I was in my most productive time
Job29:5 when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me;
Job29:6 when my steps were bathed with butter and the rock poured out for me streams of olive oil!
Job29:7 When I went out to the city gate and secured my seat in the public square
Job29:8 the young men would see me and step aside
Job29:9 the chief men refrained from talking and covered their mouths with their hands;
Job29:10 the voices of the nobles fell silent
Job29:11 “As soon as the ear heard these things
Job29:12 for I rescued the poor who cried out for help
Job29:13 the blessing of the dying man descended on me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness and it clothed me
Job29:15 I was eyes for the blind and feet for the lame;
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 I broke the fangs of the wicked
Job29:18 “Then I thought
Job29:19 My roots reach the water
Job29:20 My glory will always be fresh in me
Job29:21 “People listened to me and waited silently; they kept silent for my advice.
Job29:22 After I had spoken
Job29:23 They waited for me as people wait for the rain
Job29:24 If I smiled at them
Job29:25 I chose the way for them and sat as their chief; I lived like a king among his troops; I was like one who comforts mourners.
Job30:1 “But now they mock me
Job30:2 Moreover
Job30:3 gaunt with want and hunger
Job30:4 By the brush they would gather herbs from the salt marshes
Job30:5 They were banished from the community — people shouted at them like they would shout at thieves —
Job30:6 so that they had to live in the dry stream beds
Job30:7 They brayed like animals among the bushes and were huddled together under the nettles.
Job30:8 Sons of senseless and nameless people
Job30:9 “And now I have become their taunt song; I have become a byword among them.
Job30:10 They detest me and maintain their distance; they do not hesitate to spit in my face.
Job30:11 Because God has untied my tent cord and afflicted me
Job30:12 On my right the young rabble rise up; they drive me from place to place
Job30:13 They destroy my path; they succeed in destroying me without anyone assisting them.
Job30:14 They come in as through a wide breach; amid the crash they come rolling in.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned loose on me; they drive away my honor like the wind
Job30:16 “And now my soul pours itself out within me; days of suffering take hold of me.
Job30:17 Night pierces my bones; my gnawing pains never cease.
Job30:18 With great power God grasps my clothing; he binds me like the collar of my tunic.
Job30:19 He has flung me into the mud
Job30:20 I cry out to you
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me; with the strength of your hand you attack me.
Job30:22 You pick me up on the wind and make me ride on it; you toss me about in the storm.
Job30:23 I know that you are bringing me to death
Job30:24 “Surely one does not stretch out his hand against a broken man when he cries for help in his distress.
Job30:25 Have I not wept for the unfortunate? Was not my soul grieved for the poor?
Job30:26 But when I hoped for good
Job30:27 My heart is in turmoil unceasingly; the days of my affliction confront me.
Job30:28 I go about blackened
Job30:29 I have become a brother to jackals and a companion of ostriches.
Job30:30 My skin has turned dark on me; my body is hot with fever.
Job30:31 My harp is used for mourning and my flute for the sound of weeping.
Job31:1 “I made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I entertain thoughts against a virgin?
Job31:2 What then would be one’s lot from God above
Job31:3 Is it not misfortune for the unjust
Job31:4 Does he not see my ways and count all my steps?
Job31:5 If I have walked in falsehood
Job31:6 let him weigh me with honest scales; then God will discover my integrity.
Job31:7 If my footsteps have strayed from the way
Job31:8 then let me sow and let another eat
Job31:9 If my heart has been enticed by a woman
Job31:10 then let my wife turn the millstone for another man
Job31:11 For I would have committed a shameful act
Job31:12 For it is a fire that devours even to Destruction
Job31:13 “If I have disregarded the right of my male servants or my female servants when they disputed with me
Job31:14 then what will I do when God confronts me in judgment; when he intervenes
Job31:15 Did not the one who made me in the womb make them? Did not the same one form us in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have refused to give the poor what they desired
Job31:17 If I ate my morsel of bread myself
Job31:18 but from my youth I raised the orphan like a father
Job31:19 If I have seen anyone about to perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 whose heart did not bless me as he warmed himself with the fleece of my sheep
Job31:21 if I have raised my hand to vote against the orphan
Job31:22 then let my arm fall from the shoulder
Job31:23 For the calamity from God was a terror to me
Job31:24 “If I have put my confidence in gold or said to pure gold
Job31:25 if I have rejoiced because of the extent of my wealth
Job31:26 if I looked at the sun when it was shining
Job31:27 so that my heart was secretly enticed
Job31:28 then this also would be iniquity to be judged
Job31:29 If I have rejoiced over the misfortune of my enemy or exulted because calamity found him —
Job31:30 I have not even permitted my mouth to sin by asking for his life through a curse —
Job31:31 if the members of my household have never said
Job31:32 But no stranger had to spend the night outside
Job31:33 if I have covered my transgressions as men do
Job31:34 because I was terrified of the great multitude
Job31:35 “If only I had someone to hear me! Here is my signature — let the Almighty answer me! If only I had an indictment that my accuser had written.
Job31:36 Surely I would wear it proudly on my shoulder
Job31:37 I would give him an accounting of my steps; like a prince I would approach him.
Job31:38 “If my land cried out against me and all its furrows wept together
Job31:39 if I have eaten its produce without paying
Job31:40 then let thorns sprout up in place of wheat
Job32:1 So these three men refused to answer Job further
Job32:2 Then Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite
Job32:3 With Job’s three friends he was also angry
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited before speaking to Job
Job32:5 But when Elihu saw that the three men had no further reply
Job32:6 So Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite spoke up: “I am young
Job32:7 I said to myself
Job32:8 But it is a spirit in people
Job32:9 It is not the aged who are wise
Job32:10 Therefore I say
Job32:11 Look
Job32:12 Now I was paying you close attention
Job32:13 So do not say
Job32:14 Job has not directed his words to me
Job32:15 “They are dismayed and cannot answer any more; they have nothing left to say.
Job32:16 And I have waited. But because they do not speak
Job32:17 I too will answer my part
Job32:18 For I am full of words
Job32:19 Inside I am like wine which has no outlet
Job32:20 I will speak
Job32:21 I will not show partiality to anyone
Job32:22 for I do not know how to give honorary titles

Job34:1 Elihu answered:
Job34:2 “Listen to my words
Job34:3 For the ear assesses words as the mouth tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us evaluate for ourselves what is right; let us come to know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job says
Job34:6 Concerning my right
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 He goes about in company with evildoers
Job34:9 For he says
Job34:10 “Therefore
Job34:11 For he repays a person for his work
Job34:12 Indeed
Job34:13 Who entrusted to him the earth? And who put him over the whole world?
Job34:14 If God were to set his heart on it
Job34:15 all flesh would perish together and human beings would return to dust.
Job34:16 “If you have understanding
Job34:17 Do you really think that one who hates justice can govern? And will you declare guilty the supremely righteous One
Job34:18 who says to a king
Job34:19 who shows no partiality to princes
Job34:20 In a moment they die
Job34:21 For his eyes are on the ways of an individual
Job34:22 There is no darkness
Job34:23 For he does not still consider a person
Job34:24 He shatters the great without inquiry
Job34:25 Therefore
Job34:26 He strikes them for their wickedness
Job34:27 because they have turned away from following him
Job34:28 so that they caused the cry of the poor to come before him
Job34:29 But if God is quiet
Job34:30 so that the godless man should not rule
Job34:31 “Has anyone said to God
Job34:32 Teach me what I cannot see. If I have done evil
Job34:33 Is it your opinion that God should recompense it
Job34:34 Men of understanding say to me — any wise man listening to me says —
Job34:35 that Job speaks without knowledge and his words are without understanding.
Job34:36 But Job will be tested to the end
Job34:37 For he adds transgression to his sin; in our midst he claps his hands
Job35:1 Then Elihu answered:
Job35:2 “Do you think this to be just: when you say
Job35:3 But you say
Job35:4 I will reply to you
Job35:5 Gaze at the heavens and see; consider the clouds
Job35:6 If you sin
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness affects only a person like yourself
Job35:9 “People cry out because of the excess of oppression; they cry out for help because of the power of the mighty.
Job35:10 But no one says
Job35:11 who teaches us more than the wild animals of the earth
Job35:12 Then they cry out — but he does not answer — because of the arrogance of the wicked.
Job35:13 Surely it is an empty cry — God does not hear it; the Almighty does not take notice of it.
Job35:14 How much less
Job35:15 And further
Job35:16 So Job opens his mouth to no purpose; without knowledge he multiplies words.”
Job36:1 Elihu said further:
Job36:2 “Be patient with me a little longer and I will instruct you
Job36:3 With my knowledge I will speak comprehensively
Job36:4 For in truth
Job36:5 Indeed
Job36:6 He does not allow the wicked to live
Job36:7 He does not take his eyes off the righteous; but with kings on the throne he seats the righteous and exalts them forever.
Job36:8 But if they are bound in chains
Job36:9 then he reveals to them what they have done
Job36:10 And he reveals this for correction
Job36:11 If they obey and serve him
Job36:12 But if they refuse to listen
Job36:13 The godless at heart nourish anger
Job36:14 They die in their youth
Job36:15 He delivers the afflicted by their afflictions
Job36:16 And surely
Job36:17 But now you are preoccupied with the judgment due the wicked
Job36:18 Be careful that no one entices you with riches; do not let a large bribe turn you aside.
Job36:19 Would your wealth sustain you
Job36:20 Do not long for the cover of night to drag people away from their homes.
Job36:21 Take heed
Job36:22 Indeed
Job36:23 Who has prescribed his ways for him? Or said to him
Job36:24 Remember to extol his work
Job36:25 All humanity has seen it; people gaze on it from afar.
Job36:26 “Yes
Job36:27 He draws up drops of water; they distill the rain into its mist
Job36:28 which the clouds pour down and shower on humankind abundantly.
Job36:29 Who can understand the spreading of the clouds
Job36:30 See how he scattered his lightning about him; he has covered the depths of the sea.
Job36:31 It is by these that he judges the nations and supplies food in abundance.
Job36:32 With his hands he covers the lightning
Job36:33 His thunder announces the coming storm
Job37:1 At this also my heart pounds and leaps from its place.
Job37:2 Listen carefully to the thunder of his voice
Job37:3 Under the whole heaven he lets it go
Job37:4 After that a voice roars; he thunders with an exalted voice
Job37:5 God thunders with his voice in marvelous ways; he does great things beyond our understanding.
Job37:6 For to the snow he says
Job37:7 He causes everyone to stop working
Job37:8 The wild animals go to their lairs
Job37:9 A tempest blows out from its chamber
Job37:10 The breath of God produces ice
Job37:11 He loads the clouds with moisture; he scatters his lightning through the clouds.

Job37:13 Whether it is for punishment for his land

Job37:18 will you

Job37:21 But now
Job37:22 From the north he comes in golden splendor; around God is awesome majesty.
Job37:23 As for the Almighty
Job37:24 Therefore people fear him
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind:
Job38:2 “Who is this who darkens counsel with words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Get ready for a difficult task like a man; I will question you and you will inform me!
Job38:4 “Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Who set its measurements — if you know — or who stretched a measuring line across it?
Job38:6 On what were its bases set
Job38:7 when the morning stars sang in chorus
Job38:8 “Who shut up the sea with doors when it burst forth
Job38:9 when I made the storm clouds its garment
Job38:10 when I prescribed its limits
Job38:11 when I said
Job38:12 Have you ever in your life commanded the morning
Job38:13 that it might seize the corners of the earth
Job38:14 The earth takes shape like clay under a seal; its features are dyed like a garment.
Job38:15 Then from the wicked the light is withheld
Job38:16 Have you gone to the springs that fill the sea
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Have you seen the gates of deepest darkness?
Job38:18 Have you considered the vast expanses of the earth? Tell me
Job38:19 “In what direction does light reside
Job38:20 that you may take them to their borders and perceive the pathways to their homes?
Job38:21 You know
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouse of the snow
Job38:23 which I reserve for the time of trouble
Job38:24 In what direction is lightning dispersed
Job38:25 Who carves out a channel for the heavy rains
Job38:26 to cause it to rain on an uninhabited land
Job38:27 to satisfy a devastated and desolate land
Job38:28 Does the rain have a father
Job38:29 From whose womb does the ice emerge
Job38:30 when the waters become hard like stone
Job38:31 Can you tie the bands of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you lead out the constellations in their seasons
Job38:33 Do you know the laws of the heavens
Job38:34 Can you raise your voice to the clouds so that a flood of water covers you?
Job38:35 Can you send out lightning bolts
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the heart
Job38:37 Who by wisdom can count the clouds
Job38:38 when the dust hardens into a mass
Job38:39 “Do you hunt prey for the lioness
Job38:40 when they crouch in their dens
Job38:41 Who prepares prey for the raven
Job39:1 “Are you acquainted with the way the mountain goats give birth? Do you watch as the wild deer give birth to their young?
Job39:2 Do you count the months they must fulfill
Job39:3 They crouch
Job39:4 Their young grow strong
Job39:5 Who let the wild donkey go free? Who released the bonds of the donkey
Job39:6 to whom I appointed the steppe for its home
Job39:7 It scorns the tumult in the town; it does not hear the shouts of a driver.
Job39:8 It ranges the hills as its pasture
Job39:9 Is the wild ox willing to be your servant? Will it spend the night at your feeding trough?
Job39:10 Can you bind the wild ox to a furrow with its rope
Job39:11 Will you rely on it because its strength is great? Will you commit your labor to it?
Job39:12 Can you count on it to bring in your grain
Job39:13 “The wings of the ostrich flap with joy
Job39:14 For she leaves her eggs on the ground
Job39:15 She forgets that a foot might crush them
Job39:16 She is harsh with her young
Job39:17 For God deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 But as soon as she springs up
Job39:19 “Do you give the horse its strength? Do you clothe its neck with a mane?
Job39:20 Do you make it leap like a locust? Its proud neighing is terrifying!
Job39:21 It paws the ground in the valley
Job39:22 It laughs at fear and is not dismayed; it does not shy away from the sword.
Job39:23 On it the quiver rattles; the lance and javelin flash.
Job39:24 In excitement and impatience it consumes the ground; it cannot stand still when the trumpet is blown.
Job39:25 At the sound of the trumpet
Job39:26 “Is it by your understanding that the hawk soars
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle soars
Job39:28 It lives on a rock and spends the night there
Job39:29 From there it spots its prey
Job39:30 And its young ones devour the blood
Job40:1 Then the LORD answered Job:
Job40:2 “Will the one who contends with the Almighty correct him? Let the person who accuses God give him an answer!”
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD:
Job40:4 “Indeed
Job40:5 I have spoken once
Job40:6 Then the LORD answered Job from the whirlwind:
Job40:7 “Get ready for a difficult task like a man. I will question you and you will inform me!
Job40:8 Would you indeed annul my justice? Would you declare me guilty so that you might be right?
Job40:9 Do you have an arm as powerful as God’s
Job40:10 Adorn yourself
Job40:11 Scatter abroad the abundance of your anger. Look at every proud man and bring him low;
Job40:12 Look at every proud man and abase him; crush the wicked on the spot!
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together
Job40:14 Then I myself will acknowledge to you that your own right hand can save you.
Job40:15 “Look now at Behemoth
Job40:16 Look at its strength in its loins
Job40:17 It makes its tail stiff like a cedar
Job40:18 Its bones are tubes of bronze
Job40:19 It ranks first among the works of God
Job40:20 For the hills bring it food
Job40:21 Under the lotus trees it lies
Job40:22 The lotus trees conceal it in their shadow; the poplars by the stream conceal it.
Job40:23 If the river rages
Job40:24 Can anyone catch it by its eyes
Job41:1 (40:25) “Can you pull in Leviathan with a hook
Job41:2 Can you put a cord through its nose
Job41:3 Will it make numerous supplications to you
Job41:4 Will it make a pact with you
Job41:5 Can you play with it
Job41:6 Will partners bargain for it? Will they divide it up among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill its hide with harpoons or its head with fishing spears?
Job41:8 If you lay your hand on it
Job41:9 (41:1) See
Job41:10 Is it not fierce when it is awakened? Who is he
Job41:11 (Who has confronted me that I should repay? Everything under heaven belongs to me!)
Job41:12 I will not keep silent about its limbs
Job41:13 Who can uncover its outer covering? Who can penetrate to the inside of its armor?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of its mouth? Its teeth all around are fearsome.
Job41:15 Its back has rows of shields
Job41:16 each one is so close to the next that no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They lock tightly together
Job41:18 Its snorting throws out flashes of light; its eyes are like the red glow of dawn.
Job41:19 Out of its mouth go flames
Job41:20 Smoke streams from its nostrils as from a boiling pot over burning rushes.
Job41:21 Its breath sets coals ablaze and a flame shoots from its mouth.
Job41:22 Strength lodges in its neck
Job41:23 The folds of its flesh are tightly joined; they are firm on it
Job41:24 Its heart is hard as rock
Job41:25 When it rises up
Job41:26 Whoever strikes it with a sword will have no effect
Job41:27 It regards iron as straw and bronze as rotten wood.
Job41:28 Arrows do not make it flee; slingstones become like chaff to it.
Job41:29 A club is counted as a piece of straw; it laughs at the rattling of the lance.
Job41:30 Its underparts are the sharp points of potsherds
Job41:31 It makes the deep boil like a cauldron and stirs up the sea like a pot of ointment
Job41:32 It leaves a glistening wake behind it; one would think the deep had a head of white hair.
Job41:33 The likes of it is not on earth
Job41:34 It looks on every haughty being; it is king over all that are proud.”
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD:
Job42:2 “I know that you can do all things; no purpose of yours can be thwarted;
Job42:3 you asked
Job42:4 You said
Job42:5 I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 Therefore I despise myself
Job42:7 After the LORD had spoken these things to Job
Job42:8 So now take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job will intercede for you
Job42:9 So they went
Job42:10 So the LORD restored what Job had lost after he prayed for his friends
Job42:11 So they came to him
Job42:12 So the LORD blessed the second part of Job’s life more than the first. He had 14
Job42:13 And he also had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 The first daughter he named Jemimah
Job42:15 Nowhere in all the land could women be found who were as beautiful as Job’s daughters
Job42:16 After this Job lived 140 years; he saw his children and their children to the fourth generation.
Job42:17 And so Job died

Psalms78:20 Yes
Psalms78:21 When the LORD heard this

Psalms78:26 He brought the east wind through the sky
Psalms78:27 He rained down meat on them like dust
Psalms78:28 He caused them to fall right in the middle of their camp
Psalms78:29 They ate until they were stuffed; he gave them what they desired.
Psalms78:30 They were not yet filled up
Psalms78:31 when the anger of God flared up against them. He killed some of the strongest of them; he brought the young men of Israel to their knees.
Psalms78:32 Despite all this
Psalms78:33 So he caused them to die unsatisfied and filled with terror.
Psalms78:34 When he struck them down
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their protector
Psalms78:36 But they deceived him with their words
Psalms78:37 They were not really committed to him
Psalms78:38 Yet he is compassionate. He forgives sin and does not destroy. He often holds back his anger
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were made of flesh

Psalms78:42 They did not remember what he had done

Psalms78:48 He rained hail down on their cattle
Psalms78:49 His raging anger lashed out against them
Psalms78:50 He sent his anger in full force; he did not spare them from death; he handed their lives over to destruction.
Psalms78:51 He struck down all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 Yet he brought out his people like sheep; he led them through the wilderness like a flock.
Psalms78:53 He guided them safely along
Psalms78:54 He brought them to the border of his holy land
Psalms78:55 He drove the nations out from before them; he assigned them their tribal allotments and allowed the tribes of Israel to settle down.
Psalms78:56 Yet they challenged and defied the sovereign God
Psalms78:57 They were unfaithful and acted as treacherously as their ancestors; they were as unreliable as a malfunctioning bow.
Psalms78:58 They made him angry with their pagan shrines
Psalms78:59 God heard and was angry; he completely rejected Israel.
Psalms78:60 He abandoned the sanctuary at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He allowed the symbol of his strong presence to be captured; he gave the symbol of his splendor into the hand of the enemy.
Psalms78:62 He delivered his people over to the sword
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 But then the Lord awoke from his sleep; he was like a warrior in a drunken rage.
Psalms78:66 He drove his enemies back; he made them a permanent target for insults.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph; he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
Psalms78:68 He chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He made his sanctuary as enduring as the heavens above; as secure as the earth
Psalms78:70 He chose David
Psalms78:71 He took him away from following the mother sheep
Psalms78:72 David cared for them with pure motives; he led them with skill.

Psalms101:1 I will sing about loyalty and justice! To you
Psalms101:2 I will walk in the way of integrity. When will you come to me? I will conduct my business with integrity in the midst of my palace.

Psalms101:4 I will have nothing to do with a perverse person; I will not permit evil.

Psalms101:7 Deceitful people will not live in my palace. Liars will not be welcome in my presence.

Psalms104:5 He established the earth on its foundations; it will never be upended.
Psalms104:6 The watery deep covered it like a garment; the waters reached above the mountains.

Psalms104:10 He turns springs into streams; they flow between the mountains.

Psalms104:12 The birds of the sky live beside them; they chirp among the bushes.
Psalms104:13 He waters the mountains from the upper rooms of his palace; the earth is full of the fruit you cause to grow.

Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD receive all the rain they need
Psalms104:17 where the birds make nests
Psalms104:18 The wild goats live in the high mountains; the rock badgers find safety in the cliffs.

Psalms104:20 You make it dark and night comes
Psalms104:21 The lions roar for prey

Psalms104:23 Men then go out to do their work

Psalms104:26 The ships travel there
Psalms104:27 All of your creatures wait for you to provide them with food on a regular basis.

Psalms119:6 Then I would not be ashamed

Psalms119:8 I will keep your statutes. Do not completely abandon me! ב ( Bet )
Psalms119:9 How can a young person maintain a pure life? By guarding it according to your instructions!

Psalms119:12 You deserve praise

Psalms119:16 I find delight in your statutes; I do not forget your instructions. ג ( Gimel )

Psalms119:20 I desperately long to know your regulations at all times.

Psalms149:8 They bind their kings in chains
Psalms149:9 and execute the judgment to which their enemies have been sentenced. All his loyal followers will be vindicated. Praise the LORD!
Psalms150:1 Praise the LORD! Praise God in his sanctuary! Praise him in the sky
Psalms150:2 Praise him for his mighty acts! Praise him for his surpassing greatness!
Psalms150:3 Praise him with the blast of the horn! Praise him with the lyre and the harp!
Psalms150:4 Praise him with the tambourine and with dancing! Praise him with stringed instruments and the flute!
Psalms150:5 Praise him with loud cymbals! Praise him with clanging cymbals!
Psalms150:6 Let everything that has breath praise the LORD! Praise the LORD!

Proverbs1:4 To impart shrewdness to the morally naive
Proverbs1:5 (Let the wise also hear and gain instruction
Proverbs1:6 To discern the meaning of a proverb and a parable
Proverbs1:7 Fearing the LORD is the beginning of moral knowledge

Proverbs1:12 We will swallow them alive like Sheol
Proverbs1:13 We will seize all kinds of precious wealth; we will fill our houses with plunder.
Proverbs1:14 Join with us! We will all share equally in what we steal.”
Proverbs1:15 My child
Proverbs1:16 for they are eager to inflict harm
Proverbs1:17 Surely it is futile to spread a net in plain sight of any bird
Proverbs1:18 but these men lie in wait for their own blood
Proverbs1:19 Such are the ways of all who gain profit unjustly; it takes away the life of those who obtain it!

Proverbs1:32 For the waywardness of the simpletons will kill them
Proverbs1:33 But the one who listens to me will live in security

Proverbs3:1 My child
Proverbs3:2 for they will provide a long and full life
Proverbs3:3 Do not let truth and mercy leave you; bind them around your neck

Proverbs3:6 Acknowledge him in all your ways
Proverbs3:7 Do not be wise in your own estimation; fear the LORD and turn away from evil.

Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be filled completely

Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than rubies
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are very pleasant
Proverbs3:18 She is like a tree of life to those who obtain her

Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Hold on to instruction

Proverbs4:15 Avoid it

Proverbs4:17 For they eat bread gained from wickedness and drink wine obtained from violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is like the bright morning light
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like gloomy darkness; they do not know what causes them to stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My child

Proverbs4:22 for they are life to those who find them and healing to one’s entire body.
Proverbs4:23 Guard your heart with all vigilance

Proverbs4:26 Make the path for your feet level
Proverbs4:27 Do not turn to the right or to the left; turn yourself away from evil.

Proverbs7:1 My child
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commands so that you may live
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your forearm; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 so that they may keep you from the adulterous woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house through my window lattice I looked out
Proverbs7:7 and I saw among the naive — I discerned among the youths — a young man who lacked wisdom.
Proverbs7:8 He was passing by the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 Suddenly a woman came out to meet him! She was dressed like a prostitute and with secret intent.
Proverbs7:11 (She is loud and rebellious
Proverbs7:12 at one time outside
Proverbs7:13 So she grabbed him and kissed him
Proverbs7:14 “I have fresh meat at home; today I have fulfilled my vows!
Proverbs7:15 That is why I came out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have spread my bed with elegant coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a journey of some distance.
Proverbs7:20 He has taken a bag of money with him; he will not return until the end of the month.”
Proverbs7:21 She persuaded him with persuasive words; with her smooth talk she compelled him.
Proverbs7:22 Suddenly he went after her like an ox that goes to the slaughter
Proverbs7:23 till an arrow pierces his liver — like a bird hurrying into a trap
Proverbs7:24 So now
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways — do not wander into her pathways;
Proverbs7:26 for she has brought down many fatally wounded
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to the grave
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom call out? Does not understanding raise her voice?
Proverbs8:2 At the top of the elevated places along the way
Proverbs8:3 beside the gates opening into the city
Proverbs8:4 “To you
Proverbs8:5 You who are naive
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 For my mouth speaks truth
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing in them twisted or crooked.
Proverbs8:9 All of them are clear to the discerning and upright to those who find knowledge.
Proverbs8:10 Receive my instruction rather than silver
Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is better than rubies
Proverbs8:12 “I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of the LORD is to hate evil; I hate arrogant pride and the evil way and perverse utterances.
Proverbs8:14 Counsel and sound wisdom belong to me; I possess understanding and might.
Proverbs8:15 Kings reign by means of me
Proverbs8:16 by me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than the purest gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the path of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 that I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth
Proverbs8:22 The LORD created me as the beginning of his works
Proverbs8:23 From eternity I was appointed
Proverbs8:24 When there were no deep oceans I was born
Proverbs8:25 before the mountains were set in place — before the hills — I was born
Proverbs8:26 before he made the earth and its fields
Proverbs8:27 When he established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 when he established the clouds above
Proverbs8:29 when he gave the sea his decree that the waters should not pass over his command
Proverbs8:30 then I was beside him as a master craftsman
Proverbs8:31 rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth
Proverbs8:32 “So now
Proverbs8:33 Listen to my instruction so that you may be wise
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the one who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For the one who finds me finds life and receives favor from the LORD.
Proverbs8:36 But the one who does not find me brings harm to himself; all who hate me love death.”

Proverbs30:14 There is a generation whose teeth are like swords and whose molars are like knives to devour the poor from the earth and the needy from among the human race.

Proverbs30:16 the grave

Proverbs30:30 a lion

Proverbs30:33 For as the churning of milk produces butter and as punching the nose produces blood

Proverbs31:19 Her hands take hold of the distaff
