Joel1:1 This is the LORD’s message that was given to Joel the son of Pethuel:
Joel1:2 Listen to this
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it
Joel1:4 What the gazam -locust left the ‘arbeh -locust consumed
Joel1:5 Wake up
Joel1:6 For a nation has invaded our land. There are so many of them they are too numerous to count. Their teeth are like those of a lion; they tear apart their prey like a lioness.
Joel1:7 They have destroyed our vines; they have turned our fig trees into mere splinters. They have completely stripped off the bark and thrown them aside; the twigs are stripped bare.
Joel1:8 Wail like a young virgin clothed in sackcloth
Joel1:9 No one brings grain offerings or drink offerings to the temple of the LORD anymore. So the priests
Joel1:10 The crops of the fields have been destroyed. The ground is in mourning because the grain has perished. The fresh wine has dried up; the olive oil languishes.
Joel1:11 Be distressed
Joel1:12 The vine has dried up; the fig tree languishes — the pomegranate
Joel1:13 Get dressed and lament
Joel1:14 Announce a holy fast; proclaim a sacred assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the temple of the LORD your God
Joel1:15 How awful that day will be! For the day of the LORD is near; it will come as destruction from the Divine Destroyer.
Joel1:16 Our food has been cut off right before our eyes! There is no longer any joy or gladness in the temple of our God!
Joel1:17 The grains of seed have shriveled beneath their shovels. Storehouses have been decimated and granaries have been torn down
Joel1:18 Listen to the cattle groan! The herds of livestock wander around in confusion because they have no pasture. Even the flocks of sheep are suffering.
Joel1:19 To you
Joel1:20 Even the wild animals cry out to you; for the river beds have dried up; fire has destroyed the grassy pastures.
Joel2:1 Blow the trumpet in Zion; sound the alarm signal on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land shake with fear
Joel2:2 It will be a day of dreadful darkness
Joel2:3 Like fire they devour everything in their path; a flame blazes behind them. The land looks like the Garden of Eden before them
Joel2:4 They look like horses; they charge ahead like war horses.
Joel2:5 They sound like chariots rumbling over mountain tops
Joel2:6 People writhe in fear when they see them. All of their faces turn pale with fright.
Joel2:7 They charge like warriors; they scale walls like soldiers. Each one proceeds on his course; they do not alter their path.
Joel2:8 They do not jostle one another; each of them marches straight ahead. They burst through the city defenses and do not break ranks.
Joel2:9 They rush into the city; they scale its walls. They climb up into the houses; they go in through the windows like a thief.
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them; the sky reverberates. The sun and the moon grow dark; the stars refuse to shine.
Joel2:11 The voice of the LORD thunders as he leads his army. Indeed
Joel2:12 “Yet even now
Joel2:13 Return to the LORD your God
Joel2:14 Who knows? Perhaps he will be compassionate and grant a reprieve
Joel2:15 Blow the trumpet in Zion. Announce a holy fast; proclaim a sacred assembly!
Joel2:16 Gather the people; sanctify an assembly! Gather the elders; gather the children and the nursing infants. Let the bridegroom come out from his bedroom and the bride from her private quarters.
Joel2:17 Let the priests
Joel2:18 Then the LORD became zealous for his land; he had compassion on his people.
Joel2:19 The LORD responded to his people
Joel2:20 I will remove the one from the north far from you. I will drive him out to a dry and desolate place. Those in front will be driven eastward into the Dead Sea
Joel2:21 Do not fear
Joel2:22 Do not fear
Joel2:23 Citizens of Zion
Joel2:24 The threshing floors are full of grain; the vats overflow with fresh wine and olive oil.
Joel2:25 I will make up for the years that the ‘arbeh -locust consumed your crops — the yeleq -locust
Joel2:26 You will have plenty to eat
Joel2:27 You will be convinced that I am in the midst of Israel. I am the LORD your God; there is no other. My people will never again be put to shame.
Joel2:28 (3:1) After all of this I will pour out my Spirit on all kinds of people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your elderly will have revelatory dreams; your young men will see prophetic visions.
Joel2:29 Even on male and female servants I will pour out my Spirit in those days.
Joel2:30 I will produce portents both in the sky and on the earth — blood
Joel2:31 The sunlight will be turned to darkness and the moon to the color of blood
Joel2:32 It will so happen that everyone who calls on the name of the LORD will be delivered. For on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there will be those who survive
Joel3:1 (4:1) For look! In those days and at that time I will return the exiles to Judah and Jerusalem.
Joel3:2 Then I will gather all the nations
Joel3:3 and they cast lots for my people. They traded a boy for a prostitute; they sold a little girl for wine so they could drink.
Joel3:4 Why are you doing these things to me
Joel3:5 For you took my silver and my gold and brought my precious valuables to your own palaces.
Joel3:6 You sold Judeans and Jerusalemites to the Greeks
Joel3:7 Look! I am rousing them from that place to which you sold them. I will repay you for what you have done!
Joel3:8 I will sell your sons and daughters to the people of Judah. They will sell them to the Sabeans
Joel3:9 Proclaim this among the nations: “Prepare for a holy war! Call out the warriors! Let all these fighting men approach and attack!
Joel3:10 Beat your plowshares into swords
Joel3:11 Lend your aid and come
Joel3:12 Let the nations be roused and let them go up to the valley of Jehoshaphat
Joel3:13 Rush forth with the sickle
Joel3:14 Crowds
Joel3:15 The sun and moon are darkened; the stars withhold their brightness.
Joel3:16 The LORD roars from Zion; from Jerusalem his voice bellows out. The heavens and the earth shake. But the LORD is a refuge for his people; he is a stronghold for the citizens of Israel.
Joel3:17 You will be convinced that I the LORD am your God
Joel3:18 On that day the mountains will drip with sweet wine
Joel3:19 Egypt will be desolate and Edom will be a desolate wilderness
Joel3:20 But Judah will reside securely forever
Joel3:21 I will avenge their blood which I had not previously acquitted. It is the LORD who dwells in Zion!
Amos1:1 The following is a record of what Amos prophesied. He was one of the herdsmen from Tekoa. These prophecies about Israel were revealed to him during the time of King Uzziah of Judah and King Jeroboam son of Joash of Israel
Amos1:2 Amos said: “The LORD comes roaring out of Zion; from Jerusalem he comes bellowing! The shepherds’ pastures wilt; the summit of Carmel withers.”
Amos1:3 This is what the LORD says: “Because Damascus has committed three crimes — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They ripped through Gilead like threshing sledges with iron teeth.
Amos1:4 So I will set Hazael’s house on fire; fire will consume Ben Hadad’s fortresses.
Amos1:5 I will break the bar on the gate of Damascus. I will remove the ruler from Wicked Valley
Amos1:6 This is what the LORD says: “Because Gaza has committed three crimes — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They deported a whole community and sold them to Edom.
Amos1:7 So I will set Gaza’s city wall on fire; fire will consume her fortresses.
Amos1:8 I will remove the ruler from Ashdod
Amos1:9 This is what the LORD says: “Because Tyre has committed three crimes — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They sold a whole community to Edom; they failed to observe a treaty of brotherhood.
Amos1:10 So I will set fire to Tyre’s city wall; fire will consume her fortresses.”
Amos1:11 This is what the LORD says: “Because Edom has committed three crimes — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. He chased his brother with a sword; he wiped out his allies. In his anger he tore them apart without stopping to rest; in his fury he relentlessly attacked them.
Amos1:12 So I will set Teman on fire; fire will consume Bozrah’s fortresses.”
Amos1:13 This is what the LORD says: “Because the Ammonites have committed three crimes — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They ripped open Gilead’s pregnant women so they could expand their territory.
Amos1:14 So I will set fire to Rabbah’s city wall; fire will consume her fortresses. War cries will be heard on the day of battle; a strong gale will blow on the day of the windstorm.
Amos1:15 Ammon’s king will be deported; he and his officials will be carried off together.” The LORD has spoken!
Amos2:1 This is what the LORD says: “Because Moab has committed three crimes — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They burned the bones of Edom’s king into lime.
Amos2:2 So I will set Moab on fire
Amos2:3 I will remove Moab’s leader; I will kill all Moab’s officials with him.” The LORD has spoken!
Amos2:4 This is what the LORD says: “Because Judah has committed three covenant transgressions — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They rejected the LORD’s law; they did not obey his commands. Their false gods
Amos2:5 So I will set Judah on fire
Amos2:6 This is what the LORD says: “Because Israel has committed three covenant transgressions — make that four! — I will not revoke my decree of judgment. They sold the innocent for silver
Amos2:7 They trample on the dirt-covered heads of the poor; they push the destitute away. A man and his father go to the same girl; in this way they show disrespect for my moral purity.
Amos2:8 They stretch out on clothing seized as collateral; they do so right beside every altar! They drink wine bought with the fines they have levied; they do so right in the temple of their God!
Amos2:9 For Israel’s sake I destroyed the Amorites. They were as tall as cedars and as strong as oaks
Amos2:10 I brought you up from the land of Egypt; I led you through the wilderness for forty years so you could take the Amorites’ land as your own.
Amos2:11 I made some of your sons prophets and some of your young men Nazirites. Is this not true
Amos2:12 “But you made the Nazirites drink wine; you commanded the prophets
Amos2:13 Look! I will press you down
Amos2:14 Fast runners will find no place to hide; strong men will have no strength left; warriors will not be able to save their lives.
Amos2:15 Archers will not hold their ground; fast runners will not save their lives
Amos2:16 Bravehearted warriors will run away naked in that day.” The LORD is speaking!
Amos3:1 Listen
Amos3:2 “I have chosen you alone from all the clans of the earth. Therefore I will punish you for all your sins.”
Amos3:3 Do two walk together without having met?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the woods if he has not cornered his prey? Does a young lion bellow from his den if he has not caught something?
Amos3:5 Does a bird swoop down into a trap on the ground if there is no bait? Does a trap spring up from the ground unless it has surely caught something?
Amos3:6 If an alarm sounds in a city
Amos3:7 Certainly the sovereign LORD does nothing without first revealing his plan to his servants the prophets.
Amos3:8 A lion has roared! Who is not afraid? The sovereign LORD has spoken! Who can refuse to prophesy?
Amos3:9 Make this announcement in the fortresses of Ashdod and in the fortresses in the land of Egypt. Say this: “Gather on the hills around Samaria! Observe the many acts of violence taking place within the city
Amos3:10 “They do not know how to do what is right.” (The LORD is speaking.) “They store up the spoils of destructive violence in their fortresses.
Amos3:11 Therefore
Amos3:12 This is what the LORD says: “Just as a shepherd salvages from the lion’s mouth a couple of leg bones or a piece of an ear
Amos3:13 Listen and warn the family of Jacob! The sovereign LORD
Amos3:14 “Certainly when I punish Israel for their covenant transgressions
Amos3:15 I will destroy both the winter and summer houses. The houses filled with ivory will be ruined
Amos4:1 Listen to this message
Amos4:2 The sovereign LORD confirms this oath by his own holy character: “Certainly the time is approaching when you will be carried away in baskets
Amos4:3 Each of you will go straight through the gaps in the walls; you will be thrown out toward Harmon.” The LORD is speaking!
Amos4:4 “Go to Bethel and rebel! At Gilgal rebel some more! Bring your sacrifices in the morning
Amos4:5 Burn a thank offering of bread made with yeast! Make a public display of your voluntary offerings! For you love to do this
Amos4:6 “But surely I gave you no food to eat in any of your cities; you lacked food everywhere you live. Still you did not come back to me.” The LORD is speaking!
Amos4:7 “I withheld rain from you three months before the harvest. I gave rain to one city
Amos4:8 People from two or three cities staggered into one city to get water
Amos4:9 “I destroyed your crops with blight and disease. Locusts kept devouring your orchards
Amos4:10 “I sent against you a plague like one of the Egyptian plagues. I killed your young men with the sword
Amos4:11 “I overthrew some of you the way God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah. You were like a burning stick snatched from the flames. Still you did not come back to me.” The LORD is speaking!
Amos4:12 “Therefore this is what I will do to you
Amos4:13 For here he is! He formed the mountains and created the wind. He reveals his plans to men. He turns the dawn into darkness and marches on the heights of the earth. The LORD
Amos5:1 Listen to this funeral song I am ready to sing about you
Amos5:2 “The virgin Israel has fallen down and will not get up again. She is abandoned on her own land with no one to help her get up.”
Amos5:3 The sovereign LORD says this: “The city that marches out with a thousand soldiers will have only a hundred left; the town that marches out with a hundred soldiers will have only ten left for the family of Israel.”
Amos5:4 The LORD says this to the family of Israel: “Seek me so you can live!
Amos5:5 Do not seek Bethel! Do not visit Gilgal! Do not journey down to Beer Sheba! For the people of Gilgal will certainly be carried into exile; and Bethel will become a place where disaster abounds.”
Amos5:6 Seek the LORD so you can live! Otherwise he will break out like fire against Joseph’s family; the fire will consume and no one will be able to quench it and save Bethel.
Amos5:7 The Israelites turn justice into bitterness; they throw what is fair and right to the ground.
Amos5:8 (But there is one who made the constellations Pleiades and Orion; he can turn the darkness into morning and daylight into night. He summons the water of the seas and pours it out on the earth’s surface. The LORD is his name!
Amos5:9 He flashes destruction down upon the strong so that destruction overwhelms the fortified places.)
Amos5:10 The Israelites hate anyone who arbitrates at the city gate; they despise anyone who speaks honestly.
Amos5:11 Therefore
Amos5:12 Certainly I am aware of your many rebellious acts and your numerous sins. You torment the innocent
Amos5:13 For this reason whoever is smart keeps quiet in such a time
Amos5:14 Seek good and not evil so you can live! Then the LORD

Amos5:16 Because of Israel’s sins this is what the LORD

Nahum1:1 The oracle against Nineveh; the book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite:
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a zealous and avenging God; the LORD is avenging and very angry. The LORD takes vengeance against his foes; he sustains his rage against his enemies.
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger but great in power; the LORD will certainly not allow the wicked to go unpunished. He marches out in the whirlwind and the raging storm; dark storm clouds billow like dust under his feet.
Nahum1:4 He shouts a battle cry against the sea and makes it dry up; he makes all the rivers run dry. Bashan and Carmel wither; the blossom of Lebanon withers.
Nahum1:5 The mountains tremble before him
Nahum1:6 No one can withstand his indignation! No one can resist his fierce anger! His wrath is poured out like volcanic fire
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good — indeed
Nahum1:8 But with an overwhelming flood he will make a complete end of Nineveh; he will drive his enemies into darkness.
Nahum1:9 Whatever you plot against the LORD
Nahum1:10 Surely they will be totally consumed like entangled thorn bushes
Nahum1:11 From you
Nahum1:12 This is what the LORD says: “Even though they are powerful — and what is more
Nahum1:13 And now
Nahum1:14 The LORD has issued a decree against you: “Your dynasty will come to an end. I will destroy the idols and images in the temples of your gods. I will desecrate your grave — because you are accursed!”

Nahum2:1 (2:2) The watchmen of Nineveh shout: “An enemy who will scatter you is marching out to attack you!” “Guard the rampart! Watch the road! Prepare yourselves for battle! Muster your mighty strength!”
Nahum2:2 For the LORD will restore the majesty of Jacob
Nahum2:3 The shields of his warriors are dyed red; the mighty soldiers are dressed in scarlet garments. The metal fittings of the chariots shine like fire on the day of battle; the soldiers brandish their spears.
Nahum2:4 The chariots race madly through the streets
Nahum2:5 The commander orders his officers; they stumble as they advance; they rush to the city wall and they set up the covered siege tower.
Nahum2:6 The sluice gates are opened; the royal palace is deluged and dissolves.
Nahum2:7 Nineveh is taken into exile and is led away; her slave girls moan like doves while they beat their breasts.
Nahum2:8 Nineveh was like a pool of water throughout her days
Nahum2:9 Her conquerors cry out: “Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold!” There is no end to the treasure; riches of every kind of precious thing.
Nahum2:10 Destruction
Nahum2:11 Where now is the den of the lions
Nahum2:12 The lion tore apart as much prey as his cubs needed and strangled prey to provide food for his lionesses; he filled his lairs with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
Nahum2:13 “I am against you!” declares the LORD who commands armies: “I will burn your chariots with fire; the sword will devour your young lions; you will no longer prey upon the land; the voices of your messengers will no longer be heard.”
Nahum3:1 Woe to the city guilty of bloodshed! She is full of lies; she is filled with plunder; she has hoarded her spoil!
Nahum3:2 The chariot drivers will crack their whips; the chariot wheels will shake the ground; the chariot horses will gallop; the war chariots will bolt forward!
Nahum3:3 The charioteers will charge ahead; their swords will flash and their spears will glimmer! There will be many people slain; there will be piles of the dead
Nahum3:4 “Because you have acted like a wanton prostitute — a seductive mistress who practices sorcery
Nahum3:5 I am against you
Nahum3:6 I will pelt you with filth; I will treat you with contempt; I will make you a public spectacle.
Nahum3:7 Everyone who sees you will turn away from you in disgust; they will say
Nahum3:8 You are no more secure than Thebes — she was located on the banks of the Nile; the waters surrounded her
Nahum3:9 Cush and Egypt had limitless strength; Put and the Libyans were among her allies.
Nahum3:10 Yet she went into captivity as an exile; even her infants were smashed to pieces at the head of every street. They cast lots for her nobility; all her dignitaries were bound with chains.
Nahum3:11 You too will act like drunkards; you will go into hiding; you too will seek refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortifications will be like fig trees with first-ripe fruit: If they are shaken
Nahum3:13 Your warriors will be like women in your midst; the gates of your land will be wide open to your enemies; fire will consume the bars of your gates.
Nahum3:14 Draw yourselves water for a siege! Strengthen your fortifications! Trample the mud and tread the clay! Make mud bricks to strengthen your walls!
Nahum3:15 There the fire will consume you; the sword will cut you down; it will devour you like the young locust would. Multiply yourself like the young locust; multiply yourself like the flying locust!
Nahum3:16 Increase your merchants more than the stars of heaven! They are like the young locust which sheds its skin and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your courtiers are like locusts
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds are sleeping
Nahum3:19 Your destruction is like an incurable wound; your demise is like a fatal injury! All who hear what has happened to you will clap their hands for joy
Habakkuk1:1 The following is the message which God revealed to Habakkuk the prophet:
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you force me to witness injustice? Why do you put up with wrongdoing? Destruction and violence confront me; conflict is present and one must endure strife.
Habakkuk1:4 For this reason the law lacks power
Habakkuk1:5 “Look at the nations and pay attention! You will be shocked and amazed! For I will do something in your lifetime that you will not believe even though you are forewarned.
Habakkuk1:6 Look
Habakkuk1:7 They are frightening and terrifying; they decide for themselves what is right.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are faster than leopards and more alert than wolves in the desert. Their horses gallop
Habakkuk1:9 All of them intend to do violence; every face is determined. They take prisoners as easily as one scoops up sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They mock kings and laugh at rulers. They laugh at every fortified city; they build siege ramps and capture them.
Habakkuk1:11 They sweep by like the wind and pass on. But the one who considers himself a god will be held guilty.”
Habakkuk1:12 LORD
Habakkuk1:13 You are too just to tolerate evil; you are unable to condone wrongdoing. So why do you put up with such treacherous people? Why do you say nothing when the wicked devour those more righteous than they are?
Habakkuk1:14 You made people like fish in the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The Babylonian tyrant pulls them all up with a fishhook; he hauls them in with his throw net. When he catches them in his dragnet
Habakkuk1:16 Because of his success he offers sacrifices to his throw net and burns incense to his dragnet; for because of them he has plenty of food
Habakkuk1:17 Will he then continue to fill and empty his throw net? Will he always destroy nations and spare none?
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my watch post; I will remain stationed on the city wall. I will keep watching
Habakkuk2:2 The LORD responded: “Write down this message! Record it legibly on tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For the message is a witness to what is decreed; it gives reliable testimony about how matters will turn out. Even if the message is not fulfilled right away
Habakkuk2:4 Look
Habakkuk2:5 Indeed
Habakkuk2:6 “But all these nations will someday taunt him and ridicule him with proverbial sayings: ‘The one who accumulates what does not belong to him is as good as dead (How long will this go on?) — he who gets rich by extortion!’
Habakkuk2:7 Your creditors will suddenly attack; those who terrify you will spring into action
Habakkuk2:8 Because you robbed many countries
Habakkuk2:9 The one who builds his house by unjust gain is as good as dead. He does this so he can build his nest way up high and escape the clutches of disaster.
Habakkuk2:10 Your schemes will bring shame to your house. Because you destroyed many nations
Habakkuk2:11 For the stones in the walls will cry out
Habakkuk2:12 The one who builds a city by bloodshed is as good as dead — he who starts a town by unjust deeds.
Habakkuk2:13 Be sure of this! The LORD who commands armies has decreed: The nations’ efforts will go up in smoke; their exhausting work will be for nothing.
Habakkuk2:14 For recognition of the LORD’s sovereign majesty will fill the earth just as the waters fill up the sea.
Habakkuk2:15 “You who force your neighbor to drink wine are as good as dead — you who make others intoxicated by forcing them to drink from the bowl of your furious anger
Habakkuk2:16 But you will become drunk with shame
Habakkuk2:17 For you will pay in full for your violent acts against Lebanon; terrifying judgment will come upon you because of the way you destroyed the wild animals living there. You have shed human blood and committed violent acts against lands
Habakkuk2:18 What good is an idol? Why would a craftsman make it? What good is a metal image that gives misleading oracles? Why would its creator place his trust in it and make such mute
Habakkuk2:19 The one who says to wood
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in his majestic palace. The whole earth is speechless in his presence!”
Habakkuk3:1 This is a prayer of Habakkuk the prophet:
Habakkuk3:2 LORD
Habakkuk3:3 God comes from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 He is as bright as lightning; a two-pronged lightning bolt flashes from his hand. This is the outward display of his power.
Habakkuk3:5 Plague goes before him; pestilence marches right behind him.
Habakkuk3:6 He takes his battle position and shakes the earth; with a mere look he frightens the nations. The ancient mountains disintegrate; the primeval hills are flattened. He travels on the ancient roads.
Habakkuk3:7 I see the tents of Cushan overwhelmed by trouble; the tent curtains of the land of Midian are shaking.
Habakkuk3:8 Is the LORD mad at the rivers? Are you angry with the rivers? Are you enraged at the sea? Is this why you climb into your horse-drawn chariots
Habakkuk3:9 Your bow is ready for action; you commission your arrows. Selah. You cause flash floods on the earth’s surface.
Habakkuk3:10 When the mountains see you
Habakkuk3:11 The sun and moon stand still in their courses; the flash of your arrows drives them away
Habakkuk3:12 You furiously stomp on the earth
Habakkuk3:13 You march out to deliver your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierce the heads of his warriors with a spear. They storm forward to scatter us; they shout with joy as if they were plundering the poor with no opposition.
Habakkuk3:15 But you trample on the sea with your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I listened and my stomach churned; the sound made my lips quiver. My frame went limp
Habakkuk3:17 When the fig tree does not bud
Habakkuk3:18 I will rejoice because of the LORD; I will be happy because of the God who delivers me!
Habakkuk3:19 The sovereign LORD is my source of strength. He gives me the agility of a deer; he enables me to negotiate the rugged terrain. (This prayer is for the song leader. It is to be accompanied by stringed instruments.)

Matthew1:1 This is the record of the genealogy of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah the father of Perez and Zerah (by Tamar)

Matthew10:31 So do not be afraid; you are more valuable than many sparrows.
Matthew10:32 “Whoever
Matthew10:33 But whoever denies me before people

Matthew10:36 and a man’s enemies will be the members of his household.

Matthew10:38 And whoever does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
Matthew10:39 Whoever finds his life will lose it
Matthew10:40 “Whoever receives you receives me
Matthew10:41 Whoever receives a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward. Whoever receives a righteous person in the name of a righteous person will receive a righteous person’s reward.
Matthew10:42 And whoever gives only a cup of cold water to one of these little ones in the name of a disciple

Matthew13:1 On that day after Jesus went out of the house
Matthew13:2 And such a large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat to sit while the whole crowd stood on the shore.
Matthew13:3 He told them many things in parables
Matthew13:4 And as he sowed
Matthew13:5 Other seeds fell on rocky ground where they did not have much soil. They sprang up quickly because the soil was not deep.
Matthew13:6 But when the sun came up
Matthew13:7 Other seeds fell among the thorns
Matthew13:8 But other seeds fell on good soil and produced grain
Matthew13:9 The one who has ears had better listen!”
Matthew13:10 Then the disciples came to him and said
Matthew13:11 He replied
Matthew13:12 For whoever has will be given more
Matthew13:13 For this reason I speak to them in parables: Although they see they do not see
Matthew13:14 And concerning them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled that says: ‘ You will listen carefully yet will never understand
Matthew13:15 For the heart of this people has become dull; they are hard of hearing
Matthew13:16 “But your eyes are blessed because they see
Matthew13:17 For I tell you the truth
Matthew13:18 “So listen to the parable of the sower:
Matthew13:19 When anyone hears the word about the kingdom and does not understand it
Matthew13:20 The seed sown on rocky ground is the person who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy.
Matthew13:21 But he has no root in himself and does not endure; when trouble or persecution comes because of the word
Matthew13:22 The seed sown among thorns is the person who hears the word
Matthew13:23 But as for the seed sown on good soil
Matthew13:24 He presented them with another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a person who sowed good seed in his field.
Matthew13:25 But while everyone was sleeping
Matthew13:26 When the plants sprouted and bore grain
Matthew13:27 So the slaves of the owner came and said to him
Matthew13:28 He said
Matthew13:29 But he said
Matthew13:30 Let both grow together until the harvest. At harvest time I will tell the reapers
Matthew13:31 He gave them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man took and sowed in his field.
Matthew13:32 It is the smallest of all the seeds
Matthew13:33 He told them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed with three measures of flour until all the dough had risen.”
Matthew13:34 Jesus spoke all these things in parables to the crowds; he did not speak to them without a parable.
Matthew13:35 This fulfilled what was spoken by the prophet: “ I will open my mouth in parables
Matthew13:36 Then he left the crowds and went into the house. And his disciples came to him saying
Matthew13:37 He answered
Matthew13:38 The field is the world and the good seed are the people of the kingdom. The weeds are the people of the evil one
Matthew13:39 and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age
Matthew13:40 As the weeds are collected and burned with fire
Matthew13:41 The Son of Man will send his angels
Matthew13:42 They will throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:43 Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. The one who has ears had better listen!
Matthew13:44 “The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure
Matthew13:45 “Again
Matthew13:46 When he found a pearl of great value
Matthew13:47 “Again
Matthew13:48 When it was full
Matthew13:49 It will be this way at the end of the age. Angels will come and separate the evil from the righteous
Matthew13:50 and throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:51 “Have you understood all these things?” They replied
Matthew13:52 Then he said to them
Matthew13:53 Now when Jesus finished these parables
Matthew13:54 Then he came to his hometown and began to teach the people in their synagogue. They were astonished and said
Matthew13:55 Isn’t this the carpenter’s son? Isn’t his mother named Mary? And aren’t his brothers James
Matthew13:56 And aren’t all his sisters here with us? Where did he get all this?”
Matthew13:57 And so they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them
Matthew13:58 And he did not do many miracles there because of their unbelief.

Matthew15:1 Then Pharisees and experts in the law came from Jerusalem to Jesus and said
Matthew15:2 “Why do your disciples disobey the tradition of the elders? For they don’t wash their hands when they eat.”
Matthew15:3 He answered them
Matthew15:4 For God said
Matthew15:5 But you say

Matthew27:29 and after braiding a crown of thorns
Matthew27:30 They spat on him and took the staff and struck him repeatedly on the head.
Matthew27:31 When they had mocked him
Matthew27:32 As they were going out
Matthew27:33 They came to a place called Golgotha (which means “Place of the Skull”)
Matthew27:34 and offered Jesus wine mixed with gall to drink. But after tasting it
Matthew27:35 When they had crucified him
Matthew27:36 Then they sat down and kept guard over him there.
Matthew27:37 Above his head they put the charge against him
Matthew27:38 Then two outlaws were crucified with him
Matthew27:39 Those who passed by defamed him
Matthew27:40 and saying
Matthew27:41 In the same way even the chief priests — together with the experts in the law and elders — were mocking him:
Matthew27:42 “He saved others
Matthew27:43 H e trusts in God — let God
Matthew27:44 The robbers who were crucified with him also spoke abusively to him.
Matthew27:45 Now from noon until three
Matthew27:46 At about three o’clock Jesus shouted with a loud voice
Matthew27:47 When some of the bystanders heard it
Matthew27:48 Immediately one of them ran and got a sponge
Matthew27:49 But the rest said
Matthew27:50 Then Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and gave up his spirit.
Matthew27:51 Just then the temple curtain was torn in two
Matthew27:52 And tombs were opened
Matthew27:53 (They came out of the tombs after his resurrection and went into the holy city and appeared to many people.)
Matthew27:54 Now when the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what took place
Matthew27:55 Many women who had followed Jesus from Galilee and given him support were also there
Matthew27:56 Among them were Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 Now when it was evening
Matthew27:58 He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered that it be given to him.
Matthew27:59 Joseph took the body
Matthew27:60 and placed it in his own new tomb that he had cut in the rock. Then he rolled a great stone across the entrance of the tomb and went away.
Matthew27:61 (Now Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting there
Matthew27:62 The next day (which is after the day of preparation) the chief priests and the Pharisees assembled before Pilate
Matthew27:63 and said
Matthew27:64 So give orders to secure the tomb until the third day. Otherwise his disciples may come and steal his body and say to the people
Matthew27:65 Pilate said to them
Matthew27:66 So they went with the soldiers of the guard and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone.
Matthew28:1 Now after the Sabbath
Matthew28:2 Suddenly there was a severe earthquake
Matthew28:3 His appearance was like lightning
Matthew28:4 The guards were shaken and became like dead men because they were so afraid of him.
Matthew28:5 But the angel said to the women
Matthew28:6 He is not here
Matthew28:7 Then go quickly and tell his disciples
Matthew28:8 So they left the tomb quickly
Matthew28:9 But Jesus met them
Matthew28:10 Then Jesus said to them
Matthew28:11 While they were going
Matthew28:12 After they had assembled with the elders and formed a plan
Matthew28:13 telling them
Matthew28:14 If this matter is heard before the governor
Matthew28:15 So they took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story is told among the Jews to this day.
Matthew28:16 So the eleven disciples went to Galilee to the mountain Jesus had designated.
Matthew28:17 When they saw him
Matthew28:18 Then Jesus came up and said to them
Matthew28:19 Therefore go and make disciples of all nations
Matthew28:20 teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And remember
Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet
Mark1:3 the voice of one shouting in the wilderness
Mark1:4 In the wilderness John the baptizer began preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
Mark1:5 People from the whole Judean countryside and all of Jerusalem were going out to him
Mark1:6 John wore a garment made of camel’s hair with a leather belt around his waist
Mark1:7 He proclaimed
Mark1:8 I baptize you with water
Mark1:9 Now in those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan River.
Mark1:10 And just as Jesus was coming up out of the water
Mark1:11 And a voice came from heaven: “You are my one dear Son; in you I take great delight.”
Mark1:12 The Spirit immediately drove him into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 He was in the wilderness forty days
Mark1:14 Now after John was imprisoned
Mark1:15 He said
Mark1:16 As he went along the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 They left their nets immediately and followed him.
Mark1:19 Going on a little farther
Mark1:20 Immediately he called them
Mark1:21 Then they went to Capernaum. When the Sabbath came
Mark1:22 The people there were amazed by his teaching
Mark1:23 Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit
Mark1:24 “Leave us alone
Mark1:25 But Jesus rebuked him: “Silence! Come out of him!”
Mark1:26 After throwing him into convulsions
Mark1:27 They were all amazed so that they asked each other
Mark1:28 So the news about him spread quickly throughout all the region around Galilee.
Mark1:29 Now as soon as they left the synagogue
Mark1:30 Simon’s mother-in-law was lying down
Mark1:31 He came and raised her up by gently taking her hand. Then the fever left her and she began to serve them.
Mark1:32 When it was evening
Mark1:33 The whole town gathered by the door.
Mark1:34 So he healed many who were sick with various diseases and drove out many demons. But he would not permit the demons to speak
Mark1:35 Then Jesus got up early in the morning when it was still very dark
Mark1:36 Simon and his companions searched for him.
Mark1:37 When they found him
Mark1:38 He replied
Mark1:39 So he went into all of Galilee preaching in their synagogues and casting out demons.
Mark1:40 Now a leper came to him and fell to his knees
Mark1:41 Moved with compassion
Mark1:42 The leprosy left him at once
Mark1:43 Immediately Jesus sent the man away with a very strong warning.
Mark1:44 He told him
Mark1:45 But as the man went out he began to announce it publicly and spread the story widely
Mark2:1 Now after some days
Mark2:2 So many gathered that there was no longer any room
Mark2:3 Some people came bringing to him a paralytic
Mark2:4 When they were not able to bring him in because of the crowd
Mark2:5 When Jesus saw their faith
Mark2:6 Now some of the experts in the law were sitting there
Mark2:7 “Why does this man speak this way? He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

Mark2:9 Which is easier
Mark2:10 But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins

Mark2:12 And immediately the man stood up
Mark2:13 Jesus went out again by the sea. The whole crowd came to him
Mark2:14 As he went along
Mark2:15 As Jesus was having a meal in Levi’s home
Mark2:16 When the experts in the law and the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors
Mark2:17 When Jesus heard this he said to them
Mark2:18 Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. So they came to Jesus and said
Mark2:19 Jesus said to them
Mark2:20 But the days are coming when the bridegroom will be taken from them
Mark2:21 No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; otherwise
Mark2:22 And no one pours new wine into old wineskins; otherwise
Mark2:23 Jesus was going through the grain fields on a Sabbath
Mark2:24 So the Pharisees said to him
Mark2:25 He said to them
Mark2:26 how he entered the house of God when Abiathar was high priest and ate the sacred bread
Mark2:27 Then he said to them
Mark2:28 For this reason the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”
Mark3:1 Then Jesus entered the synagogue again
Mark3:2 They watched Jesus closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath
Mark3:3 So he said to the man who had the withered hand

Mark3:8 Jerusalem
Mark3:9 Because of the crowd
Mark3:10 For he had healed many
Mark3:11 And whenever the unclean spirits saw him

Mark3:13 Now Jesus went up the mountain and called for those he wanted

Mark3:15 and to have authority to cast out demons.

Mark3:17 to James and his brother John
Mark3:18 and Andrew

Mark3:24 If a kingdom is divided against itself
Mark3:25 If a house is divided against itself
Mark3:26 And if Satan rises against himself and is divided
Mark3:27 But no one is able to enter a strong man’s house and steal his property unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can thoroughly plunder his house.
Mark3:28 I tell you the truth
Mark3:29 But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven

Mark3:35 For whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
Mark4:1 Again he began to teach by the lake. Such a large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat on the lake and sat there while the whole crowd was on the shore by the lake.
Mark4:2 He taught them many things in parables
Mark4:3 “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
Mark4:4 And as he sowed
Mark4:5 Other seed fell on rocky ground where it did not have much soil. It sprang up at once because the soil was not deep.
Mark4:6 When the sun came up it was scorched
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among the thorns
Mark4:8 But other seed fell on good soil and produced grain
Mark4:9 And he said
Mark4:10 When he was alone
Mark4:11 He said to them
Mark4:12 so that although they look they may look but not see
Mark4:13 He said to them
Mark4:14 The sower sows the word.
Mark4:15 These are the ones on the path where the word is sown: Whenever they hear
Mark4:16 These are the ones sown on rocky ground: As soon as they hear the word
Mark4:17 But they have no root in themselves and do not endure. Then
Mark4:18 Others are the ones sown among thorns: They are those who hear the word
Mark4:19 but worldly cares
Mark4:20 But these are the ones sown on good soil: They hear the word and receive it and bear fruit
Mark4:21 He also said to them
Mark4:22 For nothing is hidden except to be revealed
Mark4:23 If anyone has ears to hear
Mark4:24 And he said to them
Mark4:25 For whoever has will be given more
Mark4:26 He also said
Mark4:27 He goes to sleep and gets up
Mark4:28 By itself the soil produces a crop
Mark4:29 And when the grain is ripe
Mark4:30 He also asked
Mark4:31 It is like a mustard seed that when sown in the ground
Mark4:32 when it is sown
Mark4:33 So with many parables like these
Mark4:34 He did not speak to them without a parable. But privately he explained everything to his own disciples.
Mark4:35 On that day
Mark4:36 So after leaving the crowd

Mark4:38 But he was in the stern
Mark4:39 So he got up and rebuked the wind
Mark4:40 And he said to them

Mark5:22 Then one of the synagogue rulers
Mark5:23 He asked him urgently
Mark5:24 Jesus went with him

Mark5:28 for she kept saying

Mark5:36 But Jesus

Mark5:39 When he entered he said to them
Mark5:40 And they began making fun of him. But he put them all outside and he took the child’s father and mother and his own companions and went into the room where the child was.
Mark5:41 Then
Mark5:42 The girl got up at once and began to walk around (she was twelve years old). They were completely astonished at this.

Acts20:3 where he stayed for three months. Because the Jews had made a plot against him as he was intending to sail for Syria
Acts20:4 Paul was accompanied by Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Berea
Acts20:5 These had gone on ahead and were waiting for us in Troas.
Acts20:6 We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread
Acts20:7 On the first day of the week
Acts20:8 (Now there were many lamps in the upstairs room where we were meeting.)
Acts20:9 A young man named Eutychus
Acts20:10 But Paul went down
Acts20:11 Then Paul went back upstairs
Acts20:12 They took the boy home alive and were greatly comforted.
Acts20:13 We went on ahead to the ship and put out to sea for Assos
Acts20:14 When he met us in Assos
Acts20:15 We set sail from there
Acts20:16 For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so as not to spend time in the province of Asia
Acts20:17 From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus
Acts20:18 When they arrived
Acts20:19 serving the Lord with all humility and with tears
Acts20:20 You know that I did not hold back from proclaiming to you anything that would be helpful
Acts20:21 testifying to both Jews and Greeks about repentance toward God and faith in our Lord Jesus.
Acts20:22 And now
Acts20:23 except that the Holy Spirit warns me in town after town that imprisonment and persecutions are waiting for me.
Acts20:24 But I do not consider my life worth anything to myself
Acts20:25 “And now I know that none of you among whom I went around proclaiming the kingdom will see me again.
Acts20:26 Therefore I declare to you today that I am innocent of the blood of you all.
Acts20:27 For I did not hold back from announcing to you the whole purpose of God.
Acts20:28 Watch out for yourselves and for all the flock of which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers
Acts20:29 I know that after I am gone fierce wolves will come in among you
Acts20:30 Even from among your own group men will arise
Acts20:31 Therefore be alert
Acts20:32 And now I entrust you to God and to the message of his grace. This message is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
Acts20:33 I have desired no one’s silver or gold or clothing.
Acts20:34 You yourselves know that these hands of mine provided for my needs and the needs of those who were with me.
Acts20:35 By all these things
Acts20:36 When he had said these things
Acts20:37 They all began to weep loudly
Acts20:38 especially saddened by what he had said
Acts21:1 After we tore ourselves away from them

Acts21:4 After we located the disciples
Acts21:5 When our time was over

Acts21:8 On the next day we left and came to Caesarea

Acts21:11 He came to us
Acts21:12 When we heard this
Acts21:13 Then Paul replied
Acts21:14 Because he could not be persuaded

Acts21:19 When Paul had greeted them

Acts21:28 shouting

Acts21:33 Then the commanding officer came up and arrested him and ordered him to be tied up with two chains; he then asked who he was and what he had done.

Acts24:13 nor can they prove to you the things they are accusing me of doing.
Acts24:14 But I confess this to you
Acts24:15 I have a hope in God (a hope that these men themselves accept too) that there is going to be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
Acts24:16 This is the reason I do my best to always have a clear conscience toward God and toward people.
Acts24:17 After several years I came to bring to my people gifts for the poor and to present offerings
Acts24:18 which I was doing when they found me in the temple
Acts24:19 But there are some Jews from the province of Asia who should be here before you and bring charges
Acts24:20 Or these men here should tell what crime they found me guilty of when I stood before the council

Acts25:1 Now three days after Festus arrived in the province
Acts25:2 So the chief priests and the most prominent men of the Jews brought formal charges against Paul to him.

Acts25:4 Then Festus replied that Paul was being kept at Caesarea

Acts25:11 If then I am in the wrong and have done anything that deserves death
Acts25:12 Then
Acts25:13 After several days had passed
Acts25:14 While they were staying there many days
Acts25:15 When I was in Jerusalem
Acts25:16 I answered them that it was not the custom of the Romans to hand over anyone before the accused had met his accusers face to face and had been given an opportunity to make a defense against the accusation.
Acts25:17 So after they came back here with me
Acts25:18 When his accusers stood up
Acts25:19 Rather they had several points of disagreement with him about their own religion and about a man named Jesus who was dead
Acts25:20 Because I was at a loss how I could investigate these matters

Acts25:26 But I have nothing definite to write to my lord about him. Therefore I have brought him before you all
Acts25:27 For it seems unreasonable to me to send a prisoner without clearly indicating the charges against him.”
Acts26:1 So Agrippa said to Paul
Acts26:2 “Regarding all the things I have been accused of by the Jews
Acts26:3 because you are especially familiar with all the customs and controversial issues of the Jews. Therefore I ask you to listen to me patiently.
Acts26:4 Now all the Jews know the way I lived from my youth
Acts26:5 They know
Acts26:6 And now I stand here on trial because of my hope in the promise made by God to our ancestors
Acts26:7 a promise that our twelve tribes hope to attain as they earnestly serve God night and day. Concerning this hope the Jews are accusing me
Acts26:8 Why do you people think it is unbelievable that God raises the dead?
Acts26:9 Of course
Acts26:10 And that is what I did in Jerusalem: Not only did I lock up many of the saints in prisons by the authority I received from the chief priests
Acts26:11 I punished them often in all the synagogues and tried to force them to blaspheme. Because I was so furiously enraged at them
Acts26:12 “While doing this very thing
Acts26:13 about noon along the road
Acts26:14 When we had all fallen to the ground
Acts26:15 So I said
Acts26:16 But get up and stand on your feet
Acts26:17 I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles
Acts26:18 to open their eyes so that they turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God
Acts26:19 “Therefore
Acts26:20 but I declared to those in Damascus first
Acts26:21 For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple courts and were trying to kill me.
Acts26:22 I have experienced help from God to this day
Acts26:23 that the Christ was to suffer and be the first to rise from the dead

Acts26:25 But Paul replied
Acts26:26 For the king knows about these things
Acts26:27 Do you believe the prophets
Acts26:28 Agrippa said to Paul
Acts26:29 Paul replied
Acts26:30 So the king got up

Acts27:1 When it was decided we would sail to Italy
Acts27:2 We went on board a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to various ports along the coast of the province of Asia and put out to sea
Acts27:3 The next day we put in at Sidon
Acts27:4 From there we put out to sea and sailed under the lee of Cyprus because the winds were against us.
Acts27:5 After we had sailed across the open sea off Cilicia and Pamphylia
Acts27:6 There the centurion found a ship from Alexandria sailing for Italy
Acts27:7 We sailed slowly for many days and arrived with difficulty off Cnidus. Because the wind prevented us from going any farther
Acts27:8 With difficulty we sailed along the coast of Crete and came to a place called Fair Havens that was near the town of Lasea.
Acts27:9 Since considerable time had passed and the voyage was now dangerous because the fast was already over
Acts27:10 “Men
Acts27:11 But the centurion was more convinced by the captain and the ship’s owner than by what Paul said.
Acts27:12 Because the harbor was not suitable to spend the winter in
Acts27:13 When a gentle south wind sprang up
Acts27:14 Not long after this
Acts27:15 When the ship was caught in it and could not head into the wind
Acts27:16 As we ran under the lee of a small island called Cauda
Acts27:17 After the crew had hoisted it aboard
Acts27:18 The next day
Acts27:19 and on the third day they threw the ship’s gear overboard with their own hands.
Acts27:20 When neither sun nor stars appeared for many days and a violent storm continued to batter us
Acts27:21 Since many of them had no desire to eat
Acts27:22 And now I advise you to keep up your courage
Acts27:23 For last night an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I serve came to me
Acts27:24 and said
Acts27:25 Therefore keep up your courage
Acts27:26 But we must run aground on some island.”
Acts27:27 When the fourteenth night had come
Acts27:28 They took soundings and found the water was twenty fathoms deep; when they had sailed a little farther they took soundings again and found it was fifteen fathoms deep.
Acts27:29 Because they were afraid that we would run aground on the rocky coast
Acts27:30 Then when the sailors tried to escape from the ship and were lowering the ship’s boat into the sea
Acts27:31 Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers
Acts27:32 Then the soldiers cut the ropes of the ship’s boat and let it drift away.
Acts27:33 As day was about to dawn
Acts27:34 Therefore I urge you to take some food
Acts27:35 After he said this
Acts27:36 So all of them were encouraged and took food themselves.
Acts27:37 (We were in all two hundred seventy-six persons on the ship.)
Acts27:38 When they had eaten enough to be satisfied
Acts27:39 When day came
Acts27:40 So they slipped the anchors and left them in the sea
Acts27:41 But they encountered a patch of crosscurrents and ran the ship aground; the bow stuck fast and could not be moved
Acts27:42 Now the soldiers’ plan was to kill the prisoners so that none of them would escape by swimming away.
Acts27:43 But the centurion
Acts27:44 and the rest were to follow
Acts28:1 After we had safely reached shore
Acts28:2 The local inhabitants showed us extraordinary kindness
Acts28:3 When Paul had gathered a bundle of brushwood and was putting it on the fire
Acts28:4 When the local people saw the creature hanging from Paul’s hand
Acts28:5 However
Acts28:6 But they were expecting that he was going to swell up or suddenly drop dead. So after they had waited a long time and had seen nothing unusual happen to him
Acts28:7 Now in the region around that place were fields belonging to the chief official of the island
Acts28:8 The father of Publius lay sick in bed
Acts28:9 After this had happened
Acts28:10 They also bestowed many honors
Acts28:11 After three months we put out to sea in an Alexandrian ship that had wintered at the island and had the “Heavenly Twins” as its figurehead.
Acts28:12 We put in at Syracuse and stayed there three days.
Acts28:13 From there we cast off and arrived at Rhegium
Acts28:14 There we found some brothers and were invited to stay with them seven days. And in this way we came to Rome.
Acts28:15 The brothers from there
Acts28:16 When we entered Rome
Acts28:17 After three days Paul called the local Jewish leaders together. When they had assembled
Acts28:18 When they had heard my case
Acts28:19 But when the Jews objected
Acts28:20 So for this reason I have asked to see you and speak with you
Acts28:21 They replied
Acts28:22 But we would like to hear from you what you think
Acts28:23 They set a day to meet with him
Acts28:24 Some were convinced by what he said
Acts28:25 So they began to leave
Acts28:26 when he said
Acts28:27 For the heart of this people has become dull
Acts28:28 “Therefore be advised that this salvation from God has been sent to the Gentiles; they will listen!”
Acts28:29 ***
Acts28:30 Paul lived there two whole years in his own rented quarters and welcomed all who came to him
Acts28:31 proclaiming the kingdom of God and teaching about the Lord Jesus Christ with complete boldness and without restriction.
Romans1:1 From Paul
Romans1:2 This gospel he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy scriptures
Romans1:3 concerning his Son who was a descendant of David with reference to the flesh
Romans1:4 who was appointed the Son-of-God-in-power according to the Holy Spirit by the resurrection from the dead
Romans1:5 Through him we have received grace and our apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles on behalf of his name.
Romans1:6 You also are among them
Romans1:7 To all those loved by God in Rome
Romans1:8 First of all
Romans1:9 For God
Romans1:10 and I always ask in my prayers
Romans1:11 For I long to see you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 I do not want you to be unaware
Romans1:14 I am a debtor both to the Greeks and to the barbarians
Romans1:15 Thus I am eager also to preach the gospel to you who are in Rome.
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel
Romans1:17 For the righteousness of God is revealed in the gospel from faith to faith
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of people who suppress the truth by their unrighteousness
Romans1:19 because what can be known about God is plain to them
Romans1:20 For since the creation of the world his invisible attributes — his eternal power and divine nature — have been clearly seen
Romans1:21 For although they knew God
Romans1:22 Although they claimed to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for an image resembling mortal human beings or birds or four-footed animals or reptiles.
Romans1:24 Therefore God gave them over in the desires of their hearts to impurity
Romans1:25 They exchanged the truth of God for a lie and worshiped and served the creation rather than the Creator
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them over to dishonorable passions. For their women exchanged the natural sexual relations for unnatural ones
Romans1:27 and likewise the men also abandoned natural relations with women and were inflamed in their passions for one another. Men committed shameless acts with men and received in themselves the due penalty for their error.
Romans1:28 And just as they did not see fit to acknowledge God
Romans1:29 They are filled with every kind of unrighteousness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 senseless
Romans1:32 Although they fully know God’s righteous decree that those who practice such things deserve to die
Romans2:1 Therefore you are without excuse
Romans2:2 Now we know that God’s judgment is in accordance with truth against those who practice such things.
Romans2:3 And do you think
Romans2:4 Or do you have contempt for the wealth of his kindness
Romans2:5 But because of your stubbornness and your unrepentant heart
Romans2:6 He will reward each one according to his works:
Romans2:7 eternal life to those who by perseverance in good works seek glory and honor and immortality
Romans2:8 but wrath and anger to those who live in selfish ambition and do not obey the truth but follow unrighteousness.
Romans2:9 There will be affliction and distress on everyone who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good
Romans2:11 For there is no partiality with God.
Romans2:12 For all who have sinned apart from the law will also perish apart from the law
Romans2:13 For it is not those who hear the law who are righteous before God
Romans2:14 For whenever the Gentiles
Romans2:15 They show that the work of the law is written in their hearts
Romans2:16 on the day when God will judge the secrets of human hearts
Romans2:17 But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast of your relationship to God
Romans2:18 and know his will and approve the superior things because you receive instruction from the law
Romans2:19 and if you are convinced that you yourself are a guide to the blind
Romans2:20 an educator of the senseless
Romans2:21 therefore you who teach someone else
Romans2:22 You who tell others not to commit adultery
Romans2:23 You who boast in the law dishonor God by transgressing the law!
Romans2:24 For just as it is written
Romans2:25 For circumcision has its value if you practice the law
Romans2:26 Therefore if the uncircumcised man obeys the righteous requirements of the law
Romans2:27 And will not the physically uncircumcised man who keeps the law judge you who
Romans2:28 For a person is not a Jew who is one outwardly
Romans2:29 but someone is a Jew who is one inwardly
Romans3:1 Therefore what advantage does the Jew have
Romans3:2 Actually
Romans3:3 What then? If some did not believe
Romans3:4 Absolutely not! Let God be proven true
Romans3:5 But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 Absolutely not! For otherwise how could God judge the world?
Romans3:7 For if by my lie the truth of God enhances his glory
Romans3:8 And why not say
Romans3:9 What then? Are we better off? Certainly not
Romans3:10 just as it is written: “ There is no one righteous
Romans3:11 there is no one who understands
Romans3:12 All have turned away
Romans3:13 “ Their throats are open graves
Romans3:14 “ Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
Romans3:15 “ Their feet are swift to shed blood
Romans3:16 ruin and misery are in their paths
Romans3:17 and the way of peace they have not known.”
Romans3:18 “ There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says
Romans3:20 For no one is declared righteous before him by the works of the law
Romans3:21 But now apart from the law the righteousness of God (which is attested by the law and the prophets) has been disclosed —
Romans3:22 namely
Romans3:23 for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
Romans3:24 But they are justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus.
Romans3:25 God publicly displayed him at his death as the mercy seat accessible through faith. This was to demonstrate his righteousness
Romans3:26 This was also to demonstrate his righteousness in the present time
Romans3:27 Where
Romans3:28 For we consider that a person is declared righteous by faith apart from the works of the law.
Romans3:29 Or is God the God of the Jews only? Is he not the God of the Gentiles too? Yes
Romans3:30 Since God is one
Romans3:31 Do we then nullify the law through faith? Absolutely not! Instead we uphold the law.
Romans4:1 What then shall we say that Abraham
Romans4:2 For if Abraham was declared righteous by the works of the law
Romans4:3 For what does the scripture say? “ Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now to the one who works
Romans4:5 But to the one who does not work
Romans4:6 So even David himself speaks regarding the blessedness of the man to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 “ Blessed are those whose lawless deeds are forgiven
Romans4:8 blessed is the one against whom the Lord will never count sin.”
Romans4:9 Is this blessedness then for the circumcision or also for the uncircumcision? For we say
Romans4:10 How then was it credited to him? Was he circumcised at the time
Romans4:11 And he received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised
Romans4:12 And he is also the father of the circumcised
Romans4:13 For the promise to Abraham or to his descendants that he would inherit the world was not fulfilled through the law
Romans4:14 For if they become heirs by the law
Romans4:15 For the law brings wrath
Romans4:16 For this reason it is by faith so that it may be by grace
Romans4:17 (as it is written
Romans4:18 Against hope Abraham believed in hope with the result that he became the father of many nations according to the pronouncement
Romans4:19 Without being weak in faith
Romans4:20 He did not waver in unbelief about the promise of God but was strengthened in faith
Romans4:21 He was fully convinced that what God promised he was also able to do.
Romans4:22 So indeed it was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
Romans4:23 But the statement it was credited to him was not written only for Abraham’s sake
Romans4:24 but also for our sake
Romans4:25 He was given over because of our transgressions and was raised for the sake of our justification.
Romans5:1 Therefore
Romans5:2 through whom we have also obtained access by faith into this grace in which we stand
Romans5:3 Not only this
Romans5:4 and endurance
Romans5:5 And hope does not disappoint
Romans5:6 For while we were still helpless
Romans5:7 (For rarely will anyone die for a righteous person
Romans5:8 But God demonstrates his own love for us
Romans5:9 Much more then
Romans5:10 For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son
Romans5:11 Not only this
Romans5:12 So then
Romans5:13 for before the law was given
Romans5:14 Yet death reigned from Adam until Moses even over those who did not sin in the same way that Adam (who is a type of the coming one) transgressed.
Romans5:15 But the gracious gift is not like the transgression. For if the many died through the transgression of the one man
Romans5:16 And the gift is not like the one who sinned. For judgment
Romans5:17 For if
Romans5:18 Consequently
Romans5:19 For just as through the disobedience of the one man many were made sinners
Romans5:20 Now the law came in so that the transgression may increase
Romans5:21 so that just as sin reigned in death
Romans6:1 What shall we say then? Are we to remain in sin so that grace may increase?
Romans6:2 Absolutely not! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
Romans6:3 Or do you not know that as many as were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
Romans6:4 Therefore we have been buried with him through baptism into death
Romans6:5 For if we have become united with him in the likeness of his death
Romans6:6 We know that our old man was crucified with him so that the body of sin would no longer dominate us
Romans6:7 (For someone who has died has been freed from sin.)
Romans6:8 Now if we died with Christ
Romans6:9 We know that since Christ has been raised from the dead
Romans6:10 For the death he died
Romans6:11 So you too consider yourselves dead to sin
Romans6:12 Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its desires
Romans6:13 and do not present your members to sin as instruments to be used for unrighteousness
Romans6:14 For sin will have no mastery over you
Romans6:15 What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? Absolutely not!
Romans6:16 Do you not know that if you present yourselves as obedient slaves
Romans6:17 But thanks be to God that though you were slaves to sin
Romans6:18 and having been freed from sin
Romans6:19 (I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh.) For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and lawlessness leading to more lawlessness
Romans6:20 For when you were slaves of sin
Romans6:21 So what benefit did you then reap from those things that you are now ashamed of? For the end of those things is death.
Romans6:22 But now
Romans6:23 For the payoff of sin is death

Romans8:1 There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
Romans8:2 For the law of the life-giving Spirit in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.

Romans13:4 for it is God’s servant for your good. But if you do wrong

Romans14:5 One person regards one day holier than other days
Romans14:6 The one who observes the day does it for the Lord. The one who eats

Romans14:12 Therefore
Romans14:13 Therefore we must not pass judgment on one another

Romans14:16 Therefore do not let what you consider good be spoken of as evil.

Romans14:23 But the man who doubts is condemned if he eats

1Corinthians6:17 But the one united with the Lord is one spirit with him.
1Corinthians6:18 Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin a person commits is outside of the body” — but the immoral person sins against his own body.
1Corinthians6:19 Or do you not know that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you
1Corinthians6:20 For you were bought at a price. Therefore glorify God with your body.
1Corinthians7:1 Now with regard to the issues you wrote about: “It is good for a man not to have sexual relations with a woman.”
1Corinthians7:2 But because of immoralities
1Corinthians7:3 A husband should give to his wife her sexual rights
1Corinthians7:4 It is not the wife who has the rights to her own body
1Corinthians7:5 Do not deprive each other
1Corinthians7:6 I say this as a concession
1Corinthians7:7 I wish that everyone was as I am. But each has his own gift from God
1Corinthians7:8 To the unmarried and widows I say that it is best for them to remain as I am.
1Corinthians7:9 But if they do not have self-control
1Corinthians7:10 To the married I give this command — not I
1Corinthians7:11 (but if she does
1Corinthians7:12 To the rest I say — I
1Corinthians7:13 And if a woman has a husband who is not a believer and he is happy to live with her
1Corinthians7:14 For the unbelieving husband is sanctified because of the wife
1Corinthians7:15 But if the unbeliever wants a divorce
1Corinthians7:16 For how do you know
1Corinthians7:17 Nevertheless
1Corinthians7:18 Was anyone called after he had been circumcised? He should not try to undo his circumcision. Was anyone called who is uncircumcised? He should not get circumcised.
1Corinthians7:19 Circumcision is nothing and uncircumcision is nothing. Instead
1Corinthians7:20 Let each one remain in that situation in life in which he was called.
1Corinthians7:21 Were you called as a slave? Do not worry about it. But if indeed you are able to be free
1Corinthians7:22 For the one who was called in the Lord as a slave is the Lord’s freedman. In the same way
1Corinthians7:23 You were bought with a price. Do not become slaves of men.
1Corinthians7:24 In whatever situation someone was called
1Corinthians7:25 With regard to the question about people who have never married
1Corinthians7:26 Because of the impending crisis I think it best for you to remain as you are.
1Corinthians7:27 The one bound to a wife should not seek divorce. The one released from a wife should not seek marriage.
1Corinthians7:28 But if you marry
1Corinthians7:29 And I say this
1Corinthians7:30 those with tears like those not weeping
1Corinthians7:31 those who use the world as though they were not using it to the full. For the present shape of this world is passing away.
1Corinthians7:32 And I want you to be free from concern. An unmarried man is concerned about the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:33 But a married man is concerned about the things of the world
1Corinthians7:34 and he is divided. An unmarried woman or a virgin is concerned about the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 I am saying this for your benefit
1Corinthians7:36 If anyone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward his virgin
1Corinthians7:37 But the man who is firm in his commitment
1Corinthians7:38 So then
1Corinthians7:39 A wife is bound as long as her husband is living. But if her husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 But in my opinion
1Corinthians8:1 With regard to food sacrificed to idols
1Corinthians8:2 If someone thinks he knows something
1Corinthians8:3 But if someone loves God
1Corinthians8:4 With regard then to eating food sacrificed to idols
1Corinthians8:5 If after all there are so-called gods
1Corinthians8:6 yet for us there is one God
1Corinthians8:7 But this knowledge is not shared by all. And some
1Corinthians8:8 Now food will not bring us close to God. We are no worse if we do not eat and no better if we do.
1Corinthians8:9 But be careful that this liberty of yours does not become a hindrance to the weak.
1Corinthians8:10 For if someone weak sees you who possess knowledge dining in an idol’s temple
1Corinthians8:11 So by your knowledge the weak brother or sister
1Corinthians8:12 If you sin against your brothers or sisters in this way and wound their weak conscience
1Corinthians8:13 For this reason
1Corinthians9:1 Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 If I am not an apostle to others
1Corinthians9:3 This is my defense to those who examine me.
1Corinthians9:4 Do we not have the right to financial support?
1Corinthians9:5 Do we not have the right to the company of a believing wife
1Corinthians9:6 Or do only Barnabas and I lack the right not to work?
1Corinthians9:7 Who ever serves in the army at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its fruit? Who tends a flock and does not consume its milk?
1Corinthians9:8 Am I saying these things only on the basis of common sense
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the law of Moses
1Corinthians9:10 Or is he not surely speaking for our benefit? It was written for us
1Corinthians9:11 If we sowed spiritual blessings among you
1Corinthians9:12 If others receive this right from you
1Corinthians9:13 Don’t you know that those who serve in the temple eat food from the temple
1Corinthians9:14 In the same way the Lord commanded those who proclaim the gospel to receive their living by the gospel.
1Corinthians9:15 But I have not used any of these rights. And I am not writing these things so that something will be done for me. In fact
1Corinthians9:16 For if I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this voluntarily
1Corinthians9:18 What then is my reward? That when I preach the gospel I may offer the gospel free of charge
1Corinthians9:19 For since I am free from all I can make myself a slave to all
1Corinthians9:20 To the Jews I became like a Jew to gain the Jews. To those under the law I became like one under the law (though I myself am not under the law) to gain those under the law.
1Corinthians9:21 To those free from the law I became like one free from the law (though I am not free from God’s law but under the law of Christ) to gain those free from the law.
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak in order to gain the weak. I have become all things to all people
1Corinthians9:23 I do all these things because of the gospel
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that all the runners in a stadium compete
1Corinthians9:25 Each competitor must exercise self-control in everything. They do it to receive a perishable crown
1Corinthians9:26 So I do not run uncertainly or box like one who hits only air.
1Corinthians9:27 Instead I subdue my body and make it my slave

1Corinthians10:5 But God was not pleased with most of them

1Corinthians10:12 So let the one who thinks he is standing be careful that he does not fall.

1Corinthians10:14 So then
1Corinthians10:15 I am speaking to thoughtful people. Consider what I say.

1Corinthians10:17 Because there is one bread

1Corinthians10:20 No

1Corinthians10:24 Do not seek your own good

1Corinthians10:28 But if someone says to you

1Corinthians15:46 However

2Corinthians1:3 Blessed is the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
2Corinthians1:4 who comforts us in all our troubles so that we may be able to comfort those experiencing any trouble with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
2Corinthians1:5 For just as the sufferings of Christ overflow toward us
2Corinthians1:6 But if we are afflicted
2Corinthians1:7 And our hope for you is steadfast because we know that as you share in our sufferings
2Corinthians1:8 For we do not want you to be unaware
2Corinthians1:9 Indeed we felt as if the sentence of death had been passed against us
2Corinthians1:10 He delivered us from so great a risk of death
2Corinthians1:11 as you also join in helping us by prayer
2Corinthians1:12 For our reason for confidence is this: the testimony of our conscience
2Corinthians1:13 For we do not write you anything other than what you can read and also understand. But I hope that you will understand completely
2Corinthians1:14 just as also you have partly understood us
2Corinthians1:15 And with this confidence I intended to come to you first so that you would get a second opportunity to see us
2Corinthians1:16 and through your help to go on into Macedonia and then from Macedonia to come back to you and be helped on our way into Judea by you.
2Corinthians1:17 Therefore when I was planning to do this
2Corinthians1:18 But as God is faithful
2Corinthians1:19 For the Son of God
2Corinthians1:20 For every one of God’s promises are “Yes” in him; therefore also through him the “Amen” is spoken
2Corinthians1:21 But it is God who establishes us together with you in Christ and who anointed us
2Corinthians1:22 who also sealed us and gave us the Spirit in our hearts as a down payment.
2Corinthians1:23 Now I appeal to God as my witness
2Corinthians1:24 I do not mean that we rule over your faith

2Corinthians2:7 so that now instead you should rather forgive and comfort him. This will keep him from being overwhelmed by excessive grief to the point of despair.
2Corinthians2:8 Therefore I urge you to reaffirm your love for him.
2Corinthians2:9 For this reason also I wrote you: to test you to see if you are obedient in everything.

2Corinthians2:11 so that we may not be exploited by Satan (for we are not ignorant of his schemes).

2Corinthians2:16 to the latter an odor from death to death

Galatians1:1 From Paul
Galatians1:2 and all the brothers with me
Galatians1:3 Grace and peace to you from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ
Galatians1:4 who gave himself for our sins to rescue us from this present evil age according to the will of our God and Father
Galatians1:5 to whom be glory forever and ever! Amen.
Galatians1:6 I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ and are following a different gospel —
Galatians1:7 not that there really is another gospel
Galatians1:8 But even if we (or an angel from heaven) should preach a gospel contrary to the one we preached to you
Galatians1:9 As we have said before
Galatians1:10 Am I now trying to gain the approval of people
Galatians1:11 Now I want you to know
Galatians1:12 For I did not receive it or learn it from any human source; instead I received it by a revelation of Jesus Christ.
Galatians1:13 For you have heard of my former way of life in Judaism
Galatians1:14 I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries in my nation
Galatians1:15 But when the one who set me apart from birth and called me by his grace was pleased
Galatians1:16 to reveal his Son in me so that I could preach him among the Gentiles
Galatians1:17 nor did I go up to Jerusalem to see those who were apostles before me
Galatians1:18 Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and get information from him
Galatians1:19 But I saw none of the other apostles except James the Lord’s brother.
Galatians1:20 I assure you that
Galatians1:21 Afterward I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
Galatians1:22 But I was personally unknown to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.
Galatians1:23 They were only hearing
Galatians1:24 So they glorified God because of me.

Galatians2:6 But from those who were influential (whatever they were makes no difference to me; God shows no favoritism between people ) — those influential leaders added nothing to my message.
Galatians2:7 On the contrary
Galatians2:8 (for he who empowered Peter for his apostleship to the circumcised also empowered me for my apostleship to the Gentiles)

Galatians2:13 And the rest of the Jews also joined with him in this hypocrisy

Galatians2:16 yet we know that no one is justified by the works of the law but by the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. And we have come to believe in Christ Jesus
