Galatians3:1 You foolish Galatians! Who has cast a spell on you? Before your eyes Jesus Christ was vividly portrayed as crucified!
Galatians3:2 The only thing I want to learn from you is this: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the law or by believing what you heard?
Galatians3:3 Are you so foolish? Although you began with the Spirit
Galatians3:4 Have you suffered so many things for nothing? — if indeed it was for nothing.
Galatians3:5 Does God then give you the Spirit and work miracles among you by your doing the works of the law or by your believing what you heard?
Galatians3:6 Just as Abraham believed God
Galatians3:7 so then
Galatians3:8 And the scripture
Galatians3:9 So then those who believe are blessed along with Abraham the believer.
Galatians3:10 For all who rely on doing the works of the law are under a curse
Galatians3:11 Now it is clear no one is justified before God by the law
Galatians3:12 But the law is not based on faith
Galatians3:13 Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us (because it is written
Galatians3:14 in order that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles
Galatians3:15 Brothers and sisters
Galatians3:16 Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his descendant. Scripture does not say
Galatians3:17 What I am saying is this: The law that came four hundred thirty years later does not cancel a covenant previously ratified by God
Galatians3:18 For if the inheritance is based on the law
Galatians3:19 Why then was the law given? It was added because of transgressions
Galatians3:20 Now an intermediary is not for one party alone
Galatians3:21 Is the law therefore opposed to the promises of God? Absolutely not! For if a law had been given that was able to give life
Galatians3:22 But the scripture imprisoned everything and everyone under sin so that the promise could be given — because of the faithfulness of Jesus Christ — to those who believe.
Galatians3:23 Now before faith came we were held in custody under the law
Galatians3:24 Thus the law had become our guardian until Christ
Galatians3:25 But now that faith has come
Galatians3:26 For in Christ Jesus you are all sons of God through faith.
Galatians3:27 For all of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
Galatians3:28 There is neither Jew nor Greek
Galatians3:29 And if you belong to Christ

Galatians6:2 Carry one another’s burdens
Galatians6:3 For if anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing
Galatians6:4 Let each one examine his own work. Then he can take pride in himself and not compare himself with someone else.
Galatians6:5 For each one will carry his own load.
Galatians6:6 Now the one who receives instruction in the word must share all good things with the one who teaches it.
Galatians6:7 Do not be deceived. God will not be made a fool. For a person will reap what he sows
Galatians6:8 because the person who sows to his own flesh will reap corruption from the flesh
Galatians6:9 So we must not grow weary in doing good
Galatians6:10 So then
Galatians6:11 See what big letters I make as I write to you with my own hand!

Galatians6:13 For those who are circumcised do not obey the law themselves

Galatians6:17 From now on let no one cause me trouble

Ephesians1:1 From Paul
Ephesians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
Ephesians1:3 Blessed is the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:4 For he chose us in Christ before the foundation of the world that we may be holy and unblemished in his sight in love.
Ephesians1:5 He did this by predestining us to adoption as his sons through Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:6 to the praise of the glory of his grace that he has freely bestowed on us in his dearly loved Son.
Ephesians1:7 In him we have redemption through his blood
Ephesians1:8 that he lavished on us in all wisdom and insight.
Ephesians1:9 He did this when he revealed to us the secret of his will
Ephesians1:10 toward the administration of the fullness of the times
Ephesians1:11 In Christ we too have been claimed as God’s own possession
Ephesians1:12 so that we
Ephesians1:13 And when you heard the word of truth (the gospel of your salvation) — when you believed in Christ — you were marked with the seal of the promised Holy Spirit
Ephesians1:14 who is the down payment of our inheritance
Ephesians1:15 For this reason
Ephesians1:16 I do not cease to give thanks for you when I remember you in my prayers.
Ephesians1:17 I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:18 — since the eyes of your heart have been enlightened — so that you may know what is the hope of his calling
Ephesians1:19 and what is the incomparable greatness of his power toward us who believe
Ephesians1:20 This power he exercised in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly realms
Ephesians1:21 far above every rule and authority and power and dominion and every name that is named
Ephesians1:22 And God put all things under Christ’s feet
Ephesians1:23 Now the church is his body
Ephesians2:1 And although you were dead in your transgressions and sins
Ephesians2:2 in which you formerly lived according to this world’s present path
Ephesians2:3 among whom all of us also formerly lived out our lives in the cravings of our flesh
Ephesians2:4 But God
Ephesians2:5 even though we were dead in transgressions
Ephesians2:6 and he raised us up with him and seated us with him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus
Ephesians2:7 to demonstrate in the coming ages the surpassing wealth of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
Ephesians2:8 For by grace you are saved through faith
Ephesians2:9 it is not from works
Ephesians2:10 For we are his workmanship
Ephesians2:11 Therefore remember that formerly you
Ephesians2:12 that you were at that time without the Messiah
Ephesians2:13 But now in Christ Jesus you who used to be far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
Ephesians2:14 For he is our peace
Ephesians2:15 when he nullified in his flesh the law of commandments in decrees. He did this to create in himself one new man out of two
Ephesians2:16 and to reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross
Ephesians2:17 And he came and preached peace to you who were far off and peace to those who were near
Ephesians2:18 so that through him we both have access in one Spirit to the Father.
Ephesians2:19 So then you are no longer foreigners and noncitizens
Ephesians2:20 because you have been built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets
Ephesians2:21 In him the whole building
Ephesians2:22 in whom you also are being built together into a dwelling place of God in the Spirit.
Ephesians3:1 For this reason I
Ephesians3:2 if indeed you have heard of the stewardship of God’s grace that was given to me for you
Ephesians3:3 that by revelation the divine secret was made known to me
Ephesians3:4 When reading this
Ephesians3:5 Now this secret was not disclosed to people in former generations as it has now been revealed to his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit
Ephesians3:6 namely
Ephesians3:7 I became a servant of this gospel according to the gift of God’s grace that was given to me by the exercise of his power.
Ephesians3:8 To me — less than the least of all the saints — this grace was given
Ephesians3:9 and to enlighten everyone about God’s secret plan — a secret that has been hidden for ages in God who has created all things.
Ephesians3:10 The purpose of this enlightenment is that through the church the multifaceted wisdom of God should now be disclosed to the rulers and the authorities in the heavenly realms.
Ephesians3:11 This was according to the eternal purpose that he accomplished in Christ Jesus our Lord
Ephesians3:12 in whom we have boldness and confident access to God because of Christ’s faithfulness.
Ephesians3:13 For this reason I ask you not to lose heart because of what I am suffering for you
Ephesians3:14 For this reason I kneel before the Father
Ephesians3:15 from whom every family in heaven and on the earth is named.
Ephesians3:16 I pray that according to the wealth of his glory he may grant you to be strengthened with power through his Spirit in the inner person
Ephesians3:17 that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith
Ephesians3:18 you may be able to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth
Ephesians3:19 and thus to know the love of Christ that surpasses knowledge
Ephesians3:20 Now to him who by the power that is working within us is able to do far beyond all that we ask or think
Ephesians3:21 to him be the glory in the church and in Christ Jesus to all generations
Ephesians4:1 I
Ephesians4:2 with all humility and gentleness
Ephesians4:3 making every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
Ephesians4:4 There is one body and one Spirit
Ephesians4:5 one Lord
Ephesians4:6 one God and Father of all
Ephesians4:7 But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
Ephesians4:8 Therefore it says
Ephesians4:9 Now what is the meaning of “ he ascended
Ephesians4:10 He
Ephesians4:11 It was he who gave some as apostles
Ephesians4:12 to equip the saints for the work of ministry
Ephesians4:13 until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God — a mature person
Ephesians4:14 So we are no longer to be children
Ephesians4:15 But practicing the truth in love
Ephesians4:16 From him the whole body grows
Ephesians4:17 So I say this
Ephesians4:18 They are darkened in their understanding
Ephesians4:19 Because they are callous
Ephesians4:20 But you did not learn about Christ like this
Ephesians4:21 if indeed you heard about him and were taught in him
Ephesians4:22 You were taught with reference to your former way of life to lay aside the old man who is being corrupted in accordance with deceitful desires
Ephesians4:23 to be renewed in the spirit of your mind
Ephesians4:24 and to put on the new man who has been created in God’s image — in righteousness and holiness that comes from truth.
Ephesians4:25 Therefore
Ephesians4:26 Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on the cause of your anger.
Ephesians4:27 Do not give the devil an opportunity.
Ephesians4:28 The one who steals must steal no longer; rather he must labor
Ephesians4:29 You must let no unwholesome word come out of your mouth
Ephesians4:30 And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God
Ephesians4:31 You must put away every kind of bitterness
Ephesians4:32 Instead
Ephesians5:1 Therefore
Ephesians5:2 and live in love
Ephesians5:3 But among you there must not be either sexual immorality
Ephesians5:4 Neither should there be vulgar speech
Ephesians5:5 For you can be confident of this one thing: that no person who is immoral
Ephesians5:6 Let nobody deceive you with empty words
Ephesians5:7 Therefore do not be partakers with them
Ephesians5:8 for you were at one time darkness
Ephesians5:9 for the fruit of the light consists in all goodness
Ephesians5:10 trying to learn what is pleasing to the Lord.
Ephesians5:11 Do not participate in the unfruitful deeds of darkness
Ephesians5:12 For the things they do in secret are shameful even to mention.
Ephesians5:13 But all things being exposed by the light are made evident.
Ephesians5:14 For everything made evident is light
Ephesians5:15 Therefore be very careful how you live — not as unwise but as wise
Ephesians5:16 taking advantage of every opportunity
Ephesians5:17 For this reason do not be foolish
Ephesians5:18 And do not get drunk with wine
Ephesians5:19 speaking to one another in psalms
Ephesians5:20 always giving thanks to God the Father for each other in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians5:21 and submitting to one another out of reverence for Christ.
Ephesians5:22 Wives
Ephesians5:23 because the husband is the head of the wife as also Christ is the head of the church — he himself being the savior of the body.
Ephesians5:24 But as the church submits to Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 to sanctify her by cleansing her with the washing of the water by the word
Ephesians5:27 so that he may present the church to himself as glorious — not having a stain or wrinkle
Ephesians5:28 In the same way husbands ought to love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
Ephesians5:29 For no one has ever hated his own body but he feeds it and takes care of it
Ephesians5:30 for we are members of his body.
Ephesians5:31 For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and will be joined to his wife
Ephesians5:32 This mystery is great — but I am actually speaking with reference to Christ and the church.
Ephesians5:33 Nevertheless
Ephesians6:1 Children
Ephesians6:2 “ Honor your father and mother
Ephesians6:3 “ that it may go well with you and that you will live a long time on the earth.”
Ephesians6:4 Fathers
Ephesians6:5 Slaves
Ephesians6:6 not like those who do their work only when someone is watching — as people-pleasers — but as slaves of Christ doing the will of God from the heart.
Ephesians6:7 Obey with enthusiasm
Ephesians6:8 because you know that each person
Ephesians6:9 Masters
Ephesians6:10 Finally
Ephesians6:11 Clothe yourselves with the full armor of God so that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.
Ephesians6:12 For our struggle is not against flesh and blood
Ephesians6:13 For this reason
Ephesians6:14 Stand firm therefore
Ephesians6:15 by fitting your feet with the preparation that comes from the good news of peace
Ephesians6:16 and in all of this
Ephesians6:17 And take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit
Ephesians6:18 With every prayer and petition
Ephesians6:19 Pray for me also
Ephesians6:20 for which I am an ambassador in chains. Pray that I may be able to speak boldly as I ought to speak.
Ephesians6:21 Tychicus
Ephesians6:22 I have sent him to you for this very purpose
Ephesians6:23 Peace to the brothers and sisters
Ephesians6:24 Grace be with all of those who love our Lord Jesus Christ with an undying love.
Philippians1:1 From Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
Philippians1:3 I thank my God every time I remember you.
Philippians1:4 I always pray with joy in my every prayer for all of you
Philippians1:5 because of your participation in the gospel from the first day until now.
Philippians1:6 For I am sure of this very thing
Philippians1:7 For it is right for me to think this about all of you
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness that I long for all of you with the affection of Christ Jesus.
Philippians1:9 And I pray this
Philippians1:10 so that you can decide what is best
Philippians1:11 filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ to the glory and praise of God.
Philippians1:12 I want you to know
Philippians1:13 The whole imperial guard and everyone else knows that I am in prison for the sake of Christ
Philippians1:14 and most of the brothers and sisters
Philippians1:15 Some
Philippians1:16 The latter do so from love because they know that I am placed here for the defense of the gospel.
Philippians1:17 The former proclaim Christ from selfish ambition
Philippians1:18 What is the result? Only that in every way
Philippians1:19 for I know that this will turn out for my deliverance through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:20 My confident hope is that I will in no way be ashamed but that with complete boldness
Philippians1:21 For to me
Philippians1:22 Now if I am to go on living in the body
Philippians1:23 I feel torn between the two
Philippians1:24 but it is more vital for your sake that I remain in the body.
Philippians1:25 And since I am sure of this
Philippians1:26 so that what you can be proud of may increase because of me in Christ Jesus
Philippians1:27 Only conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ so that — whether I come and see you or whether I remain absent — I should hear that you are standing firm in one spirit
Philippians1:28 and by not being intimidated in any way by your opponents. This is a sign of their destruction
Philippians1:29 For it has been granted to you not only to believe in Christ but also to suffer for him
Philippians1:30 since you are encountering the same conflict that you saw me face and now hear that I am facing.
Philippians2:1 Therefore
Philippians2:2 complete my joy and be of the same mind
Philippians2:3 Instead of being motivated by selfish ambition or vanity
Philippians2:4 Each of you should be concerned not only about your own interests
Philippians2:5 You should have the same attitude toward one another that Christ Jesus had
Philippians2:6 who though he existed in the form of God did not regard equality with God as something to be grasped
Philippians2:7 but emptied himself by taking on the form of a slave
Philippians2:8 He humbled himself
Philippians2:9 As a result God exalted him and gave him the name that is above every name
Philippians2:10 so that at the name of Jesus every knee will bow — in heaven and on earth and under the earth —
Philippians2:11 and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord to the glory of God the Father.
Philippians2:12 So then
Philippians2:13 for the one bringing forth in you both the desire and the effort — for the sake of his good pleasure — is God.
Philippians2:14 Do everything without grumbling or arguing
Philippians2:15 so that you may be blameless and pure
Philippians2:16 by holding on to the word of life so that on the day of Christ I will have a reason to boast that I did not run in vain nor labor in vain.
Philippians2:17 But even if I am being poured out like a drink offering on the sacrifice and service of your faith
Philippians2:18 And in the same way you also should be glad and rejoice together with me.
Philippians2:19 Now I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon
Philippians2:20 For there is no one here like him who will readily demonstrate his deep concern for you.
Philippians2:21 Others are busy with their own concerns
Philippians2:22 But you know his qualifications
Philippians2:23 So I hope to send him as soon as I know more about my situation
Philippians2:24 though I am confident in the Lord that I too will be coming to see you soon.
Philippians2:25 But for now I have considered it necessary to send Epaphroditus to you. For he is my brother
Philippians2:26 Indeed
Philippians2:27 In fact he became so ill that he nearly died. But God showed mercy to him — and not to him only
Philippians2:28 Therefore I am all the more eager to send him
Philippians2:29 So welcome him in the Lord with great joy
Philippians2:30 since it was because of the work of Christ that he almost died. He risked his life so that he could make up for your inability to serve me.
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Beware of the dogs
Philippians3:3 For we are the circumcision
Philippians3:4 — though mine too are significant. If someone thinks he has good reasons to put confidence in human credentials
Philippians3:5 I was circumcised on the eighth day
Philippians3:6 In my zeal for God I persecuted the church. According to the righteousness stipulated in the law I was blameless.
Philippians3:7 But these assets I have come to regard as liabilities because of Christ.
Philippians3:8 More than that
Philippians3:9 and be found in him
Philippians3:10 My aim is to know him
Philippians3:11 and so
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already attained this — that is
Philippians3:13 Brothers and sisters
Philippians3:14 with this goal in mind
Philippians3:15 Therefore let those of us who are “perfect” embrace this point of view. If you think otherwise
Philippians3:16 Nevertheless
Philippians3:17 Be imitators of me
Philippians3:18 For many live
Philippians3:19 Their end is destruction
Philippians3:20 But our citizenship is in heaven — and we also await a savior from there
Philippians3:21 who will transform these humble bodies of ours into the likeness of his glorious body by means of that power by which he is able to subject all things to himself.
Philippians4:1 So then
Philippians4:2 I appeal to Euodia and to Syntyche to agree in the Lord.
Philippians4:3 Yes
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always. Again I say
Philippians4:5 Let everyone see your gentleness. The Lord is near!
Philippians4:6 Do not be anxious about anything. Instead
Philippians4:7 And the peace of God that surpasses all understanding will guard your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 And what you learned and received and heard and saw in me
Philippians4:10 I have great joy in the Lord because now at last you have again expressed your concern for me. (Now I know you were concerned before but had no opportunity to do anything.)
Philippians4:11 I am not saying this because I am in need
Philippians4:12 I have experienced times of need and times of abundance. In any and every circumstance I have learned the secret of contentment
Philippians4:13 I am able to do all things through the one who strengthens me.
Philippians4:14 Nevertheless
Philippians4:15 And as you Philippians know
Philippians4:16 For even in Thessalonica on more than one occasion you sent something for my need.
Philippians4:17 I do not say this because I am seeking a gift. Rather
Philippians4:18 For I have received everything
Philippians4:19 And my God will supply your every need according to his glorious riches in Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 May glory be given to God our Father forever and ever. Amen.
Philippians4:21 Give greetings to all the saints in Christ Jesus. The brothers with me here send greetings.
Philippians4:22 All the saints greet you
Philippians4:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Colossians1:1 From Paul
Colossians1:2 to the saints
Colossians1:3 We always give thanks to God
Colossians1:4 since we heard about your faith in Christ Jesus and the love that you have for all the saints.
Colossians1:5 Your faith and love have arisen from the hope laid up for you in heaven
Colossians1:6 that has come to you. Just as in the entire world this gospel is bearing fruit and growing
Colossians1:7 You learned the gospel from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 who also told us of your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this reason we also
Colossians1:10 so that you may live worthily of the Lord and please him in all respects — bearing fruit in every good deed
Colossians1:11 being strengthened with all power according to his glorious might for the display of all patience and steadfastness
Colossians1:12 giving thanks to the Father who has qualified you to share in the saints’ inheritance in the light.
Colossians1:13 He delivered us from the power of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of the Son he loves
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 for all things in heaven and on earth were created by him — all things
Colossians1:17 He himself is before all things and all things are held together in him.
Colossians1:18 He is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For God was pleased to have all his fullness dwell in the Son
Colossians1:20 and through him to reconcile all things to himself by making peace through the blood of his cross — through him
Colossians1:21 And you were at one time strangers and enemies in your minds as expressed through your evil deeds
Colossians1:22 but now he has reconciled you by his physical body through death to present you holy
Colossians1:23 if indeed you remain in the faith
Colossians1:24 Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you
Colossians1:25 I became a servant of the church according to the stewardship from God — given to me for you — in order to complete the word of God
Colossians1:26 that is
Colossians1:27 God wanted to make known to them the glorious riches of this mystery among the Gentiles
Colossians1:28 We proclaim him by instructing and teaching all people with all wisdom so that we may present every person mature in Christ.
Colossians1:29 Toward this goal I also labor
Colossians2:1 For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you
Colossians2:2 My goal is that their hearts
Colossians2:3 in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 I say this so that no one will deceive you through arguments that sound reasonable.
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent from you in body
Colossians2:6 Therefore
Colossians2:7 rooted and built up in him and firm in your faith just as you were taught
Colossians2:8 Be careful not to allow anyone to captivate you through an empty
Colossians2:9 For in him all the fullness of deity lives in bodily form
Colossians2:10 and you have been filled in him
Colossians2:11 In him you also were circumcised — not
Colossians2:12 Having been buried with him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And even though you were dead in your transgressions and in the uncircumcision of your flesh
Colossians2:14 He has destroyed what was against us
Colossians2:15 Disarming the rulers and authorities
Colossians2:16 Therefore do not let anyone judge you with respect to food or drink
Colossians2:17 these are only the shadow of the things to come
Colossians2:18 Let no one who delights in humility and the worship of angels pass judgment on you. That person goes on at great lengths about what he has supposedly seen
Colossians2:19 He has not held fast to the head from whom the whole body
Colossians2:20 If you have died with Christ to the elemental spirits of the world
Colossians2:21 “Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!”
Colossians2:22 These are all destined to perish with use
Colossians2:23 Even though they have the appearance of wisdom with their self-imposed worship and false humility achieved by an unsparing treatment of the body — a wisdom with no true value — they in reality result in fleshly indulgence.
Colossians3:1 Therefore
Colossians3:2 Keep thinking about things above
Colossians3:3 for you have died and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
Colossians3:4 When Christ (who is your life) appears
Colossians3:5 So put to death whatever in your nature belongs to the earth: sexual immorality
Colossians3:6 Because of these things the wrath of God is coming on the sons of disobedience.
Colossians3:7 You also lived your lives in this way at one time
Colossians3:8 But now
Colossians3:9 Do not lie to one another since you have put off the old man with its practices
Colossians3:10 and have been clothed with the new man that is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of the one who created it.
Colossians3:11 Here there is neither Greek nor Jew
Colossians3:12 Therefore
Colossians3:13 bearing with one another and forgiving one another
Colossians3:14 And to all these virtues add love
Colossians3:15 Let the peace of Christ be in control in your heart (for you were in fact called as one body to this peace)
Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly
Colossians3:17 And whatever you do in word or deed
Colossians3:18 Wives
Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Slaves
Colossians3:23 Whatever you are doing
Colossians3:24 because you know that you will receive your inheritance from the Lord as the reward. Serve the Lord Christ.
Colossians3:25 For the one who does wrong will be repaid for his wrong
Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Be devoted to prayer
Colossians4:3 At the same time pray for us too
Colossians4:4 Pray that I may make it known as I should.
Colossians4:5 Conduct yourselves with wisdom toward outsiders
Colossians4:6 Let your speech always be gracious
Colossians4:7 Tychicus
Colossians4:8 I sent him to you for this very purpose
Colossians4:9 I sent him with Onesimus
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus
Colossians4:11 And Jesus who is called Justus also sends greetings. In terms of Jewish converts
Colossians4:12 Epaphras
Colossians4:13 For I can testify that he has worked hard for you and for those in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
Colossians4:14 Our dear friend Luke the physician and Demas greet you.
Colossians4:15 Give my greetings to the brothers and sisters who are in Laodicea and to Nympha and the church that meets in her house.
Colossians4:16 And after you have read this letter
Colossians4:17 And tell Archippus
Colossians4:18 I
1Thessalonians1:1 From Paul and Silvanus and Timothy
1Thessalonians1:2 We thank God always for all of you as we mention you constantly in our prayers
1Thessalonians1:3 because we recall in the presence of our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and endurance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians1:4 We know
1Thessalonians1:5 in that our gospel did not come to you merely in words
1Thessalonians1:6 And you became imitators of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 As a result you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For from you the message of the Lord has echoed forth not just in Macedonia and Achaia
1Thessalonians1:9 For people everywhere report how you welcomed us and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For you yourselves know
1Thessalonians2:2 But although we suffered earlier and were mistreated in Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For the appeal we make does not come from error or impurity or with deceit
1Thessalonians2:4 but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For we never appeared with flattering speech
1Thessalonians2:6 nor to seek glory from people
1Thessalonians2:7 although we could have imposed our weight as apostles of Christ; instead we became little children among you. Like a nursing mother caring for her own children
1Thessalonians2:8 with such affection for you we were happy to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own lives
1Thessalonians2:9 For you recall
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 As you know
1Thessalonians2:12 exhorting and encouraging you and insisting that you live in a way worthy of God who calls you to his own kingdom and his glory.
1Thessalonians2:13 And so we too constantly thank God that when you received God’s message that you heard from us
1Thessalonians2:14 For you became imitators
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets and persecuted us severely. They are displeasing to God and are opposed to all people
1Thessalonians2:16 because they hinder us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. Thus they constantly fill up their measure of sins
1Thessalonians2:17 But when we were separated from you
1Thessalonians2:18 For we wanted to come to you (I
1Thessalonians2:19 For who is our hope or joy or crown to boast of before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not of course you?
1Thessalonians2:20 For you are our glory and joy!
1Thessalonians3:1 So when we could bear it no longer
1Thessalonians3:2 We sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 so that no one would be shaken by these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are destined for this.
1Thessalonians3:4 For in fact when we were with you
1Thessalonians3:5 So when I could bear it no longer
1Thessalonians3:6 But now Timothy has come to us from you and given us the good news of your faith and love and that you always think of us with affection and long to see us just as we also long to see you!
1Thessalonians3:7 So in all our distress and affliction
1Thessalonians3:8 For now we are alive again
1Thessalonians3:9 For how can we thank God enough for you
1Thessalonians3:10 We pray earnestly night and day to see you in person and make up what may be lacking in your faith.
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may God our Father himself and our Lord Jesus direct our way to you.
1Thessalonians3:12 And may the Lord cause you to increase and abound in love for one another and for all
1Thessalonians3:13 so that your hearts are strengthened in holiness to be blameless before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally then
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know what commands we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is God’s will: that you become holy
1Thessalonians4:4 that each of you know how to possess his own body in holiness and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not in lustful passion like the Gentiles who do not know God.
1Thessalonians4:6 In this matter no one should violate the rights of his brother or take advantage of him
1Thessalonians4:7 For God did not call us to impurity but in holiness.
1Thessalonians4:8 Consequently the one who rejects this is not rejecting human authority but God
1Thessalonians4:9 Now on the topic of brotherly love you have no need for anyone to write you
1Thessalonians4:10 And indeed you are practicing it toward all the brothers and sisters in all of Macedonia. But we urge you
1Thessalonians4:11 to aspire to lead a quiet life
1Thessalonians4:12 In this way you will live a decent life before outsiders and not be in need.
1Thessalonians4:13 Now we do not want you to be uninformed
1Thessalonians4:14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For we tell you this by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself will come down from heaven with a shout of command
1Thessalonians4:17 Then we who are alive
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore encourage one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 Now on the topic of times and seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For you know quite well that the day of the Lord will come in the same way as a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 Now when they are saying
1Thessalonians5:4 But you
1Thessalonians5:5 For you all are sons of the light and sons of the day. We are not of the night nor of the darkness.
1Thessalonians5:6 So then we must not sleep as the rest
1Thessalonians5:7 For those who sleep
1Thessalonians5:8 But since we are of the day
1Thessalonians5:9 For God did not destine us for wrath but for gaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians5:10 He died for us so that whether we are alert or asleep we will come to life together with him.
1Thessalonians5:11 Therefore encourage one another and build up each other
1Thessalonians5:12 Now we ask you
1Thessalonians5:13 and to esteem them most highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 And we urge you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that no one pays back evil for evil to anyone
1Thessalonians5:16 Always rejoice
1Thessalonians5:17 constantly pray
1Thessalonians5:18 in everything give thanks. For this is God’s will for you in Christ Jesus.
1Thessalonians5:19 Do not extinguish the Spirit.
1Thessalonians5:20 Do not treat prophecies with contempt.
1Thessalonians5:21 But examine all things; hold fast to what is good.
1Thessalonians5:22 Stay away from every form of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 Now may the God of peace himself make you completely holy and may your spirit and soul and body be kept entirely blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians5:24 He who calls you is trustworthy
1Thessalonians5:25 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians5:26 Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
1Thessalonians5:27 I call on you solemnly in the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers and sisters.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
2Thessalonians1:1 From Paul and Silvanus and Timothy
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
2Thessalonians1:3 We ought to thank God always for you
2Thessalonians1:4 As a result we ourselves boast about you in the churches of God for your perseverance and faith in all the persecutions and afflictions you are enduring.
2Thessalonians1:5 This is evidence of God’s righteous judgment
2Thessalonians1:6 For it is right for God to repay with affliction those who afflict you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to you who are being afflicted to give rest together with us when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels.
2Thessalonians1:8 With flaming fire he will mete out punishment on those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
2Thessalonians1:9 They will undergo the penalty of eternal destruction
2Thessalonians1:10 when he comes to be glorified among his saints and admired on that day among all who have believed — and you did in fact believe our testimony.
2Thessalonians1:11 And in this regard we pray for you always
2Thessalonians1:12 that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now regarding the arrival of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered to be with him
2Thessalonians2:2 not to be easily shaken from your composure or disturbed by any kind of spirit or message or letter allegedly from us
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no one deceive you in any way. For that day will not arrive until the rebellion comes and the man of lawlessness is revealed
2Thessalonians2:4 He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship
2Thessalonians2:5 Surely you recall that I used to tell you these things while I was still with you.
2Thessalonians2:6 And so you know what holds him back
2Thessalonians2:7 For the hidden power of lawlessness is already at work. However
2Thessalonians2:8 and then the lawless one will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The arrival of the lawless one will be by Satan’s working with all kinds of miracles and signs and false wonders
2Thessalonians2:10 and with every kind of evil deception directed against those who are perishing
2Thessalonians2:11 Consequently God sends on them a deluding influence so that they will believe what is false.
2Thessalonians2:12 And so all of them who have not believed the truth but have delighted in evil will be condemned.
2Thessalonians2:13 But we ought to thank God always for you
2Thessalonians2:14 He called you to this salvation through our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 Therefore
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father
2Thessalonians2:17 encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good thing you do or say.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we may be delivered from perverse and evil people. For not all have faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 And we are confident about you in the Lord that you are both doing — and will do — what we are commanding.
2Thessalonians3:5 Now may the Lord direct your hearts toward the love of God and the endurance of Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 But we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For you know yourselves how you must imitate us
2Thessalonians3:8 and we did not eat anyone’s food without paying. Instead
2Thessalonians3:9 It was not because we do not have that right
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that some among you are living an undisciplined life
2Thessalonians3:12 Now such people we command and urge in the Lord Jesus Christ to work quietly and so provide their own food to eat.
2Thessalonians3:13 But you
2Thessalonians3:14 But if anyone does not obey our message through this letter
2Thessalonians3:15 Yet do not regard him as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 I
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 From Paul
1Timothy1:2 to Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I urged you when I was leaving for Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 nor to occupy themselves with myths and interminable genealogies. Such things promote useless speculations rather than God’s redemptive plan that operates by faith.
1Timothy1:5 But the aim of our instruction is love that comes from a pure heart
1Timothy1:6 Some have strayed from these and turned away to empty discussion.
1Timothy1:7 They want to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 But we know that the law is good if someone uses it legitimately
1Timothy1:9 realizing that law is not intended for a righteous person
1Timothy1:10 sexually immoral people
1Timothy1:11 This accords with the glorious gospel of the blessed God that was entrusted to me.
1Timothy1:12 I am grateful to the one who has strengthened me
1Timothy1:13 even though I was formerly a blasphemer and a persecutor
1Timothy1:14 and our Lord’s grace was abundant
1Timothy1:15 This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance: “Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners” — and I am the worst of them!
1Timothy1:16 But here is why I was treated with mercy: so that in me as the worst
1Timothy1:17 Now to the eternal king
1Timothy1:18 I put this charge before you
1Timothy1:19 To do this you must hold firmly to faith and a good conscience
1Timothy1:20 Among these are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 even for kings and all who are in authority
1Timothy2:3 Such prayer for all is good and welcomed before God our Savior
1Timothy2:4 since he wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God and one intermediary between God and humanity
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself as a ransom for all
1Timothy2:7 For this I was appointed a preacher and apostle — I am telling the truth; I am not lying — and a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
1Timothy2:8 So I want the men to pray in every place
1Timothy2:9 Likewise the women are to dress in suitable apparel
1Timothy2:10 but with good deeds
1Timothy2:11 A woman must learn quietly with all submissiveness.
1Timothy2:12 But I do not allow a woman to teach or exercise authority over a man. She must remain quiet.
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was formed first and then Eve.
1Timothy2:14 And Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 But she will be delivered through childbearing
1Timothy3:1 This saying is trustworthy: “If someone aspires to the office of overseer
1Timothy3:2 The overseer then must be above reproach
1Timothy3:3 not a drunkard
1Timothy3:4 He must manage his own household well and keep his children in control without losing his dignity.
1Timothy3:5 But if someone does not know how to manage his own household
1Timothy3:6 He must not be a recent convert or he may become arrogant and fall into the punishment that the devil will exact.
1Timothy3:7 And he must be well thought of by those outside the faith
1Timothy3:8 Deacons likewise must be dignified
1Timothy3:9 holding to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And these also must be tested first and then let them serve as deacons if they are found blameless.
1Timothy3:11 Likewise also their wives must be dignified
1Timothy3:12 Deacons must be husbands of one wife and good managers of their children and their own households.
1Timothy3:13 For those who have served well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 I hope to come to you soon
1Timothy3:15 in case I am delayed
1Timothy3:16 And we all agree
1Timothy4:1 Now the Spirit explicitly says that in the later times some will desert the faith and occupy themselves with deceiving spirits and demonic teachings
1Timothy4:2 influenced by the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared.
1Timothy4:3 They will prohibit marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
1Timothy4:4 For every creation of God is good and no food is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving.
1Timothy4:5 For it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.
1Timothy4:6 By pointing out such things to the brothers and sisters
1Timothy4:7 But reject those myths fit only for the godless and gullible
1Timothy4:8 For “physical exercise has some value
1Timothy4:9 This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance.
1Timothy4:10 In fact this is why we work hard and struggle
1Timothy4:11 Command and teach these things.
1Timothy4:12 Let no one look down on you because you are young
1Timothy4:13 Until I come
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the spiritual gift you have
1Timothy4:15 Take pains with these things; be absorbed in them
1Timothy4:16 Be conscientious about how you live and what you teach. Persevere in this

1Timothy5:2 older women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows who are truly in need.
1Timothy5:4 But if a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 But the widow who is truly in need
1Timothy5:6 But the one who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
1Timothy5:7 Reinforce these commands
1Timothy5:8 But if someone does not provide for his own
1Timothy5:9 No widow should be put on the list unless she is at least sixty years old
1Timothy5:10 and has a reputation for good works: as one who has raised children
1Timothy5:11 But do not accept younger widows on the list
1Timothy5:12 and so incur judgment for breaking their former pledge.
1Timothy5:13 And besides that
1Timothy5:14 So I want younger women to marry
1Timothy5:15 For some have already wandered away to follow Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If a believing woman has widows in her family
1Timothy5:17 Elders who provide effective leadership must be counted worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 For the scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Do not accept an accusation against an elder unless it can be confirmed by two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 Those guilty of sin must be rebuked before all
1Timothy5:21 Before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels
1Timothy5:22 Do not lay hands on anyone hastily and so identify with the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
1Timothy5:23 (Stop drinking just water
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some people are obvious
1Timothy5:25 Similarly good works are also obvious
1Timothy6:1 Those who are under the yoke as slaves must regard their own masters as deserving of full respect. This will prevent the name of God and Christian teaching from being discredited.
1Timothy6:2 But those who have believing masters must not show them less respect because they are brothers. Instead they are to serve all the more
1Timothy6:3 If someone spreads false teachings and does not agree with sound words (that is
1Timothy6:4 he is conceited and understands nothing
1Timothy6:5 and constant bickering by people corrupted in their minds and deprived of the truth
1Timothy6:6 Now godliness combined with contentment brings great profit.
1Timothy6:7 For we have brought nothing into this world and so we cannot take a single thing out either.
1Timothy6:8 But if we have food and shelter
1Timothy6:9 Those who long to be rich
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is the root of all evils. Some people in reaching for it have strayed from the faith and stabbed themselves with many pains.
1Timothy6:11 But you
1Timothy6:12 Compete well for the faith and lay hold of that eternal life you were called for and made your good confession for in the presence of many witnesses.
1Timothy6:13 I charge you before God who gives life to all things and Christ Jesus who made his good confession before Pontius Pilate
1Timothy6:14 to obey this command without fault or failure until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ
1Timothy6:15 — whose appearing the blessed and only Sovereign
1Timothy6:16 He alone possesses immortality and lives in unapproachable light
1Timothy6:17 Command those who are rich in this world’s goods not to be haughty or to set their hope on riches
1Timothy6:18 Tell them to do good
1Timothy6:19 In this way they will save up a treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the future and so lay hold of what is truly life.
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 By professing it
2Timothy1:1 From Paul
2Timothy1:2 to Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I am thankful to God
2Timothy1:4 As I remember your tears
2Timothy1:5 I recall your sincere faith that was alive first in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice
2Timothy1:6 Because of this I remind you to rekindle God’s gift that you possess through the laying on of my hands.
2Timothy1:7 For God did not give us a Spirit of fear but of power and love and self-control.
2Timothy1:8 So do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord or of me
2Timothy1:9 He is the one who saved us and called us with a holy calling
2Timothy1:10 but now made visible through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus. He has broken the power of death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel!
2Timothy1:11 For this gospel I was appointed a preacher and apostle and teacher.
2Timothy1:12 Because of this
2Timothy1:13 Hold to the standard of sound words that you heard from me and do so with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy1:14 Protect that good thing entrusted to you
2Timothy1:15 You know that everyone in the province of Asia deserted me
2Timothy1:16 May the Lord grant mercy to the family of Onesiphorus
2Timothy1:17 But when he arrived in Rome
2Timothy1:18 May the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well all the ways he served me in Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 So you
2Timothy2:2 And entrust what you heard me say in the presence of many others as witnesses to faithful people who will be competent to teach others as well.
2Timothy2:3 Take your share of suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
2Timothy2:4 No one in military service gets entangled in matters of everyday life; otherwise he will not please the one who recruited him.
2Timothy2:5 Also
2Timothy2:6 The farmer who works hard ought to have the first share of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Think about what I am saying and the Lord will give you understanding of all this.
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 for which I suffer hardship to the point of imprisonment as a criminal
2Timothy2:10 So I endure all things for the sake of those chosen by God
2Timothy2:11 This saying is trustworthy: If we died with him
2Timothy2:12 If we endure
2Timothy2:13 If we are unfaithful
2Timothy2:14 Remind people of these things and solemnly charge them before the Lord not to wrangle over words. This is of no benefit; it just brings ruin on those who listen.
2Timothy2:15 Make every effort to present yourself before God as a proven worker who does not need to be ashamed
2Timothy2:16 But avoid profane chatter
2Timothy2:17 and their message will spread its infection like gangrene. Hymenaeus and Philetus are in this group.
2Timothy2:18 They have strayed from the truth by saying that the resurrection has already occurred
2Timothy2:19 However
2Timothy2:20 Now in a wealthy home there are not only gold and silver vessels
2Timothy2:21 So if someone cleanses himself of such behavior
2Timothy2:22 But keep away from youthful passions
2Timothy2:23 But reject foolish and ignorant controversies
2Timothy2:24 And the Lord’s slave must not engage in heated disputes but be kind toward all
2Timothy2:25 correcting opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will grant them repentance and then knowledge of the truth
2Timothy2:26 and they will come to their senses and escape the devil’s trap where they are held captive to do his will.

2Timothy3:7 Such women are always seeking instruction
2Timothy3:8 And just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses
2Timothy3:9 But they will not go much further

2Timothy3:12 Now in fact all who want to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.

2Timothy3:14 You

2Timothy3:17 that the person dedicated to God may be capable and equipped for every good work.

2Timothy4:3 For there will be a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. Instead
2Timothy4:4 And they will turn away from hearing the truth
2Timothy4:5 You
2Timothy4:6 For I am already being poured out as an offering

2Timothy4:8 Finally the crown of righteousness is reserved for me. The Lord

2Timothy4:10 For Demas deserted me
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you
2Timothy4:12 Now I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 When you come
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me a great deal of harm. The Lord will repay him in keeping with his deeds.
2Timothy4:15 You be on guard against him too
2Timothy4:16 At my first defense no one appeared in my support; instead they all deserted me — may they not be held accountable for it.
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me
2Timothy4:18 The Lord will deliver me from every evil deed and will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory for ever and ever! Amen.

2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
Titus1:1 From Paul
Titus1:2 in hope of eternal life
Titus1:3 But now in his own time he has made his message evident through the preaching I was entrusted with according to the command of God our Savior.
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 The reason I left you in Crete was to set in order the remaining matters and to appoint elders in every town
Titus1:6 An elder must be blameless
Titus1:7 For the overseer must be blameless as one entrusted with God’s work
Titus1:8 Instead he must be hospitable
Titus1:9 He must hold firmly to the faithful message as it has been taught
Titus1:10 For there are many rebellious people
Titus1:11 who must be silenced because they mislead whole families by teaching for dishonest gain what ought not to be taught.
Titus1:12 A certain one of them
Titus1:13 Such testimony is true. For this reason rebuke them sharply that they may be healthy in the faith
Titus1:14 and not pay attention to Jewish myths and commands of people who reject the truth.
Titus1:15 All is pure to those who are pure. But to those who are corrupt and unbelieving
Titus1:16 They profess to know God but with their deeds they deny him
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 Older men are to be temperate
Titus2:3 Older women likewise are to exhibit behavior fitting for those who are holy
Titus2:4 In this way they will train the younger women to love their husbands
Titus2:5 to be self-controlled
Titus2:6 Encourage younger men likewise to be self-controlled
Titus2:7 showing yourself to be an example of good works in every way. In your teaching show integrity
Titus2:8 and a sound message that cannot be criticized
Titus2:9 Slaves are to be subject to their own masters in everything
Titus2:10 not pilfering
Titus2:11 For the grace of God has appeared
Titus2:12 It trains us to reject godless ways and worldly desires and to live self-controlled
Titus2:13 as we wait for the happy fulfillment of our hope in the glorious appearing of our great God and Savior
Titus2:14 He gave himself for us to set us free from every kind of lawlessness and to purify for himself a people who are truly his
Titus2:15 So communicate these things with the sort of exhortation or rebuke that carries full authority. Don’t let anyone look down on you.
Titus3:1 Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities
Titus3:2 They must not slander anyone
Titus3:3 For we too were once foolish
Titus3:4 But “when the kindness of God our Savior and his love for mankind appeared
Titus3:5 he saved us not by works of righteousness that we have done but on the basis of his mercy
Titus3:6 whom he poured out on us in full measure through Jesus Christ our Savior.
Titus3:7 And so
Titus3:8 This saying is trustworthy
Titus3:9 But avoid foolish controversies
Titus3:10 Reject a divisive person after one or two warnings.
Titus3:11 You know that such a person is twisted by sin and is conscious of it himself.
Titus3:12 When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you
Titus3:13 Make every effort to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; make sure they have what they need.
Titus3:14 Here is another way that our people can learn to engage in good works to meet pressing needs and so not be unfruitful.
Titus3:15 Everyone with me greets you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.

Philemon1:2 to Apphia our sister

Hebrews1:4 Thus he became so far better than the angels as he has inherited a name superior to theirs.
Hebrews1:5 For to which of the angels did God ever say
Hebrews1:6 But when he again brings his firstborn into the world
Hebrews1:7 And he says of the angels
Hebrews1:8 but of the Son he says
Hebrews1:9 You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness. So God
Hebrews1:10 And
Hebrews1:11 They will perish
Hebrews1:12 and like a robe you will fold them up and like a garment they will be changed
Hebrews1:13 But to which of the angels has he ever said
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits

Hebrews2:2 For if the message spoken through angels proved to be so firm that every violation or disobedience received its just penalty

Hebrews2:4 while God confirmed their witness with signs and wonders and various miracles and gifts of the Holy Spirit distributed according to his will.
Hebrews2:5 For he did not put the world to come

Hebrews2:11 For indeed he who makes holy and those being made holy all have the same origin
Hebrews2:12 saying

Hebrews3:2 who is faithful to the one who appointed him

Hebrews3:6 But Christ is faithful as a son over God’s house. We are of his house
