Hebrews3:8 “ Do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion
Hebrews3:9 “ There your fathers tested me and tried me
Hebrews3:10 “ Therefore
Hebrews3:11 “ As I swore in my anger
Hebrews3:12 See to it
Hebrews3:13 But exhort one another each day
Hebrews3:14 For we have become partners with Christ
Hebrews3:15 As it says
Hebrews3:16 For which ones heard and rebelled? Was it not all who came out of Egypt under Moses’ leadership?
Hebrews3:17 And against whom was God provoked for forty years? Was it not those who sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom did he swear they would never enter into his rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they could not enter because of unbelief.
Hebrews4:1 Therefore we must be wary that
Hebrews4:2 For we had good news proclaimed to us just as they did. But the message they heard did them no good
Hebrews4:3 For we who have believed enter that rest
Hebrews4:4 For he has spoken somewhere about the seventh day in this way: “ And God rested on the seventh day from all his works
Hebrews4:5 but to repeat the text cited earlier: “ They will never enter my rest! ”
Hebrews4:6 Therefore it remains for some to enter it
Hebrews4:7 So God again ordains a certain day
Hebrews4:8 For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 Consequently a Sabbath rest remains for the people of God.
Hebrews4:10 For the one who enters God’s rest has also rested from his works
Hebrews4:11 Thus we must make every effort to enter that rest
Hebrews4:12 For the word of God is living and active and sharper than any double-edged sword
Hebrews4:13 And no creature is hidden from God
Hebrews4:14 Therefore since we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens
Hebrews4:15 For we do not have a high priest incapable of sympathizing with our weaknesses
Hebrews4:16 Therefore let us confidently approach the throne of grace to receive mercy and find grace whenever we need help.
Hebrews5:1 For every high priest is taken from among the people and appointed to represent them before God
Hebrews5:2 He is able to deal compassionately with those who are ignorant and erring
Hebrews5:3 and for this reason he is obligated to make sin offerings for himself as well as for the people.
Hebrews5:4 And no one assumes this honor on his own initiative
Hebrews5:5 So also Christ did not glorify himself in becoming high priest
Hebrews5:6 as also in another place God says
Hebrews5:7 During his earthly life Christ offered both requests and supplications
Hebrews5:8 Although he was a son
Hebrews5:9 And by being perfected in this way
Hebrews5:10 and he was designated by God as high priest in the order of Melchizedek.
Hebrews5:11 On this topic we have much to say and it is difficult to explain
Hebrews5:12 For though you should in fact be teachers by this time
Hebrews5:13 For everyone who lives on milk is inexperienced in the message of righteousness
Hebrews5:14 But solid food is for the mature
Hebrews6:1 Therefore we must progress beyond the elementary instructions about Christ and move on to maturity
Hebrews6:2 teaching about baptisms
Hebrews6:3 And this is what we intend to do
Hebrews6:4 For it is impossible in the case of those who have once been enlightened
Hebrews6:5 tasted the good word of God and the miracles of the coming age
Hebrews6:6 and then have committed apostasy
Hebrews6:7 For the ground that has soaked up the rain that frequently falls on it and yields useful vegetation for those who tend it receives a blessing from God.
Hebrews6:8 But if it produces thorns and thistles
Hebrews6:9 But in your case
Hebrews6:10 For God is not unjust so as to forget your work and the love you have demonstrated for his name
Hebrews6:11 But we passionately want each of you to demonstrate the same eagerness for the fulfillment of your hope until the end
Hebrews6:12 so that you may not be sluggish
Hebrews6:13 Now when God made his promise to Abraham
Hebrews6:14 saying
Hebrews6:15 And so by persevering
Hebrews6:16 For people swear by something greater than themselves
Hebrews6:17 In the same way God wanted to demonstrate more clearly to the heirs of the promise that his purpose was unchangeable
Hebrews6:18 so that we who have found refuge in him may find strong encouragement to hold fast to the hope set before us through two unchangeable things
Hebrews6:19 We have this hope as an anchor for the soul
Hebrews6:20 where Jesus our forerunner entered on our behalf
Hebrews7:1 Now this Melchizedek
Hebrews7:2 To him also Abraham apportioned a tithe of everything. His name first means king of righteousness
Hebrews7:3 Without father
Hebrews7:4 But see how great he must be
Hebrews7:5 And those of the sons of Levi who receive the priestly office have authorization according to the law to collect a tithe from the people
Hebrews7:6 But Melchizedek who does not share their ancestry collected a tithe from Abraham and blessed the one who possessed the promise.
Hebrews7:7 Now without dispute the inferior is blessed by the superior
Hebrews7:8 and in one case tithes are received by mortal men
Hebrews7:9 And it could be said that Levi himself
Hebrews7:10 For he was still in his ancestor Abraham’s loins when Melchizedek met him.
Hebrews7:11 So if perfection had in fact been possible through the Levitical priesthood — for on that basis the people received the law — what further need would there have been for another priest to arise
Hebrews7:12 For when the priesthood changes
Hebrews7:13 Yet the one these things are spoken about belongs to a different tribe
Hebrews7:14 For it is clear that our Lord is descended from Judah
Hebrews7:15 And this is even clearer if another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek
Hebrews7:16 who has become a priest not by a legal regulation about physical descent but by the power of an indestructible life.
Hebrews7:17 For here is the testimony about him: “ You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”
Hebrews7:18 On the one hand a former command is set aside because it is weak and useless
Hebrews7:19 for the law made nothing perfect. On the other hand a better hope is introduced
Hebrews7:20 And since this was not done without a sworn affirmation — for the others have become priests without a sworn affirmation
Hebrews7:21 but Jesus did so with a sworn affirmation by the one who said to him
Hebrews7:22 accordingly Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
Hebrews7:23 And the others who became priests were numerous
Hebrews7:24 but he holds his priesthood permanently since he lives forever.
Hebrews7:25 So he is able to save completely those who come to God through him
Hebrews7:26 For it is indeed fitting for us to have such a high priest: holy
Hebrews7:27 He has no need to do every day what those priests do
Hebrews7:28 For the law appoints as high priests men subject to weakness
Hebrews8:1 Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have such a high priest
Hebrews8:2 a minister in the sanctuary and the true tabernacle that the Lord
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices. So this one too had to have something to offer.
Hebrews8:4 Now if he were on earth
Hebrews8:5 The place where they serve is a sketch and shadow of the heavenly sanctuary
Hebrews8:6 But now Jesus has obtained a superior ministry
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 But showing its fault
Hebrews8:9 “ It will not be like the covenant that I made with their fathers
Hebrews8:10 “ For this is the covenant that I will establish with the house of Israel after those days
Hebrews8:11 “ And there will be no need at all for each one to teach his countryman or each one to teach his brother saying
Hebrews8:12 “ For I will be merciful toward their evil deeds
Hebrews8:13 When he speaks of a new covenant
Hebrews9:1 Now the first covenant
Hebrews9:2 For a tent was prepared
Hebrews9:3 And after the second curtain there was a tent called the holy of holies.
Hebrews9:4 It contained the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered entirely with gold. In this ark were the golden urn containing the manna
Hebrews9:5 And above the ark were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Now is not the time to speak of these things in detail.
Hebrews9:6 So with these things prepared like this
Hebrews9:7 But only the high priest enters once a year into the inner tent
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit is making clear that the way into the holy place had not yet appeared as long as the old tabernacle was standing.
Hebrews9:9 This was a symbol for the time then present
Hebrews9:10 They served only for matters of food and drink and various washings; they are external regulations imposed until the new order came.
Hebrews9:11 But now Christ has come as the high priest of the good things to come. He passed through the greater and more perfect tent not made with hands
Hebrews9:12 and he entered once for all into the most holy place not by the blood of goats and calves but by his own blood
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a young cow sprinkled on those who are defiled consecrated them and provided ritual purity
Hebrews9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 And so he is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where there is a will
Hebrews9:17 For a will takes effect only at death
Hebrews9:18 So even the first covenant was inaugurated with blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when Moses had spoken every command to all the people according to the law
Hebrews9:20 and said
Hebrews9:21 And both the tabernacle and all the utensils of worship he likewise sprinkled with blood.
Hebrews9:22 Indeed according to the law almost everything was purified with blood
Hebrews9:23 So it was necessary for the sketches of the things in heaven to be purified with these sacrifices
Hebrews9:24 For Christ did not enter a sanctuary made with hands — the representation of the true sanctuary — but into heaven itself
Hebrews9:25 And he did not enter to offer himself again and again
Hebrews9:26 for then he would have had to suffer again and again since the foundation of the world. But now he has appeared once for all at the consummation of the ages to put away sin by his sacrifice.
Hebrews9:27 And just as people are appointed to die once
Hebrews9:28 so also
Hebrews10:1 For the law possesses a shadow of the good things to come but not the reality itself
Hebrews10:2 For otherwise would they not have ceased to be offered
Hebrews10:3 But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins year after year.
Hebrews10:4 For the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins.
Hebrews10:5 So when he came into the world
Hebrews10:6 “ Whole burnt offerings and sin-offerings you took no delight in.
Hebrews10:7 “ Then I said
Hebrews10:8 When he says above
Hebrews10:9 then he says
Hebrews10:10 By his will we have been made holy through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
Hebrews10:11 And every priest stands day after day serving and offering the same sacrifices again and again — sacrifices that can never take away sins.
Hebrews10:12 But when this priest had offered one sacrifice for sins for all time
Hebrews10:13 where he is now waiting until his enemies are made a footstool for his feet.
Hebrews10:14 For by one offering he has perfected for all time those who are made holy.
Hebrews10:15 And the Holy Spirit also witnesses to us
Hebrews10:16 “ This is the covenant that I will establish with them after those days
Hebrews10:17 then he says
Hebrews10:18 Now where there is forgiveness of these
Hebrews10:19 Therefore
Hebrews10:20 by the fresh and living way that he inaugurated for us through the curtain
Hebrews10:21 and since we have a great priest over the house of God
Hebrews10:22 let us draw near with a sincere heart in the assurance that faith brings
Hebrews10:23 And let us hold unwaveringly to the hope that we confess
Hebrews10:24 And let us take thought of how to spur one another on to love and good works
Hebrews10:25 not abandoning our own meetings
Hebrews10:26 For if we deliberately keep on sinning after receiving the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 but only a certain fearful expectation of judgment and a fury of fire that will consume God’s enemies.
Hebrews10:28 Someone who rejected the law of Moses was put to death without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
Hebrews10:29 How much greater punishment do you think that person deserves who has contempt for the Son of God
Hebrews10:30 For we know the one who said
Hebrews10:31 It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 But remember the former days when you endured a harsh conflict of suffering after you were enlightened.
Hebrews10:33 At times you were publicly exposed to abuse and afflictions
Hebrews10:34 For in fact you shared the sufferings of those in prison
Hebrews10:35 So do not throw away your confidence
Hebrews10:36 For you need endurance in order to do God’s will and so receive what is promised.
Hebrews10:37 For just a little longer and he who is coming will arrive and not delay.
Hebrews10:38 But my righteous one will live by faith
Hebrews10:39 But we are not among those who shrink back and thus perish
Hebrews11:1 Now faith is being sure of what we hope for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the people of old received God’s commendation.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the worlds were set in order at God’s command
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered God a greater sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he did not see death
Hebrews11:6 Now without faith it is impossible to please him
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place he would later receive as an inheritance
Hebrews11:9 By faith he lived as a foreigner in the promised land as though it were a foreign country
Hebrews11:10 For he was looking forward to the city with firm foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith
Hebrews11:12 So in fact children were fathered by one man — and this one as good as dead — like the number of stars in the sky and like the innumerable grains of sand on the seashore.
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith without receiving the things promised
Hebrews11:14 For those who speak in such a way make it clear that they are seeking a homeland.
Hebrews11:15 In fact
Hebrews11:16 But as it is
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 God had told him
Hebrews11:19 and he reasoned that God could even raise him from the dead
Hebrews11:20 By faith also Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning the future.
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith
Hebrews11:24 By faith
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to be ill-treated with the people of God than to enjoy sin’s fleeting pleasure.
Hebrews11:26 He regarded abuse suffered for Christ to be greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 By faith he left Egypt without fearing the king’s anger
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of the blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if on dry ground
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the prostitute escaped the destruction of the disobedient
Hebrews11:32 And what more shall I say? For time will fail me if I tell of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 Through faith they conquered kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched raging fire
Hebrews11:35 and women received back their dead raised to life. But others were tortured
Hebrews11:36 And others experienced mocking and flogging
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 (the world was not worthy of them); they wandered in deserts and mountains and caves and openings in the earth.
Hebrews11:39 And these all were commended for their faith
Hebrews11:40 For God had provided something better for us
Hebrews12:1 Therefore
Hebrews12:2 keeping our eyes fixed on Jesus
Hebrews12:3 Think of him who endured such opposition against himself by sinners
Hebrews12:4 You have not yet resisted to the point of bloodshed in your struggle against sin.
Hebrews12:5 And have you forgotten the exhortation addressed to you as sons? “ My son
Hebrews12:6 “ For the Lord disciplines the one he loves and chastises every son he accepts.”
Hebrews12:7 Endure your suffering as discipline; God is treating you as sons. For what son is there that a father does not discipline?
Hebrews12:8 But if you do not experience discipline
Hebrews12:9 Besides
Hebrews12:10 For they disciplined us for a little while as seemed good to them
Hebrews12:11 Now all discipline seems painful at the time
Hebrews12:12 Therefore
Hebrews12:13 and make straight paths for your feet
Hebrews12:14 Pursue peace with everyone
Hebrews12:15 See to it that no one comes short of the grace of God
Hebrews12:16 And see to it that no one becomes an immoral or godless person like Esau
Hebrews12:17 For you know that later when he wanted to inherit the blessing
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come to something that can be touched
Hebrews12:19 and the blast of a trumpet and a voice uttering words such that those who heard begged to hear no more.
Hebrews12:20 For they could not bear what was commanded: “ If even an animal touches the mountain
Hebrews12:21 In fact
Hebrews12:22 But you have come to Mount Zion
Hebrews12:23 and congregation of the firstborn
Hebrews12:24 and to Jesus
Hebrews12:25 Take care not to refuse the one who is speaking! For if they did not escape when they refused the one who warned them on earth
Hebrews12:26 Then his voice shook the earth
Hebrews12:27 Now this phrase “ once more ” indicates the removal of what is shaken
Hebrews12:28 So since we are receiving an unshakable kingdom
Hebrews12:29 For our God is indeed a devouring fire.
Hebrews13:1 Brotherly love must continue.
Hebrews13:2 Do not neglect hospitality
Hebrews13:3 Remember those in prison as though you were in prison with them
Hebrews13:4 Marriage must be honored among all and the marriage bed kept undefiled
Hebrews13:5 Your conduct must be free from the love of money and you must be content with what you have
Hebrews13:6 So we can say with confidence
Hebrews13:7 Remember your leaders
Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever!
Hebrews13:9 Do not be carried away by all sorts of strange teachings. For it is good for the heart to be strengthened by grace
Hebrews13:10 We have an altar that those who serve in the tabernacle have no right to eat from.
Hebrews13:11 For the bodies of those animals whose blood the high priest brings into the sanctuary as an offering for sin are burned outside the camp.
Hebrews13:12 Therefore
Hebrews13:13 We must go out to him
Hebrews13:14 For here we have no lasting city
Hebrews13:15 Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God
Hebrews13:16 And do not neglect to do good and to share what you have
Hebrews13:17 Obey your leaders and submit to them
Hebrews13:18 Pray for us
Hebrews13:19 I especially ask you to pray that I may be restored to you very soon.
Hebrews13:20 Now may the God of peace who by the blood of the eternal covenant brought back from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep
Hebrews13:21 equip you with every good thing to do his will
Hebrews13:22 Now I urge you
Hebrews13:23 You should know that our brother Timothy has been released. If he comes soon
Hebrews13:24 Greetings to all your leaders and all the saints. Those from Italy send you greetings.
Hebrews13:25 Grace be with you all.
James1:1 From James
James1:2 My brothers and sisters
James1:3 because you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
James1:4 And let endurance have its perfect effect
James1:5 But if anyone is deficient in wisdom
James1:6 But he must ask in faith without doubting
James1:7 For that person must not suppose that he will receive anything from the Lord
James1:8 since he is a double-minded individual
James1:9 Now the believer of humble means should take pride in his high position.
James1:10 But the rich person’s pride should be in his humiliation
James1:11 For the sun rises with its heat and dries up the meadow; the petal of the flower falls off and its beauty is lost forever. So also the rich person in the midst of his pursuits will wither away.
James1:12 Happy is the one who endures testing
James1:13 Let no one say when he is tempted
James1:14 But each one is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desires.
James1:15 Then when desire conceives
James1:16 Do not be led astray
James1:17 All generous giving and every perfect gift is from above
James1:18 By his sovereign plan he gave us birth through the message of truth
James1:19 Understand this
James1:20 For human anger does not accomplish God’s righteousness.
James1:21 So put away all filth and evil excess and humbly welcome the message implanted within you
James1:22 But be sure you live out the message and do not merely listen to it and so deceive yourselves.
James1:23 For if someone merely listens to the message and does not live it out
James1:24 For he gazes at himself and then goes out and immediately forgets what sort of person he was.
James1:25 But the one who peers into the perfect law of liberty and fixes his attention there
James1:26 If someone thinks he is religious yet does not bridle his tongue
James1:27 Pure and undefiled religion before God the Father is this: to care for orphans and widows in their misfortune and to keep oneself unstained by the world.
James2:1 My brothers and sisters
James2:2 For if someone comes into your assembly wearing a gold ring and fine clothing
James2:3 do you pay attention to the one who is finely dressed and say
James2:4 If so
James2:5 Listen
James2:6 But you have dishonored the poor! Are not the rich oppressing you and dragging you into the courts?
James2:7 Do they not blaspheme the good name of the one you belong to?
James2:8 But if you fulfill the royal law as expressed in this scripture
James2:9 But if you show prejudice
James2:10 For the one who obeys the whole law but fails in one point has become guilty of all of it.
James2:11 For he who said
James2:12 Speak and act as those who will be judged by a law that gives freedom.
James2:13 For judgment is merciless for the one who has shown no mercy. But mercy triumphs over judgment.
James2:14 What good is it
James2:15 If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacks daily food
James2:16 and one of you says to them
James2:17 So also faith
James2:18 But someone will say
James2:19 You believe that God is one; well and good. Even the demons believe that — and tremble with fear.
James2:20 But would you like evidence
James2:21 Was not Abraham our father justified by works when he offered Isaac his son on the altar?
James2:22 You see that his faith was working together with his works and his faith was perfected by works.
James2:23 And the scripture was fulfilled that says
James2:24 You see that a person is justified by works and not by faith alone.
James2:25 And similarly
James2:26 For just as the body without the spirit is dead
James3:1 Not many of you should become teachers
James3:2 For we all stumble in many ways. If someone does not stumble in what he says
James3:3 And if we put bits into the mouths of horses to get them to obey us
James3:4 Look at ships too: Though they are so large and driven by harsh winds
James3:5 So too the tongue is a small part of the body
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire! The tongue represents the world of wrongdoing among the parts of our bodies. It pollutes the entire body and sets fire to the course of human existence — and is set on fire by hell.
James3:7 For every kind of animal
James3:8 But no human being can subdue the tongue; it is a restless evil
James3:9 With it we bless the Lord and Father
James3:10 From the same mouth come blessing and cursing. These things should not be so
James3:11 A spring does not pour out fresh water and bitter water from the same opening
James3:12 Can a fig tree produce olives
James3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you? By his good conduct he should show his works done in the gentleness that wisdom brings.
James3:14 But if you have bitter jealousy and selfishness in your hearts
James3:15 Such wisdom does not come from above but is earthly
James3:16 For where there is jealousy and selfishness
James3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure
James3:18 And the fruit that consists of righteousness is planted in peace among those who make peace.
James4:1 Where do the conflicts and where do the quarrels among you come from? Is it not from this
James4:2 You desire and you do not have; you murder and envy and you cannot obtain; you quarrel and fight. You do not have because you do not ask;
James4:3 you ask and do not receive because you ask wrongly
James4:4 Adulterers
James4:5 Or do you think the scripture means nothing when it says
James4:6 But he gives greater grace. Therefore it says
James4:7 So submit to God. But resist the devil and he will flee from you.
James4:8 Draw near to God and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands
James4:9 Grieve
James4:10 Humble yourselves before the Lord and he will exalt you.
James4:11 Do not speak against one another
James4:12 But there is only one who is lawgiver and judge — the one who is able to save and destroy. On the other hand
James4:13 Come now
James4:14 You do not know about tomorrow. What is your life like? For you are a puff of smoke that appears for a short time and then vanishes.
James4:15 You ought to say instead
James4:16 But as it is
James4:17 So whoever knows what is good to do and does not do it is guilty of sin.
James5:1 Come now
James5:2 Your riches have rotted and your clothing has become moth-eaten.
James5:3 Your gold and silver have rusted and their rust will be a witness against you. It will consume your flesh like fire. It is in the last days that you have hoarded treasure!
James5:4 Look
James5:5 You have lived indulgently and luxuriously on the earth. You have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
James5:6 You have condemned and murdered the righteous person
James5:7 So be patient
James5:8 You also be patient and strengthen your hearts
James5:9 Do not grumble against one another
James5:10 As an example of suffering and patience
James5:11 Think of how we regard as blessed those who have endured. You have heard of Job’s endurance and you have seen the Lord’s purpose
James5:12 And above all
James5:13 Is anyone among you suffering? He should pray. Is anyone in good spirits? He should sing praises.
James5:14 Is anyone among you ill? He should summon the elders of the church
James5:15 And the prayer of faith will save the one who is sick and the Lord will raise him up — and if he has committed sins
James5:16 So confess your sins to one another and pray for one another so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person has great effectiveness.
James5:17 Elijah was a human being like us
James5:18 Then he prayed again
James5:19 My brothers and sisters
James5:20 he should know that the one who turns a sinner back from his wandering path will save that person’s soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.
1Peter1:1 From Peter
1Peter1:2 according to the foreknowledge of God the Father by being set apart by the Spirit for obedience and for sprinkling with Jesus Christ’s blood. May grace and peace be yours in full measure!
1Peter1:3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! By his great mercy he gave us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead
1Peter1:4 that is
1Peter1:5 who by God’s power are protected through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
1Peter1:6 This brings you great joy
1Peter1:7 Such trials show the proven character of your faith
1Peter1:8 You have not seen him
1Peter1:9 because you are attaining the goal of your faith — the salvation of your souls.
1Peter1:10 Concerning this salvation
1Peter1:11 They probed into what person or time the Spirit of Christ within them was indicating when he testified beforehand about the sufferings appointed for Christ and his subsequent glory.
1Peter1:12 They were shown that they were serving not themselves but you
1Peter1:13 Therefore
1Peter1:14 Like obedient children
1Peter1:15 but
1Peter1:16 for it is written
1Peter1:17 And if you address as Father the one who impartially judges according to each one’s work
1Peter1:18 You know that from your empty way of life inherited from your ancestors you were ransomed — not by perishable things like silver or gold
1Peter1:19 but by precious blood like that of an unblemished and spotless lamb
1Peter1:20 He was foreknown before the foundation of the world but was manifested in these last times for your sake.
1Peter1:21 Through him you now trust in God
1Peter1:22 You have purified your souls by obeying the truth in order to show sincere mutual love. So love one another earnestly from a pure heart.
1Peter1:23 You have been born anew
1Peter1:24 For all flesh is like grass and all its glory like the flower of the grass; the grass withers and the flower falls off
1Peter1:25 but the word of the Lord endures forever. And this is the word that was proclaimed to you.

1Peter2:2 And yearn like newborn infants for pure
1Peter2:3 if you have experienced the Lord’s kindness.
1Peter2:4 So as you come to him
1Peter2:5 you yourselves
1Peter2:6 For it says in scripture
1Peter2:7 So you who believe see his value
1Peter2:8 and a stumbling-stone and a rock to trip over. They stumble because they disobey the word
1Peter2:9 But you are a chosen race
1Peter2:10 You once were not a people
1Peter2:11 Dear friends
1Peter2:12 and maintain good conduct among the non-Christians
1Peter2:13 Be subject to every human institution for the Lord’s sake
1Peter2:14 or to governors as those he commissions to punish wrongdoers and praise those who do good.
1Peter2:15 For God wants you to silence the ignorance of foolish people by doing good.
1Peter2:16 Live as free people
1Peter2:17 Honor all people
1Peter2:18 Slaves
1Peter2:19 For this finds God’s favor
1Peter2:20 For what credit is it if you sin and are mistreated and endure it? But if you do good and suffer and so endure
1Peter2:21 For to this you were called
1Peter2:22 He committed no sin nor was deceit found in his mouth.
1Peter2:23 When he was maligned
1Peter2:24 He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree
1Peter2:25 For you were going astray like sheep but now you have turned back to the shepherd and guardian of your souls.
1Peter3:1 In the same way
1Peter3:2 when they see your pure and reverent conduct.
1Peter3:3 Let your beauty not be external — the braiding of hair and wearing of gold jewelry or fine clothes —
1Peter3:4 but the inner person of the heart
1Peter3:5 For in the same way the holy women who hoped in God long ago adorned themselves by being subject to their husbands
1Peter3:6 like Sarah who obeyed Abraham
1Peter3:7 Husbands
1Peter3:8 Finally
1Peter3:9 Do not return evil for evil or insult for insult
1Peter3:10 For the one who wants to love life and see good days must keep his tongue from evil and his lips from uttering deceit.
1Peter3:11 And he must turn away from evil and do good; he must seek peace and pursue it.
1Peter3:12 For the eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous and his ears are open to their prayer. But the Lord’s face is against those who do evil.
1Peter3:13 For who is going to harm you if you are devoted to what is good?
1Peter3:14 But in fact
1Peter3:15 But set Christ apart as Lord in your hearts and always be ready to give an answer to anyone who asks about the hope you possess.
1Peter3:16 Yet do it with courtesy and respect
1Peter3:17 For it is better to suffer for doing good
1Peter3:18 Because Christ also suffered once for sins
1Peter3:19 In it he went and preached to the spirits in prison
1Peter3:20 after they were disobedient long ago when God patiently waited in the days of Noah as an ark was being constructed. In the ark a few
1Peter3:21 And this prefigured baptism
1Peter3:22 who went into heaven and is at the right hand of God with angels and authorities and powers subject to him.
1Peter4:1 So
1Peter4:2 in that he spends the rest of his time on earth concerned about the will of God and not human desires.
1Peter4:3 For the time that has passed was sufficient for you to do what the non-Christians desire. You lived then in debauchery
1Peter4:4 So they are astonished when you do not rush with them into the same flood of wickedness
1Peter4:5 They will face a reckoning before Jesus Christ who stands ready to judge the living and the dead.
1Peter4:6 Now it was for this very purpose that the gospel was preached to those who are now dead

1John2:2 and he himself is the atoning sacrifice for our sins

1John3:1 (See what sort of love the Father has given to us: that we should be called God’s children — and indeed we are! For this reason the world does not know us: because it did not know him.
1John3:2 Dear friends
1John3:3 And everyone who has this hope focused on him purifies himself
1John3:4 Everyone who practices sin also practices lawlessness; indeed
1John3:5 And you know that Jesus was revealed to take away sins
1John3:6 Everyone who resides in him does not sin; everyone who sins has neither seen him nor known him.
1John3:7 Little children
1John3:8 The one who practices sin is of the devil
1John3:9 Everyone who has been fathered by God does not practice sin
1John3:10 By this the children of God and the children of the devil are revealed: Everyone who does not practice righteousness — the one who does not love his fellow Christian — is not of God.
1John3:11 For this is the gospel message that you have heard from the beginning: that we should love one another
1John3:12 not like Cain who was of the evil one and brutally murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his deeds were evil
1John3:13 Therefore do not be surprised
1John3:14 We know that we have crossed over from death to life because we love our fellow Christians. The one who does not love remains in death.
1John3:15 Everyone who hates his fellow Christian is a murderer
1John3:16 We have come to know love by this: that Jesus laid down his life for us; thus we ought to lay down our lives for our fellow Christians.
1John3:17 But whoever has the world’s possessions and sees his fellow Christian in need and shuts off his compassion against him
1John3:18 Little children
1John3:19 And by this we will know that we are of the truth and will convince our conscience in his presence
1John3:20 that if our conscience condemns us
1John3:21 Dear friends
1John3:22 and whatever we ask we receive from him
1John3:23 Now this is his commandment: that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another
1John3:24 And the person who keeps his commandments resides in God

1John4:2 By this you know the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesses Jesus as the Christ who has come in the flesh is from God
1John4:3 but every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God

1John4:5 They are from the world; therefore they speak from the world’s perspective and the world listens to them.
1John4:6 We are from God; the person who knows God listens to us
1John4:7 Dear friends
1John4:8 The person who does not love does not know God
1John4:9 By this the love of God is revealed in us: that God has sent his one and only Son into the world so that we may live through him.
1John4:10 In this is love: not that we have loved God
1John4:11 Dear friends
1John4:12 No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another
1John4:13 By this we know that we reside in God and he in us: in that he has given us of his Spirit.

1John4:15 If anyone confesses that Jesus is the Son of God
1John4:16 And we have come to know and to believe the love that God has in us. God is love
1John4:17 By this love is perfected with us
1John4:18 There is no fear in love
1John4:19 We love because he loved us first.
1John4:20 If anyone says “I love God” and yet hates his fellow Christian
1John4:21 And the commandment we have from him is this: that the one who loves God should love his fellow Christian too.

1John5:3 For this is the love of God: that we keep his commandments. And his commandments do not weigh us down
1John5:4 because everyone who has been fathered by God conquers the world. This is the conquering power that has conquered the world: our faith.
